eoportal.name: CartoSat-2
eoportal.summary: The satellite was decommissioned in 2019, ending 12 years of mission life.

faq.a: CartoSat-2 was able to provide specific spot images that were useful for cartographic applications, urban and rural infrastructure development and management, and various other Land Information System (LIS) and Geographical Information System (GIS) applications.
faq.q: What was the use of CartoSat-2?

faq.a: Yes, CartoSat-2 was built, launched and operated by the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO).
faq.q: Was CartoSat-2 an Indian satellite?

faq.a: The name CartoSat-2 comes from the concatenation of cartography (the science or practice of drawing maps) and satellite. CartoSat-2 is the second satellite of the CartoSat Series.
faq.q: What is the full form of CartoSat-2?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该卫星于2019年退役，结束了为期12年的任务寿命。
eoportal.summary_en: The satellite was decommissioned in 2019, ending 12 years of mission life.

faq_zh.a: CartoSat-2卫星能够提供特定的点位影像，这些影像可用于制图应用、城市和农村基础设施的开发与管理，以及多种其他陆地信息系统（LIS）和地理信息系统（GIS）应用。
是的，CartoSat-2由印度空间研究组织（ISRO）建造、发射和运营。
CartoSat-2这个名称来源于制图学（绘制地图的科学或实践）与卫星两个词的组合。CartoSat-2是CartoSat系列的第二颗卫星。
faq_zh.q: CartoSat-2这个名称的全称是什么？

faq_en.a: CartoSat-2 was able to provide specific spot images that were useful for cartographic applications, urban and rural infrastructure development and management, and various other Land Information System (LIS) and Geographical Information System (GIS) applications.
faq_en.q: What was the use of CartoSat-2?

faq_en.a: Yes, CartoSat-2 was built, launched and operated by the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO).
faq_en.q: Was CartoSat-2 an Indian satellite?

faq_en.a: The name CartoSat-2 comes from the concatenation of cartography (the science or practice of drawing maps) and satellite. CartoSat-2 is the second satellite of the CartoSat Series.
faq_en.q: What is the full form of CartoSat-2?

eoportal.name: CartoSat-2A
eoportal.summary: The spacecraft is being monitored and controlled from the ISRO mission control center in Bangalore, India using the ISTRAC network of stations at Bangalore, Lucknow, Mauritius, Bearslake in Russia, Biak in Indonesia and Svalbard in Norway.
eoportal.summary_zh: 该航天器由位于印度班加罗尔的ISRO任务控制中心负责监控，通过ISTRAC地面站网络进行控制，这些站点分布于班加罗尔、勒克瑙、毛里求斯、俄罗斯的贝尔斯克、印度尼西亚的比亚克岛以及挪威的斯瓦尔巴群岛。
eoportal.summary_en: The spacecraft is being monitored and controlled from the ISRO mission control center in Bangalore, India using the ISTRAC network of stations at Bangalore, Lucknow, Mauritius, Bearslake in Russia, Biak in Indonesia and Svalbard in Norway.

eoportal.name: CartoSat-2B
eoportal.summary: CartoSat-2B is the third satellite in the CartoSat-2 series, including CartoSat-2 and CartoSat-2A, launched in January 2007 and April 2008, respectively.

faq.a: CartoSat-2B was built by the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) for the Ministry of Defence of the Government of India.
faq.q: Who built CartoSat-2B?

faq.a: There are three CartoSat-2 satellite missions: CartoSat-2, CartoSat-2A, and CartoSat-2B.
faq.q: How many satellites are in the CartoSat-2 satellite series?

faq.a: CartoSat-2B was launched on July 12, 2010 from the Satish Dhawan Space Centre (SDSC) in southern India.
faq.q: When was CartoSat-2B launched?

eoportal.summary_zh: CartoSat-2B 是 CartoSat-2 系列的第三颗卫星，该系列包括于 2007 年 1 月发射的 CartoSat-2 和于 2008 年 4 月发射的 CartoSat-2A。
eoportal.summary_en: CartoSat-2B is the third satellite in the CartoSat-2 series, including CartoSat-2 and CartoSat-2A, launched in January 2007 and April 2008, respectively.

faq_zh.a: CartoSat-2B 由印度空间研究组织 (ISRO) 为印度政府国防部建造。
CartoSat-2 卫星系列包括三颗卫星：CartoSat-2、CartoSat-2A 和 CartoSat-2B。
faq_zh.q: CartoSat-2 卫星系列有多少颗卫星？

faq_zh.a: CartoSat-2B 于 2010 年 7 月 12 日从印度南部的萨迪什·达万航天中心 (SDSC) 发射升空。

faq_en.a: CartoSat-2B was built by the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) for the Ministry of Defence of the Government of India.
faq_en.q: Who built CartoSat-2B?

faq_en.a: There are three CartoSat-2 satellite missions: CartoSat-2, CartoSat-2A, and CartoSat-2B.
faq_en.q: How many satellites are in the CartoSat-2 satellite series?

faq_en.a: CartoSat-2B was launched on July 12, 2010 from the Satish Dhawan Space Centre (SDSC) in southern India.
faq_en.q: When was CartoSat-2B launched?

eoportal.name: CartoSat-2D
eoportal.summary: It is the fifth in a series of seven satellites of the CartoSat-2 series.

faq.a: CartoSat-2D provides regular, high resolution remote sensing imagery, which can be used for cartography, environmental monitoring, disaster relief, and event monitoring. For example, the satellite may be used in determining the status of water sources, which can allow effective planning for efficient usage of water.
faq.q: What is the use of CartoSat-2D?

faq.a: CartoSat-2D is operated by ISRO (Indian Space Research Organisation).
faq.q: Who operates CartoSat-2D?

faq.a: There are seven CartoSat-2 satellites: CartoSat 2, 2A, 2B, 2C, 2D, 2E, and 2F. CartoSat-2D is the fifth satellite in this series.
faq.q: How many CartoSat-2 satellites are there?

eoportal.summary_zh: 它是CartoSat-2系列七颗卫星中的第五颗。
eoportal.summary_en: It is the fifth in a series of seven satellites of the CartoSat-2 series.

faq_zh.a: CartoSat-2D提供定期的高分辨率遥感图像，可用于测绘、环境监测、灾害救援和事件监测。例如，该卫星可用于确定水源状况，从而为有效规划高效用水提供依据。
CartoSat-2D由ISRO（印度空间研究组织）运营。
共有七颗CartoSat-2卫星：CartoSat 2、2A、2B、2C、2D、2E和2F。CartoSat-2D是该系列的第五颗卫星。

faq_en.a: CartoSat-2D provides regular, high resolution remote sensing imagery, which can be used for cartography, environmental monitoring, disaster relief, and event monitoring. For example, the satellite may be used in determining the status of water sources, which can allow effective planning for efficient usage of water.
faq_en.q: What is the use of CartoSat-2D?

faq_en.a: CartoSat-2D is operated by ISRO (Indian Space Research Organisation).
faq_en.q: Who operates CartoSat-2D?

faq_en.a: There are seven CartoSat-2 satellites: CartoSat 2, 2A, 2B, 2C, 2D, 2E, and 2F. CartoSat-2D is the fifth satellite in this series.
faq_en.q: How many CartoSat-2 satellites are there?

eoportal.name: CartoSat-2E
eoportal.summary: The mission is currently operational,  having been launched in June 2017 with the objective to collect high-resolution, large-scale imagery to be used in urban planning, infrastructure development, utilities planning and traffic management.

faq.a: CartoSat-2E is an Earth observation mission, aiming to design, develop, launch and operate an advanced optical remote sensing satellite for providing spot imagery with high spatial resolution, to be used in urban planning, infrastructure development, utilities planning and traffic management.
faq.q: What is the purpose of CartoSat-2E?

faq.a: CartoSat-2E was launched on 23 June 2017 on ISRO’S PSLV-C38 vehicle from the Satish Dhawan Space Center in India, along with 29 co-passenger satellites onboard.
faq.q: How was CartoSat-2E launched?

faq.a: CartoSat-2E has an orbital period of 97.4 minutes.
faq.q: What is the satellite's orbital period?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该任务自2017年6月发射以来一直运行正常，旨在采集高分辨率、大范围的遥感影像，用于城市规划、基础设施建设、公用事业规划和交通管理。
eoportal.summary_en: The mission is currently operational,  having been launched in June 2017 with the objective to collect high-resolution, large-scale imagery to be used in urban planning, infrastructure development, utilities planning and traffic management.

faq_zh.a: CartoSat-2E是一项地球观测任务，旨在设计、开发、发射并运行一颗先进的光学遥感卫星，以提供高空间分辨率的点状影像，应用于城市规划、基础设施建设、公用事业规划和交通管理。
CartoSat-2E于2017年6月23日由ISRO的PSLV-C38运载火箭从印度的萨迪什·达万航天中心发射升空，同时还搭载了29颗同行卫星。
CartoSat-2E的轨道周期为97.4分钟。
faq_zh.q: 该卫星的轨道周期是多少？

faq_en.a: CartoSat-2E is an Earth observation mission, aiming to design, develop, launch and operate an advanced optical remote sensing satellite for providing spot imagery with high spatial resolution, to be used in urban planning, infrastructure development, utilities planning and traffic management.
faq_en.q: What is the purpose of CartoSat-2E?

faq_en.a: CartoSat-2E was launched on 23 June 2017 on ISRO’S PSLV-C38 vehicle from the Satish Dhawan Space Center in India, along with 29 co-passenger satellites onboard.
faq_en.q: How was CartoSat-2E launched?

faq_en.a: CartoSat-2E has an orbital period of 97.4 minutes.
faq_en.q: What is the satellite's orbital period?

eoportal.name: CloudSat
taxonomy: Cloud type, amount and cloud top temperature
Downward long-wave irradiance at Earth surface
Precipitation intensity at the surface (liquid or solid)
eoportal.summary: By collecting data concerning clouds, it improves the way clouds are represented in global models and improves predictions of cloud activity.

faq.a: CloudSat emits microwaves and detects how much is reflected back from clouds to calculate their vertical structure and ice/water content.
faq.q: What does CloudSat measure?

faq.a: The two cloud observing satellites CloudSat and CALIPSO (Cloud-Aerosol Lidar and Infrared Pathfinder Satellite Observations) were launched aboard NASA’s Boeing Delta II launch vehicle at the Vandenberg Air Force Base in California, USA, on 28 April 2006.
faq.q: What were the launch details of CloudSat?

faq.a: C-Train is a constellation of two cloud observing satellites named CloudSat and CALIPSO (Cloud-Aerosol Lidar and Infrared Pathfinder Satellite Observations), that travel along the same orbit within seconds of each other.
faq.q: What is C-Train?

eoportal.summary_zh: 通过收集有关云的数据，CloudSat改进了云在全球模型中的表征方式，并提升了对云活动的预测能力。
eoportal.summary_en: By collecting data concerning clouds, it improves the way clouds are represented in global models and improves predictions of cloud activity.

faq_zh.a: CloudSat发射微波并检测从云层反射回来的微波量，从而计算云层的垂直结构以及冰/水含量。
CloudSat和CALIPSO（云-气溶胶激光雷达和红外探路者卫星观测）这两颗云观测卫星于2006年4月28日搭载NASA的波音Delta II运载火箭，从美国加利福尼亚州范登堡空军基地发射升空。
C-Train是一个由两颗云观测卫星CloudSat和CALIPSO（云-气溶胶激光雷达和红外探路者卫星观测）组成的星座，它们沿着同一轨道运行，彼此间隔仅数秒。

faq_en.a: CloudSat emits microwaves and detects how much is reflected back from clouds to calculate their vertical structure and ice/water content.
faq_en.q: What does CloudSat measure?

faq_en.a: The two cloud observing satellites CloudSat and CALIPSO (Cloud-Aerosol Lidar and Infrared Pathfinder Satellite Observations) were launched aboard NASA’s Boeing Delta II launch vehicle at the Vandenberg Air Force Base in California, USA, on 28 April 2006.
faq_en.q: What were the launch details of CloudSat?

faq_en.a: C-Train is a constellation of two cloud observing satellites named CloudSat and CALIPSO (Cloud-Aerosol Lidar and Infrared Pathfinder Satellite Observations), that travel along the same orbit within seconds of each other.
faq_en.q: What is C-Train?

eoportal.name: CO2Image
eoportal.summary:
CO2Image aims to monitor facility-scale CO2 emissions, as well as to complement existing and planned Copernicus greenhouse gas monitoring missions, such as CO2M
.

faq.a: CO2Image has been developed by a number of German Aerospace Centre (DLR) Institutes, the Institute of Atmospheric Physics, the Institute of Optical Sensor Systems, the Space Operations centre and the Earth Observation Centre.
faq.q: Who developed the CO2Image mission?

faq.a: CO2Image aims to measure atmospheric column concentrations of CO2 at the scale of individual small and medium size power plants and industrial facilities.
faq.q: What is the aim of CO2Image?

faq.a: The CO2Image mission carries a single instrument, known as the CO2 Sensing Imaging Spectrometer (COSIS).
faq.q: What instruments does CO2Image carry?

eoportal.summary_zh: CO2Image卫星旨在监测设施级别的二氧化碳排放，并补充现有及规划中的哥白尼温室气体监测任务，例如CO2M。
eoportal.summary_en:
CO2Image aims to monitor facility-scale CO2 emissions, as well as to complement existing and planned Copernicus greenhouse gas monitoring missions, such as CO2M
.

faq_zh.a: CO2Image由德国宇航中心（DLR）的多个研究所共同开发，包括大气物理研究所、光学传感器系统研究所、空间操作中心和地球观测中心。
CO2Image旨在测量个体中小型发电厂和工业设施尺度的大气二氧化碳柱浓度。
CO2Image搭载了一台名为CO2 Sensing Imaging Spectrometer (COSIS)的单一仪器。

faq_en.a: CO2Image has been developed by a number of German Aerospace Centre (DLR) Institutes, the Institute of Atmospheric Physics, the Institute of Optical Sensor Systems, the Space Operations centre and the Earth Observation Centre.
faq_en.q: Who developed the CO2Image mission?

faq_en.a: CO2Image aims to measure atmospheric column concentrations of CO2 at the scale of individual small and medium size power plants and industrial facilities.
faq_en.q: What is the aim of CO2Image?

faq_en.a: The CO2Image mission carries a single instrument, known as the CO2 Sensing Imaging Spectrometer (COSIS).
faq_en.q: What instruments does CO2Image carry?

eoportal.name: DubaiSat-1
eoportal.summary: The satellite provided high-resolution images in the visible spectrum for numerous applications, including infrastructure planning and disaster monitoring.

faq.a: DubaiSat-1 launched on 29 July, 2009 at 11:46 GMT-7.
faq.q: When was DubaiSat-1 launched?

faq.a: Dubai-Sat-1 was replaced by DubaiSat-2 which was launched on 21 November, 2013.
faq.q: Was DubaiSat-1 replaced by another satellite?

faq.a: DubaiSat-1 supported infrastructure development and aided decision makers in rural and urban planning, transportation and mapping. It was the UAE’s first satellite and launched them into the Space industry.
faq.q: Why was DubaiSat-1 special for the UAE?

eoportal.agency_zh: MBRSC（迪拜穆罕默德·本·拉希德航天中心），UAE SA（阿联酋航天局）
eoportal.summary_zh: 该卫星在可见光谱段提供高分辨率图像，服务于众多应用领域，包括基础设施规划和灾害监测。
eoportal.summary_en: The satellite provided high-resolution images in the visible spectrum for numerous applications, including infrastructure planning and disaster monitoring.

faq_zh.a: DubaiSat-1于2009年7月29日 GMT-7时间11:46发射。
DubaiSat-1由2013年11月21日发射的DubaiSat-2接替。
faq_zh.q: DubaiSat-1是否被另一颗卫星替代了？

faq_zh.a: DubaiSat-1支持了基础设施发展，并协助决策者进行城乡规划、交通及测绘工作。它是阿联酋的首颗卫星，引领该国进入了航天工业领域。
faq_zh.q: DubaiSat-1对阿联酋有何特殊意义？

faq_en.a: DubaiSat-1 launched on 29 July, 2009 at 11:46 GMT-7.
faq_en.q: When was DubaiSat-1 launched?

faq_en.a: Dubai-Sat-1 was replaced by DubaiSat-2 which was launched on 21 November, 2013.
faq_en.q: Was DubaiSat-1 replaced by another satellite?

faq_en.a: DubaiSat-1 supported infrastructure development and aided decision makers in rural and urban planning, transportation and mapping. It was the UAE’s first satellite and launched them into the Space industry.
faq_en.q: Why was DubaiSat-1 special for the UAE?

eoportal.name: DubaiSat-2
eoportal.summary: DubaiSat-2 was launched in November 2013 from the Dombarovsky launch site in Russia.

faq.a: DubaiSat-2 was launched in November 2013 on a Dnepr launch vehicle, from the Dombarovsky launch site in Russia.
faq.q: When did DubaiSat-2 launch?

faq.a: The main objectives of DubaiSat-2 are to further develop the space program and associated sectors in the UAE, in addition to providing continuity and major improvements to the previous DubaiSat mission, DubaiSat-1.
faq.q: What are the main objectives of DubaiSat-2?

faq.a: DubaiSat-2 was developed by EIAST (Emirates Institute for Advanced Science and Technology) beginning in 2009 in a collaborative effort with SI (Satrec Initiative) from Daejeon, Korea.
faq.q: Who built DubaiSat-2?

eoportal.agency_zh: MBRSC（穆罕默德·本·拉希德航天中心），UAE SA（阿联酋航天局）
eoportal.summary_zh: 迪拜卫星-2（DubaiSat-2）于2013年11月从俄罗斯的多姆巴罗夫斯基发射场发射升空。
eoportal.summary_en: DubaiSat-2 was launched in November 2013 from the Dombarovsky launch site in Russia.

faq_zh.a: 迪拜卫星-2（DubaiSat-2）于2013年11月搭载第聂伯运载火箭，从俄罗斯的多姆巴罗夫斯基发射场发射升空。
faq_zh.q: 迪拜卫星-2（DubaiSat-2）是什么时候发射的？

faq_zh.a: 迪拜卫星-2（DubaiSat-2）的主要目标是进一步发展阿联酋的太空计划及相关产业，并为之前的迪拜卫星-1（DubaiSat-1）任务提供延续性和重大改进。
faq_zh.q: 迪拜卫星-2（DubaiSat-2）的主要目标是什么？

faq_zh.a: 迪拜卫星-2（DubaiSat-2）由EIAST（阿联酋先进技术研究院）于2009年开始研发，并与韩国大田的SI（Satrec Initiative）合作完成。

faq_en.a: DubaiSat-2 was launched in November 2013 on a Dnepr launch vehicle, from the Dombarovsky launch site in Russia.
faq_en.q: When did DubaiSat-2 launch?

faq_en.a: The main objectives of DubaiSat-2 are to further develop the space program and associated sectors in the UAE, in addition to providing continuity and major improvements to the previous DubaiSat mission, DubaiSat-1.
faq_en.q: What are the main objectives of DubaiSat-2?

faq_en.a: DubaiSat-2 was developed by EIAST (Emirates Institute for Advanced Science and Technology) beginning in 2009 in a collaborative effort with SI (Satrec Initiative) from Daejeon, Korea.
faq_en.q: Who built DubaiSat-2?

eoportal.name: Himawari-10
eoportal.summary: This mission will succeed Himiwari-9, providing continuity of observations and next-generation meteorological and environmental monitoring.

faq.a: Himawari-10 will monitor Earth’s atmosphere, oceans and surface conditions to provide higher accuracy weather forecasting for the Asia-Oceania region.
faq.q: What is the purpose of Himawari-10?

faq.a: Himawari-10 data will be shared publicly online, as with the Himawari-9 mission, through the National Institute of Information and Communications Technology.
faq.q: Can the public access data from Himawari-10?

faq.a: Himawari-10 will feature upgraded imaging and sounding instruments (GHMI and GHMS) with improved spatial and spectral resolution for more precise meteorological and environmental monitoring.
faq.q: How is Himawari-10 different from Himawari-9?

eoportal.agency_zh: JMA（日本气象厅），NICT（日本情报通信研究机构）
eoportal.summary_zh: 该任务将接替Himawari-9卫星，以确保观测的延续性，并提供下一代气象与环境监测能力。
eoportal.summary_en: This mission will succeed Himiwari-9, providing continuity of observations and next-generation meteorological and environmental monitoring.

faq_zh.a: Himawari-10将监测地球大气、海洋和地表状况，为亚太地区提供更高精度的天气预报。
与Himawari-9任务类似，Himawari-10的数据将通过日本情报通信研究机构在网上公开共享。
Himawari-10配备了升级的成像和探测仪器（GHMI和GHMS），其空间和光谱分辨率得到提升，可实现更精确的气象与环境监测。

faq_en.a: Himawari-10 will monitor Earth’s atmosphere, oceans and surface conditions to provide higher accuracy weather forecasting for the Asia-Oceania region.
faq_en.q: What is the purpose of Himawari-10?

faq_en.a: Himawari-10 data will be shared publicly online, as with the Himawari-9 mission, through the National Institute of Information and Communications Technology.
faq_en.q: Can the public access data from Himawari-10?

faq_en.a: Himawari-10 will feature upgraded imaging and sounding instruments (GHMI and GHMS) with improved spatial and spectral resolution for more precise meteorological and environmental monitoring.
faq_en.q: How is Himawari-10 different from Himawari-9?

eoportal.name: Landsat-6
eoportal.summary: As a result, Landsat-4 and -5 operations were continued well beyond their expected operational lifetimes.

faq.a: Landsat-6 was launched in October 1993 on a Titan 2 booster from Vandenberg Air Force Base (VAFB) California, United States.
faq.q: When was Landsat-6 launched?

faq.a: A formal review conducted by the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) revealed a ruptured fuel chamber was the reason for the failure of Landsat-6. The separation from the booster rocket occurred properly but the ruptured fuel chamber prevented fuel from reaching the motor, resulting in the spacecraft tumbling instead of accumulating enough energy to reach its planned orbit.
faq.q: Why did Landsat-6 fail?

faq.a: No, Landsat-6 failed upon launch in October 1993.
faq.q: Is Landsat-6 still operational?

eoportal.summary_zh: 因此，Landsat-4 和 Landsat-5 的运行时间远远超出了其预期的在轨寿命。
eoportal.summary_en: As a result, Landsat-4 and -5 operations were continued well beyond their expected operational lifetimes.

faq_zh.a: Landsat-6 于 1993 年 10 月从美国加利福尼亚州范登堡空军基地（VAFB）由 Titan 2 运载火箭发射升空。
美国国家海洋和大气管理局（NOAA）进行的一项正式调查表明，燃料室破裂是导致 Landsat-6 失败的原因。卫星与运载火箭的分离正常，但破裂的燃料室阻止了燃料流向发动机，导致卫星翻滚而非积累足够能量进入预定轨道。
不，Landsat-6 于 1993 年 10 月发射失败，并未投入运行。
faq_zh.q: Landsat-6 目前是否仍在运行？

faq_en.a: Landsat-6 was launched in October 1993 on a Titan 2 booster from Vandenberg Air Force Base (VAFB) California, United States.
faq_en.q: When was Landsat-6 launched?

faq_en.a: A formal review conducted by the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) revealed a ruptured fuel chamber was the reason for the failure of Landsat-6. The separation from the booster rocket occurred properly but the ruptured fuel chamber prevented fuel from reaching the motor, resulting in the spacecraft tumbling instead of accumulating enough energy to reach its planned orbit.
faq_en.q: Why did Landsat-6 fail?

faq_en.a: No, Landsat-6 failed upon launch in October 1993.
faq_en.q: Is Landsat-6 still operational?

eoportal.name: Landsat-7
taxonomy: Cloud type, amount and cloud top temperature
Ocean imagery and water leaving spectral radiance
Sea ice cover, edge and thickness
eoportal.summary: Although it had an initial design life of seven years, Landsat-7 remains operational as of July 2022 and was joined by Landsat-8 in February 2013, and Landsat-9 in September 2021.

faq.a: Landsat 7's nominal science mission ended in April 2022. Landsat 7 is now in an extended science mission after having its orbit lowered by about 8 kilometers to make way for Landsat 9.
faq.q: Is Landsat-7 still operational?

faq.a: Landsat-7 was developed to enhance the medium-resolution multispectral imagery of Earth’s continental surfaces provided by previous Landsat satellites. This data is used for land cover/change monitoring, agricultural forecasting, disaster response, urban planning, land and water resource management, and ecosystem monitoring.
faq.q: What is Landsat-7 used for?

faq.a: The ETM+ (Enhanced Thermal Mapper Plus) onboard Landsat-7 images with a spatial resolution of 15 m for the PAN band, 30 m for VNIR/SWIR bands and 60 m for the TIR band.
faq.q: What spatial resolution can Landsat-7 image?

eoportal.summary_zh: 尽管初始设计寿命为七年，陆地卫星-7截至2022年7月仍在运行，并与2013年2月发射的陆地卫星-8和2021年9月发射的陆地卫星-9共同执行任务。
eoportal.summary_en: Although it had an initial design life of seven years, Landsat-7 remains operational as of July 2022 and was joined by Landsat-8 in February 2013, and Landsat-9 in September 2021.

faq_zh.a: 陆地卫星-7的名义科学任务已于2022年4月结束。在为陆地卫星-9让路将其轨道降低约8公里后，陆地卫星-7目前正在进行扩展科学任务。
faq_zh.q: 陆地卫星-7是否仍在运行？

faq_zh.a: 陆地卫星-7旨在增强前代陆地卫星提供的地球大陆表面中分辨率多光谱图像。这些数据用于土地覆盖/变化监测、农业预测、灾害响应、城市规划、土地和水资源管理以及生态系统监测。
陆地卫星-7搭载的ETM+（增强型热辐射加成仪）可在全色波段提供15米空间分辨率，在可见光-近红外/短波红外波段提供30米空间分辨率，在热红外波段提供60米空间分辨率。
faq_zh.q: 陆地卫星-7的图像空间分辨率是多少？

faq_en.a: Landsat 7's nominal science mission ended in April 2022. Landsat 7 is now in an extended science mission after having its orbit lowered by about 8 kilometers to make way for Landsat 9.
faq_en.q: Is Landsat-7 still operational?

faq_en.a: Landsat-7 was developed to enhance the medium-resolution multispectral imagery of Earth’s continental surfaces provided by previous Landsat satellites. This data is used for land cover/change monitoring, agricultural forecasting, disaster response, urban planning, land and water resource management, and ecosystem monitoring.
faq_en.q: What is Landsat-7 used for?

faq_en.a: The ETM+ (Enhanced Thermal Mapper Plus) onboard Landsat-7 images with a spatial resolution of 15 m for the PAN band, 30 m for VNIR/SWIR bands and 60 m for the TIR band.
faq_en.q: What spatial resolution can Landsat-7 image?

eoportal.name: Landsat-9
taxonomy: Cloud type, amount and cloud top temperature
Ocean imagery and water leaving spectral radiance
Sea ice cover, edge and thickness
eoportal.summary: Landsat-9 mission objectives include the collection and archival of moderate-resolution multispectral data, to be made freely available to worldwide users.

faq.a: The resolution of the images taken by Landsat-9 vary depending on  the spectral band used. Thermal bands have a spatial resolution of 100 m, multispectral bands have a resolution of 30 m, and the panchromatic band has a spatial resolution of 15 m.
faq.q: What is the resolution of Landsat-9?

faq.a: Landsat-9 was launched by the National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA) and the United States Geological Survey (USGS) in September 2021, with a design life of five years. The satellite was launched on board an Atlas V rocket from Vandenberg’s Space Launch Complex, California USA.
faq.q: Who launched Landsat-9?

faq.a: Landsat-9 is designed to provide data continuity to previous Landsat missions, as well as complement Landsat-8 in a coplanar orbit. Landsat-9 is mainly used for monitoring and managing land resources, which includes land, ocean, snow and ice measurements, as well as cloud and radiation measurements through thermal and multispectral imagery.
faq.q: What will Landsat-9 do?

eoportal.summary_zh: Landsat-9 任务的目标包括收集并归档中等分辨率的多光谱数据，这些数据将免费提供给全球用户。
eoportal.summary_en: Landsat-9 mission objectives include the collection and archival of moderate-resolution multispectral data, to be made freely available to worldwide users.

faq_zh.a: Landsat-9 拍摄图像的分辨率因使用的光谱波段而异。热红外波段的空间分辨率为100米，多光谱波段分辨率为30米，而全色波段的空间分辨率为15米。
Landsat-9 由美国国家航空航天局（NASA）和美国地质调查局（USGS）于2021年9月发射，设计寿命为五年。该卫星搭载一枚 Atlas V 火箭从美国加州范登堡太空发射场发射升空。
Landsat-9 旨在为之前的陆地卫星任务提供数据连续性，并作为 Landsat-8 在共面轨道上的补充。Landsat-9 主要用于监测和管理土地资源，这包括土地、海洋、冰雪测量，以及通过热红外和多光谱成像进行云和辐射测量。
faq_zh.q: Landsat-9 的主要任务是什么？

faq_en.a: The resolution of the images taken by Landsat-9 vary depending on  the spectral band used. Thermal bands have a spatial resolution of 100 m, multispectral bands have a resolution of 30 m, and the panchromatic band has a spatial resolution of 15 m.
faq_en.q: What is the resolution of Landsat-9?

faq_en.a: Landsat-9 was launched by the National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA) and the United States Geological Survey (USGS) in September 2021, with a design life of five years. The satellite was launched on board an Atlas V rocket from Vandenberg’s Space Launch Complex, California USA.
faq_en.q: Who launched Landsat-9?

faq_en.a: Landsat-9 is designed to provide data continuity to previous Landsat missions, as well as complement Landsat-8 in a coplanar orbit. Landsat-9 is mainly used for monitoring and managing land resources, which includes land, ocean, snow and ice measurements, as well as cloud and radiation measurements through thermal and multispectral imagery.
faq_en.q: What will Landsat-9 do?

eoportal.name: MethaneSAT
eoportal.summary: The mission came to an early end when the operations team lost contact with the satellite on June 20, 2025, concluding its scientific activities after just over a year in orbit.

faq.a: MethaneSAT was launched on 4 March 2024.
faq.q: When was MethaneSAT launched?

faq.a: The imaging spectrometer aboard MethanSAT observed a large swath with a high resolution, which enabled it to determine the source and rate of emission, something only few other methane monitoring satellites accomplish.
faq.q: How did MethaneSAT surpass other methane monitoring satellites?

faq.a: MethaneSAT operated on a sun-synchronous polar orbit with an altitude of 526 km and a period of 100 minutes.
faq.q: What orbit did MethaneSAT undertake?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该任务于2025年6月20日提前结束，当时运营团队与卫星失去了联系，其在轨科学活动仅持续了一年多。
eoportal.summary_en: The mission came to an early end when the operations team lost contact with the satellite on June 20, 2025, concluding its scientific activities after just over a year in orbit.

faq_zh.a: MethaneSAT于2024年3月4日发射。
MethaneSAT搭载的成像光谱仪以高分辨率观测了大范围区域，使其能够确定排放源和排放速率，这是其他少数甲烷监测卫星才能实现的。
faq_zh.q: MethaneSAT如何超越了其他甲烷监测卫星？

faq_zh.a: MethaneSAT运行在太阳同步极轨道，轨道高度526公里，周期100分钟。

faq_en.a: MethaneSAT was launched on 4 March 2024.
faq_en.q: When was MethaneSAT launched?

faq_en.a: The imaging spectrometer aboard MethanSAT observed a large swath with a high resolution, which enabled it to determine the source and rate of emission, something only few other methane monitoring satellites accomplish.
faq_en.q: How did MethaneSAT surpass other methane monitoring satellites?

faq_en.a: MethaneSAT operated on a sun-synchronous polar orbit with an altitude of 526 km and a period of 100 minutes.
faq_en.q: What orbit did MethaneSAT undertake?

eoportal.name: NigeriaSat-2
taxonomy: NigeriaSat 2 Remote Sensing (Med and High Res)
eoportal.summary: It aims to provide high-resolution imagery of the Earth’s surface, with applications in supporting food security and agricultural data collection, mapping and security, as well as the provision of data continuity for the NigeriaSat-1 system.
instruments: NigeriaSat 2 Remote Sensing (Med and High Res)

faq.a: NigeriaSat-2 was launched August 17, 2011 aboard a Dnepr-1 launch vehicle from the Yasny/Dombarovsky launch site in Russia.
faq.q: When was NigeriaSat-2 launched?

faq.a: NigeriaSat-2 has the primary objective of providing high resolution optical imagery of the Earth’s surface. Its data has applications in supporting food security and agricultural data collection, mapping and security, as well as the provision of data continuity for the NigeriaSat-1 system.
faq.q: What does NigeriaSat-2 do?

faq.a: NigeriaSat-2 was designed and developed by Surrey Satellite Technology Ltd., but is owned and operated by the Nigerian government organisation NASRDA (National Space Research and Development Agency).
faq.q: Who made NigeriaSat-2?

eoportal.agency_zh: NASRDA（尼日利亚国家空间研究与发展局）
eoportal.summary_zh: 该卫星旨在提供地球表面的高分辨率影像，其应用包括支持粮食安全与农业数据收集、测绘与国土安全，并为NigeriaSat-1系统提供数据连续性。
eoportal.summary_en: It aims to provide high-resolution imagery of the Earth’s surface, with applications in supporting food security and agricultural data collection, mapping and security, as well as the provision of data continuity for the NigeriaSat-1 system.

faq_zh.a: NigeriaSat-2 于2011年8月17日搭载 Dnepr-1 运载火箭，从俄罗斯的亚斯尼/多姆巴罗夫斯基发射场发射升空。
NigeriaSat-2 的主要目标是提供地球表面的高分辨率光学影像。其数据可应用于支持粮食安全与农业数据收集、测绘与国土安全，并为NigeriaSat-1系统提供数据连续性。
faq_zh.q: NigeriaSat-2 的主要任务是什么？

faq_zh.a: NigeriaSat-2 由萨里卫星技术有限公司设计开发，但所有权和运营权归尼日利亚政府机构 NASRDA（尼日利亚国家空间研究与发展局）所有。

faq_en.a: NigeriaSat-2 was launched August 17, 2011 aboard a Dnepr-1 launch vehicle from the Yasny/Dombarovsky launch site in Russia.
faq_en.q: When was NigeriaSat-2 launched?

faq_en.a: NigeriaSat-2 has the primary objective of providing high resolution optical imagery of the Earth’s surface. Its data has applications in supporting food security and agricultural data collection, mapping and security, as well as the provision of data continuity for the NigeriaSat-1 system.
faq_en.q: What does NigeriaSat-2 do?

faq_en.a: NigeriaSat-2 was designed and developed by Surrey Satellite Technology Ltd., but is owned and operated by the Nigerian government organisation NASRDA (National Space Research and Development Agency).
faq_en.q: Who made NigeriaSat-2?

eoportal.name: NigeriaSat-X
eoportal.summary: The satellite aims to assist in the fields of disaster management, agriculture monitoring, and security.

faq.a: NigeriaSat-X undertakes a sun-synchronous near-circular orbit with an altitude of 700 km, an inclination of 98°, and a period of 97 minutes.
faq.q: What orbit does NigeriaSat-X undertake?

faq.a: NigeriaSat-X was launched in August 2011 aboard a Russian-made Dnepr-1 launch vehicle at the Dombarovsky Air Base in Russia.
faq.q: What were the launch details of NigeriSat-X?

faq.a: Whilst NigeriaSat-X does play an important role in monitoring land and vegetation, it was largely built as an exercise to develop the skills of Nigerian engineers. They were guided in the construction by Surrey Satellite Technology Ltd (SSTL).
faq.q: What is the purpose of NigeriaSat-X?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该卫星旨在协助灾害管理、农业监测和安全等领域的工作。
eoportal.summary_en: The satellite aims to assist in the fields of disaster management, agriculture monitoring, and security.

faq_zh.a: NigeriaSat-X运行在太阳同步近圆轨道上，轨道高度为700公里，倾角为98度，轨道周期为97分钟。
NigeriaSat-X于2011年8月从俄罗斯Dombarovsky空军基地，由一枚俄罗斯制造的第聂伯-1号运载火箭发射升空。
虽然NigeriaSat-X在监测土地和植被方面确实发挥着重要作用，但它主要是作为一项培养尼日利亚工程师技能的实践项目而建造的。他们在Surrey Satellite Technology Ltd (SSTL)的指导下完成了建造。

faq_en.a: NigeriaSat-X undertakes a sun-synchronous near-circular orbit with an altitude of 700 km, an inclination of 98°, and a period of 97 minutes.
faq_en.q: What orbit does NigeriaSat-X undertake?

faq_en.a: NigeriaSat-X was launched in August 2011 aboard a Russian-made Dnepr-1 launch vehicle at the Dombarovsky Air Base in Russia.
faq_en.q: What were the launch details of NigeriSat-X?

faq_en.a: Whilst NigeriaSat-X does play an important role in monitoring land and vegetation, it was largely built as an exercise to develop the skills of Nigerian engineers. They were guided in the construction by Surrey Satellite Technology Ltd (SSTL).
faq_en.q: What is the purpose of NigeriaSat-X?

eoportal.name: Nimbus-7
taxonomy: Cloud type, amount and cloud top temperature
Sea ice cover, edge and thickness
eoportal.summary: Nimbus-7 served as a stabilised platform for the testing of advanced systems for sensing and collecting data in atmospheric, oceanographic and meteorological disciplines until October 1994.

faq.a: Nimbus-7 served as a stabilised platform for the testing of advanced systems for sensing and collecting data in the atmospheric, oceanographic and meteorological disciplines from 1978 until 1994.
faq.q: What did Nimbus-7 do?

faq.a: Nimbus-7 was the last of the Nimbus satellites, however many satellites can trace their heritage to experiments from the Nimbus satellites. These include but are not limited to: Aura, Aqua, Terra, NOAA TIROS/POES,  Landsat, IceSat, SeaSat, and Suomi NPP.
faq.q: What satellites succeeded from Nimbus-7?

faq.a: The Nimbus series was a series of seven satellites operated by the National Aerospace and Space Administration (NASA) first launched in October 1964. The Nimbus satellites were the first three-axis stabilised meteorological satellites which tested many experiments for collecting data in the atmospheric, oceanographic and meteorological disciplines until 1994.
faq.q: What is the Nimbus series?

eoportal.summary_zh: Nimbus-7卫星作为一个稳定的平台，用于测试先进的传感和数据收集系统，服务于大气、海洋和气象学科，直至1994年10月。
eoportal.summary_en: Nimbus-7 served as a stabilised platform for the testing of advanced systems for sensing and collecting data in atmospheric, oceanographic and meteorological disciplines until October 1994.

faq_zh.a: Nimbus-7卫星从1978年到1994年，作为一个稳定的平台，用于测试先进的传感和数据收集系统，服务于大气、海洋和气象学科。
Nimbus-7是Nimbus系列的最后一颗卫星，但许多卫星都可以追溯到Nimbus卫星的实验。这些卫星包括但不限于：Aura、Aqua、Terra、NOAA TIROS/POES、Landsat、IceSat、SeaSat和Suomi NPP。
faq_zh.q: 哪些卫星继承了Nimbus-7的任务？

faq_zh.a: Nimbus系列是由美国国家航空航天局（NASA）运营的七颗卫星系列，首颗于1964年10月发射。Nimbus系列是第一代三轴稳定气象卫星，测试了许多用于收集大气、海洋和气象学科数据的实验，直至1994年。

faq_en.a: Nimbus-7 served as a stabilised platform for the testing of advanced systems for sensing and collecting data in the atmospheric, oceanographic and meteorological disciplines from 1978 until 1994.
faq_en.q: What did Nimbus-7 do?

faq_en.a: Nimbus-7 was the last of the Nimbus satellites, however many satellites can trace their heritage to experiments from the Nimbus satellites. These include but are not limited to: Aura, Aqua, Terra, NOAA TIROS/POES,  Landsat, IceSat, SeaSat, and Suomi NPP.
faq_en.q: What satellites succeeded from Nimbus-7?

faq_en.a: The Nimbus series was a series of seven satellites operated by the National Aerospace and Space Administration (NASA) first launched in October 1964. The Nimbus satellites were the first three-axis stabilised meteorological satellites which tested many experiments for collecting data in the atmospheric, oceanographic and meteorological disciplines until 1994.
faq_en.q: What is the Nimbus series?

eoportal.name: OceanSat-2
eoportal.summary: Other application areas include the study of surface wind, ocean surface strata, observation of chlorophyll concentrations, monitoring phytoplankton blooms, and study of atmospheric aerosols and suspended sediments in the water.

faq.a: OceanSat-2 was developed by the Indian Space Research Organization (ISRO) and launched on a Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle (PSLV) from Satish Dhawan Space Centre (SDSC) on the east coast of India.
faq.q: Who launched OceanSat-2?

faq.a: OceanSat-2 provides service continuity for operational users of Ocean Color Monitor (OCM) flown on OceanSat-1. It provides data for the study of surface wind, ocean surface strata, observation of chlorophyll concentrations, monitoring phytoplankton blooms, study of atmospheric aerosols and suspended sediments in the water.
faq.q: What is OceanSat-2 used for?

faq.a: OceanSat-2 was launched on 24 September 2009.
faq.q: When was OceanSat-2 launched?

eoportal.summary_zh: 其他应用领域包括研究海面风场、海洋表面分层、观测叶绿素浓度、监测浮游植物水华，以及研究大气气溶胶和水中悬浮沉积物。
eoportal.summary_en: Other application areas include the study of surface wind, ocean surface strata, observation of chlorophyll concentrations, monitoring phytoplankton blooms, and study of atmospheric aerosols and suspended sediments in the water.

faq_zh.a: OceanSat-2卫星由印度空间研究组织（ISRO）开发，并由极地卫星运载火箭（PSLV）从印度东海岸的萨迪什·达万航天中心（SDSC）发射升空。
OceanSat-2卫星为OceanSat-1上搭载的海洋水色监测仪（OCM）的运行用户提供服务连续性。它提供的数据用于研究海面风场、海洋表面分层、观测叶绿素浓度、监测浮游植物水华，以及研究大气气溶胶和水中悬浮沉积物。
OceanSat-2卫星于2009年9月24日发射升空。
faq_zh.q: OceanSat-2卫星是什么时候发射的？

faq_en.a: OceanSat-2 was developed by the Indian Space Research Organization (ISRO) and launched on a Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle (PSLV) from Satish Dhawan Space Centre (SDSC) on the east coast of India.
faq_en.q: Who launched OceanSat-2?

faq_en.a: OceanSat-2 provides service continuity for operational users of Ocean Color Monitor (OCM) flown on OceanSat-1. It provides data for the study of surface wind, ocean surface strata, observation of chlorophyll concentrations, monitoring phytoplankton blooms, study of atmospheric aerosols and suspended sediments in the water.
faq_en.q: What is OceanSat-2 used for?

faq_en.a: OceanSat-2 was launched on 24 September 2009.
faq_en.q: When was OceanSat-2 launched?

eoportal.name: PRISMA2GEN
eoportal.summary: The mission will collect high resolution hyperspectral imagery and will consist of two satellites: the high performance PRISMA2GEN satellite, and an accompanying satellite, designated Platino-4 (PLT-4), an all-electric small platform satellite that will collect complementary hyperspectral imagery to assist with PRISMA2GEN data processing.

faq.a: PRSIMA2GEN aims to provide data continuity for customers of the original PRISMA satellite, as well as providing a higher spatial resolution for hyperspectral imagery.
faq.q: What is the aim of the Second PRISMA Satellite?

faq.a: Thales Alenia Space began conducting a feasibility study for PRISMA2GEN in February 2022, aiming to identify user requirements for hyperspectral imagery and improvements on the original PRISMA mission.
faq.q: What stage of development is PRISMA2GEN in?

faq.a: The PRISMA2GEN hyperspectral camera will image in two bands, one in the Visible to Near Infrared (VIS/NIR) range, and one in the Near Infrared to Shortwave Infrared (NIR/SWIR) range.
faq.q: How many bands will PRISMA2GEN image in?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该任务将收集高分辨率高光谱图像，由两颗卫星组成：高性能的PRISMA2GEN卫星，以及一颗名为Platino-4 (PLT-4)的伴飞卫星。后者是一颗全电动小平台卫星，将收集互补的高光谱图像，以辅助PRISMA2GEN的数据处理。
eoportal.summary_en: The mission will collect high resolution hyperspectral imagery and will consist of two satellites: the high performance PRISMA2GEN satellite, and an accompanying satellite, designated Platino-4 (PLT-4), an all-electric small platform satellite that will collect complementary hyperspectral imagery to assist with PRISMA2GEN data processing.

faq_zh.a: PRISMA2GEN旨在为原始PRISMA卫星的用户提供数据延续性，同时提高高光谱图像的空间分辨率。
faq_zh.q: 第二颗PRISMA卫星的目标是什么？

faq_zh.a: Thales Alenia Space于2022年2月开始对PRISMA2GEN进行可行性研究，旨在确定高光谱图像的用户需求以及对原始PRISMA任务的改进。
faq_zh.q: PRISMA2GEN目前处于什么发展阶段？

faq_zh.a: PRISMA2GEN高光谱相机将在两个波段内成像，一个在可见光至近红外（VIS/NIR）范围，另一个在近红外至短波红外（NIR/SWIR）范围。
faq_zh.q: PRISMA2GEN将使用多少个波段进行成像？

faq_en.a: PRSIMA2GEN aims to provide data continuity for customers of the original PRISMA satellite, as well as providing a higher spatial resolution for hyperspectral imagery.
faq_en.q: What is the aim of the Second PRISMA Satellite?

faq_en.a: Thales Alenia Space began conducting a feasibility study for PRISMA2GEN in February 2022, aiming to identify user requirements for hyperspectral imagery and improvements on the original PRISMA mission.
faq_en.q: What stage of development is PRISMA2GEN in?

faq_en.a: The PRISMA2GEN hyperspectral camera will image in two bands, one in the Visible to Near Infrared (VIS/NIR) range, and one in the Near Infrared to Shortwave Infrared (NIR/SWIR) range.
faq_en.q: How many bands will PRISMA2GEN image in?

eoportal.name: QuickSounder
taxonomy: Precipitation intensity at the surface (liquid or solid)
Sea ice cover, edge and thickness
eoportal.summary: QuickSounder will serve as a prototype for NOAA's next generation of environmental satellites.

faq.a: The Near Earth Orbit Network (NEON) is the program of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA), which will develop the next generation of low-Earth orbit (LEO) environmental satellites.
faq.q: What is the Near Earth Orbit Network (NEON) program?

faq.a: Firefly Aerospace has secured a NASA contract to launch the QuickSounder satellite from the Vandenberg Space Force Base.
faq.q: What role will Firefly Aerospace play in the QuickSounder mission?

faq.a: NASA will manage the development and launch of the QuickSounder satellite, and NOAA will operate the satellite on-orbit and process its data.
faq.q: How will NOAA and NASA collaborate on the NEON project?

eoportal.summary_zh: QuickSounder将作为NOAA下一代环境卫星的原型。
eoportal.summary_en: QuickSounder will serve as a prototype for NOAA's next generation of environmental satellites.

faq_zh.a: 近地轨道网络（NEON）是美国国家海洋和大气管理局（NOAA）的一项计划，旨在开发下一代近地轨道（LEO）环境卫星。
faq_zh.q: 近地轨道网络（NEON）计划是什么？

faq_zh.a: 萤火虫航天公司已获得一份NASA合同，负责从范登堡太空军基地发射QuickSounder卫星。
faq_zh.q: 萤火虫航天公司将在QuickSounder任务中扮演什么角色？

faq_zh.a: NASA将负责QuickSounder卫星的开发和发射管理，NOAA则将负责卫星的在轨运行和数据处理。
faq_zh.q: NOAA和NASA将如何在NEON项目上合作？

faq_en.a: The Near Earth Orbit Network (NEON) is the program of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA), which will develop the next generation of low-Earth orbit (LEO) environmental satellites.
faq_en.q: What is the Near Earth Orbit Network (NEON) program?

faq_en.a: Firefly Aerospace has secured a NASA contract to launch the QuickSounder satellite from the Vandenberg Space Force Base.
faq_en.q: What role will Firefly Aerospace play in the QuickSounder mission?

faq_en.a: NASA will manage the development and launch of the QuickSounder satellite, and NOAA will operate the satellite on-orbit and process its data.
faq_en.q: How will NOAA and NASA collaborate on the NEON project?

eoportal.summary: RADARSAT-1 was operational for 17 years and was decommissioned in March 2013.

faq.a: RADARSAT-1 produced radar imagery that was used for applications in many areas, including agriculture, cartography, hydrology, forestry, oceanography, geology, ice and ocean monitoring, arctic surveillance, and detecting ocean oil slicks.
faq.q: What was RADARSAT-1 used for?

faq.a: No, RADARSAT-1 was decommissioned on 29 March 2013.
faq.q: Is RADARSAT-1 still working?

faq.a: RADARSAT-1 was an Earth-imaging radar satellite, with a Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) sensor as its primary payload.
faq.q: What type of satellite was RADARSAT-1?

eoportal.summary_zh: RADARSAT-1 在轨运行了17年，于2013年3月退役。
eoportal.summary_en: RADARSAT-1 was operational for 17 years and was decommissioned in March 2013.

faq_zh.a: RADARSAT-1 生成的雷达影像被用于多个领域，包括农业、测绘制图、水文、林业、海洋学、地质学、冰与海洋监测、北极监视以及海洋溢油探测。
不，RADARSAT-1 已于2013年3月29日退役。
RADARSAT-1 是一颗地球成像雷达卫星，其主要有效载荷是一台合成孔径雷达（SAR）传感器。

faq_en.q: What was RADARSAT-1 used for?

faq_en.a: No, RADARSAT-1 was decommissioned on 29 March 2013.

faq_en.a: RADARSAT-1 was an Earth-imaging radar satellite, with a Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) sensor as its primary payload.

RADARSAT-2, the successor to RADARSAT-1, is a commercial radar imaging satellite providing continuity in data collection for environmental monitoring, natural resource management, and coastal surveillance.

faq.a: RADARSAT-2 is an Earth observation mission with the primary objectives of monitoring glaciers, ice sheets and sea ice.
faq.q: What does RADARSAT-2 do?

faq.a: As of May 2022, RADARSAT-2 is still fully operational and has entered its 14th operational year, exceeding its expected lifetime of 7.25 years. RADARSAT-2 has undergone multiple enhancements to both its ground and space segments since its 2007 launch.
faq.q: Is RADARSAT-2 still working?

faq.a: Through the Third Party Mission (TPM) programme, ESA (European Space Agency) provides free access to data from both RADARSAT-1 and RADARSAT-2 for scientific and research purposes.
faq.q: Is RADARSAT-2 data free?

eoportal.summary_zh: RADARSAT-2是RADARSAT-1的后继型号，是一颗商业雷达成像卫星，为环境监测、自然资源管理和海岸监视提供连续的数据收集服务。

RADARSAT-2, the successor to RADARSAT-1, is a commercial radar imaging satellite providing continuity in data collection for environmental monitoring, natural resource management, and coastal surveillance.

faq_zh.a: RADARSAT-2是一项地球观测任务，其主要目标是监测冰川、冰盖和海冰。
截至2022年5月，RADARSAT-2仍完全正常运行，并已进入其第14个运行年，超过了7.25年的设计寿命。自2007年发射以来，RADARSAT-2的地面和空间段均经历了多次升级。
通过第三方任务（TPM）计划，欧空局（ESA）为科学研究目的免费提供RADARSAT-1和RADARSAT-2的数据访问。

faq_en.a: RADARSAT-2 is an Earth observation mission with the primary objectives of monitoring glaciers, ice sheets and sea ice.
faq_en.q: What does RADARSAT-2 do?

faq_en.a: As of May 2022, RADARSAT-2 is still fully operational and has entered its 14th operational year, exceeding its expected lifetime of 7.25 years. RADARSAT-2 has undergone multiple enhancements to both its ground and space segments since its 2007 launch.
faq_en.q: Is RADARSAT-2 still working?

faq_en.a: Through the Third Party Mission (TPM) programme, ESA (European Space Agency) provides free access to data from both RADARSAT-1 and RADARSAT-2 for scientific and research purposes.
faq_en.q: Is RADARSAT-2 data free?

eoportal.name: ResourceSat-2
eoportal.summary: ResourceSat-2 provides continuity for the environmental monitoring provided by ResourceSat-1, and boosts observation timeliness.

faq.a: Linear Image Self-Scanning Sensor (LISS) III is a medium resolution remote sensing multispectral camera developed by Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) and is carried by ResourceSat-2 and IRS-1C/-1D. The LISS III camera  has a 23.5 m spatial resolution and is operated to acquire multi-resolution data over India and its surroundings.
faq.q: What is LISS III?

faq.a: The AWiFS sensor has a 56 m spatial resolution.
faq.q: What is the resolution of the AWiFS sensor?

faq.a: LISS-4 has three spectral bands B2 (Green): 0.52 - 0.59 µm, B3 (Red): 0.62 - 0.68 µm, B4 (NIR): 0.77 - 0.86 µm. B3 is the default band for monochromatic imaging.
faq.q: How many bands are there in LISS 4?

eoportal.summary_zh: ResourceSat-2卫星延续了ResourceSat-1卫星提供的环境监测能力，并提升了观测的时效性。
eoportal.summary_en: ResourceSat-2 provides continuity for the environmental monitoring provided by ResourceSat-1, and boosts observation timeliness.

faq_zh.a: 线性自扫描传感器（LISS）III是由印度空间研究组织（ISRO）开发的中分辨率遥感多光谱相机，搭载于ResourceSat-2及IRS-1C/-1D卫星上。LISS III相机的空间分辨率为23.5米，用于获取印度及其周边地区的多分辨率数据。
AWiFS传感器的空间分辨率为56米。
faq_zh.q: AWiFS传感器的分辨率是多少？

faq_zh.a: LISS-4拥有三个光谱波段：B2（绿色）：0.52 - 0.59 µm，B3（红色）：0.62 - 0.68 µm，B4（近红外）：0.77 - 0.86 µm。其中B3波段是单色成像的默认波段。

faq_en.a: Linear Image Self-Scanning Sensor (LISS) III is a medium resolution remote sensing multispectral camera developed by Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) and is carried by ResourceSat-2 and IRS-1C/-1D. The LISS III camera  has a 23.5 m spatial resolution and is operated to acquire multi-resolution data over India and its surroundings.
faq_en.q: What is LISS III?

faq_en.a: The AWiFS sensor has a 56 m spatial resolution.
faq_en.q: What is the resolution of the AWiFS sensor?

faq_en.a: LISS-4 has three spectral bands B2 (Green): 0.52 - 0.59 µm, B3 (Red): 0.62 - 0.68 µm, B4 (NIR): 0.77 - 0.86 µm. B3 is the default band for monochromatic imaging.
faq_en.q: How many bands are there in LISS 4?

eoportal.name: ResourceSat-3
eoportal.summary: It will follow ResourceSat-2 and ResourceSat-2A, continuing the series for natural resources management, in particular, supporting the agricultural sector.

faq.a: ResourceSat-3 continues the Indian Space Research Organisation’s (ISRO) ResourceSat missions, which have been developed to observe and manage natural resources across India. Specifically, ResourceSat-3 has been improved to better monitor agricultural concerns in the Himalaya and Ladakh regions, among others.
faq.q: What is the purpose of ResourceSat-3?

faq.a: ResourceSat-3 hosts an advanced model of the Linear Imaging Self-Scanning Sensor previously used on ResourceSat-2 (ALISS-3), as well as the ATCOR atmospheric correction sensor.
faq.q: What instruments are on ResourceSat-3?

faq.a: ResourceSat-3 will launch aboard ISRO’s Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle (PSLV), which was also used for all previous ResourceSat missions.
faq.q: What rocket will ResourceSat-3 be launched on?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该卫星将接替资源卫星-2（ResourceSat-2）和资源卫星-2A（ResourceSat-2A），延续该系列在自然资源管理方面的使命，尤其侧重于支持农业领域。
eoportal.summary_en: It will follow ResourceSat-2 and ResourceSat-2A, continuing the series for natural resources management, in particular, supporting the agricultural sector.

faq_zh.a: 资源卫星-3延续了印度空间研究组织（ISRO）的资源卫星系列任务，旨在观测和管理印度全境的自然资源。具体而言，资源卫星-3经过改进，能更好地监测喜马拉雅和拉达克等地区的农业问题。
faq_zh.q: 资源卫星-3的任务目的是什么？

faq_zh.a: 资源卫星-3搭载了此前用于资源卫星-2的线性成像自扫描传感器的高级型号（ALISS-3），以及ATCOR大气校正传感器。
faq_zh.q: 资源卫星-3搭载了哪些载荷？

faq_zh.a: 资源卫星-3将搭载印度空间研究组织（ISRO）的极地卫星运载火箭（PSLV）升空，此前所有的资源卫星任务也都使用了该型火箭。
faq_zh.q: 资源卫星-3将使用哪种火箭发射？

faq_en.a: ResourceSat-3 continues the Indian Space Research Organisation’s (ISRO) ResourceSat missions, which have been developed to observe and manage natural resources across India. Specifically, ResourceSat-3 has been improved to better monitor agricultural concerns in the Himalaya and Ladakh regions, among others.
faq_en.q: What is the purpose of ResourceSat-3?

faq_en.a: ResourceSat-3 hosts an advanced model of the Linear Imaging Self-Scanning Sensor previously used on ResourceSat-2 (ALISS-3), as well as the ATCOR atmospheric correction sensor.
faq_en.q: What instruments are on ResourceSat-3?

faq_en.a: ResourceSat-3 will launch aboard ISRO’s Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle (PSLV), which was also used for all previous ResourceSat missions.
faq_en.q: What rocket will ResourceSat-3 be launched on?

eoportal.name: SPOT-4
taxonomy: Ocean imagery and water leaving spectral radiance
eoportal.summary: Also, an L-band telemetry subassembly is installed to send image data directly to ground stations.

faq.a: SPOT-4  is used for environmental studies, crop monitoring and management, and environmental forecasting.
faq.q: What is the SPOT-4 satellite used for?

faq.a: SPOT-4 contains High Resolution Visible and Infrared Sensors (HRVIR), a Vegetation Monitoring Instrument (VMI) and a Doppler Orbitography and Radio-positioning Integrated Satellite (DORIS). These technologies collect data from land and ocean environments such as landscape topography, vegetation, and albedo and reflectance. There are also experimental sensors such as PASTEC and POAM-II which provide data on the orbital environment and atmospheric properties in space.
faq.q: What sensors are on the SPOT-4 satellite?

faq.a: SPOT-4 ceased operation in June, 2013.
faq.q: Is SPOT-4 still functional?

eoportal.agency_zh: CNES（法国国家空间研究中心）
eoportal.summary_zh: 此外，该卫星还安装了一个L波段遥测子组件，用于直接向地面站发送图像数据。
eoportal.summary_en: Also, an L-band telemetry subassembly is installed to send image data directly to ground stations.

faq_zh.a: SPOT-4卫星用于环境研究、作物监测与管理以及环境预报。
SPOT-4卫星搭载了高分辨率可见光和红外传感器（HRVIR）、植被监测仪器（VMI）以及多普勒轨道学和无线电定位集成卫星（DORIS）系统。这些技术用于收集陆地和海洋环境的数据，如地形、植被、反照率和反射率。此外，还搭载了PASTEC和POAM-II等实验传感器，用于提供轨道环境和太空大气特性数据。
faq_zh.q: SPOT-4卫星上搭载了哪些传感器？

faq_zh.a: SPOT-4卫星已于2013年6月停止运行。

faq_en.a: SPOT-4  is used for environmental studies, crop monitoring and management, and environmental forecasting.
faq_en.q: What is the SPOT-4 satellite used for?

faq_en.a: SPOT-4 contains High Resolution Visible and Infrared Sensors (HRVIR), a Vegetation Monitoring Instrument (VMI) and a Doppler Orbitography and Radio-positioning Integrated Satellite (DORIS). These technologies collect data from land and ocean environments such as landscape topography, vegetation, and albedo and reflectance. There are also experimental sensors such as PASTEC and POAM-II which provide data on the orbital environment and atmospheric properties in space.
faq_en.q: What sensors are on the SPOT-4 satellite?

faq_en.a: SPOT-4 ceased operation in June, 2013.
faq_en.q: Is SPOT-4 still functional?

eoportal.name: WorldView-1
eoportal.summary: WV-1 is the successor to QuickBird-2 and aims to provide high spatial resolution imagery of the Earth’s surface to defence, intelligence and commercial industries.

faq.a: The aim of WorldView-1 is to provide high spatial resolution imagery of the Earth’s surface for commercial, defence and intelligence applications.
faq.q: What is the purpose of WorldView-1?

faq.a: WorldView-1 has outlasted its 7.25 year design life, which expired in December 2015, and remains operational as of July 2022.
faq.q: Is WorldView-1 still operating?

faq.a: WorldView-1 was developed by and is owned and operated by Maxar Technologies Inc.
faq.q: Who owns WorldView-1?

eoportal.summary_zh: WorldView-1 是 QuickBird-2 的后续星，旨在为国防、情报及商业行业提供高空间分辨率的地球表面影像。
eoportal.summary_en: WV-1 is the successor to QuickBird-2 and aims to provide high spatial resolution imagery of the Earth’s surface to defence, intelligence and commercial industries.

faq_zh.a: WorldView-1 的目的是为商业、国防和情报应用提供高空间分辨率的地球表面影像。
WorldView-1 的设计寿命为7.25年，已于2015年12月到期，截至2022年7月仍在运行。
WorldView-1 由 Maxar Technologies Inc. 开发、拥有并运营。

faq_en.a: The aim of WorldView-1 is to provide high spatial resolution imagery of the Earth’s surface for commercial, defence and intelligence applications.
faq_en.q: What is the purpose of WorldView-1?

faq_en.a: WorldView-1 has outlasted its 7.25 year design life, which expired in December 2015, and remains operational as of July 2022.
faq_en.q: Is WorldView-1 still operating?

faq_en.a: WorldView-1 was developed by and is owned and operated by Maxar Technologies Inc.
faq_en.q: Who owns WorldView-1?

eoportal.name: WorldView-2
eoportal.summary: It is the successor to WorldView-1 (WV-1) and aims to meet the increasing demand for high spatial resolution imagery.

faq.a: As of July 2022, WorldView-2 is currently operational, having outlasted its expected lifetime of 7.25 years, which expired in January 2017.
faq.q: Is WorldView-2 still operating?

faq.a: WorldView-2 is owned and operated by Maxar Technologies Inc., a commercial entity specialising in high spatial resolution imagery. The WorldView series was previously owned by DigitalGlobe, before the company merged with MDA to form Maxar Technologies in February 2017.
faq.q: Who owns WorldView-2?

faq.a: WorldView-2 imagery is available by direct purchase from Maxar Technologies Inc., the owner and operator of the WorldView series.
faq.q: How can I access WorldView-2 imagery?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该卫星是WorldView-1的后续型号，旨在满足对高空间分辨率影像日益增长的需求。
eoportal.summary_en: It is the successor to WorldView-1 (WV-1) and aims to meet the increasing demand for high spatial resolution imagery.

faq_zh.a: 截至2022年7月，WorldView-2 仍在运行，已超过其预计的7.25年设计寿命（该寿命已于2017年1月到期）。
WorldView-2 由Maxar Technologies Inc.拥有和运营，这是一家专注于高空间分辨率影像的商业公司。WorldView系列此前由DigitalGlobe拥有，该公司于2017年2月与MDA合并成立Maxar Technologies。
WorldView-2 影像可通过WorldView系列的所有者和运营商Maxar Technologies Inc.直接购买获得。
faq_zh.q: 如何获取 WorldView-2 影像？

faq_en.a: As of July 2022, WorldView-2 is currently operational, having outlasted its expected lifetime of 7.25 years, which expired in January 2017.
faq_en.q: Is WorldView-2 still operating?

faq_en.a: WorldView-2 is owned and operated by Maxar Technologies Inc., a commercial entity specialising in high spatial resolution imagery. The WorldView series was previously owned by DigitalGlobe, before the company merged with MDA to form Maxar Technologies in February 2017.
faq_en.q: Who owns WorldView-2?

faq_en.a: WorldView-2 imagery is available by direct purchase from Maxar Technologies Inc., the owner and operator of the WorldView series.

eoportal.name: WorldView-3
eoportal.summary: WorldView-3 added to DigitalGlobe’s existing constellation of QuickBird, WorldView-1 and WorldView-2, with WorldView-4 joining the constellation in 2016.

faq.a: There are 29 total spectral bands including a panchromatic band, eight multispectral bands, 8 SWIR (shortwave infrared) bands and 12 CAVIS (Cloud, Atmosphere, Vapours, Ice, and Snow) bands.
faq.q: How many bands are there in WorldView-3?

faq.a:
WorldView-3 collects high-resolution imagery, which is assisted by the CAVIS bands of the imager. The objective of CAVIS is to monitor the atmosphere and provide correction data to improve WorldView-3's high-resolution imagery when it images Earth objects through haze, soot, dust or other obscurants.
WorldView-3 uses Control Moment Gyroscopes developed by BATC (Ball Aerospace & Technologies Corporation), which reorient a satellite over a desired collection area in 4-5 seconds, compared to the 30-45 seconds needed for traditional reaction wheels.
faq.q: How does WorldView-3 work?

faq.a: WorldView Legion is the next evolution of Maxar’s industry leading WorldView constellation. WorldView Legion is a fleet of high-performance satellites that dramatically expands the ability to revisit the most rapidly changing areas on Earth to better inform critical, time-sensitive decisions. There will be six satellites in the fleet, with launch planned for 2022.
faq.q: What is WorldView Legion?

eoportal.summary_zh: WorldView-3 加入了 DigitalGlobe 现有的由 QuickBird、WorldView-1 和 WorldView-2 组成的星座，WorldView-4 于 2016 年加入该星座。
eoportal.summary_en: WorldView-3 added to DigitalGlobe’s existing constellation of QuickBird, WorldView-1 and WorldView-2, with WorldView-4 joining the constellation in 2016.

faq_zh.a: 共有 29 个光谱波段，包括 1 个全色波段、8 个多光谱波段、8 个短波红外 (SWIR) 波段和 12 个 CAVIS（云、大气、水蒸气、冰和雪）波段。

WorldView-3 采集高分辨率影像，其 CAVIS 波段为此提供辅助。CAVIS 的目标是监测大气，并在卫星通过烟雾、烟尘、尘埃或其他遮蔽物对地球目标进行成像时，提供校正数据以改善 WorldView-3 的高分辨率影像质量。
WorldView-3 使用了由 BATC（鲍尔航空航天技术公司）开发的控制力矩陀螺仪，它能在 4-5 秒内将卫星重新定向至所需的采集区域，而传统反作用轮则需要 30-45 秒。
WorldView Legion 是 Maxar 行业领先的 WorldView 星座的下一代演进。WorldView Legion 是一支高性能卫星舰队，显著提升了对地球上变化最快区域的重访能力，以便为关键的时间敏感决策提供更佳信息。该舰队将包含六颗卫星，计划于 2022 年发射。

faq_en.a: There are 29 total spectral bands including a panchromatic band, eight multispectral bands, 8 SWIR (shortwave infrared) bands and 12 CAVIS (Cloud, Atmosphere, Vapours, Ice, and Snow) bands.
faq_en.q: How many bands are there in WorldView-3?

faq_en.a:
WorldView-3 collects high-resolution imagery, which is assisted by the CAVIS bands of the imager. The objective of CAVIS is to monitor the atmosphere and provide correction data to improve WorldView-3's high-resolution imagery when it images Earth objects through haze, soot, dust or other obscurants.
WorldView-3 uses Control Moment Gyroscopes developed by BATC (Ball Aerospace & Technologies Corporation), which reorient a satellite over a desired collection area in 4-5 seconds, compared to the 30-45 seconds needed for traditional reaction wheels.
faq_en.q: How does WorldView-3 work?

faq_en.a: WorldView Legion is the next evolution of Maxar’s industry leading WorldView constellation. WorldView Legion is a fleet of high-performance satellites that dramatically expands the ability to revisit the most rapidly changing areas on Earth to better inform critical, time-sensitive decisions. There will be six satellites in the fleet, with launch planned for 2022.
faq_en.q: What is WorldView Legion?

eoportal.name: WorldView-4
eoportal.summary: WorldView-4 went out of action in January 2019, after its orientation was unable to be controlled.

faq.a: WorldView-4 had the capabilities of providing high-resolution images and film of Earth to businesses and governments.
faq.q: What did WorldView-4 do?

faq.a: WorldView-4, due to its altitude of 617 km, had a flight velocity of 7.5 km/s. This allowed the satellite to observe 680,000 km2 of Earth’s surface per day. However, with the entire fleet of Maxar imagery satellites, 60% of Earth's surface would have been observed monthly.
faq.q: How fast was WorldView-4?

faq.a: WorldView-4 became inactive after the gyroscopes that controlled the rotation of the satellite failed. Without control of where the instrument was pointed, images are no longer able to be obtained.
faq.q: Is WorldView-4 still active?

eoportal.summary_zh: WorldView-4卫星于2019年1月停止运行，原因是其姿态控制系统失效，无法再进行定向控制。
eoportal.summary_en: WorldView-4 went out of action in January 2019, after its orientation was unable to be controlled.

faq_zh.a: WorldView-4具备为商业和政府用户提供高分辨率地球影像和视频的能力。
WorldView-4运行在617公里的轨道高度，飞行速度为7.5公里/秒。这使得该卫星每天可观测地球表面约68万平方公里。如果结合Maxar整个卫星星座的数据，每月可观测全球约60%的地表。
WorldView-4已停止工作，原因是用于控制卫星旋转的陀螺仪发生故障。由于无法控制仪器指向，该卫星已无法获取影像。

faq_en.a: WorldView-4 had the capabilities of providing high-resolution images and film of Earth to businesses and governments.
faq_en.q: What did WorldView-4 do?

faq_en.a: WorldView-4, due to its altitude of 617 km, had a flight velocity of 7.5 km/s. This allowed the satellite to observe 680,000 km2 of Earth’s surface per day. However, with the entire fleet of Maxar imagery satellites, 60% of Earth's surface would have been observed monthly.
faq_en.q: How fast was WorldView-4?

faq_en.a: WorldView-4 became inactive after the gyroscopes that controlled the rotation of the satellite failed. Without control of where the instrument was pointed, images are no longer able to be obtained.
faq_en.q: Is WorldView-4 still active?

eoportal.name: Aalto-1: The Finnish Student Nanosatellite
eoportal.summary: Launched in June 2017, Aalto-1 is the first Finnish Earth observation nanosatellite, and demonstrates the use of compact instruments that are applicable in similar nanosatellites.

faq.a: Aalto-1 is operated by students from University of Aalto, with cooperation from the Universities of Helsinki and Turku and the Finnish Meteorological Institute.
faq.q: Who runs Aalto-1?

faq.a: Aalto-1 is the first Finnish Earth observing nanosatellite, as well as demonstrating the functionality and applications of a compact spectrometer and radiation monitor, and the effectiveness of an experimental electrostatic plasma brake (EPB).
faq.q: What is the purpose of Aalto-1?

faq.a: Aalto-1 carries the AaSI (Aalto-1 Spectral Imager) module, a purpose built compact spectrometer, the RadMon (Radiation Monitor) sensor, a compact radiation monitor, and an experimental EPB (Electrostatic Plasma Brake).
faq.q: What instruments does Aalto-1 carry?

eoportal.summary_zh: Aalto-1 于 2017 年 6 月发射，是芬兰首颗地球观测纳卫星，演示了适用于同类纳卫星的紧凑型仪器的应用。
eoportal.summary_en: Launched in June 2017, Aalto-1 is the first Finnish Earth observation nanosatellite, and demonstrates the use of compact instruments that are applicable in similar nanosatellites.

faq_zh.a: Aalto-1 由阿尔托大学的学生运营，并与赫尔辛基大学、图尔库大学以及芬兰气象研究所合作。
Aalto-1 是芬兰首颗地球观测纳卫星，同时展示了紧凑型光谱仪和辐射监测仪的功能与应用，以及实验性静电等离子体刹车的有效性。
Aalto-1 搭载了 AaSI（Aalto-1 光谱成像仪）模块（一种专用的紧凑型光谱仪）、RadMon（辐射监测仪）传感器（一种紧凑型辐射监测仪）以及实验性 EPB（静电等离子体刹车）。

faq_en.a: Aalto-1 is operated by students from University of Aalto, with cooperation from the Universities of Helsinki and Turku and the Finnish Meteorological Institute.
faq_en.q: Who runs Aalto-1?

faq_en.a: Aalto-1 is the first Finnish Earth observing nanosatellite, as well as demonstrating the functionality and applications of a compact spectrometer and radiation monitor, and the effectiveness of an experimental electrostatic plasma brake (EPB).
faq_en.q: What is the purpose of Aalto-1?

faq_en.a: Aalto-1 carries the AaSI (Aalto-1 Spectral Imager) module, a purpose built compact spectrometer, the RadMon (Radiation Monitor) sensor, a compact radiation monitor, and an experimental EPB (Electrostatic Plasma Brake).
faq_en.q: What instruments does Aalto-1 carry?

eoportal.summary: ACTS uses an adaptive rain fade compensation protocol to reduce the impact of signal attenuation resulting from propagation effects.
eoportal.summary_zh: ACTS采用自适应雨衰补偿协议，以减轻由传播效应引起的信号衰减影响。
eoportal.summary_en: ACTS uses an adaptive rain fade compensation protocol to reduce the impact of signal attenuation resulting from propagation effects.

eoportal.name: ADEOS (Advanced Earth Observing Satellite) / Midori
eoportal.summary: The satellite’s objectives were to observe changes in the Earth’s atmosphere, track depletion of the ozone layer and model maritime meteorological conditions.

faq.a: The ADEOS mission objectives was to observe environmental changes to Earth’s atmosphere, with particular areas of focus being deforestation, reduction of the ozone layer and a wider study of global warming.
faq.q: What were the objectives of the ADEOS satellite?

faq.a: In June 1997, ADEOS spacecraft operations were terminated following several critical malfunctions, the cause of which is believed to be structural damage of the solar array, prompting loss of power.
faq.q: Why did the ADEOS mission fail?

faq.a: ADEOS was managed by NASDA, the National Space Development Agency of Japan. NASDA was later superseded by JAXA (Japan Aerospace Exploration Agency) but throughout the lifetime of ADEOS, NASDA were responsible for the development, launch and operation of ADEOS.
faq.q: Who managed the ADEOS mission?

eoportal.name_zh: ADEOS（先进对地观测卫星）/ Midori
eoportal.agency_zh: JAXA（日本宇宙航空研究开发机构）、NASA、CNES（法国国家空间研究中心）、MOE（日本环境省）、MITI（日本通商产业省）
eoportal.summary_zh: 该卫星的目标是观测地球大气层的变化、追踪臭氧层的损耗以及建立海洋气象条件模型。
eoportal.summary_en: The satellite’s objectives were to observe changes in the Earth’s atmosphere, track depletion of the ozone layer and model maritime meteorological conditions.

faq_zh.a: ADEOS任务的目标是观测地球大气的环境变化，重点关注森林砍伐、臭氧层减少，并对全球变暖进行更广泛的研究。
1997年6月，在发生多次严重故障后，ADEOS航天器的运行被终止，故障原因被认为是太阳电池阵的结构损坏导致电力丧失。
ADEOS由日本宇宙开发事业团（NASDA）管理。NASDA后来被JAXA（日本宇宙航空研究开发机构）取代，但在ADEOS的整个生命周期内，NASDA负责其研发、发射和运行。

faq_en.a: The ADEOS mission objectives was to observe environmental changes to Earth’s atmosphere, with particular areas of focus being deforestation, reduction of the ozone layer and a wider study of global warming.
faq_en.q: What were the objectives of the ADEOS satellite?

faq_en.a: In June 1997, ADEOS spacecraft operations were terminated following several critical malfunctions, the cause of which is believed to be structural damage of the solar array, prompting loss of power.
faq_en.q: Why did the ADEOS mission fail?

faq_en.a: ADEOS was managed by NASDA, the National Space Development Agency of Japan. NASDA was later superseded by JAXA (Japan Aerospace Exploration Agency) but throughout the lifetime of ADEOS, NASDA were responsible for the development, launch and operation of ADEOS.
faq_en.q: Who managed the ADEOS mission?

faq.q: Who owns ADEOS-II?

faq.a: ADEOS-II aimed to provide Earth observation services with advanced instruments, for applications in climate science, meteorology and fishery.
faq.q: What was the aim of ADEOS-II?

faq.a: ADEOS-II carried five instruments, Advanced Microwave Scanning Radiometer (AMSR), Global Imager (GLI), SeaWinds scatterometer, Improved Limb Atmospheric Spectrometer-II (ILAS-II), Polarisation and Directionality of the Earth’s Reflection-2 (POLDER-2).

ADEOS-II was an environmental observation satellite aimed to provide Earth observation services with advanced instruments, for applications in climate science, meteorology and fishery.

faq_zh.a: ADEOS-II是由日本宇宙航空研究开发机构（JAXA）开发和运营的地球观测任务。
ADEOS-II旨在通过先进仪器提供地球观测服务，应用于气候科学、气象学和渔业等领域。
ADEOS-II搭载了五种仪器：先进微波扫描辐射计（AMSR）、全球成像仪（GLI）、SeaWinds散射计、改进型临边大气光谱仪-II（ILAS-II）、地球反射偏振与方向性-2（POLDER-2）。

faq_en.a: ADEOS-II was an Earth observation mission developed and operated by the Japan Aerospace Exploration Agency (JAXA).
faq_en.q: Who owns ADEOS-II?

faq_en.a: ADEOS-II aimed to provide Earth observation services with advanced instruments, for applications in climate science, meteorology and fishery.
faq_en.q: What was the aim of ADEOS-II?

faq_en.a: ADEOS-II carried five instruments, Advanced Microwave Scanning Radiometer (AMSR), Global Imager (GLI), SeaWinds scatterometer, Improved Limb Atmospheric Spectrometer-II (ILAS-II), Polarisation and Directionality of the Earth’s Reflection-2 (POLDER-2).
faq_en.q: What instruments did ADEOS-II carry?

eoportal.name: Aeneas nanosatellite of USC/SERC
eoportal.summary: Launched in 2012, Aeneas is the first CubeSat with the capability to track the location of cargo containers on a global scale over the open ocean using a 1 watt WiFi-like transceiver.

faq.a: Aenaes tracks the location of cargo containers on a global scale using a 1 Watt Wi-Fi-like transceiver. This data is used by both governmental and non-governmental bodies to track cargo containers, including the US Department of Homeland Security which uses Aeneas data in its Secure Transit Corridors program. Aeneas also provided students with hands-on experience in satellite design assembly and integration.
faq.q: What is the purpose of Aeneas?

faq.a: The Aeneas nanosatellite was launched on 13 September, 2012, as a secondary payload on an Atlas-5-411 vehicle of ULA (United Launch Alliance) from Vandenberg Air Force Base, California.
faq.q: When was Aeneas launched?

faq.a: Aeneas is a 3U (three unit) CubeSat nanosatellite. The nanosatellite is the first of its kind, hosting a custom-built deployable mesh antenna and a 1 Watt Wi-Fi-like transceiver. Aeneas demonstrates the capability of nanosatellites to track cargo containers  on the Earth’s surface at a global scale.
faq.q: What type of satellite is Aeneas?

eoportal.summary_zh: Aeneas 纳卫星于2012年发射，是首个具备在全球公海范围内追踪货运集装箱位置能力的立方体卫星，其使用一个1瓦特的类WiFi收发器实现该功能。
eoportal.summary_en: Launched in 2012, Aeneas is the first CubeSat with the capability to track the location of cargo containers on a global scale over the open ocean using a 1 watt WiFi-like transceiver.

faq_zh.a: Aeneas 使用一个1瓦特的类WiFi收发器在全球范围内追踪货运集装箱的位置。这些数据被政府及非政府机构用于追踪货运集装箱，例如美国国土安全部就在其“安全运输走廊”计划中使用了 Aeneas 的数据。Aeneas 也为学生提供了卫星设计、组装与集成的实践经验。
faq_zh.q: Aeneas 卫星的目的是什么？

faq_zh.a: Aeneas 纳卫星于2012年9月13日，作为次要载荷从美国加利福尼亚州范登堡空军基地由联合发射联盟（ULA）的 Atlas-5-411 火箭发射升空。
Aeneas 是一颗3单元（3U）立方体纳卫星。该纳卫星是同类中首颗，搭载了定制的可展开网状天线和一个1瓦特的类WiFi收发器。Aeneas 展示了纳卫星在全球范围内追踪地球表面货运集装箱的能力。
faq_zh.q: Aeneas 是什么类型的卫星？

faq_en.a: Aenaes tracks the location of cargo containers on a global scale using a 1 Watt Wi-Fi-like transceiver. This data is used by both governmental and non-governmental bodies to track cargo containers, including the US Department of Homeland Security which uses Aeneas data in its Secure Transit Corridors program. Aeneas also provided students with hands-on experience in satellite design assembly and integration.
faq_en.q: What is the purpose of Aeneas?

faq_en.a: The Aeneas nanosatellite was launched on 13 September, 2012, as a secondary payload on an Atlas-5-411 vehicle of ULA (United Launch Alliance) from Vandenberg Air Force Base, California.
faq_en.q: When was Aeneas launched?

faq_en.a: Aeneas is a 3U (three unit) CubeSat nanosatellite. The nanosatellite is the first of its kind, hosting a custom-built deployable mesh antenna and a 1 Watt Wi-Fi-like transceiver. Aeneas demonstrates the capability of nanosatellites to track cargo containers  on the Earth’s surface at a global scale.
faq_en.q: What type of satellite is Aeneas?

eoportal.name: Aeolus / formerly ADM (Atmospheric Dynamics Mission)
taxonomy: Cloud type, amount and cloud top temperature
eoportal.summary: The satellite was the first to acquire profiles of Earth’s wind on a global scale.

faq.a: Aeolus accurately measured wind profiles in the Planetary Boundary Layer (PBL), troposphere and lower stratosphere.
faq.q: What did Aeolus measure?

faq.a: Aeolus carried onboard an Atmospheric Laser Doppler Instrument (ALADIN) which employed the Doppler Wind Lidar measurement technique. The retrieval of wind speed relied on direct measurement along the LOS (Line-of-Sight) by lidar using Doppler shift information from atmospheric molecules and particles advected by wind.
faq.q: How did the Aeolus laser work?

faq.a: Aeolus was launched in August 2018 on a Vega vehicle from Kourou, French Guiana in France.
faq.q: When was Aeolus launched?

eoportal.name_zh: Aeolus（原名 ADM，大气动力学任务）
eoportal.summary_zh: 该卫星是首颗能够在全球范围内获取地球风场廓线的卫星。
eoportal.summary_en: The satellite was the first to acquire profiles of Earth’s wind on a global scale.

faq_zh.a: Aeolus 精确测量了行星边界层、对流层和平流层低层的风廓线。
Aeolus 搭载了一台大气激光多普勒仪器（ALADIN），该仪器采用了多普勒测风激光雷达技术。风速的反演依赖于激光雷达沿视线方向，利用受风平流的大气分子和粒子的多普勒频移信息进行直接测量。
faq_zh.q: Aeolus 的激光器是如何工作的？

faq_zh.a: Aeolus 于 2018 年 8 月在法属圭亚那库鲁由 Vega 运载火箭发射升空。

faq_en.a: Aeolus accurately measured wind profiles in the Planetary Boundary Layer (PBL), troposphere and lower stratosphere.
faq_en.q: What did Aeolus measure?

faq_en.a: Aeolus carried onboard an Atmospheric Laser Doppler Instrument (ALADIN) which employed the Doppler Wind Lidar measurement technique. The retrieval of wind speed relied on direct measurement along the LOS (Line-of-Sight) by lidar using Doppler shift information from atmospheric molecules and particles advected by wind.
faq_en.q: How did the Aeolus laser work?

faq_en.a: Aeolus was launched in August 2018 on a Vega vehicle from Kourou, French Guiana in France.
faq_en.q: When was Aeolus launched?

eoportal.name: AERO-VISTA (Auroral Emissions Radio Observer - Vector Interferometry Space Technology using AERO)
eoportal.summary: The mission is led by the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT).

faq.a: AERO (Auroral Emissions Radio Observer) and VISTA (Vector Interferometry Space Technology using AERO) are identical satellites that have complementary and differing objectives. AERO will focus on auroral observations with an electromagnetic vector sensor (EMVS) and VISTA will focus on vector interferometry and validating interferometry from Low-Earth Orbit (LEO).
faq.q: What is the difference between AERO and VISTA?

faq.a: AERO-VISTA are both owned and operated by the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT).
faq.q: Who operates AERO-VISTA?

eoportal.name_zh: AERO-VISTA（极光发射无线电观测器 - 使用AERO的矢量干涉空间技术）
eoportal.summary_zh: 该任务由麻省理工学院（MIT）主导。
eoportal.summary_en: The mission is led by the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT).

faq_zh.a: AERO（极光发射无线电观测器）和VISTA（使用AERO的矢量干涉空间技术）是两颗完全相同的卫星，其目标既有互补之处也存在差异。AERO将利用电磁矢量传感器（EMVS）专注于极光观测，而VISTA则专注于矢量干涉测量，并验证从低地球轨道（LEO）进行干涉测量的可行性。
AERO-VISTA均由麻省理工学院（MIT）拥有和运营。

faq_en.a: AERO (Auroral Emissions Radio Observer) and VISTA (Vector Interferometry Space Technology using AERO) are identical satellites that have complementary and differing objectives. AERO will focus on auroral observations with an electromagnetic vector sensor (EMVS) and VISTA will focus on vector interferometry and validating interferometry from Low-Earth Orbit (LEO).
faq_en.q: What is the difference between AERO and VISTA?

faq_en.a: AERO-VISTA are both owned and operated by the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT).
faq_en.q: Who operates AERO-VISTA?
