eoportal.name: AeroCube-11R3
eoportal.summary: Launched in December 2018, the AeroCube-11 consists of two nearly identical satellites which carry multispectral sensors, AeroCube-11A/TOMSat EagleScout, and AeroCube-11B/TOMSat R3 (Rapid + Responsive + Re-imagined).

faq.a: The AeroCube-11/TOMSat mission will demonstrate the ability of CubeSats to perform remote-sensing missions at a level comparable to that of larger spacecraft. In addition to the remote sensing mission objectives, the AeroCube-11 satellites will demonstrate and verify new methods of data transmission and processing, including refining laser communication operations.
faq.q: What is the goal of AeroCube-11?

faq.a: The Aerospace Corporation is an American federally funded non-profit research and development centre based in California.  Aerospace Corporation provides technical guidance on space missions for military, civilian and commercial customers.
faq.q: What is Aerospace Corporation?

faq.a: AeroCube-11A/TOMSat R3 collects spectral data of Earth's surface in five visible bands (0.43 – 0.45 µm, 0.45 - 0.51 µm, 0.53 - 0.59 µm, 0.64 - 0.67 µm, 0.50 - 0.68 µm) and one NIR (near infrared) band (0.85 - 0.88 µm). The satellite will produce data applicable in agricultural and mineral surveys, as well as coastal water quality monitoring.
faq.q: What data does the AeroCube-11A (TOMSat R3) obtain?

eoportal.summary_zh: AeroCube-11系列于2018年12月发射，由两颗几乎相同的卫星组成：AeroCube-11A/TOMSat EagleScout 和 AeroCube-11B/TOMSat R3（快速响应与重新构想），它们搭载了多光谱传感器。
eoportal.summary_en: Launched in December 2018, the AeroCube-11 consists of two nearly identical satellites which carry multispectral sensors, AeroCube-11A/TOMSat EagleScout, and AeroCube-11B/TOMSat R3 (Rapid + Responsive + Re-imagined).

faq_zh.a: AeroCube-11/TOMSat 任务旨在展示立方体卫星能够执行与大型航天器相当的遥感任务。除了遥感任务目标外，AeroCube-11 卫星还将演示和验证新的数据传输与处理方法，包括优化激光通信操作。
美国航空航天公司（Aerospace Corporation）是一家位于美国加利福尼亚州的联邦资助的非营利研究与开发中心，为军事、民用和商业客户提供太空任务方面的技术指导。
faq_zh.q: 什么是美国航空航天公司（Aerospace Corporation）？

faq_zh.a: AeroCube-11A/TOMSat R3 在五个可见光波段（0.43–0.45 µm、0.45–0.51 µm、0.53–0.59 µm、0.64–0.67 µm、0.50–0.68 µm）和一个近红外波段（0.85–0.88 µm）收集地球表面的光谱数据。该卫星产生的数据可用于农业与矿产调查，以及沿海水质监测。

faq_en.a: The AeroCube-11/TOMSat mission will demonstrate the ability of CubeSats to perform remote-sensing missions at a level comparable to that of larger spacecraft. In addition to the remote sensing mission objectives, the AeroCube-11 satellites will demonstrate and verify new methods of data transmission and processing, including refining laser communication operations.
faq_en.q: What is the goal of AeroCube-11?

faq_en.a: The Aerospace Corporation is an American federally funded non-profit research and development centre based in California.  Aerospace Corporation provides technical guidance on space missions for military, civilian and commercial customers.
faq_en.q: What is Aerospace Corporation?

faq_en.a: AeroCube-11A/TOMSat R3 collects spectral data of Earth's surface in five visible bands (0.43 – 0.45 µm, 0.45 - 0.51 µm, 0.53 - 0.59 µm, 0.64 - 0.67 µm, 0.50 - 0.68 µm) and one NIR (near infrared) band (0.85 - 0.88 µm). The satellite will produce data applicable in agricultural and mineral surveys, as well as coastal water quality monitoring.
faq_en.q: What data does the AeroCube-11A (TOMSat R3) obtain?

eoportal.name: AeroCube-3 CubeSat
eoportal.summary: AeroCube-3 was the follow-on mission to AeroCube and AeroCube-2, which both encountered failures during or after launch.

faq.a: AeroCube-3 aimed to complete mission objectives that past AeroCube missions were not able to complete, which included technological demonstrations of bus performance and simple photography, as well as further development of mission operations for CubeSats.
faq.q: What was the purpose of AeroCube-3?

faq.a: A CubeSat refers to a one unit standard-sized satellite, which is defined as a 0.1 m x 0.1 m x 0.1 m cubic volume. Larger N-unit satellites refer to 0.1 m x 0.1 m x (N x 0.1) m volumetric satellites.
faq.q: What does the CubeSat form factor refer to?

faq.a: AeroCube-3 encountered ground-station communication issues after 205 days on orbit, as well as balloon deployment failure. Control commands were not accepted by the satellite, and re-entered the Earth’s atmosphere in January 2011.
faq.q: What issues did AeroCube-3 encounter?

eoportal.summary_zh: AeroCube-3 是 AeroCube 和 AeroCube-2 的后续任务，前两者均在发射期间或发射后遭遇失败。
eoportal.summary_en: AeroCube-3 was the follow-on mission to AeroCube and AeroCube-2, which both encountered failures during or after launch.

faq_zh.a: AeroCube-3 旨在完成过去 AeroCube 任务未能完成的目标，包括对卫星平台性能进行技术演示、执行简易摄影，以及进一步开发立方体卫星的任务操作能力。
CubeSat 指的是一个单位的标准尺寸卫星，其定义为 0.1 米 x 0.1 米 x 0.1 米的立方体体积。更大的 N 单位卫星指的是体积为 0.1 米 x 0.1 米 x (N x 0.1) 米的卫星。
faq_zh.q: CubeSat 的外形规格指的是什么？

faq_zh.a: AeroCube-3 在轨运行 205 天后遇到地面站通信问题，并且气球展开失败。卫星未接收控制指令，于 2011 年 1 月再入地球大气层。

faq_en.a: AeroCube-3 aimed to complete mission objectives that past AeroCube missions were not able to complete, which included technological demonstrations of bus performance and simple photography, as well as further development of mission operations for CubeSats.
faq_en.q: What was the purpose of AeroCube-3?

faq_en.a: A CubeSat refers to a one unit standard-sized satellite, which is defined as a 0.1 m x 0.1 m x 0.1 m cubic volume. Larger N-unit satellites refer to 0.1 m x 0.1 m x (N x 0.1) m volumetric satellites.
faq_en.q: What does the CubeSat form factor refer to?

faq_en.a: AeroCube-3 encountered ground-station communication issues after 205 days on orbit, as well as balloon deployment failure. Control commands were not accepted by the satellite, and re-entered the Earth’s atmosphere in January 2011.
faq_en.q: What issues did AeroCube-3 encounter?

eoportal.name: AeroCube-4 CubeSats
eoportal.summary: A 1U CubeSat can provide responsive, targeted remote sensing data such as in forest fire verification and in celestial events.
eoportal.summary_zh: AeroCube-4 是一颗1U立方星，能够提供快速、针对性的遥感数据，例如用于森林火灾验证和天文事件观测。
eoportal.summary_en: A 1U CubeSat can provide responsive, targeted remote sensing data such as in forest fire verification and in celestial events.

eoportal.name: AEROS MH-1
eoportal.summary: The nanosatellite was developed by Thales Edisoft Portugal in partnership with the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT), with support from a consortium of Portuguese organisations, universities, and research centres.

faq.a: Portugal’s second satellite in orbit is AEROS MH-1, which follows the PoSAT-1, launched in 1993.
faq.q: What is the second Portuguese satellite?

faq.a: AEROS MH-1 is the the first Portuguese satellite launched in 30 years, following PoSAT-1, which was launched in 1993 and is no longer operational.
faq.q: Does Portugal have satellites?

faq.a: The purpose of the AEROS MH-1 mission is to monitor ocean and coastal areas, focusing on ocean colour and marine ecosystems, particularly in the Portuguese Atlantic region.
faq.q: What is the purpose of the AEROS MH-1 mission?

eoportal.agency_zh: 麻省理工学院（MIT）、葡萄牙泰雷兹爱迪索软件公司
eoportal.summary_zh: 该纳米卫星由葡萄牙泰雷兹爱迪索软件公司与麻省理工学院（MIT）合作开发，并得到葡萄牙多家组织、大学和研究中心组成联盟的支持。
eoportal.summary_en: The nanosatellite was developed by Thales Edisoft Portugal in partnership with the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT), with support from a consortium of Portuguese organisations, universities, and research centres.

faq_zh.q: 葡萄牙的第二颗卫星是什么？

faq_zh.a: 是的，AEROS MH-1是葡萄牙在30年后发射的第一颗卫星，继承了于1993年发射、现已停止运行的PoSAT-1卫星的任务。
AEROS MH-1任务的目标是监测海洋和沿海地区，重点关注海洋水色和海洋生态系统，特别是葡萄牙的大西洋区域。

faq_en.a: AEROS MH-1 is the the first Portuguese satellite launched in 30 years, following PoSAT-1, which was launched in 1993 and is no longer operational.

faq_en.a: The purpose of the AEROS MH-1 mission is to monitor ocean and coastal areas, focusing on ocean colour and marine ecosystems, particularly in the Portuguese Atlantic region.

eoportal.name: AISat (Automatic Identification System Nanosatellite)
eoportal.summary: The mission aims to demonstrate maritime traffic navigation with improved antenna directionality compared to existing AIS satellites.

faq.a: Automatic Identification System Nanosatellite (AISat) is a technology demonstration mission that detects AIS signals from maritime traffic using a high-gain helical antenna capable of tracking individual ships.
faq.q: What is AISat used for?

faq.a: AISat is operated and built by the German Aerospace Center (DLR). The Institute of Space Systems in Bremen developed the main body of the nanosatellite and the system engineering, and the Institute of Composite Structures and Adaptive Systems in Braunschweig verified the successful use of the helical antenna.
faq.q: Who operates AISat?

faq.a: Automatic Identification System Nanosatellite (AISat) was launched as a secondary payload on June 30, 2014 from the Satish Dhawan Space Center in Sriharikota, India by the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO).
faq.q: When was AISat launched?

eoportal.name_zh: AISat（自动识别系统纳米卫星）
eoportal.summary_zh: 该任务旨在通过比现有AIS卫星更具方向性的改进天线，演示海上交通导航。
eoportal.summary_en: The mission aims to demonstrate maritime traffic navigation with improved antenna directionality compared to existing AIS satellites.

faq_zh.a: 自动识别系统纳米卫星（AISat）是一项技术验证任务，它利用能够跟踪单艘船舶的高增益螺旋天线，探测来自海上交通的AIS信号。
AISat由德国航天中心（DLR）运营和建造。不来梅空间系统研究所负责纳米卫星主体和系统工程，不伦瑞克复合结构与自适应系统研究所验证了螺旋天线的成功应用。
自动识别系统纳米卫星（AISat）于2014年6月30日由印度空间研究组织（ISRO）从印度斯里赫里戈达岛的萨迪什·达万航天中心作为次级载荷发射。

faq_en.a: Automatic Identification System Nanosatellite (AISat) is a technology demonstration mission that detects AIS signals from maritime traffic using a high-gain helical antenna capable of tracking individual ships.
faq_en.q: What is AISat used for?

faq_en.a: AISat is operated and built by the German Aerospace Center (DLR). The Institute of Space Systems in Bremen developed the main body of the nanosatellite and the system engineering, and the Institute of Composite Structures and Adaptive Systems in Braunschweig verified the successful use of the helical antenna.
faq_en.q: Who operates AISat?

faq_en.a: Automatic Identification System Nanosatellite (AISat) was launched as a secondary payload on June 30, 2014 from the Satish Dhawan Space Center in Sriharikota, India by the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO).
faq_en.q: When was AISat launched?

eoportal.name: AISSat (Automatic Identification System Satellite) Nanosatellite Constellation
eoportal.summary: AISSat aims to improve the maritime safety and efficiency of navigation, safety of life at sea and the protection of the marine environment.

faq.a: The AISSat nanosatellite constellation aims to  improve the maritime safety and efficiency of navigation, safety of life at sea and the protection of the marine environment.
faq.q: What is the aim of the AISSat Nanosatellite Constellation?

faq.a: AISSat-1 left service in June 2022, after 12 years of operations, while AISSat-2, -3 and -4 are still operational.
faq.q: Are all the AISSat satellites still operational?

faq.a: AISSat satellites carry a single instrument, the AIS receiver (ASR 100).

eoportal.name: Alba Orbital Unicorn PocketQubes
eoportal.summary: The first two satellites were launched in December 2019.

faq.a: Alba Cluster 3 is the largest PocketQube launch in history to date. It builds on the company's successful Alba Cluster 2 launch in 2019, which deployed six PocketQubes into orbit, including two of Alba Orbital's Unicorn-2 pico-satellites. Alba Cluster 3 was launched using the company's AlbaPod v2, which allows PocketQubes to be integrated into any launch vehicle as part of a ‘rideshare' mission.
faq.q: How does Alba Cluster 3 differ from other Alba Cluster missions?

faq.a: Alba Orbital (UK, USA, Germany) is making space access easier, faster and more affordable for small organisations. Alba's January SpaceX flight alone broke records as the world's largest PocketQube launch to date with 13 satellites manifested on board. Future rideshare plans with Alba AMSAT-EA (Spain) have already been announced.
faq.q: What records have Alba Orbital broken?

faq.a: PocketQubes are highly miniaturised satellites, one eighth the size of a CubeSat, consisting of one or more cubic units of 5 cm with a maximum mass of 250 g per unit or ‘P'. This reduces the costs and development time involved with satellite development, widening the doors of space access for small organisations to develop their own space program.
faq.q: How is Alba Orbital democratising access to space?

eoportal.name_zh: Alba Orbital独角兽微型卫星（Unicorn PocketQubes）
eoportal.summary_zh: 前两颗卫星于2019年12月发射。
eoportal.summary_en: The first two satellites were launched in December 2019.

faq_zh.a: Alba Cluster 3是迄今为止规模最大的PocketQube发射任务。它建立在该公司2019年成功的Alba Cluster 2发射基础上，那次任务将6颗PocketQubes送入轨道，其中包括两颗Alba Orbital的Unicorn-2皮卫星。Alba Cluster 3使用公司的AlbaPod v2发射，该装置允许PocketQubes作为‘共享搭载’任务的一部分集成到任何运载火箭中。
faq_zh.q: Alba Cluster 3与其它Alba Cluster任务有何不同？

faq_zh.a: Alba Orbital（英国、美国、德国）正在让小型组织能更轻松、更快捷、更经济地进入太空。仅Alba Orbital今年1月通过SpaceX的一次发射就打破了记录，成为迄今规模最大的PocketQube发射，共搭载了13颗卫星。未来与Alba AMSAT-EA（西班牙）的共享搭载计划也已公布。
PocketQubes是高度微型化的卫星，大小仅为立方体卫星的八分之一，由一个或多个5厘米的立方体单元组成，每个单元（或称‘P’）的最大质量为250克。这降低了卫星开发的成本和时间，为小型组织开发自己的太空计划敞开了大门。
faq_zh.q: Alba Orbital如何使太空准入变得民主化？

faq_en.a: Alba Cluster 3 is the largest PocketQube launch in history to date. It builds on the company's successful Alba Cluster 2 launch in 2019, which deployed six PocketQubes into orbit, including two of Alba Orbital's Unicorn-2 pico-satellites. Alba Cluster 3 was launched using the company's AlbaPod v2, which allows PocketQubes to be integrated into any launch vehicle as part of a ‘rideshare' mission.
faq_en.q: How does Alba Cluster 3 differ from other Alba Cluster missions?

faq_en.a: Alba Orbital (UK, USA, Germany) is making space access easier, faster and more affordable for small organisations. Alba's January SpaceX flight alone broke records as the world's largest PocketQube launch to date with 13 satellites manifested on board. Future rideshare plans with Alba AMSAT-EA (Spain) have already been announced.
faq_en.q: What records have Alba Orbital broken?

faq_en.a: PocketQubes are highly miniaturised satellites, one eighth the size of a CubeSat, consisting of one or more cubic units of 5 cm with a maximum mass of 250 g per unit or ‘P'. This reduces the costs and development time involved with satellite development, widening the doors of space access for small organisations to develop their own space program.

eoportal.name: ALISIO-1 (Advanced Land-Imaging Satellite for Infrared Observations)
eoportal.summary: Via an optical camera, the satellite observes a variety of environmental concerns from atmospheric conditions, wildfires, desertification, heatwaves, volcanic activity and other conditions that can trigger natural disasters.

faq.a: The ALISIO-1 satellite’s objective is to monitor the global environment and gather data for agriculture, forestry, disaster management and more. It collects important environmental data that can help scientists recognise trends and warning signs over time, better preparing us for extreme climate events.
faq.q: What are the objectives of the ALISIO-1 satellite?

faq.a: ALISIO-1 was launched on December 1, 2023 aboard D-Orbit’s ION satellite carrier on Space X’s Falcon-9 rocket.
faq.q: When was ALISIO-1 launched?

faq.a: IACTEC in conjunction with Open Cosmos, who designed and built the satellite, and Deimos and D-Orbit, who handled the in-orbit gate.
faq.q: Who developed ALISIO-1?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该卫星通过光学相机观测多种环境问题，包括大气状况、野火、荒漠化、热浪、火山活动及其他可能引发自然灾害的状况。
eoportal.summary_en: Via an optical camera, the satellite observes a variety of environmental concerns from atmospheric conditions, wildfires, desertification, heatwaves, volcanic activity and other conditions that can trigger natural disasters.

faq_zh.a: ALISIO-1 卫星的目标是监测全球环境，并为农业、林业、灾害管理等领域收集数据。它收集重要的环境数据，帮助科学家识别随时间变化的趋势和预警信号，从而更好地应对极端气候事件。
faq_zh.q: ALISIO-1 卫星的目标是什么？

faq_zh.a: ALISIO-1 于 2023 年 12 月 1 日搭载 SpaceX 的猎鹰-9 号火箭，通过 D-Orbit 的 ION 卫星运载器发射升空。
ALISIO-1 由 IACTEC 与负责设计和建造的 Open Cosmos，以及处理在轨接口的 Deimos 和 D-Orbit 联合开发。

It collects important environmental data that can help scientists recognise trends and warning signs over time, better preparing us for extreme climate events.

Discover the ALOS-2 (Daichi) satellite mission and its advanced SAR capabilities for Earth observation and disaster monitoring.

faq.a: ALOS only had one paddle to ensure that the mission sensor onboard could secure a broader view, but there was no alternative source of power if that paddle was broken. ALOS-2’s radar pointed towards the nadir direction (directly towards Earth), so a second paddle would not obstruct its view. Thus, a second paddle was installed to ensure a backup source of power if one paddle is damaged.
faq.q: Why does ALOS-2 have two solar array paddles, but ALOS only had one?

faq.a: Radio waves have the benefit of being able to be used day and night as it does not rely on sunlight being reflected from Earth. L-band radio waves are special due to their longer wavelength of 15 - 30 cm, which allows them to penetrate more objects than shorter-wavelength radio waves. They can penetrate rain and clouds allowing ALOS-2 to operate regardless of weather, and can also penetrate forest canopies. The ability to penetrate vegetation is useful for studying land deformations which are prevalent in earthquake-prone Japan.
faq.q: Why does the imaging radar on ALOS-2 use L-band radio waves?

faq.a: After separating from the launch vehicle, ALOS-2 entered Rate Damping Mode in which the rate at which it was spinning was slowed using thrusters. Sun Acquisition Mode then began in which the solar array paddles extended and the satellite orientated itself such that the paddles faced the sun. ALOS-2 then turned to face the Earth in Earth Acquisition Mode. Following this, the L-band SAR antenna and transmission antenna was deployed before Fine Normal Mode, in which the equipment and orbit were verified for operation.
faq.q: What steps were taken from the launch of ALOS-2 for it to be operational?

faq_zh.a: ALOS只有一块太阳能电池板，以确保其搭载的传感器能获得更宽的视野，但如果该电池板损坏，则没有备用电源。ALOS-2的雷达指向星下点（直接朝向地球），因此第二块电池板不会阻碍其视野。因此，ALOS-2安装了两块电池板，以确保在一块损坏时仍有备用电源。
faq_zh.q: 为什么ALOS-2有两块太阳能电池板，而ALOS只有一块？

faq_zh.a: 无线电波的优势在于可以全天候工作，因为它不依赖阳光的反射。L波段无线电波的波长较长（15-30厘米），这使得它们能够比短波长的无线电波穿透更多物体。它们可以穿透雨云，使ALOS-2能够无视天气运行，也可以穿透森林冠层。这种穿透植被的能力对于研究地震多发的日本常见的地壳形变非常有用。
faq_zh.q: 为什么ALOS-2上的成像雷达使用L波段无线电波？

faq_zh.a: ALOS-2与运载火箭分离后，进入阻尼模式，利用推进器减缓其自转速度。随后进入太阳捕获模式，太阳能电池板展开，卫星调整姿态使电池板朝向太阳。接着进入地球捕获模式，卫星转向朝向地球。此后，L波段SAR天线和传输天线展开，最后进入正常精调模式，验证设备和轨道以备运行。
faq_zh.q: ALOS-2从发射到运行经历了哪些步骤？

faq_en.a: ALOS only had one paddle to ensure that the mission sensor onboard could secure a broader view, but there was no alternative source of power if that paddle was broken. ALOS-2’s radar pointed towards the nadir direction (directly towards Earth), so a second paddle would not obstruct its view. Thus, a second paddle was installed to ensure a backup source of power if one paddle is damaged.
faq_en.q: Why does ALOS-2 have two solar array paddles, but ALOS only had one?

faq_en.a: Radio waves have the benefit of being able to be used day and night as it does not rely on sunlight being reflected from Earth. L-band radio waves are special due to their longer wavelength of 15 - 30 cm, which allows them to penetrate more objects than shorter-wavelength radio waves. They can penetrate rain and clouds allowing ALOS-2 to operate regardless of weather, and can also penetrate forest canopies. The ability to penetrate vegetation is useful for studying land deformations which are prevalent in earthquake-prone Japan.
faq_en.q: Why does the imaging radar on ALOS-2 use L-band radio waves?

faq_en.a: After separating from the launch vehicle, ALOS-2 entered Rate Damping Mode in which the rate at which it was spinning was slowed using thrusters. Sun Acquisition Mode then began in which the solar array paddles extended and the satellite orientated itself such that the paddles faced the sun. ALOS-2 then turned to face the Earth in Earth Acquisition Mode. Following this, the L-band SAR antenna and transmission antenna was deployed before Fine Normal Mode, in which the equipment and orbit were verified for operation.
faq_en.q: What steps were taken from the launch of ALOS-2 for it to be operational?

eoportal.name: ALOS-3 (Advanced Land Observing Satellite-3)
taxonomy: Cloud type, amount and cloud top temperature
Ocean imagery and water leaving spectral radiance
Sea ice cover, edge and thickness
eoportal.summary: The launcher failed shortly after launch, and the payload did not make it into orbit.

faq.a: ALOS-3 aimed to provide supporting imagery in the applications of environmental and disaster monitoring, the surveying of crops and coastal fishing conditions, and the continuous updating of existing geographical data archives.
faq.q: What was the aim of ALOS-3?

faq.a: ALOS-3 is owned, operated and developed by JAXA (Japan Aerospace Exploration Agency).
faq.q: Who operates ALOS-3?

faq.a: The ALOS-3 mission failed due to the second stage engine failing to ignite, which made the spacecraft unable to enter the Earth’s orbit as planned. The rocket and payload were destroyed in a destruct command, sent to avoid risks of failing debris.
faq.q: Why did the ALOS-3 mission fail?

faq.a: ALOS-3 carried two instruments, PRISM-2 (Panchromatic Remote-sensing Instrument for Stereo Mapping), a stereo high-resolution instrument, and HISUI (Hyperspectral Imager Suite), which consists of a multispectral sensor (MSS) and a hyperspectral sensor (HSS).
faq.q: What instruments did ALOS-3 carry?

eoportal.agency_zh: JAXA (日本宇宙航空研究开发机构)
eoportal.summary_zh: 该卫星在发射后不久运载火箭即告失败，有效载荷未能进入预定轨道。
eoportal.summary_en: The launcher failed shortly after launch, and the payload did not make it into orbit.

faq_zh.a: ALOS-3 旨在为环境与灾害监测、农作物及沿海渔业状况调查，以及持续更新现有地理数据档案等应用提供支持性影像。
ALOS-3 由 JAXA (日本宇宙航空研究开发机构) 所有、运营和开发。
ALOS-3 任务失败是由于第二级发动机未能点火，导致航天器无法按计划进入地球轨道。为避免碎片坠落风险，火箭和有效载荷在收到自毁指令后被摧毁。
ALOS-3 携带了两台仪器：用于立体测绘的 PRISM-2（全色遥感立体测绘仪，一种高分辨率立体观测仪器）和 HISUI（高光谱成像套件），后者由一台多光谱传感器 (MSS) 和一台高光谱传感器 (HSS) 组成。

faq_en.a: ALOS-3 aimed to provide supporting imagery in the applications of environmental and disaster monitoring, the surveying of crops and coastal fishing conditions, and the continuous updating of existing geographical data archives.
faq_en.q: What was the aim of ALOS-3?

faq_en.a: ALOS-3 is owned, operated and developed by JAXA (Japan Aerospace Exploration Agency).
faq_en.q: Who operates ALOS-3?

faq_en.a: The ALOS-3 mission failed due to the second stage engine failing to ignite, which made the spacecraft unable to enter the Earth’s orbit as planned. The rocket and payload were destroyed in a destruct command, sent to avoid risks of failing debris.
faq_en.q: Why did the ALOS-3 mission fail?

faq_en.a: ALOS-3 carried two instruments, PRISM-2 (Panchromatic Remote-sensing Instrument for Stereo Mapping), a stereo high-resolution instrument, and HISUI (Hyperspectral Imager Suite), which consists of a multispectral sensor (MSS) and a hyperspectral sensor (HSS).
faq_en.q: What instruments did ALOS-3 carry?

eoportal.name: ALOS-4 (Advanced Land Observing Satellite-4 )
taxonomy: Ocean imagery and water leaving spectral radiance
Sea ice cover, edge and thickness

faq.a: The Advanced Land Observing Satellite-4 (ALOS-4) was launched on July 1, 2024on a JAXA H3 rocket from the Tanegashima Space Centre.
faq.q: When was ALOS-4 be launched?

faq.a: The Phased Array type L-band Synthetic Aperture Radar-3 (PALSAR-3) onboard the Advanced Land Observing Satellite-4 (ALOS-4) will be able to measure at a spatial resolution of 3 m x 1 m in its spotlight mode.
faq.q: What is the greatest spatial resolution the ALOS-4 can measure?

faq.a: The Advanced Land Observing Satellite-4 (ALOS-4) mission will replace the current ALOS-2 mission and aims at observing and monitoring disaster-hit areas, forests, sea-ice, and monitoring infrastructure displacement with a greater resolution than ALOS-2. The mission will also be able to monitor vessels in the oceans and heavy marine traffic areas.
faq.q: What is the purpose of ALOS-4?

faq_zh.a: 先进陆地观测卫星-4（ALOS-4）于2024年7月1日由JAXA的H3火箭从种子岛航天中心发射升空。
先进陆地观测卫星-4（ALOS-4）搭载的相控阵L波段合成孔径雷达-3（PALSAR-3）在聚束模式下能够实现3米×1米的空间分辨率。
faq_zh.q: ALOS-4能实现的最大空间分辨率是多少？

faq_zh.a: 先进陆地观测卫星-4（ALOS-4）任务旨在取代当前的ALOS-2任务，其目标是观测和监测灾区、森林和海冰，并以高于ALOS-2的分辨率监测基础设施位移。此外，该任务还能监测海洋及繁忙海上交通区域的船舶。

faq_en.a: The Advanced Land Observing Satellite-4 (ALOS-4) was launched on July 1, 2024on a JAXA H3 rocket from the Tanegashima Space Centre.
faq_en.q: When was ALOS-4 be launched?

faq_en.a: The Phased Array type L-band Synthetic Aperture Radar-3 (PALSAR-3) onboard the Advanced Land Observing Satellite-4 (ALOS-4) will be able to measure at a spatial resolution of 3 m x 1 m in its spotlight mode.
faq_en.q: What is the greatest spatial resolution the ALOS-4 can measure?

faq_en.a: The Advanced Land Observing Satellite-4 (ALOS-4) mission will replace the current ALOS-2 mission and aims at observing and monitoring disaster-hit areas, forests, sea-ice, and monitoring infrastructure displacement with a greater resolution than ALOS-2. The mission will also be able to monitor vessels in the oceans and heavy marine traffic areas.
faq_en.q: What is the purpose of ALOS-4?

eoportal.name: AlSat-2 (Algeria Satellite-2)
eoportal.summary: These optical imaging satellites are operated by the Algerian Space Agency (ASAL) for a diverse number of applications: cartography, agricultural management, crop monitoring, and disaster management.

faq.a: Algerian engineers of the ALAS (Algerian Space Agency) worked with the French aerospace manufacturer, Astrium, to design and build AlSat-2A. ALAS then built AlSat-2B independently.
faq.q: Who designed and built the AlSat-2 satellites?

faq.a: Both AlSat-2A and -2B were launched aboard an ISRO (Indian Space Research Organisation) built PSLV (Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle) from the Satish Dhawan Space Centre (SDSC) in India.
faq.q: What were the launch details of Al-Sat-2A and -2B?

faq.a: The purpose of AlSat-2 is to widely observe Algeria so high-resolution images can be used for a variety of applications such as cartography, agricultural management, crop monitoring, and disaster management.
faq.q: What is the purpose of the AlSat-2 mission?

eoportal.summary_zh: 这些光学成像卫星由阿尔及利亚航天局（ASAL）运营，应用于制图、农业管理、作物监测和灾害管理等多个领域。
eoportal.summary_en: These optical imaging satellites are operated by the Algerian Space Agency (ASAL) for a diverse number of applications: cartography, agricultural management, crop monitoring, and disaster management.

faq_zh.a: 阿尔及利亚航天局（ALAS）的工程师与法国航空航天制造商Astrium合作设计和建造了AlSat-2A卫星。随后，ALAS独立建造了AlSat-2B卫星。
AlSat-2A和AlSat-2B卫星均由印度空间研究组织（ISRO）研制的极地卫星运载火箭（PSLV）从印度的萨迪什·达万航天中心（SDSC）发射升空。
faq_zh.q: AlSat-2A和AlSat-2B卫星的发射详情是什么？

faq_zh.a: AlSat-2任务的目标是对阿尔及利亚进行广泛观测，以便获取的高分辨率图像可用于制图、农业管理、作物监测和灾害管理等多种应用。

faq_en.a: Algerian engineers of the ALAS (Algerian Space Agency) worked with the French aerospace manufacturer, Astrium, to design and build AlSat-2A. ALAS then built AlSat-2B independently.
faq_en.q: Who designed and built the AlSat-2 satellites?

faq_en.a: Both AlSat-2A and -2B were launched aboard an ISRO (Indian Space Research Organisation) built PSLV (Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle) from the Satish Dhawan Space Centre (SDSC) in India.
faq_en.q: What were the launch details of Al-Sat-2A and -2B?

faq_en.a: The purpose of AlSat-2 is to widely observe Algeria so high-resolution images can be used for a variety of applications such as cartography, agricultural management, crop monitoring, and disaster management.
faq_en.q: What is the purpose of the AlSat-2 mission?

eoportal.name: Altair (Orbitworks)
eoportal.summary: Using onboard edge computing for real-time, in-orbit data processing, the constellation will serve applications in national security, disaster response, and various commercial sectors.

faq.a: Orbitworks, the United Arab Emirates’ (UAE) next-generation satellite manufacturing venture, is formed by Marlan Space, an Abu Dhabi-based space company affiliated with International Holding Company (IHC), and Loft Orbital.
faq.q: Which companies form Orbitworks?

faq.a: The Altair satellites will be built on Loft Orbital’s Longbow platform, which is an ESA-funded upgraded Airbus Arrow 150 platform. The upgrades to the platform were supported under the In Orbit Demonstrator by Airbus (IODA), an ESA Pioneer Partnership Project. The upgraded platform has been used to host the YAM-8 (Yet Another Mission 8) which successfully launched on 14 March, 2025.
faq.q: What platform are the Altair satellites being built on?

faq.a: Each satellite will feature sub-metre optical, shortwave infrared, thermal, hyperspectral, and radio frequency sensors. The onboard computer will allow edge computing for real-time, in-orbit data processing.
faq.q: What sensor types will the Altair satellites carry?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该星座将利用星载边缘计算进行实时在轨数据处理，服务于国家安全、灾害应急响应及多个商业领域的应用。
eoportal.summary_en: Using onboard edge computing for real-time, in-orbit data processing, the constellation will serve applications in national security, disaster response, and various commercial sectors.

faq_zh.a: Orbitworks是阿联酋的下一代卫星制造合资企业，由总部位于阿布扎比、隶属于国际控股公司（IHC）的太空企业Marlan Space和Loft Orbital共同组建。
Altair卫星将基于Loft Orbital的Longbow平台建造，该平台是欧洲航天局（ESA）资助升级的空客Arrow 150平台。平台的升级工作是在欧洲航天局Pioneer合作项目——空客在轨演示器（IODA）的支持下完成的。升级后的平台已用于搭载YAM-8（又一项任务8）载荷，该载荷于2025年3月14日成功发射。
faq_zh.q: Altair卫星基于哪个平台建造？

faq_zh.a: 每颗卫星将配备亚米级光学、短波红外、热红外、高光谱和射频传感器。星载计算机将支持边缘计算，实现数据的实时在轨处理。
faq_zh.q: Altair卫星将搭载哪些类型的传感器？

faq_en.a: Orbitworks, the United Arab Emirates’ (UAE) next-generation satellite manufacturing venture, is formed by Marlan Space, an Abu Dhabi-based space company affiliated with International Holding Company (IHC), and Loft Orbital.
faq_en.q: Which companies form Orbitworks?

faq_en.a: The Altair satellites will be built on Loft Orbital’s Longbow platform, which is an ESA-funded upgraded Airbus Arrow 150 platform. The upgrades to the platform were supported under the In Orbit Demonstrator by Airbus (IODA), an ESA Pioneer Partnership Project. The upgraded platform has been used to host the YAM-8 (Yet Another Mission 8) which successfully launched on 14 March, 2025.
faq_en.q: What platform are the Altair satellites being built on?

faq_en.a: Each satellite will feature sub-metre optical, shortwave infrared, thermal, hyperspectral, and radio frequency sensors. The onboard computer will allow edge computing for real-time, in-orbit data processing.
faq_en.q: What sensor types will the Altair satellites carry?

eoportal.name: ALTIUS (Atmospheric Limb Tracker for the Investigation of the Upcoming Stratosphere)
eoportal.summary: ALTIUS will provide support to services such as weather forecasting, and monitor long-term trends in atmospheric ozone.

faq.a: ALTIUS will be built by Redwire Corporation, formerly QinetiQ Space, for the European Space Agency (ESA).
faq.q: Who is building ALTUIS?

faq.a: The long form of ALTIUS is Atmospheric Limb Tracker for Investigation of the Upcoming Stratosphere.
faq.q: What does ALTIUS stand for?

faq.a: ALTIUS will monitor the distribution and evolution of stratospheric ozone in the Earth’s atmosphere.
faq.q: What will ALTIUS measure?

eoportal.name_zh: ALTIUS (Atmospheric Limb Tracker for the Investigation of the Upcoming Stratosphere)
eoportal.summary_zh: ALTIUS卫星将为天气预报等服务提供支持，并监测大气臭氧的长期变化趋势。
eoportal.summary_en: ALTIUS will provide support to services such as weather forecasting, and monitor long-term trends in atmospheric ozone.

faq_zh.a: ALTIUS卫星将由Redwire公司（前身为QinetiQ Space）为欧洲空间局（ESA）建造。
ALTIUS的全称是Atmospheric Limb Tracker for Investigation of the Upcoming Stratosphere（即用于平流层研究的大气临边追踪器）。
ALTIUS卫星将监测地球大气中平流层臭氧的分布与演变。

faq_en.a: ALTIUS will be built by Redwire Corporation, formerly QinetiQ Space, for the European Space Agency (ESA).
faq_en.q: Who is building ALTUIS?

faq_en.a: The long form of ALTIUS is Atmospheric Limb Tracker for Investigation of the Upcoming Stratosphere.
faq_en.q: What does ALTIUS stand for?

faq_en.a: ALTIUS will monitor the distribution and evolution of stratospheric ozone in the Earth’s atmosphere.
faq_en.q: What will ALTIUS measure?

eoportal.name: Amazonia-1
eoportal.summary: Launched in February 2021, its main goal is to monitor global deforestation, with a focus on the Brazilian Amazon rainforest.

faq.a: Amazonia-1 is the first Earth observation satellite completely designed, integrated, tested and operated by Brazil.
faq.q: Why is Amazonia-1 special for Brazil?

faq.a: The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) launched Brazil's optical Earth observation satellite, Amazonia-1, on 28 February 2021, along with 18 smaller co-passenger satellites, onboard a PSDL-C51 launch vehicle.
faq.q: Who launched Amazonia-1?

faq.a: Amazonia-1 has an orbital period of 99.8 minutes.
faq.q: What is Amazonia-1's orbital period?

eoportal.summary_zh: Amazonia-1于2021年2月发射，其主要目标是监测全球森林砍伐情况，重点聚焦于巴西亚马逊雨林。
eoportal.summary_en: Launched in February 2021, its main goal is to monitor global deforestation, with a focus on the Brazilian Amazon rainforest.

faq_zh.a: Amazonia-1是首颗完全由巴西设计、集成、测试和运营的对地观测卫星。
faq_zh.q: Amazonia-1对巴西而言有何特殊意义？

faq_zh.a: 印度空间研究组织（ISRO）于2021年2月28日使用PSLV-C51运载火箭，将巴西的光学对地观测卫星Amazonia-1与18颗较小的搭载卫星一同发射升空。
Amazonia-1的轨道周期为99.8分钟。

faq_en.a: Amazonia-1 is the first Earth observation satellite completely designed, integrated, tested and operated by Brazil.
faq_en.q: Why is Amazonia-1 special for Brazil?

faq_en.a: The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) launched Brazil's optical Earth observation satellite, Amazonia-1, on 28 February 2021, along with 18 smaller co-passenger satellites, onboard a PSDL-C51 launch vehicle.
faq_en.q: Who launched Amazonia-1?

faq_en.a: Amazonia-1 has an orbital period of 99.8 minutes.
faq_en.q: What is Amazonia-1's orbital period?

eoportal.name: ANDESITE (Ad-hoc Network Demonstration for Spatial-ly Extended Satellite-based Inquiry and Other Team Endeavors)
eoportal.summary: Once in space, the ANDESITE satellite will initiate measurements of the magnetosphere with onboard sensors, later releasing eight picosatellites carrying small magnetometer sensors to track electric currents flowing in and out of the atmosphere, a phenomenon also known as space weather.
eoportal.name_zh: ANDESITE (Ad-hoc Network Demonstration for Spatial-ly Extended Satellite-based Inquiry and Other Team Endeavors)
eoportal.summary_zh: ANDESITE卫星入轨后，将利用星载传感器开始测量磁层，随后释放八颗皮卫星，这些皮卫星携带小型磁力计传感器，用于追踪流入和流出大气层的电流，这种现象也被称为太空天气。
eoportal.summary_en: Once in space, the ANDESITE satellite will initiate measurements of the magnetosphere with onboard sensors, later releasing eight picosatellites carrying small magnetometer sensors to track electric currents flowing in and out of the atmosphere, a phenomenon also known as space weather.

eoportal.name: ANSER (Advanced Nanosatellites System for Earth Observation Research)
eoportal.summary: The satellite is owned and operated by the Spanish Institute of Aerospace Technology (INTA).

faq.a: Originally, the constellation consisted of three ANSER satellites, but due to the deployment failure of ANSER Leader, only two remain operational.
faq.q: How many ANSER Satellites are there?

faq.a: ANSER aims to monitor the water quality of swamps and reservoirs in Spain, using a low cost nanosatellite constellation.
faq.q: What is the aim of the ANSER mission?

faq.a: The passive control system is a novel method of in-orbit manoeuvring that uses adjustable fins to generate lift and drag from Earth’s minimal atmosphere in the constellation’s low orbit.
faq.q: What is the ANSER passive control system?

eoportal.name_zh: ANSER（先进纳卫星地球观测研究系统）
eoportal.agency_zh: INTA（西班牙航空航天技术研究所）
eoportal.summary_zh: 该卫星由西班牙航空航天技术研究所（INTA）拥有并运营。
eoportal.summary_en: The satellite is owned and operated by the Spanish Institute of Aerospace Technology (INTA).

faq_zh.a: 最初，该星座由三颗 ANSER 卫星组成，但由于 ANSER Leader 部署失败，目前仅有两颗在轨运行。
faq_zh.q: 有多少颗 ANSER 卫星？

faq_zh.a: ANSER 旨在利用低成本纳卫星星座，监测西班牙沼泽和水库的水质。
faq_zh.q: ANSER 任务的目标是什么？

faq_zh.a: 被动控制系统是一种新颖的在轨机动方法，它利用可调鳍片，在星座的低轨道上通过地球稀薄的大气产生升力和阻力。
faq_zh.q: 什么是 ANSER 被动控制系统？

faq_en.a: Originally, the constellation consisted of three ANSER satellites, but due to the deployment failure of ANSER Leader, only two remain operational.
faq_en.q: How many ANSER Satellites are there?

faq_en.a: ANSER aims to monitor the water quality of swamps and reservoirs in Spain, using a low cost nanosatellite constellation.
faq_en.q: What is the aim of the ANSER mission?

faq_en.a: The passive control system is a novel method of in-orbit manoeuvring that uses adjustable fins to generate lift and drag from Earth’s minimal atmosphere in the constellation’s low orbit.

eoportal.name: AOS-PMM (Atmosphere Observing System - Precipitation Measuring Mission)
taxonomy: Precipitation intensity at the surface (liquid or solid)
eoportal.summary: AOS-PMM will acquire  measurements of aerosol and cloud particles to understand their influence on extreme weather conditions, and to further our understanding of climate change, mitigate natural hazards, and improve agricultural processes.

faq.a: The Atmosphere Observing System is planned to consist of four satellites, AOS-PMM, AOS-Storm, AOS-Sky, and HAWCSat. AOS-PMM will operate in tandem with AOS-Storm.
faq.q: How many satellites are in the AOS Constellation?

faq.a: AOS-PMM carries out measurements to provide information about aerosols and cloud processes that influence extreme weather conditions and drive climate change.
faq.q: What is AOS-PMM’s purpose?

faq.a: There are two instruments onboard AOS-PMM, the JAXA Ku-Band Doppler Radar which carries out cloud profiling and rain radar, and SAPHIR-NG which will measure ice water path and vertical mass flux.
faq.q: What instruments are onboard AOS-PMM?

eoportal.name_zh: AOS-PMM（大气观测系统 - 降水测量任务）
eoportal.summary_zh: AOS-PMM任务旨在获取气溶胶和云粒子的测量数据，以理解它们对极端天气条件的影响，进而加深我们对气候变化的认识，减轻自然灾害风险，并改进农业过程。
eoportal.summary_en: AOS-PMM will acquire  measurements of aerosol and cloud particles to understand their influence on extreme weather conditions, and to further our understanding of climate change, mitigate natural hazards, and improve agricultural processes.

faq_zh.a: 大气观测系统计划由四颗卫星组成，分别是AOS-PMM、AOS-Storm、AOS-Sky和HAWCSat。AOS-PMM将与AOS-Storm协同工作。
AOS-PMM进行测量，旨在提供有关气溶胶和云过程的信息，这些过程影响极端天气条件并驱动气候变化。
AOS-PMM搭载了两台仪器：JAXA Ku波段多普勒雷达，用于进行云廓线探测和雨量雷达观测；以及SAPHIR-NG，用于测量冰水路径和垂直质量通量。

faq_en.a: The Atmosphere Observing System is planned to consist of four satellites, AOS-PMM, AOS-Storm, AOS-Sky, and HAWCSat. AOS-PMM will operate in tandem with AOS-Storm.
faq_en.q: How many satellites are in the AOS Constellation?

faq_en.a: AOS-PMM carries out measurements to provide information about aerosols and cloud processes that influence extreme weather conditions and drive climate change.
faq_en.q: What is AOS-PMM’s purpose?

faq_en.a: There are two instruments onboard AOS-PMM, the JAXA Ku-Band Doppler Radar which carries out cloud profiling and rain radar, and SAPHIR-NG which will measure ice water path and vertical mass flux.
faq_en.q: What instruments are onboard AOS-PMM?

eoportal.name: Aqua (EOS/PM-1)
taxonomy: Cloud type, amount and cloud top temperature
Ocean imagery and water leaving spectral radiance
Downward long-wave irradiance at Earth surface
Precipitation intensity at the surface (liquid or solid)
Sea ice cover, edge and thickness
Downward short-wave irradiance at Earth surface
Upwelling (Outgoing) long-wave radiation at Earth surface
eoportal.summary: Launched in May 2002 from Vandenberg Air Force Base, California, USA, Aqua had a design life of six years, however remains operational as of July 2022.

faq.a: Aqua was launched in May 2002 from Vandenberg Air Force Base, California, USA.
faq.q: When and where did Aqua launch?

faq.a: Aqua has six instruments: the Atmospheric Infra-red Sounder (AIRS), the Advanced Microwave Scanning Radiometer-EOS (AMSR-E), the Advanced Microwave Sounding Unit-A (AMSU-A), the Cloud and the Earth’s Radiant Energy System (CERES), the Humidity Sounder for Brazil (HSB), and the Moderate-Resolution Imaging Spectroradiometer (MODIS).
faq.q: How many instruments does Aqua have?

faq.a: The focus of the Aqua mission is the multidisciplinary study of the Earth's water cycle, including the interrelated processes (atmosphere, oceans, and land surface) and their relationship to Earth system changes. Aqua collects data on cloud formation, precipitation, radiative properties, air-sea fluxes of energy, carbon and moisture, and sea ice concentrations and extents.
faq.q: What is the purpose of a satellite like Aqua?

eoportal.summary_zh: Aqua卫星于2002年5月从美国加利福尼亚州范登堡空军基地发射，设计寿命为六年，但截至2022年7月仍在轨运行。
eoportal.summary_en: Launched in May 2002 from Vandenberg Air Force Base, California, USA, Aqua had a design life of six years, however remains operational as of July 2022.

faq_zh.a: Aqua卫星于2002年5月从美国加利福尼亚州范登堡空军基地发射。
Aqua卫星搭载了六台仪器：大气红外探测器（AIRS）、先进微波扫描辐射计-EOS（AMSR-E）、先进微波探测单元-A（AMSU-A）、云与地球辐射能量系统（CERES）、巴西湿度探测器（HSB）以及中分辨率成像光谱仪（MODIS）。
faq_zh.q: Aqua卫星搭载了多少台仪器？

faq_zh.a: Aqua任务的核心是跨学科研究地球水循环，包括其相互关联的过程（大气、海洋和地表）及其与地球系统变化的关系。Aqua收集有关云形成、降水、辐射特性、海气能量、碳和水分通量以及海冰浓度和范围的数据。
faq_zh.q: 像Aqua这样的卫星有什么目的？

faq_en.a: Aqua was launched in May 2002 from Vandenberg Air Force Base, California, USA.
faq_en.q: When and where did Aqua launch?

faq_en.a: Aqua has six instruments: the Atmospheric Infra-red Sounder (AIRS), the Advanced Microwave Scanning Radiometer-EOS (AMSR-E), the Advanced Microwave Sounding Unit-A (AMSU-A), the Cloud and the Earth’s Radiant Energy System (CERES), the Humidity Sounder for Brazil (HSB), and the Moderate-Resolution Imaging Spectroradiometer (MODIS).
faq_en.q: How many instruments does Aqua have?

faq_en.a: The focus of the Aqua mission is the multidisciplinary study of the Earth's water cycle, including the interrelated processes (atmosphere, oceans, and land surface) and their relationship to Earth system changes. Aqua collects data on cloud formation, precipitation, radiative properties, air-sea fluxes of energy, carbon and moisture, and sea ice concentrations and extents.
faq_en.q: What is the purpose of a satellite like Aqua?

eoportal.summary: The mission aims to monitor the Earth’s atmosphere and surface in the Arctic, collect and relay information from Arctic facilities, monitor cosmic rays, and assist in the international search and rescue service, COSPAR-SARSAT.

faq.a: Arktika-M is a multipurpose mission that aims to monitor the Earth’s atmosphere and surface in the polar regions, collect and relay information from polar-based facilities, monitor cosmic rays, and assist in the international search and rescue service, COSPAR-SARSAT.
faq.q: What is the purpose of Arktika-M?

faq.a: Arktika-M N1 satellite was launched aboard a Soyuz-2 launch vehicle on February 28, 2021, at 09:55 Moscow Time (06:55 UTC) from the Baikonur Cosmodrome in Kazakhstan.
faq.q: What were the launch details of Arkitka-M N1?

faq.a: A Molniya orbit is a highly eccentric orbit (HEO) with an inclination of 63.4° and an argument of perigee of 270°. It has an apogee of 39750 km, a perigee of 1043 km, and a period of half a sidereal day (11 hours and 58 minutes). It’s able to monitor polar regions for extended periods of time which is extremely useful due to the inability of satellites to maintain a geostationary orbit over the polar regions.
faq.q: What is a Molniya orbit?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该任务旨在监测北极地区的大气层和地表，收集并转发来自北极设施的信息，监测宇宙射线，并协助参与国际搜索与救援服务COSPAR-SARSAT。
eoportal.summary_en: The mission aims to monitor the Earth’s atmosphere and surface in the Arctic, collect and relay information from Arctic facilities, monitor cosmic rays, and assist in the international search and rescue service, COSPAR-SARSAT.

faq_zh.a: Arktika-M是一项多用途任务，旨在监测极地地区的大气层和地表，收集并转发来自极地设施的信息，监测宇宙射线，并协助参与国际搜索与救援服务COSPAR-SARSAT。
Arktika-M N1卫星于2021年2月28日莫斯科时间09:55（协调世界时06:55）从哈萨克斯坦拜科努尔航天发射场搭乘联盟-2运载火箭发射升空。
faq_zh.q: Arktika-M N1的发射细节是什么？

faq_zh.a: 闪电轨道是一种大偏心轨道，轨道倾角为63.4°，近地点幅角为270°。其远地点高度为39750公里，近地点高度为1043公里，轨道周期为半个恒星日（11小时58分钟）。该轨道能够长时间监测极地地区，由于卫星无法在极地地区维持地球静止轨道，这一特性极为有用。

faq_en.a: Arktika-M is a multipurpose mission that aims to monitor the Earth’s atmosphere and surface in the polar regions, collect and relay information from polar-based facilities, monitor cosmic rays, and assist in the international search and rescue service, COSPAR-SARSAT.
faq_en.q: What is the purpose of Arktika-M?

faq_en.a: Arktika-M N1 satellite was launched aboard a Soyuz-2 launch vehicle on February 28, 2021, at 09:55 Moscow Time (06:55 UTC) from the Baikonur Cosmodrome in Kazakhstan.
faq_en.q: What were the launch details of Arkitka-M N1?

faq_en.a: A Molniya orbit is a highly eccentric orbit (HEO) with an inclination of 63.4° and an argument of perigee of 270°. It has an apogee of 39750 km, a perigee of 1043 km, and a period of half a sidereal day (11 hours and 58 minutes). It’s able to monitor polar regions for extended periods of time which is extremely useful due to the inability of satellites to maintain a geostationary orbit over the polar regions.
faq_en.q: What is a Molniya orbit?

eoportal.name: ARMADILLO (Attitude Related Maneuvers And Debris Instrument in Low (L) Orbit)
eoportal.summary: The mission’s primary purpose is to demonstrate space debris detection and characterization technologies, including the Miniature Electrostatic Analyzer (MESA) payload for measuring charged particles in low Earth orbit.
eoportal.name_zh: ARMADILLO (Attitude Related Maneuvers And Debris Instrument in Low (L) Orbit)
eoportal.summary_zh: 该任务的主要目的是演示空间碎片探测与表征技术，包括用于测量低地球轨道带电粒子的小型静电分析仪（MESA）载荷。
eoportal.summary_en: The mission’s primary purpose is to demonstrate space debris detection and characterization technologies, including the Miniature Electrostatic Analyzer (MESA) payload for measuring charged particles in low Earth orbit.

eoportal.name: Artemis Program
eoportal.summary: It aims to establish a sustained human presence on the Moon and build a foundation for long-term exploration beyond Earth.

faq.a: The Artemis program was developed to return humans to the Moon, support sustained exploration on and around the Moon, and build experience and capabilities for future human missions to Mars. Before later landing missions, NASA is using Artemis flights to test the Orion spacecraft, the Space Launch System, and commercially developed Human Landing Systems.
faq.q: Why was the Artemis Program developed?

faq.a: NASA has five currently planned Artemis missions, Artemis I through V, with six additional missions proposed.
faq.q: How many Artemis missions are planned?

faq.a: The Artemis Base Camp is being developed to allow astronauts to stay on the Moon’s surface for more than a month at a time, enabling them to collect and analyse minerals and resources there.
faq.q: How will the astronauts be able to live on the Moon?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该计划旨在月球上建立可持续的人类驻留，并为超越地球的长期探索奠定基础。
eoportal.summary_en: It aims to establish a sustained human presence on the Moon and build a foundation for long-term exploration beyond Earth.

faq_zh.a: 阿耳忒弥斯计划旨在将人类送回月球，支持在月球表面及轨道的持续探索，并为未来载人火星任务积累经验与能力。在后续的着陆任务之前，NASA 正利用阿耳忒弥斯飞行任务测试猎户座飞船、太空发射系统以及商业开发的载人着陆系统。
faq_zh.q: 阿耳忒弥斯计划为何而制定？

faq_zh.a: NASA 目前规划了五次阿耳忒弥斯任务（阿耳忒弥斯 I 至 V），并提议再增加六次任务。
faq_zh.q: 阿耳忒弥斯计划规划了多少次任务？

faq_zh.a: 阿耳忒弥斯大本营正在建设中，旨在使宇航员能够一次在月球表面驻留超过一个月，从而能够收集和分析那里的矿物和资源。
faq_zh.q: 宇航员将如何在月球上生活？

faq_en.a: The Artemis program was developed to return humans to the Moon, support sustained exploration on and around the Moon, and build experience and capabilities for future human missions to Mars. Before later landing missions, NASA is using Artemis flights to test the Orion spacecraft, the Space Launch System, and commercially developed Human Landing Systems.
faq_en.q: Why was the Artemis Program developed?

faq_en.a: NASA has five currently planned Artemis missions, Artemis I through V, with six additional missions proposed.

eoportal.name: ASBM (Arctic Satellite Broadband Mission)
eoportal.summary: The mission is jointly operated by Space Norway’s subsidiary HEOSAT, Viasat, and the US Space Force (USSF), with the satellites developed by Northrop Grumman.

faq.a: The Arctic Satellite Broadband Mission (ASBM) is owned by Space Norway, operated by the state-owned company’s subsidiary HEOSAT and the US Space Force, and developed by the company Northrop Grumman.
faq.q: Who owns the Arctic Satellite Broadband Mission?

faq.a: The Arctic Satellite Broadband Mission (ASBM) aims to provide broadband coverage to the Arctic, closing a connectivity gap. The mission will fulfil an emerging commercial and strategic interest to the Arctic driven by retreating polar ice, which requires the development of crucial infrastructure like mobile broadband.
faq.q: What is the purpose of the Arctic Satellite Broadband Mission?

faq.a: The ASBM satellites carry radio communication payloads, each of them with a Ka-band Global Xpress 10 from Viasat, an X-band radio communications instrument for the Norwegian Ministry of Defence, and the Enhanced Polar Systems-Recapitalization (EPS-R) instrument from the US Space Force. ASBM-1 will also carry the Norwegian Radiation Monitor (NORM).
faq.q: What instruments does the Arctic Satellite Broadband Mission carry?

eoportal.name_zh: 北极卫星宽带任务 (Arctic Satellite Broadband Mission, ASBM)
eoportal.summary_zh: 该任务由Space Norway的子公司HEOSAT、Viasat和美国太空军（USSF）联合运营，卫星由诺斯罗普·格鲁曼公司研制。
eoportal.summary_en: The mission is jointly operated by Space Norway’s subsidiary HEOSAT, Viasat, and the US Space Force (USSF), with the satellites developed by Northrop Grumman.

faq_zh.a: 北极卫星宽带任务（ASBM）由Space Norway所有，由这家国有公司的子公司HEOSAT和美国太空军运营，并由诺斯罗普·格鲁曼公司研制。
faq_zh.q: 谁拥有北极卫星宽带任务？

faq_zh.a: 北极卫星宽带任务（ASBM）旨在为北极地区提供宽带覆盖，弥合通信空白。该任务将满足因极地冰盖退缩而产生的对北极的商业和战略利益需求，这要求开发移动宽带等关键基础设施。
faq_zh.q: 北极卫星宽带任务的目标是什么？

faq_zh.a: ASBM卫星搭载了无线电通信有效载荷，每颗卫星都包括Viasat公司的Ka波段Global Xpress 10设备、挪威国防部的X波段无线电通信设备，以及美国太空军的“增强极地系统-再资本化”（EPS-R）设备。ASBM-1还将搭载挪威辐射监测仪（NORM）。
faq_zh.q: 北极卫星宽带任务携带了哪些仪器？

faq_en.a: The Arctic Satellite Broadband Mission (ASBM) is owned by Space Norway, operated by the state-owned company’s subsidiary HEOSAT and the US Space Force, and developed by the company Northrop Grumman.
faq_en.q: Who owns the Arctic Satellite Broadband Mission?

faq_en.a: The Arctic Satellite Broadband Mission (ASBM) aims to provide broadband coverage to the Arctic, closing a connectivity gap. The mission will fulfil an emerging commercial and strategic interest to the Arctic driven by retreating polar ice, which requires the development of crucial infrastructure like mobile broadband.
faq_en.q: What is the purpose of the Arctic Satellite Broadband Mission?

faq_en.a: The ASBM satellites carry radio communication payloads, each of them with a Ka-band Global Xpress 10 from Viasat, an X-band radio communications instrument for the Norwegian Ministry of Defence, and the Enhanced Polar Systems-Recapitalization (EPS-R) instrument from the US Space Force. ASBM-1 will also carry the Norwegian Radiation Monitor (NORM).
faq_en.q: What instruments does the Arctic Satellite Broadband Mission carry?

eoportal.name: ASNARO (Advanced Satellite with New system ARchitecture for Observation)
eoportal.summary: The aim for the mission is to use open architecture techniques and manufacturing methods to develop a high-performance minisatellite bus system, in turn allowing for up-to-date technology to be utilised, as well as drastically reducing costs and development time.

faq.a: ASNARO was launched in November 2014, on board a Dnepr-1 launch vehicle from Dombarovsky in Russia.
faq.q: When was ASNARO launched?

faq.a: The NEC Corporation and the Institute for Unmanned Space Experiment Free Flyer (USEF) is the agency responsible for developing and managing ASNARO, with funding provided through the Japanese Government.
faq.q: Which agencies are responsible for managing ASNARO?

faq.a: ASNARO aims to develop next-generation minisatellite bus systems which utilise open architecture techniques and manufacturing methods to reduce the overall cost and development period of a satellite, whilst providing high-resolution Earth observation imaging.
faq.q: What were the aims of ASNARO?

eoportal.name_zh: ASNARO（先进观测系统新架构卫星）
eoportal.summary_zh: 该任务的目标是采用开放架构技术和制造方法，开发一种高性能的小卫星平台系统，从而能够利用最新技术，同时大幅降低卫星的成本和研发周期。
eoportal.summary_en: The aim for the mission is to use open architecture techniques and manufacturing methods to develop a high-performance minisatellite bus system, in turn allowing for up-to-date technology to be utilised, as well as drastically reducing costs and development time.

faq_zh.a: ASNARO于2014年11月搭载第聂伯-1运载火箭，从俄罗斯的多姆巴罗夫斯基发射升空。
NEC公司和无人空间实验自由飞行器研究所（USEF）负责开发和管理ASNARO，资金由日本政府提供。
ASNARO的目标是利用开放架构技术和制造方法，开发下一代小卫星平台系统，以降低卫星的整体成本和研发周期，同时提供高分辨率的地球观测图像。

faq_en.a: ASNARO was launched in November 2014, on board a Dnepr-1 launch vehicle from Dombarovsky in Russia.
faq_en.q: When was ASNARO launched?

faq_en.a: The NEC Corporation and the Institute for Unmanned Space Experiment Free Flyer (USEF) is the agency responsible for developing and managing ASNARO, with funding provided through the Japanese Government.
faq_en.q: Which agencies are responsible for managing ASNARO?

faq_en.a: ASNARO aims to develop next-generation minisatellite bus systems which utilise open architecture techniques and manufacturing methods to reduce the overall cost and development period of a satellite, whilst providing high-resolution Earth observation imaging.
faq_en.q: What were the aims of ASNARO?

eoportal.summary: USEF (Institute for Unmanned Space Experiment Free Flyer), and funded by NEDO (New Energy and Industrial Technology Development Organization) and the Government of Japan for commercial use.

faq.a: ASNARO-2 was launched on January 17, 2018 on an Epsilon-3 vehicle from the JAXA (Japan Aerospace Exploration Agency) Uchinoura Space Centre in Kagoshima Japan.
faq.q: When was ASNARO-2 launched?

faq.a: ASNARO-2 was developed by the NEC (Nippon Electric Company, Ltd.) corporation and USEF (Institute for Unmanned Space Experiment Free Flyer) and funded by NEDO (New Energy and Industrial Technology Development Organization) and the Government of Japan.
faq.q: Who developed ASNARO-2?

faq.a: ASNARO-2 carries an X-band Synthetic Aperture Radar (XSAR).

eoportal.name_zh: ASNARO-2 (高级观测卫星系统架构-2)
eoportal.summary_zh: ASNARO-2 由 USEF（无人空间实验自由飞行器研究所）运营，并由 NEDO（新能源产业技术综合开发机构）和日本政府资助，用于商业应用。
eoportal.summary_en: USEF (Institute for Unmanned Space Experiment Free Flyer), and funded by NEDO (New Energy and Industrial Technology Development Organization) and the Government of Japan for commercial use.

faq_zh.a: ASNARO-2 于2018年1月17日从日本鹿儿岛县 JAXA（日本宇宙航空研究开发机构）的内之浦宇宙空间观测所，使用 Epsilon-3 运载火箭发射升空。
ASNARO-2 由 NEC（日本电气公司）和 USEF（无人空间实验自由飞行器研究所）共同开发，并由 NEDO（新能源产业技术综合开发机构）和日本政府资助。
ASNARO-2 搭载了一台 X 波段合成孔径雷达（XSAR）。

faq_en.q: When was ASNARO-2 launched?

faq_en.a: ASNARO-2 was developed by the NEC (Nippon Electric Company, Ltd.) corporation and USEF (Institute for Unmanned Space Experiment Free Flyer) and funded by NEDO (New Energy and Industrial Technology Development Organization) and the Government of Japan.
faq_en.q: Who developed ASNARO-2?

faq_en.a: ASNARO-2 carries an X-band Synthetic Aperture Radar (XSAR).

eoportal.name: AstroSat (Astronomy Satellite)
eoportal.summary: Its purpose was to observe celestial sources in visible, ultraviolet, and X-ray wavelengths, enabling studies of star formation, high-energy phenomena, and distant galaxies.
eoportal.summary_zh: AstroSat卫星旨在对天体在可见光、紫外线和X射线波段进行观测，从而支持对恒星形成、高能现象及遥远星系的研究。
eoportal.summary_en: Its purpose was to observe celestial sources in visible, ultraviolet, and X-ray wavelengths, enabling studies of star formation, high-energy phenomena, and distant galaxies.

eoportal.name: Aura (EOS/Chem-1)
taxonomy: Cloud type, amount and cloud top temperature
eoportal.summary: Launched in July 2004, Aura is the third mission in the Earth Observing Satellite (EOS) series, following on from Terra and Aqua.

faq.a: Yes, the Aura satellite has surpassed its six-year nominal life from 2004 and is still operational, however the HIRDLS instrument failed in 2008.
faq.q: Is the Aura satellite still in orbit and operational?

faq.a: The Afternoon-train (A-train) is a constellation of satellites that closely follow one another along the same, or very similar, orbital track. The original constellation consisted of CloudSat, CALIPSO, Aqua, Aura, and Parasol with GCOM-W1 and OCO-2 joining later. Cloudsat exited the A-train following the loss of one of its reaction wheels in 2018 and it was decided that CALIPSO would also change its orbit to join CloudSat in the new C-train.
faq.q: What is the A-train?

faq.a: Aura, which is owned and operated by the National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA), has the overall objective of studying the chemistry and dynamics of Earth’s atmosphere from the ground through the mesosphere.
faq.q: What is the aim of Aura?

eoportal.summary_zh: Aura卫星于2004年7月发射，是地球观测卫星（EOS）系列继Terra和Aqua之后的第三个任务。
eoportal.summary_en: Launched in July 2004, Aura is the third mission in the Earth Observing Satellite (EOS) series, following on from Terra and Aqua.

faq_zh.a: 是的，Aura卫星已超过其自2004年起的六年设计寿命，目前仍在运行，但其HIRDLS仪器已于2008年发生故障。
A-列车（Afternoon-train）是一个卫星星座，其成员卫星沿着相同或非常相似的轨道轨迹紧密跟随。最初的星座由CloudSat、CALIPSO、Aqua、Aura和Parasol组成，后来GCOM-W1和OCO-2也加入其中。CloudSat在2018年因一个反作用轮失效而退出了A-列车，CALIPSO也决定改变轨道，加入CloudSat组成新的C-列车。
Aura卫星由NASA拥有并运营，其总体目标是研究从地面到中间层的地球大气化学成分和动力学过程。

faq_en.a: Yes, the Aura satellite has surpassed its six-year nominal life from 2004 and is still operational, however the HIRDLS instrument failed in 2008.
faq_en.q: Is the Aura satellite still in orbit and operational?

faq_en.a: The Afternoon-train (A-train) is a constellation of satellites that closely follow one another along the same, or very similar, orbital track. The original constellation consisted of CloudSat, CALIPSO, Aqua, Aura, and Parasol with GCOM-W1 and OCO-2 joining later. Cloudsat exited the A-train following the loss of one of its reaction wheels in 2018 and it was decided that CALIPSO would also change its orbit to join CloudSat in the new C-train.
faq_en.q: What is the A-train?

faq_en.a: Aura, which is owned and operated by the National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA), has the overall objective of studying the chemistry and dynamics of Earth’s atmosphere from the ground through the mesosphere.
faq_en.q: What is the aim of Aura?

eoportal.name: Aurora / Shasta (Astro Digital Demo 9)
eoportal.summary: The satellite is a precursor to Orbital Sidekick’s GHOSt (Global Hyperspectral Observation Satellite) constellation of six satellites, which has its first launch in April 2023.

faq.a: Aurora is a microsatellite built by Astro Digital and Orbital Sidekick.
faq.q: Who built Aurora?

faq.a: Aurora is a technology demonstration mission and precursor to Orbital Sidekick’s GHOSt (Global Hyperspectral Observation Satellite) satellite constellation. The satellite will detect greenhouse gas emissions such as pipe leaks.
faq.q: What is Aurora used for?

faq.a: Aurora was launched on 30 June 2021 from the U.S. Air Force Eastern test range on a SpaceX Falcon 9 Rideshare mission.
faq.q: When was Aurora launched?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该卫星是Orbital Sidekick公司GHOSt（全球高光谱观测卫星）六星星座的先导任务，GHOSt星座的首次发射于2023年4月进行。
eoportal.summary_en: The satellite is a precursor to Orbital Sidekick’s GHOSt (Global Hyperspectral Observation Satellite) constellation of six satellites, which has its first launch in April 2023.

faq_zh.a: Aurora是由Astro Digital和Orbital Sidekick联合建造的一颗微卫星。
Aurora是一项技术验证任务，同时也是Orbital Sidekick公司GHOSt（全球高光谱观测卫星）卫星星座的先导卫星。该卫星将用于探测温室气体排放，例如管道泄漏。
Aurora于2021年6月30日在美国空军东靶场搭载SpaceX Falcon 9拼车任务发射升空。

faq_en.a: Aurora is a microsatellite built by Astro Digital and Orbital Sidekick.

eoportal.name: AWS (Arctic Weather Satellite)
taxonomy: Precipitation intensity at the surface (liquid or solid)
Sea ice cover, edge and thickness
eoportal.summary: The program aims to build on existing Arctic monitoring satellites, such as MetOp and Metop SG, and provide precise, short-term weather forecasts for the Arctic region.

faq.a: The Arctic Weather Satellite (AWS) is a planned mission of the European Space Agency (ESA).
faq.q: Who owns AWS?

faq.a: The Arctic Weather Satellite (AWS) ​​aims to build on existing arctic monitoring systems, such as MetOp and Metop SG, and provide precise, short-term weather forecasts for the arctic region.
faq.q: What is the aim of AWS?

faq.a: The first Arctic Weather Satellite was launched on August 16, 2024 aboard SpaceX’s  Transporter-11 mission. This satellite is a prototype used for testing, in preparation for the EPS-Sterna Constellation mission.
faq.q: When was the first Arctic Weather Satellite launched?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该计划旨在依托现有的北极监测卫星，如MetOp和Metop SG系列，为北极地区提供精确的短期天气预报。
eoportal.summary_en: The program aims to build on existing Arctic monitoring satellites, such as MetOp and Metop SG, and provide precise, short-term weather forecasts for the Arctic region.

北极气象卫星（AWS）旨在依托现有的北极监测系统，如MetOp和Metop SG系列，为北极地区提供精确的短期天气预报。
第一颗北极气象卫星于2024年8月16日搭载SpaceX的Transporter-11任务发射升空。这颗卫星是一个用于测试的原型，旨在为EPS-Sterna星座任务做准备。

faq_en.a: The Arctic Weather Satellite (AWS) ​​aims to build on existing arctic monitoring systems, such as MetOp and Metop SG, and provide precise, short-term weather forecasts for the arctic region.
faq_en.q: What is the aim of AWS?

faq_en.a: The first Arctic Weather Satellite was launched on August 16, 2024 aboard SpaceX’s  Transporter-11 mission. This satellite is a prototype used for testing, in preparation for the EPS-Sterna Constellation mission.
faq_en.q: When was the first Arctic Weather Satellite launched?

eoportal.name: AzTechSat-1 CubeSat
eoportal.summary: Its primary purpose was to demonstrate inter-satellite communication by relaying data through the GlobalStar satellite network, reducing reliance on ground stations.
eoportal.summary_zh: 该卫星的主要目的是通过GlobalStar卫星网络中继数据，演示卫星间通信技术，从而减少对地面站的依赖。
eoportal.summary_en: Its primary purpose was to demonstrate inter-satellite communication by relaying data through the GlobalStar satellite network, reducing reliance on ground stations.

eoportal.name: Balkan Constellation
eoportal.summary: Balkan-2 is a smaller, 6U CubeSat developed by EnduroSat to improve the constellation’s revisit frequency, which was launched in May 2026.

faq.a: The purpose of the Balkan Constellation is to provide high-resolution multispectral Earth observation imagery to support applications in land use and land cover mapping, emergency management, coastal and maritime monitoring, and security services, including those delivered through the Copernicus Programme.
faq.q: What is the purpose of the Balkan Constellation?

faq.a: The Balkan Constellation is planned as a multi-satellite system comprising up to 120 satellites deployed in coordinated orbital planes to increase revisit frequency.
faq.q: How many satellites will the Balkan Constellation include?

faq.a: Balkan-1 was launched on 14 January 2025 aboard a SpaceX Falcon 9 rideshare mission.
faq.q: When was Balkan-1 launched?

eoportal.summary_zh: Balkan-2是一颗由EnduroSat开发的更小的6U立方星，旨在提高星座的重访频率，于2026年5月发射。
eoportal.summary_en: Balkan-2 is a smaller, 6U CubeSat developed by EnduroSat to improve the constellation’s revisit frequency, which was launched in May 2026.

faq_zh.a: 巴尔干星座旨在提供高分辨率多光谱地球观测图像，以支持土地利用和土地覆盖测绘、应急管理、沿海和海事监测以及安全服务等应用，包括通过哥白尼计划（Copernicus Programme）提供的服务。
faq_zh.q: 巴尔干星座的目的是什么？

faq_zh.a: 巴尔干星座计划是一个由多达120颗卫星组成的多卫星系统，部署在协调的轨道平面上以提高重访频率。
faq_zh.q: 巴尔干星座将包含多少颗卫星？

faq_zh.a: Balkan-1于2025年1月14日搭载SpaceX猎鹰9号（Falcon 9）的拼车任务发射升空。

faq_en.a: The purpose of the Balkan Constellation is to provide high-resolution multispectral Earth observation imagery to support applications in land use and land cover mapping, emergency management, coastal and maritime monitoring, and security services, including those delivered through the Copernicus Programme.
faq_en.q: What is the purpose of the Balkan Constellation?

faq_en.a: The Balkan Constellation is planned as a multi-satellite system comprising up to 120 satellites deployed in coordinated orbital planes to increase revisit frequency.
faq_en.q: How many satellites will the Balkan Constellation include?

faq_en.a: Balkan-1 was launched on 14 January 2025 aboard a SpaceX Falcon 9 rideshare mission.
faq_en.q: When was Balkan-1 launched?

eoportal.name: BeaverCube CubeSat Mission
eoportal.summary: Launched in June 2021, BeaverCube measures VIS (visible) and LWIR (long wave infrared) spectral data to obtain colour and thermal images of the ocean surface and cloud tops, which have many applications including monitoring regional biological productivity and the effects of extreme weather events.

faq.a: BeaverCube is an educational mission led by MIT (Massachusetts Institute of Technology) to introduce students worldwide to aerospace science and technology. Data collected by BeaverCube has many applications, including improving the understanding of regional biological productivity and the effects of extreme weather events. BeaverCube also demonstrates the use of low-cost commercial cameras to obtain measurements and demonstrates the operation of Tiled Ionic Liquid Electrospray (TILE) propulsion technology.
faq.q: What is the purpose of BeaverCube?

faq.a: BeaverCube hosts two Long-Wave Infrared (LWIR) cameras and one Visible Spectrum (VIS) camera. These instruments measure ocean colour as well as sea surface and cloud top temperature. This data has many applications in the scientific study of Earth’s climate and weather.
faq.q: What does BeaverCube measure?

faq.a: On 03 June, 2021, the BeaverCube 3U CubeSat was launched as a payload on the SpaceX CRS-22 cargo mission from the Kennedy Space Center in Florida.
faq.q: When was BeaverCube launched?

eoportal.summary_zh: BeaverCube 于2021年6月发射，旨在测量可见光和长波红外光谱数据，以获取海洋表面和云顶的彩色及热图像。这些数据在监测区域生物生产力和极端天气事件影响等多个领域具有应用价值。
eoportal.summary_en: Launched in June 2021, BeaverCube measures VIS (visible) and LWIR (long wave infrared) spectral data to obtain colour and thermal images of the ocean surface and cloud tops, which have many applications including monitoring regional biological productivity and the effects of extreme weather events.

faq_zh.a: BeaverCube 是一项由 MIT（麻省理工学院）主导的教育任务，旨在向全球学生介绍航空航天科学技术。其收集的数据具有多种应用，包括增进对区域生物生产力和极端天气事件影响的理解。该任务还演示了使用低成本商业相机进行测量，以及平铺式离子液体电喷雾推进技术的操作。
faq_zh.q: BeaverCube 任务的目的是什么？

faq_zh.a: BeaverCube 搭载了两台长波红外相机和一台可见光相机。这些仪器用于测量海洋颜色以及海表和云顶温度。这些数据在地球气候和天气的科学研究中具有广泛应用。
2021年6月3日，BeaverCube 3U立方星作为 SpaceX CRS-22 货运任务的有效载荷，从佛罗里达州肯尼迪航天中心发射升空。

faq_en.a: BeaverCube is an educational mission led by MIT (Massachusetts Institute of Technology) to introduce students worldwide to aerospace science and technology. Data collected by BeaverCube has many applications, including improving the understanding of regional biological productivity and the effects of extreme weather events. BeaverCube also demonstrates the use of low-cost commercial cameras to obtain measurements and demonstrates the operation of Tiled Ionic Liquid Electrospray (TILE) propulsion technology.
faq_en.q: What is the purpose of BeaverCube?

faq_en.a: BeaverCube hosts two Long-Wave Infrared (LWIR) cameras and one Visible Spectrum (VIS) camera. These instruments measure ocean colour as well as sea surface and cloud top temperature. This data has many applications in the scientific study of Earth’s climate and weather.
faq_en.q: What does BeaverCube measure?

faq_en.a: On 03 June, 2021, the BeaverCube 3U CubeSat was launched as a payload on the SpaceX CRS-22 cargo mission from the Kennedy Space Center in Florida.
faq_en.q: When was BeaverCube launched?

eoportal.name: Beijing-3
eoportal.summary: Beijing-3A was launched on 11 June 2021, and Beijing-3B was launched on 24 August 2022.

faq.a: Beijing-3A and 3B are owned and operated by Twenty First Century Aerospace Technology Co., Ltd (21AT).
faq.q: Who owns the Beijing-3 satellites?

faq.a: The Beijing-2 satellites (TripleSat) were built by Surrey Satellite Technology Ltd (SSTL) for the DMC3 (Disaster Monitoring Constellation 3), and funded by 21AT.
faq.q: Are the Beijing-3 satellites the same as Beijing-2?

faq.a: There are two satellites in the Beijing-3 constellation, Beijing-3A and -3B.
faq.q: How many satellites are in the Beijing-3 constellation?

eoportal.agency_zh: 二十一世纪空间技术应用股份有限公司
eoportal.summary_zh: 北京三号A星于2021年6月11日发射，北京三号B星于2022年8月24日发射。
eoportal.summary_en: Beijing-3A was launched on 11 June 2021, and Beijing-3B was launched on 24 August 2022.

faq_zh.a: 北京三号A星和B星由二十一世纪空间技术应用股份有限公司（21AT）拥有并运营。
北京二号卫星（三颗卫星，即TripleSat）由萨里卫星技术有限公司（SSTL）为DMC3（灾害监测星座3）建造，并由21AT资助。
faq_zh.q: 北京三号卫星与北京二号相同吗？

faq_zh.a: 北京三号星座由两颗卫星组成，分别是北京三号A星和B星。
