faq_en.a: Beijing-3A and 3B are owned and operated by Twenty First Century Aerospace Technology Co., Ltd (21AT).
faq_en.q: Who owns the Beijing-3 satellites?

faq_en.a: The Beijing-2 satellites (TripleSat) were built by Surrey Satellite Technology Ltd (SSTL) for the DMC3 (Disaster Monitoring Constellation 3), and funded by 21AT.
faq_en.q: Are the Beijing-3 satellites the same as Beijing-2?

faq_en.a: There are two satellites in the Beijing-3 constellation, Beijing-3A and -3B.
faq_en.q: How many satellites are in the Beijing-3 constellation?

eoportal.name: BepiColombo
eoportal.summary: Hz, and 17 Hz, which are all correlated to the SADM operation.
eoportal.summary_zh: 赫兹，以及17赫兹，这些频率都与太阳翼驱动机构（SADM）的操作相关。
eoportal.summary_en: Hz, and 17 Hz, which are all correlated to the SADM operation.

eoportal.name: Biomass (Earth Explorer 7)
eoportal.summary: Launched in April 2025 and led by the European Space Agency (ESA) as the seventh Earth Explorer, Biomass addresses the status and dynamics of forests for a better understanding of the carbon cycle.

faq.a: Biomass will address a fundamental gap in our understanding of terrestrial Earth - the status and dynamics of Earth’s forests. The mission aims to take measurements of forest biomass to assess terrestrial carbon stocks and fluxes for a better understanding of the carbon cycle.
faq.q: What is the purpose of the Biomass satellite mission?

faq.a: Biomass carries a P-band Synthetic Aperture Radar (P-SAR) onboard alongside its corresponding P-band Antenna Feed Subsystem.
faq.q: What instruments are onboard the Biomass satellite mission?

eoportal.summary_zh: Biomass卫星于2025年4月发射，由欧洲空间局（ESA）作为第七颗地球探测者卫星主导，旨在研究森林的状态与动态，以更好地理解碳循环。
eoportal.summary_en: Launched in April 2025 and led by the European Space Agency (ESA) as the seventh Earth Explorer, Biomass addresses the status and dynamics of forests for a better understanding of the carbon cycle.

faq_zh.a: Biomass卫星将填补我们对陆地地球认知中的一个基本空白——即地球森林的状态与动态。该任务旨在测量森林生物量，以评估陆地碳储量与通量，从而更好地理解碳循环。
faq_zh.q: Biomass卫星任务的目的是什么？

faq_zh.a: Biomass卫星搭载了一台P波段合成孔径雷达（P-SAR）及其相应的P波段天线馈电子系统。

faq_en.a: Biomass will address a fundamental gap in our understanding of terrestrial Earth - the status and dynamics of Earth’s forests. The mission aims to take measurements of forest biomass to assess terrestrial carbon stocks and fluxes for a better understanding of the carbon cycle.
faq_en.q: What is the purpose of the Biomass satellite mission?

faq_en.a: Biomass carries a P-band Synthetic Aperture Radar (P-SAR) onboard alongside its corresponding P-band Antenna Feed Subsystem.
faq_en.q: What instruments are onboard the Biomass satellite mission?

eoportal.name: BioSentinel nanosatellite on EM-1
eoportal.summary: BioSentinel was launched in November 2022 onboard the Artemis 1 mission.

faq.a: BioSentinel’s flight path will initially take it past the moon and then subsequently into an Earth trailing, Heliocentric orbit.
faq.q: What is the flight trajectory of BioSentinel?

faq.a: BioSentinel is set to quantify the long term effects of space radiation on genetic material, allowing future missions to minimise the detrimental health impacts on humans in space.
faq.q: How will BioSentinel advance human deep space flight?

faq.a: The biosensor payload contains three forms of Saccharomyces cerevisiae (baker’s yeast), two controlled strains and one engineered strain.
faq.q: What type of cells are present in the BioSentinel biosensor?

eoportal.name_zh: BioSentinel 纳米卫星（搭载于 EM-1）
eoportal.summary_zh: BioSentinel 于 2022 年 11 月搭载阿尔忒弥斯1号任务发射升空。
eoportal.summary_en: BioSentinel was launched in November 2022 onboard the Artemis 1 mission.

faq_zh.a: BioSentinel 的飞行路径最初将经过月球，随后进入一个尾随地球的绕日轨道。
faq_zh.q: BioSentinel 的飞行轨迹是怎样的？

faq_zh.a: BioSentinel 旨在量化太空辐射对遗传物质的长期影响，从而使未来的任务能够最大限度地减少太空环境对人体健康的不利影响。
faq_zh.q: BioSentinel 将如何推动人类深空飞行？

faq_zh.a: 生物传感器载荷包含三种形式的酿酒酵母（面包酵母），其中两种是对照菌株，一种是经过工程改造的菌株。
faq_zh.q: BioSentinel 生物传感器中包含了哪类细胞？

faq_en.a: BioSentinel’s flight path will initially take it past the moon and then subsequently into an Earth trailing, Heliocentric orbit.
faq_en.q: What is the flight trajectory of BioSentinel?

faq_en.a: BioSentinel is set to quantify the long term effects of space radiation on genetic material, allowing future missions to minimise the detrimental health impacts on humans in space.
faq_en.q: How will BioSentinel advance human deep space flight?

faq_en.a: The biosensor payload contains three forms of Saccharomyces cerevisiae (baker’s yeast), two controlled strains and one engineered strain.
faq_en.q: What type of cells are present in the BioSentinel biosensor?

eoportal.name: BIRD (Bi-Spectral Infrared Detection)
eoportal.summary: BIRD was launched in October 2001, as a secondary payload on the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO)’s PSLV0C3 launcher, and ended its operations in December 2005.

faq.a: The spacecraft design life of BIRD was only one year. However, the spacecraft carried out its operations for just over four years.
faq.q: What is the design life of BIRD?

faq.a: BIRD data is used for measurements of vegetation, albedo and reflectance, surface temperature on land, and multi-purpose imagery.
faq.q: What is BIRD data used for?

faq.a: The BIRD satellite carried Imaging Multi-Spectral Radiometers in the visible and infrared bands.
faq.q: What instrument types are on board BIRD?

eoportal.summary_zh: BIRD卫星于2001年10月作为印度空间研究组织（ISRO）PSLV-C3运载火箭的次要载荷发射升空，并于2005年12月结束了其运营。
eoportal.summary_en: BIRD was launched in October 2001, as a secondary payload on the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO)’s PSLV0C3 launcher, and ended its operations in December 2005.

faq_zh.a: BIRD卫星的设计寿命仅为一年。然而，该卫星实际运行了四年多。
faq_zh.q: BIRD卫星的设计寿命是多久？

faq_zh.a: BIRD卫星的数据用于植被、反照率与反射率测量，以及陆地表面温度测量和多用途成像。
BIRD卫星搭载了可见光和红外波段的成像多光谱辐射计。
faq_zh.q: BIRD卫星搭载了哪些类型的仪器？

faq_en.a: The spacecraft design life of BIRD was only one year. However, the spacecraft carried out its operations for just over four years.
faq_en.q: What is the design life of BIRD?

faq_en.a: BIRD data is used for measurements of vegetation, albedo and reflectance, surface temperature on land, and multi-purpose imagery.
faq_en.q: What is BIRD data used for?

faq_en.a: The BIRD satellite carried Imaging Multi-Spectral Radiometers in the visible and infrared bands.
faq_en.q: What instrument types are on board BIRD?

eoportal.name: BIROS (Bi-spectral InfraRed Optical System)
eoportal.summary: Launched in June 2016, BIROS served emergency response agencies internationally until its end of life in December 2020.

faq.a: BIROS stands for Bi-spectral InfraRed Optical System.
faq.q: What does BIROS stand for?

faq.a: The primary goal of BIROS was the detection and monitoring of HTEs (High Temperature Events), e.g. forest fires or other hot spots.
faq.q: What was the primary goal of BIROS?

faq.a: BIROS was equipped with a new optical telecommunications terminal, OSIRIS, demonstrating downlink communication up to 1 Gbit/s. BIROS also tested the AVANTI software, a vision-based navigation architecture, demonstrating the rendezvous to a non-cooperative satellite, e.g. a malfunctioning satellite.
faq.q: What were the secondary goals of BIROS?

eoportal.summary_zh: BIROS卫星于2016年6月发射，一直为国际应急响应机构服务，直至其于2020年12月结束任务。
eoportal.summary_en: Launched in June 2016, BIROS served emergency response agencies internationally until its end of life in December 2020.

faq_zh.a: BIROS代表双光谱红外光学系统。
BIROS的主要目标是探测和监测高温事件，例如森林火灾或其他热点。
BIROS配备了一个新的光通信终端OSIRIS，演示了高达1 Gbit/s的下行通信能力。BIROS还测试了AVANTI软件，这是一个基于视觉的导航架构，演示了与非合作卫星（例如故障卫星）的交会对接。

faq_en.a: BIROS stands for Bi-spectral InfraRed Optical System.
faq_en.q: What does BIROS stand for?

faq_en.a: The primary goal of BIROS was the detection and monitoring of HTEs (High Temperature Events), e.g. forest fires or other hot spots.
faq_en.q: What was the primary goal of BIROS?

faq_en.a: BIROS was equipped with a new optical telecommunications terminal, OSIRIS, demonstrating downlink communication up to 1 Gbit/s. BIROS also tested the AVANTI software, a vision-based navigation architecture, demonstrating the rendezvous to a non-cooperative satellite, e.g. a malfunctioning satellite.
faq_en.q: What were the secondary goals of BIROS?

eoportal.name: BlackSky Constellation
eoportal.summary: The constellation aims to provide higher temporal resolution and lower cost Earth imaging, and currently contains 19 operational microsatellites, each with an expected lifetime of 4 years.

faq.a: The final planned BlackSky Constellation will consist of 60 microsatellites.
faq.q: How many BlackSky Constellation satellites are there?

faq.a: Data collected by the BlackSky Constellation is not free, but is commercially available through BlackSky Global, the owner and operator of the program.
faq.q: Is BlackSky Constellation data free?

faq.a: BlackSky Constellation program aims to provide high temporal and spatial resolution imaging of the Earth for purchase by corporations, governments and other entities.
faq.q: What does BlackSky Constellation do?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该星座旨在提供更高时间分辨率、更低成本的地球成像服务，目前包含19颗在轨运行的微卫星，每颗卫星预期寿命为4年。
eoportal.summary_en: The constellation aims to provide higher temporal resolution and lower cost Earth imaging, and currently contains 19 operational microsatellites, each with an expected lifetime of 4 years.

faq_zh.a: 最终规划的BlackSky星座将由60颗微卫星组成。
BlackSky星座采集的数据并非免费，而是通过该项目的所有者和运营商BlackSky Global进行商业销售。
faq_zh.q: BlackSky星座的数据是免费的吗？

faq_zh.a: BlackSky星座项目旨在提供高时间分辨率和高空间分辨率的地球成像服务，供企业、政府及其他实体购买使用。

faq_en.a: The final planned BlackSky Constellation will consist of 60 microsatellites.
faq_en.q: How many BlackSky Constellation satellites are there?

faq_en.a: Data collected by the BlackSky Constellation is not free, but is commercially available through BlackSky Global, the owner and operator of the program.
faq_en.q: Is BlackSky Constellation data free?

faq_en.a: BlackSky Constellation program aims to provide high temporal and spatial resolution imaging of the Earth for purchase by corporations, governments and other entities.
faq_en.q: What does BlackSky Constellation do?

eoportal.name: Blue Moon
eoportal.summary: The mission will support applications in climate resilience, urban heat management, agriculture, and national security, while also demonstrating novel deployable telescope technology.

faq.a: Blue Moon will provide high-resolution thermal infrared imagery for climate monitoring, urban monitoring, agriculture, and security applications.
faq.q: What is the purpose of the Blue Moon mission?

faq.a: The satellite is developed jointly by SuperSharp and NanoAvionics, with support from the UK Space Agency.
faq.q: Who is developing the Blue Moon satellite?

faq.a: The Blue Moon mission carries an uncooled micro-bolometer array telescope operating between 8 and 13 microns, with a 120 cm unfolding aperture and 3.3 m Ground Sample Distance (GSD).
faq.q: What payload does Blue Moon carry?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该任务旨在支持气候韧性、城市热管理、农业和国家安全等领域的应用，同时还将演示新型可展开望远镜技术。
eoportal.summary_en: The mission will support applications in climate resilience, urban heat management, agriculture, and national security, while also demonstrating novel deployable telescope technology.

faq_zh.a: Blue Moon 将提供高分辨率热红外图像，用于气候监测、城市监测、农业和安全应用。
faq_zh.q: Blue Moon 任务的目的是什么？

faq_zh.a: 该卫星由 SuperSharp 和 NanoAvionics 联合开发，并得到英国航天局的支持。
faq_zh.q: 谁在开发 Blue Moon 卫星？

faq_zh.a: Blue Moon 任务搭载了一个工作波段在 8 至 13 微米之间的非制冷微型测辐射热计阵列望远镜，其展开孔径为 120 厘米，地面采样距离（GSD）为 3.3 米。
faq_zh.q: Blue Moon 搭载了什么有效载荷？

faq_en.a: Blue Moon will provide high-resolution thermal infrared imagery for climate monitoring, urban monitoring, agriculture, and security applications.
faq_en.q: What is the purpose of the Blue Moon mission?

faq_en.a: The satellite is developed jointly by SuperSharp and NanoAvionics, with support from the UK Space Agency.
faq_en.q: Who is developing the Blue Moon satellite?

faq_en.a: The Blue Moon mission carries an uncooled micro-bolometer array telescope operating between 8 and 13 microns, with a 120 cm unfolding aperture and 3.3 m Ground Sample Distance (GSD).
faq_en.q: What payload does Blue Moon carry?

eoportal.name: BlueBon
eoportal.summary: Launched on 14 January 2025, the satellite uses an AI-powered onboard processor and data analysis software to analyse blue carbon in optical imagery acquired from BlueBon’s multispectral camera.

faq.a: The BlueBon satellite was developed by TelePIX, a Korean total satellite AI solution provider, in collaboration with Polish aerospace company SatRev. The AI-powered onboard processor, TetraPLEX, and the optical multispectral camera payload were developed by TelePIX, while the satellite bus was designed based on the SatRev ScopeSat nanosatellite platform.
faq.q: Which companies developed the BlueBon satellite?

faq.a: BlueBon was launched on January 14, 2025, from Vandenberg Space Force Base, in California, U.S, aboard a Falcon 9 rocket as part of the SpaceX Transporter-12 rideshare mission.
faq.q: When did the BlueBon satellite launch?

faq.a: BlueBon’s proprietary payload is a multispectral camera developed by TelePIX to verify the quantity and distribution of floating algae for applications in the carbon credit market and climate crisis responses.
faq.q: What is the primary payload aboard BlueBon?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该卫星于2025年1月14日发射，搭载了由人工智能驱动的星上处理器和数据分析软件，用于分析BlueBon多光谱相机获取的光学图像中的蓝碳信息。
eoportal.summary_en: Launched on 14 January 2025, the satellite uses an AI-powered onboard processor and data analysis software to analyse blue carbon in optical imagery acquired from BlueBon’s multispectral camera.

faq_zh.a: BlueBon卫星由韩国卫星人工智能整体解决方案供应商TelePIX与波兰航空航天公司SatRev合作开发。其中，由人工智能驱动的星上处理器TetraPLEX及光学多光谱相机有效载荷由TelePIX研发，而卫星平台则基于SatRev的ScopeSat纳米卫星平台设计。
faq_zh.q: BlueBon卫星是由哪些公司开发的？

faq_zh.a: BlueBon卫星于2025年1月14日，搭乘猎鹰9号火箭，从美国加利福尼亚州范登堡太空军基地发射升空，执行的是SpaceX的Transporter-12拼车任务。
BlueBon卫星的专用有效载荷是由TelePIX开发的多光谱相机，用于验证漂浮藻类的数量和分布，应用于碳信用市场和气候危机应对。
faq_zh.q: BlueBon卫星的主要有效载荷是什么？

faq_en.a: The BlueBon satellite was developed by TelePIX, a Korean total satellite AI solution provider, in collaboration with Polish aerospace company SatRev. The AI-powered onboard processor, TetraPLEX, and the optical multispectral camera payload were developed by TelePIX, while the satellite bus was designed based on the SatRev ScopeSat nanosatellite platform.
faq_en.q: Which companies developed the BlueBon satellite?

faq_en.a: BlueBon was launched on January 14, 2025, from Vandenberg Space Force Base, in California, U.S, aboard a Falcon 9 rocket as part of the SpaceX Transporter-12 rideshare mission.
faq_en.q: When did the BlueBon satellite launch?

faq_en.a: BlueBon’s proprietary payload is a multispectral camera developed by TelePIX to verify the quantity and distribution of floating algae for applications in the carbon credit market and climate crisis responses.
faq_en.q: What is the primary payload aboard BlueBon?

eoportal.name: BRO (Breizh Reconnaissance Orbiter) / Unseenlabs
eoportal.summary: The constellation is designed to enhance Maritime Domain Awareness by delivering spectrum monitoring and electromagnetic intelligence (SIGINT) services for maritime traffic surveillance.
eoportal.summary_zh: 该星座旨在通过提供频谱监测和电磁情报（SIGINT）服务，增强对海上交通的监视能力，从而提升海事领域感知能力。
eoportal.summary_en: The constellation is designed to enhance Maritime Domain Awareness by delivering spectrum monitoring and electromagnetic intelligence (SIGINT) services for maritime traffic surveillance.

eoportal.name: CALIPSO (Cloud-Aerosol Lidar and Infrared Pathfinder Satellite Observations)
taxonomy: Cloud type, amount and cloud top temperature
eoportal.summary: Its observations are used to determine the role of clouds and aerosols in regulating the Earth’s climate.

faq.a: The Earth Science Data Systems (ESDS) program provides open access to NASA’s collection of Earth observation data. The data can be accessed on the Earth Data website, free of charge.
faq.q: Can I access CALIPSO data?

faq.a: CALIPSO is the first  satellite to provide information on the vertical structure of clouds and aerosols, as well as provide vertically-resolved estimates of the amount of rain and ice in clouds. These observations are then used to make an assessment as to how much clouds and aerosols contribute to global warming.
faq.q: What makes CALIPSO unique?

faq.a: CALIPSO is also referred to as Earth System Science Pathfinder-3 (ESSP-3), as well as Pathfinder Instruments for Cloud and Aerosol Spaceborne Observations - Climatologie Etendue des Nuages et des Aérosols (PICASSO-CENA).
faq.q: What other names exist for CALIPSO?

eoportal.name_zh: CALIPSO (云-气溶胶激光雷达和红外探路者卫星观测)
eoportal.summary_zh: 该卫星的观测数据用于确定云和气溶胶在调节地球气候中的作用。
eoportal.summary_en: Its observations are used to determine the role of clouds and aerosols in regulating the Earth’s climate.

faq_zh.a: 地球科学数据系统（ESDS）计划提供对 NASA 地球观测数据集的开放访问。可以在 Earth Data 网站上免费获取这些数据。
faq_zh.q: 我能访问 CALIPSO 数据吗？

faq_zh.a: CALIPSO 是首颗提供云和气溶胶垂直结构信息的卫星，同时还提供云中雨冰含量的垂直分布估算。这些观测数据随后被用于评估云和气溶胶对全球变暖的贡献程度。
CALIPSO 也被称为地球系统科学探路者-3（ESSP-3），以及用于云和气溶胶天基观测的探路者仪器——气候学与云及气溶胶扩展观测（PICASSO-CENA）。
faq_zh.q: CALIPSO 还有哪些其他名称？

faq_en.a: The Earth Science Data Systems (ESDS) program provides open access to NASA’s collection of Earth observation data. The data can be accessed on the Earth Data website, free of charge.
faq_en.q: Can I access CALIPSO data?

faq_en.a: CALIPSO is the first  satellite to provide information on the vertical structure of clouds and aerosols, as well as provide vertically-resolved estimates of the amount of rain and ice in clouds. These observations are then used to make an assessment as to how much clouds and aerosols contribute to global warming.
faq_en.q: What makes CALIPSO unique?

faq_en.a: CALIPSO is also referred to as Earth System Science Pathfinder-3 (ESSP-3), as well as Pathfinder Instruments for Cloud and Aerosol Spaceborne Observations - Climatologie Etendue des Nuages et des Aérosols (PICASSO-CENA).
faq_en.q: What other names exist for CALIPSO?

calipso (cloud-aerosol lidar and infrare.display: CALIPSO (Cloud-Aerosol Lidar and Infrared Pathfinder Satellite Observations)

eoportal.name: CanX-2 (Canadian Advanced Nanosatellite eXperiment-2)
eoportal.summary: Launched in 2008, the mission has two objectives: technology demonstration and provision of cost-effective access to space for the Canadian research and development community.

faq.a: CanX-2 had the primary goal to demonstrate several technologies critical for the CanX-4/5 formation flying mission, including a custom cold-gas propulsion system, a 30 mNms nanosatellite reaction wheel as part of a three-axis stabilised momentum-bias attitude control system, and a commercially available GPS receiver.
faq.q: What was the main purpose of CanX-2?

faq.a: CanX-4 and -5 are two satellites, utilised in 2014 to demonstrate formation flying using the nanosatellite scale technology that had been tested on CanX-2. These two satellites were launched together, commissioned together, and then separated in orbit.
faq.q: What are CanX-4 and -5?

faq.a: CanX-2 was launched on 28 April 2008 from the Satish Dhawan Space Centre (SHAR) in India, aboard the Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle (PSLV) C-9.
faq.q: When did CanX-2 launch?

eoportal.name_zh: CanX-2（加拿大先进纳卫星实验-2）
eoportal.agency_zh: UTIAS / SFL（多伦多大学航空航天研究所 / 太空飞行实验室）
eoportal.summary_zh: 该卫星于2008年发射，其任务目标有两个：一是进行技术验证，二是为加拿大研究与开发群体提供一种经济高效的太空访问途径。
eoportal.summary_en: Launched in 2008, the mission has two objectives: technology demonstration and provision of cost-effective access to space for the Canadian research and development community.

faq_zh.a: CanX-2 的首要目标是演示多项对 CanX-4/5 编队飞行任务至关重要的技术，包括一个定制的冷气推进系统、一个用于三轴稳定动量偏置姿态控制系统的 30 mNms 纳卫星反作用轮，以及一个商用 GPS 接收器。
faq_zh.q: CanX-2 的主要目的是什么？

faq_zh.a: CanX-4 和 -5 是两颗卫星，于2014年利用在 CanX-2 上测试过的纳卫星尺度技术进行编队飞行演示。这两颗卫星一同发射、一同调试，然后在轨分离。
CanX-2 于2008年4月28日从印度萨迪什·达万航天中心 (SHAR) 搭载极地卫星运载火箭 (PSLV) C-9 发射升空。

faq_en.a: CanX-2 had the primary goal to demonstrate several technologies critical for the CanX-4/5 formation flying mission, including a custom cold-gas propulsion system, a 30 mNms nanosatellite reaction wheel as part of a three-axis stabilised momentum-bias attitude control system, and a commercially available GPS receiver.
faq_en.q: What was the main purpose of CanX-2?

faq_en.a: CanX-4 and -5 are two satellites, utilised in 2014 to demonstrate formation flying using the nanosatellite scale technology that had been tested on CanX-2. These two satellites were launched together, commissioned together, and then separated in orbit.
faq_en.q: What are CanX-4 and -5?

faq_en.a: CanX-2 was launched on 28 April 2008 from the Satish Dhawan Space Centre (SHAR) in India, aboard the Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle (PSLV) C-9.
faq_en.q: When did CanX-2 launch?

faq.a: Capella space is based in Palo Alto, California, USA.

faq.a: Each satellite in the Capella X-SAR constellation features an X-band SAR (Synthetic Aperture Radar).

faq_zh.a: 截至2024年9月，Capella X-SAR星座拥有约15颗卫星，但计划将星座规模扩展至36颗。
Capella Space总部位于美国加利福尼亚州帕洛阿尔托。
Capella X-SAR星座中的每颗卫星都搭载了一台X波段合成孔径雷达。

eoportal.name: Carbon Mapper Mission
eoportal.summary: The company launched its first satellite for the Carbon Mapper hyperspectral constellation, Tanager-1, on August 16, 2024.
eoportal.summary_zh: 该公司于2024年8月16日发射了其“碳追踪者”高光谱星座的首颗卫星——Tanager-1。
eoportal.summary_en: The company launched its first satellite for the Carbon Mapper hyperspectral constellation, Tanager-1, on August 16, 2024.

eoportal.name: Carbonite
eoportal.summary: Operated and owned by Surrey Satellite Technology Ltd (SSTL), the series aims to pave the way for implementation of full production spacecraft and constellations by reducing the cost and schedule per satellite and providing unique ability for sub-daily accesses.

faq.a: Carbonite-1 and -2 were developed, owned and operated by Surrey Satellite Technology Ltd (SSTL).
faq.q: Who operates the Carbonite series?

faq.a: Carbonite-1 was launched on July 10 2015 on a PSLV-C28 vehicle and Carbonite-2 was launched on January 12 2018 on a PSLV-40 vehicle. Both were launched from the Satish Dhawan Space Center (SDSC) in India.
faq.q: When were the Carbonite satellites launched?

faq.a: Carbonite-1 and -2 are technology demonstration missions that aim to demonstrate and validate low cost, high performance video imaging using Commercial-Off-The-Shelf components.
faq.q: What is the purpose of Carbonite?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该系列卫星由萨里卫星技术有限公司（SSTL）拥有并运营，旨在通过降低每颗卫星的成本和研制周期，并提供独特的亚日级访问能力，为批量生产航天器和星座的部署铺平道路。
eoportal.summary_en: Operated and owned by Surrey Satellite Technology Ltd (SSTL), the series aims to pave the way for implementation of full production spacecraft and constellations by reducing the cost and schedule per satellite and providing unique ability for sub-daily accesses.

faq_zh.a: Carbonite-1和-2卫星由萨里卫星技术有限公司（SSTL）开发、拥有并运营。
Carbonite-1于2015年7月10日由PSLV-C28运载火箭发射，Carbonite-2于2018年1月12日由PSLV-40运载火箭发射。两者均从印度的萨迪什·达万航天中心（SDSC）发射。
Carbonite-1和-2是技术验证任务，旨在验证并展示利用商用现货组件实现低成本、高性能视频成像的能力。

faq_en.a: Carbonite-1 and -2 were developed, owned and operated by Surrey Satellite Technology Ltd (SSTL).
faq_en.q: Who operates the Carbonite series?

faq_en.a: Carbonite-1 was launched on July 10 2015 on a PSLV-C28 vehicle and Carbonite-2 was launched on January 12 2018 on a PSLV-40 vehicle. Both were launched from the Satish Dhawan Space Center (SDSC) in India.
faq_en.q: When were the Carbonite satellites launched?

faq_en.a: Carbonite-1 and -2 are technology demonstration missions that aim to demonstrate and validate low cost, high performance video imaging using Commercial-Off-The-Shelf components.
faq_en.q: What is the purpose of Carbonite?

eoportal.name: Cartosat-3 (Cartographic Satellite-3)
eoportal.summary: Launched in November 2019 aboard PSLV-C47, it supports national mapping and monitoring applications through advanced panchromatic and multispectral imaging.

faq.a: Cartosat-3’s purpose is to provide imagery to support cartographic mapping, urban and rural planning, infrastructure development and disaster management. It is operated by ISRO and launched on the PSLV-C47 mission, supplying data primarily for national applications, with additional use in commercial mapping and monitoring applications.
faq.q: What is the purpose of Cartosat-3?

faq.a: There have been nine Cartosat satellites launched to date, beginning with Cartosat-1 in 2005 and followed by multiple Cartosat-2 series missions and Cartosat-3 in 2019. Further satellites in the Cartosat series are anticipated as part of ISRO’s Earth observation programme.
faq.q: How many Cartosat satellites are there?

faq.a: The PSLV-C47 was used to launch Cartosat-3 into orbit on 27 November 2019.
faq.q: What rocket was used for the launch of Cartosat-3?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该卫星于2019年11月由PSLV-C47运载火箭发射升空，通过先进的全色和多光谱成像能力，为国家测绘与监测应用提供支持。
eoportal.summary_en: Launched in November 2019 aboard PSLV-C47, it supports national mapping and monitoring applications through advanced panchromatic and multispectral imaging.

faq_zh.a: Cartosat-3旨在提供影像，以支持制图测绘、城乡规划、基础设施建设和灾害管理。它由印度空间研究组织（ISRO）运营，并通过PSLV-C47任务发射，数据主要服务于国家级应用，同时也用于商业测绘和监测。
迄今为止，已发射了九颗Cartosat系列卫星，始于2005年的Cartosat-1，随后是多颗Cartosat-2系列任务，以及2019年的Cartosat-3。作为印度空间研究组织（ISRO）地球观测计划的一部分，未来预计将有更多Cartosat系列卫星发射。
Cartosat-3于2019年11月27日通过PSLV-C47运载火箭送入轨道。
faq_zh.q: 发射Cartosat-3使用的是什么火箭？

faq_en.a: Cartosat-3’s purpose is to provide imagery to support cartographic mapping, urban and rural planning, infrastructure development and disaster management. It is operated by ISRO and launched on the PSLV-C47 mission, supplying data primarily for national applications, with additional use in commercial mapping and monitoring applications.
faq_en.q: What is the purpose of Cartosat-3?

faq_en.a: There have been nine Cartosat satellites launched to date, beginning with Cartosat-1 in 2005 and followed by multiple Cartosat-2 series missions and Cartosat-3 in 2019. Further satellites in the Cartosat series are anticipated as part of ISRO’s Earth observation programme.
faq_en.q: How many Cartosat satellites are there?

faq_en.a: The PSLV-C47 was used to launch Cartosat-3 into orbit on 27 November 2019.
faq_en.q: What rocket was used for the launch of Cartosat-3?

eoportal.name: CAS500 (Compact Advanced Satellite 500)
eoportal.summary: The first satellite, CAS500-1, was launched in March 2021.

faq.a: CAS500-1 was launched on 22 March, 2021, and the remaining 4 satellites in the series are yet to launch as of November 2022.
faq.q: When was CAS500 launched?

faq.a: The Compact Advanced Satellite 500 (CAS500) is a research and development program of the Korean Aerospace Research Institute (KARI) with the aims of reducing the satellite development period and cost by using a common platform that can easily accommodate various payloads such as optical cameras, synthetic aperture radar, microwave sounder, and hyper-spectrometers.
faq.q: What is the purpose of CAS500?

faq.a: KARI is proud to have achieved a Koreanization rate of 98.6% for the CAS500 program by localising optical payload components and technologies that have been dependent on overseas technology in the past.
faq.q: What is the Koreanization rate of the CAS500 program?

eoportal.summary_zh: 首颗卫星CAS500-1于2021年3月发射。
eoportal.summary_en: The first satellite, CAS500-1, was launched in March 2021.

faq_zh.a: CAS500-1于2021年3月22日发射，截至2022年11月，该系列剩余的4颗卫星尚未发射。
CAS500（紧凑型先进卫星500）是韩国航空宇宙研究院（KARI）的一项研发计划，旨在通过使用一个通用平台来缩短卫星研制周期并降低成本，该平台可轻松适配光学相机、合成孔径雷达、微波探测仪和高光谱仪等多种有效载荷。
KARI自豪地宣布，通过将过去依赖海外技术的光学有效载荷组件和技术本地化，CAS500项目的国产化率已达到98.6%。
faq_zh.q: CAS500项目的国产化率是多少？

faq_en.a: CAS500-1 was launched on 22 March, 2021, and the remaining 4 satellites in the series are yet to launch as of November 2022.
faq_en.q: When was CAS500 launched?

faq_en.a: The Compact Advanced Satellite 500 (CAS500) is a research and development program of the Korean Aerospace Research Institute (KARI) with the aims of reducing the satellite development period and cost by using a common platform that can easily accommodate various payloads such as optical cameras, synthetic aperture radar, microwave sounder, and hyper-spectrometers.
faq_en.q: What is the purpose of CAS500?

faq_en.a: KARI is proud to have achieved a Koreanization rate of 98.6% for the CAS500 program by localising optical payload components and technologies that have been dependent on overseas technology in the past.
faq_en.q: What is the Koreanization rate of the CAS500 program?

eoportal.name: CASSIOPE (Cascade SmallSat and Ionospheric Polar Explorer)
taxonomy: Neutral Particle Composition and Flow Velocity
Ion Density, Drift Velocity, and Temperature
Electron Energy and Pitch Angle Distribution
eoportal.summary: It is a three part mission, with the cascade payload demonstrating the world’s first commercial space-based digital courier service, the ePOP (Enhanced Polar Outflow Probe) measuring the interaction of the Earth’s upper atmosphere with the solar wind, and new minisatellite bus that may be used for future missions.

faq.a: CASSIOPE is divided into three sections. The ePOP section gathers scientific data, where it observes the Earth’s ionosphere. The instruments are used in conjunction with other satellite-based and ground-based instruments to analyse radio wave propagation in the ionosphere, measure the densities of ionised particles, and observe the aurora from space, all as they respond to space weather
faq.q: What is CASSIOPE data used for?

faq.a: CASSIOPE is owned by MacDonald Dettwiler and Associates (MDA) and operated by the University of Calgary.
faq.q: Who owns CASSIOPE?

faq.a: CASSIOPE data can be found in the University of Calgary website by typing ePOP into the search bar at the top right of the webpage.
faq.q: Where can I find CASSIOPE data?

eoportal.agency_zh: ESA（欧洲空间局）、MDA、卡尔加里大学
eoportal.summary_zh: 这是一项由三部分组成的任务：Cascade 载荷用于演示全球首个基于太空的商业数字信使服务；ePOP（增强极地外流探测器）用于测量地球高层大气与太阳风的相互作用；此外还采用了一种可用于未来任务的新型迷你卫星平台。
eoportal.summary_en: It is a three part mission, with the cascade payload demonstrating the world’s first commercial space-based digital courier service, the ePOP (Enhanced Polar Outflow Probe) measuring the interaction of the Earth’s upper atmosphere with the solar wind, and new minisatellite bus that may be used for future missions.

faq_zh.a: CASSIOPE 分为三个部分。ePOP 部分收集科学数据，观测地球电离层。这些仪器与其他星基和地基仪器配合使用，用于分析无线电波在电离层中的传播、测量电离粒子密度以及从太空观测极光，所有这些研究都旨在应对空间天气的影响。
CASSIOPE 由 MacDonald, Dettwiler and Associates (MDA) 所有，并由卡尔加里大学负责运营。
您可以在卡尔加里大学的网站上通过在网页右上角的搜索栏中输入 ePOP 来找到 CASSIOPE 数据。
faq_zh.q: 我在哪里可以找到 CASSIOPE 数据？

faq_en.a: CASSIOPE is divided into three sections. The ePOP section gathers scientific data, where it observes the Earth’s ionosphere. The instruments are used in conjunction with other satellite-based and ground-based instruments to analyse radio wave propagation in the ionosphere, measure the densities of ionised particles, and observe the aurora from space, all as they respond to space weather
faq_en.q: What is CASSIOPE data used for?

faq_en.a: CASSIOPE is owned by MacDonald Dettwiler and Associates (MDA) and operated by the University of Calgary.
faq_en.q: Who owns CASSIOPE?

faq_en.a: CASSIOPE data can be found in the University of Calgary website by typing ePOP into the search bar at the top right of the webpage.
faq_en.q: Where can I find CASSIOPE data?

cassiope (cascade smallsat and ionospher.display: CASSIOPE (Cascade SmallSat and Ionospheric Polar Explorer)

eoportal.name: CBERS-1 and 2 (China-Brazil Earth Resources Satellite)
eoportal.summary: CBERS-2 was launched in October 2003 and the third mission, CBERS-2b, was launched in September 2007.

faq.a: CBERS is a collaboration between the Chinese Academy of Space Technology (CAST) and the Brazilian Instituto de Pesquisas Espaciais (INPE).
faq.q: Who runs the CBERS satellites?

faq.a: There were three satellites in the first generation of CBERS: CBERS-1, CBERS-2 and CBERS-2B.
faq.q: How many satellites were in the first generation of CBERS?

faq.a: The main objective of the CBERS satellites is to provide the Chinese and Brazilian government with both high spatial and high temporal resolution images, across visible and infrared bands.

eoportal.name_zh: CBERS-1 和 2（中巴地球资源卫星）

eoportal.summary_en: CBERS-2 was launched in October 2003 and the third mission, CBERS-2b, was launched in September 2007.

faq_zh.a: CBERS是中国空间技术研究院（CAST）与巴西空间研究院（INPE）的合作项目。
CBERS第一代卫星包括三颗：CBERS-1、CBERS-2和CBERS-2B。

eoportal.name: CBERS-3 and 4 (China-Brazil Earth Resources Satellite) - 2nd Generation Satellite Series
taxonomy: Cloud type, amount and cloud top temperature
eoportal.summary: CBERS-4 was then built as a replacement and was launched on 7 December 2014.

faq.a: CBERS-3 and 4 gather data to generate maps that can be used for managing and monitoring Earth resources, monitoring environmental change, and making general observations of the Earth’s surface. For example, CBERS-4 may observe deforestation in the Amazon Rainforest and the data collected can be used to guide decisions relating to conservation efforts.
faq.q: What is CBERS-3 & 4 used for?

faq.a: CBERS images can be found in the Common Metadata Repository (CMR) section of the official NASA website.
faq.q: Where can I find CBERS images?

faq.a: CBERS-3 & 4 data is open source, and so can be accessed by anyone with no payment necessary.
faq.q: Is CBERS-3 & 4 data free?

eoportal.name_zh: CBERS-3和4（中巴地球资源卫星） - 第二代卫星系列
eoportal.summary_zh: CBERS-4作为替代卫星随后建造，并于2014年12月7日发射升空。
eoportal.summary_en: CBERS-4 was then built as a replacement and was launched on 7 December 2014.

faq_zh.a: CBERS-3和4收集数据以生成地图，这些地图可用于管理和监测地球资源、监测环境变化以及对地球表面进行常规观测。例如，CBERS-4可以观测亚马逊雨林的森林砍伐情况，收集的数据可用于指导与保护工作相关的决策。
CBERS图像可以在NASA官方网站的公共元数据存储库（CMR）部分找到。
CBERS-3和4的数据是开源的，因此任何人无需付费即可访问。

For example, CBERS-4 may observe deforestation in the Amazon Rainforest and the data collected can be used to guide decisions relating to conservation efforts.

faq_en.a: CBERS images can be found in the Common Metadata Repository (CMR) section of the official NASA website.

faq_en.a: CBERS-3 & 4 data is open source, and so can be accessed by anyone with no payment necessary.

eoportal.name: CBERS-4A (China-Brazil Earth Resources Satellite-4A)
eoportal.summary: CBERS-4A is used to monitor water resources, agriculture, urban growth, and land use, with a strong focus on the preservation of the Amazon rainforest.

faq.a: The satellite operates in a recurrent, sun synchronous orbit at an altitude of 628.6 km, an inclination of 97.89° and with an orbital period of 97.25 minutes.
faq.q: What orbit is CBERS-4A in?

faq.a: CBERS-4A uses a Phoenix Eye 1 bus, designed by the China Association for Science and Technology (CAST). The bus is three axis stabilised with dimensions of 1.8 x 2.0 x 2.6 m.
faq.q: What bus does CBERS-4A use?

faq.a: CBERS-4A has three imaging instruments on board, a high-resolution optical imager, the Multispectral and Panchromatic Wide-Scan Camera (WPM), and two different multi-spectral radiometers, a Multispectral Camera (MUX) and a Wide Field Imager (WFI).
faq.q: What instruments does CBERS-4A carry?

eoportal.summary_zh: 中巴地球资源卫星-4A主要用于监测水资源、农业、城市扩张和土地利用，并特别关注亚马逊雨林的保护。
eoportal.summary_en: CBERS-4A is used to monitor water resources, agriculture, urban growth, and land use, with a strong focus on the preservation of the Amazon rainforest.

faq_zh.a: 该卫星运行在高度为628.6公里、倾角为97.89°的循环太阳同步轨道上，轨道周期为97.25分钟。
CBERS-4A采用由中国空间技术研究院（CAST）设计的凤凰一号平台，该平台为三轴稳定，尺寸为1.8 x 2.0 x 2.6米。
CBERS-4A搭载了三种成像仪器：高分辨率光学成像仪、多光谱全色宽幅扫描相机（WPM），以及两种不同的多光谱辐射计——多光谱相机（MUX）和宽视场成像仪（WFI）。

faq_en.a: The satellite operates in a recurrent, sun synchronous orbit at an altitude of 628.6 km, an inclination of 97.89° and with an orbital period of 97.25 minutes.
faq_en.q: What orbit is CBERS-4A in?

faq_en.a: CBERS-4A uses a Phoenix Eye 1 bus, designed by the China Association for Science and Technology (CAST). The bus is three axis stabilised with dimensions of 1.8 x 2.0 x 2.6 m.
faq_en.q: What bus does CBERS-4A use?

faq_en.a: CBERS-4A has three imaging instruments on board, a high-resolution optical imager, the Multispectral and Panchromatic Wide-Scan Camera (WPM), and two different multi-spectral radiometers, a Multispectral Camera (MUX) and a Wide Field Imager (WFI).
faq_en.q: What instruments does CBERS-4A carry?

eoportal.name: CFOSAT (Chinese-French Oceanography Satellite)
eoportal.summary: CFOSAT internationally monitors the wind and waves of the ocean surface to advance our knowledge and models of them, thus improving our forecasting ability.

faq.a: CFOSAT was launched on 29 October, 2018, onboard a Chinese-built Long March 2C launch vehicle from the Jiuquan Satellite Launch Centre (JSLC) in China.
faq.q: What were the launch details of CFOSAT?

faq.a: CFOSAT measures several characteristics of ocean winds and waves: wind magnitude, wind direction, wave height, wave propagation direction, and wave wavelength.
faq.q: What does CFOSAT measure?

faq.a: CFOSAT is being collaboratively operated by the Chinese National Space Administration (CNSA) and the Centre National d’Etudes Spatiales (CNES), the French space agency.
faq.q: Who is operating CFOSAT?

eoportal.summary_zh: 中法海洋卫星（CFOSAT）对全球海洋表面风场和波浪进行联合观测，旨在增进我们对海洋风浪的认识并改进相关模型，从而提升我们的预报能力。
eoportal.summary_en: CFOSAT internationally monitors the wind and waves of the ocean surface to advance our knowledge and models of them, thus improving our forecasting ability.

faq_zh.a: CFOSAT于2018年10月29日，搭载中国研制的长征二号丙运载火箭，从中国酒泉卫星发射中心（JSLC）发射升空。
CFOSAT测量海洋风场和波浪的多种特性，包括风速、风向、浪高、波浪传播方向和波浪波长。
CFOSAT由法国国家空间研究中心（CNES）和中国国家航天局（CNSA）合作运营。

faq_en.a: CFOSAT was launched on 29 October, 2018, onboard a Chinese-built Long March 2C launch vehicle from the Jiuquan Satellite Launch Centre (JSLC) in China.
faq_en.q: What were the launch details of CFOSAT?

faq_en.a: CFOSAT measures several characteristics of ocean winds and waves: wind magnitude, wind direction, wave height, wave propagation direction, and wave wavelength.
faq_en.q: What does CFOSAT measure?

faq_en.a: CFOSAT is being collaboratively operated by the Chinese National Space Administration (CNSA) and the Centre National d’Etudes Spatiales (CNES), the French space agency.
faq_en.q: Who is operating CFOSAT?

eoportal.name: CHAMP (Challenging Minisatellite Payload)
taxonomy: CHAMP Magnetometry Package (1 Scalar + 2 Vector Magnetometer)
eoportal.summary: It acquired spatial and temporal measurements of Earth’s gravitational, magnetic, and electric fields which assisted in a variety of fields such as climate studies, geodesy, and geophysics.
instruments: CHAMP Magnetometry Package (1 Scalar + 2 Vector Magnetometer)

faq.a: CHAMP was launched on July 15, 2000, aboard a Kosmos-3M launch vehicle from the Plesetsk Cosmodrome in Russia.
faq.q: What were the launch details of CHAMP?

faq.a: CHAMP had a 4 m long boom on which the magnetometers rest so the satellite's electronics did not interfere with the magnetic field readings.
faq.q: Why did CHAMP have a boom?

faq.a: CHAMP’s mission came to an end upon burning up in Earth’s atmosphere on September 19, 2010, above the Sea of Okhotsk.
faq.q: When did CHAMP become no longer operational?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该卫星通过获取地球重力场、磁场和电场的时空测量数据，为气候研究、大地测量和地球物理学等多个领域提供了支持。
eoportal.summary_en: It acquired spatial and temporal measurements of Earth’s gravitational, magnetic, and electric fields which assisted in a variety of fields such as climate studies, geodesy, and geophysics.

faq_zh.a: CHAMP于2000年7月15日从俄罗斯普列谢茨克航天发射场由Kosmos-3M运载火箭发射升空。
faq_zh.q: CHAMP卫星的发射细节是什么？

faq_zh.a: CHAMP卫星上安装了一根4米长的悬臂用于承载磁力仪，以避免卫星自身的电子设备干扰磁场读数。
faq_zh.q: 为什么CHAMP卫星要有一根悬臂？

faq_zh.a: CHAMP的使命于2010年9月19日在鄂霍次克海上空于地球大气层中烧毁时终结。

faq_en.a: CHAMP was launched on July 15, 2000, aboard a Kosmos-3M launch vehicle from the Plesetsk Cosmodrome in Russia.
faq_en.q: What were the launch details of CHAMP?

faq_en.a: CHAMP had a 4 m long boom on which the magnetometers rest so the satellite's electronics did not interfere with the magnetic field readings.
faq_en.q: Why did CHAMP have a boom?

faq_en.a: CHAMP’s mission came to an end upon burning up in Earth’s atmosphere on September 19, 2010, above the Sea of Okhotsk.
faq_en.q: When did CHAMP become no longer operational?

eoportal.name: Chandrayaan-3
eoportal.summary: The mission demonstrated the first soft landing in the Southern Polar region of the Moon, as well as placing India as the fourth country to land on the Moon.

faq.a: Chandrayaan-3 of the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) had the objective to demonstrate a safe and soft landing on the Lunar surface, the exploration with a lunar rover, and to conduct in-situ scientific experiments.
faq.q: What was the objective of Chandrayaan-3?

faq.a: Chandrayaan-3’s Vikram Lander touched down on the lunar surface on the 23rd August 2023, in the Southern polar region of the Moon.
faq.q: When did Chandrayaan-3 land on the Moon?

faq.a: ISRO attempted to re-establish contact with the Vikram Lander and Pragyan Rover on the 22nd September 2023, but the harsh and cold lunar nights (lasting 14 Earth days) seems to have affected their batteries irreparably. On the 4th December 2023, ISRO tasked the Propulsion Module (PM) to move back into an Earth orbit with its fuel reserves to make additional observations in support of future lunar missions.
faq.q: What happened to Chandrayaan-3?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该任务实现了在月球南极区域的首次软着陆，并使印度成为第四个在月球着陆的国家。
eoportal.summary_en: The mission demonstrated the first soft landing in the Southern Polar region of the Moon, as well as placing India as the fourth country to land on the Moon.

faq_zh.a: 印度空间研究组织（ISRO）的月船-3任务目标是：演示在月球表面的安全软着陆、使用月球车进行勘探，以及进行就地科学实验。
faq_zh.q: 月船-3的任务目标是什么？

faq_zh.a: 月船-3的维克拉姆着陆器于2023年8月23日在月球南极区域着陆。
ISRO曾于2023年9月22日尝试与维克拉姆着陆器和智慧号月球车重新建立联系，但严酷寒冷的月球夜晚（持续14个地球日）似乎已对其电池造成不可逆的损害。2023年12月4日，ISRO指令推进模块利用其剩余燃料储备返回地球轨道，以便为未来的月球任务进行额外观测。

faq_en.a: Chandrayaan-3 of the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) had the objective to demonstrate a safe and soft landing on the Lunar surface, the exploration with a lunar rover, and to conduct in-situ scientific experiments.
faq_en.q: What was the objective of Chandrayaan-3?

faq_en.a: Chandrayaan-3’s Vikram Lander touched down on the lunar surface on the 23rd August 2023, in the Southern polar region of the Moon.
faq_en.q: When did Chandrayaan-3 land on the Moon?

faq_en.a: ISRO attempted to re-establish contact with the Vikram Lander and Pragyan Rover on the 22nd September 2023, but the harsh and cold lunar nights (lasting 14 Earth days) seems to have affected their batteries irreparably. On the 4th December 2023, ISRO tasked the Propulsion Module (PM) to move back into an Earth orbit with its fuel reserves to make additional observations in support of future lunar missions.
faq_en.q: What happened to Chandrayaan-3?

eoportal.name: CHIME (Copernicus Hyperspectral Imaging Mission for the Environment)
eoportal.summary: It consists of two identical satellites, CHIME-A and CHIME-B, each equipped with a hyperspectral imager for applications in sustainable agriculture, food security, raw materials, and ecosystem assessment.

faq.a: The Copernicus Hyperspectral Imaging Mission for the Environment (CHIME) is an Earth observation mission designed to provide hyperspectral data in support of environmental and resource monitoring.
faq.q: What is the CHIME mission?

faq.a: CHIME will support applications in agriculture, food security, and raw materials. Secondary applications include biodiversity, water quality, and ecosystem monitoring.
faq.q: What are the applications of CHIME data?

faq.a: Thales Alenia Space France is the prime contractor. The hyperspectral imaging  instrument is developed by OHB (Germany), with Leonardo (Italy) and AMOS (Belgium) contributing key components.
faq.q: Who is building the CHIME satellites and instruments?

eoportal.name_zh: CHIME (Copernicus Hyperspectral Imaging Mission for the Environment)
eoportal.summary_zh: 该任务由两颗相同的卫星CHIME-A和CHIME-B组成，每颗卫星均搭载高光谱成像仪，旨在为可持续农业、粮食安全、原材料和生态系统评估等应用提供支持。
eoportal.summary_en: It consists of two identical satellites, CHIME-A and CHIME-B, each equipped with a hyperspectral imager for applications in sustainable agriculture, food security, raw materials, and ecosystem assessment.

eoportal.name: CHIPSat (Cosmic Hot Interstellar Plasma Spectrometer Satellite)
eoportal.summary: The software running on the spacecraft periodically requests a time update from the ground station, and after a successful SNTP echo, the spacecraft clock is aligned to UTC (estimated at better than 100 milliseconds).
eoportal.name_zh: CHIPSat (宇宙热星际等离子体光谱仪卫星)
eoportal.summary_zh: 卫星上运行的软件会定期从地面站请求时间更新，在成功接收到SNTP（简单网络时间协议）的响应后，卫星时钟将与UTC（协调世界时）对齐（估计精度优于100毫秒）。
eoportal.summary_en: The software running on the spacecraft periodically requests a time update from the ground station, and after a successful SNTP echo, the spacecraft clock is aligned to UTC (estimated at better than 100 milliseconds).

eoportal.name: CHORUS
eoportal.summary: The mission will use tipping and cueing techniques, as well as near-real time data exploitation aided by machine-learning and artificial intelligence, to deliver products for a variety of maritime and land-based applications.

faq.a: CHORUS is not an acronym, rather refers to the mission concept “to bring together multiple diverse and unique perspectives in harmony, to provide a new level of real-time insight about our planet”.
faq.q: What does CHORUS stand for?

faq.a: MDA Ltd. (MacDonald, Dettwiler and Associates Ltd.), along with ICEYE, are developing CHORUS. MDA is responsible for the C-band SAR satellite, and ICEYE is responsible for the X-band satellite. Other contractors include Magellan Aerospace Corporation, for the spacecraft avionics, and OHB Sweden, for the reaction control subsystem.
faq.q: Who is developing CHORUS?

faq.a: CHORUS C will carry a C-band synthetic aperture radar (SAR) instrument, which operates at frequencies of 4-8 GHz (wavelengths 75 – 38 mm), while CHORUS X will carry an X-band SAR instrument, which operates at frequencies of 8-12 GHz (wavelengths 38 – 24 mm).
faq.q: What is the difference between CHORUS C and CHORUS X?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该任务将运用“倾斜与提示”技术，并借助机器学习和人工智能辅助的近实时数据处理，为各种海事和陆基应用提供产品。
eoportal.summary_en: The mission will use tipping and cueing techniques, as well as near-real time data exploitation aided by machine-learning and artificial intelligence, to deliver products for a variety of maritime and land-based applications.

faq_zh.a: CHORUS并非缩写，而是指其任务理念：“将多种多样且独特的视角和谐地汇集在一起，以提供关于我们星球的全新实时洞察水平”。
MDA有限公司（MacDonald, Dettwiler and Associates Ltd.）与ICEYE公司正在共同开发CHORUS。MDA负责C波段SAR卫星，ICEYE负责X波段卫星。其他承包商包括负责航天器航电设备的Magellan Aerospace Corporation，以及负责反作用控制子系统的OHB Sweden。
CHORUS C将搭载一台C波段合成孔径雷达（SAR）仪器，工作频率为4-8 GHz（波长75-38毫米）；而CHORUS X将搭载一台X波段SAR仪器，工作频率为8-12 GHz（波长38-24毫米）。
faq_zh.q: CHORUS C和CHORUS X有什么区别？

faq_en.a: CHORUS is not an acronym, rather refers to the mission concept “to bring together multiple diverse and unique perspectives in harmony, to provide a new level of real-time insight about our planet”.
faq_en.q: What does CHORUS stand for?

faq_en.a: MDA Ltd. (MacDonald, Dettwiler and Associates Ltd.), along with ICEYE, are developing CHORUS. MDA is responsible for the C-band SAR satellite, and ICEYE is responsible for the X-band satellite. Other contractors include Magellan Aerospace Corporation, for the spacecraft avionics, and OHB Sweden, for the reaction control subsystem.
faq_en.q: Who is developing CHORUS?

faq_en.a: CHORUS C will carry a C-band synthetic aperture radar (SAR) instrument, which operates at frequencies of 4-8 GHz (wavelengths 75 – 38 mm), while CHORUS X will carry an X-band SAR instrument, which operates at frequencies of 8-12 GHz (wavelengths 38 – 24 mm).
faq_en.q: What is the difference between CHORUS C and CHORUS X?

eoportal.summary:

CICERO (Community Initiative for Continuing Earth Radio Occultation)

EO

Operational (nominal)

|

GeoOptics

CICERO satellites perform GNSS-RO of Earth's atmosphere and surface remote sensing by GNSS reflection and obtain data for weather forecasting, climate research, and space weather monitoring.

faq.a: CICERO GNSS-RO data is processed to create high-accuracy profiles of atmospheric pressure, temperature, and moisture; 3D maps of the electron distribution in the ionosphere; and ocean and ice properties. This data is used in weather forecasting, climate research, and space weather monitoring. The data is commercially available to government and business users and is free for research purposes. CICERO currently has a contract with NOAA (National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration) to provide daily radio occultation soundings.
faq.q: What is CICERO data used for?

faq.a: GeoOptics is an international consortium founded by working scientists. GeoOptics created the CICERO mission, following a community approach, to ensure continuing GNSS-RO observations. Their goal is to provide high-quality radio occultation data from space.
faq.q: What is GeoOptics?

faq.a: GNSS-RO stands for Global Navigation Satellite System Radio Occultation. GNSS-RO is a remote sensing technique in which low Earth-orbiting satellites measure the atmospheric refraction of radio signals sent by GNSS satellites. Atmospheric refractivity is a function of temperature, density, pressure, and water vapour content meaning these measurements can profile the Earth's atmosphere and ionosphere with high vertical resolution. GNSS-RO is the primary sensing method of CICERO satellites.
faq.q: What is GNSS-RO?

eoportal.name_zh: CICERO (持续地球无线电掩星社区倡议)
eoportal.summary_zh: CICERO卫星通过GNSS掩星技术探测地球大气层，并通过GNSS反射进行地表遥感，获取用于天气预报、气候研究和空间天气监测的数据。
eoportal.summary_en:
Portal

Table of contents
CICERO (Community Initiative for Continuing Earth Radio Occultation)

EO

|

Operational (nominal)

GeoOptics

CICERO satellites perform GNSS-RO of Earth's atmosphere and surface remote sensing by GNSS reflection and obtain data for weather forecasting, climate research, and space weather monitoring.

faq_zh.a: CICERO的GNSS掩星数据经处理可生成高精度的大气压力、温度和湿度廓线，电离层电子分布的三维图，以及海洋和冰层特性信息。这些数据用于天气预报、气候研究和空间天气监测。数据对政府和商业用户有偿提供，对研究用途免费。CICERO目前与NOAA（美国国家海洋和大气管理局）签有合同，为其提供每日无线电掩星探测数据。
GeoOptics是一个由在职科学家发起的国际联盟。GeoOptics遵循社区协作理念，创建了CICERO任务，以确保GNSS掩星观测的持续性。其目标是提供来自太空的高质量无线电掩星数据。
GNSS掩星（GNSS-RO）全称为全球导航卫星系统无线电掩星。它是一种遥感技术，由低轨卫星测量GNSS卫星发射的无线电信号在大气中的折射。大气折射率是温度、密度、压力和水汽含量的函数，这意味着这些测量可以高垂直分辨率地探测地球大气和电离层。GNSS掩星是CICERO卫星的主要探测方法。

faq_en.a: CICERO GNSS-RO data is processed to create high-accuracy profiles of atmospheric pressure, temperature, and moisture; 3D maps of the electron distribution in the ionosphere; and ocean and ice properties. This data is used in weather forecasting, climate research, and space weather monitoring. The data is commercially available to government and business users and is free for research purposes. CICERO currently has a contract with NOAA (National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration) to provide daily radio occultation soundings.
faq_en.q: What is CICERO data used for?

faq_en.a: GeoOptics is an international consortium founded by working scientists. GeoOptics created the CICERO mission, following a community approach, to ensure continuing GNSS-RO observations. Their goal is to provide high-quality radio occultation data from space.
faq_en.q: What is GeoOptics?

faq_en.a: GNSS-RO stands for Global Navigation Satellite System Radio Occultation. GNSS-RO is a remote sensing technique in which low Earth-orbiting satellites measure the atmospheric refraction of radio signals sent by GNSS satellites. Atmospheric refractivity is a function of temperature, density, pressure, and water vapour content meaning these measurements can profile the Earth's atmosphere and ionosphere with high vertical resolution. GNSS-RO is the primary sensing method of CICERO satellites.
faq_en.q: What is GNSS-RO?

cicero (community initiative for continu.display: CICERO (Community Initiative for Continuing Earth Radio Occultation)

eoportal.name: CIMR (Copernicus Imaging Microwave Radiometer)
taxonomy: Sea ice cover, edge and thickness
Soil moisture in the roots region
eoportal.summary: The mission responds to the European Commission’s objectives pertaining to improved spatial and temporal coverage of sea ice and the Arctic environment in support of Arctic user communities.

faq.a: With climate change causing wide-spread ocean warming, sea level rise and melting of the polar ice caps, there are knock-on effects for the global economy and national security. CIMR will hope to track these changes and help scientists and policy makers mitigate their effects.
faq.q: Why is the CIMR mission important?

faq.a: The European Commission (COM) issued a proposal for ‘an integrated europe Union Policy for the arctic’ in 2016. The mission is managed by the European Union Agency for the Space Programme, on behalf of the European Commission.
faq.q: Who has commissioned the CIMR mission?

faq.a: CIMR will look to make observations covering three measurements: Sea Surface Temperature (SST), the Sea-Surface Salinity (SSS) and the Sea Ice Concentration (SIC).
faq.q: What will the CIMR mission observe?

eoportal.name_zh: CIMR（哥白尼成像微波辐射计）
eoportal.summary_zh: 该任务旨在响应欧洲委员会关于提高海冰及北极环境时空覆盖度的目标，以支持北极用户群体。
eoportal.summary_en: The mission responds to the European Commission’s objectives pertaining to improved spatial and temporal coverage of sea ice and the Arctic environment in support of Arctic user communities.

faq_zh.a: 随着气候变化导致海洋广泛变暖、海平面上升和极地冰盖融化，其连锁反应将影响全球经济与国家安全。CIMR旨在追踪这些变化，帮助科学家和政策制定者减轻其影响。
欧洲委员会于2016年发布了《欧盟综合北极政策》提案。该任务由欧洲联盟空间计划局代表欧洲委员会管理。
CIMR将致力于获取三类观测数据：海表温度、海表盐度和海冰浓度。

faq_en.a: With climate change causing wide-spread ocean warming, sea level rise and melting of the polar ice caps, there are knock-on effects for the global economy and national security. CIMR will hope to track these changes and help scientists and policy makers mitigate their effects.
faq_en.q: Why is the CIMR mission important?

faq_en.a: The European Commission (COM) issued a proposal for ‘an integrated europe Union Policy for the arctic’ in 2016. The mission is managed by the European Union Agency for the Space Programme, on behalf of the European Commission.
faq_en.q: Who has commissioned the CIMR mission?

faq_en.a: CIMR will look to make observations covering three measurements: Sea Surface Temperature (SST), the Sea-Surface Salinity (SSS) and the Sea Ice Concentration (SIC).

eoportal.name: Clarity (Albedo Space)
eoportal.summary: The constellation’s first satellite, Clarity-1, launched in March 2025.

faq.a: The aim of the Clarity constellation is to provide high-resolution satellite imagery, previously only obtainable by aircraft imaging, which is less efficient and more expensive to maintain.
faq.q: What is the purpose of the Clarity constellation?

faq.a: The optical imager can capture imagery with Ground Sample Distance (GSD) of 10 cm at Nadir and 12.4 cm 30° off Nadir. The thermal infrared (LWIR) imager provides imagery with a GSD of 2 m at Nadir.
faq.q: What are the resolutions of the instruments onboard the Clarity satellites from Albedo Space?

faq.a: Albedo Space utilises a custom cloud delivery pipeline to provide imagery to users within an hour of its collection. Customers can access analysis-ready data and imagery through a user-friendly Application Programming Interface (API).
faq.q: How does Albedo Space deliver its imagery to customers?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该星座的首颗卫星 Clarity-1 于 2025 年 3 月发射。
eoportal.summary_en: The constellation’s first satellite, Clarity-1, launched in March 2025.

faq_zh.a: Clarity 星座旨在提供高分辨率卫星影像，这些影像此前只能通过航空摄影获取，但航空摄影效率较低且维护成本更高。
faq_zh.q: Clarity 星座的目的是什么？

faq_zh.a: 光学成像仪在星下点可捕获地面采样距离（GSD）为 10 厘米的影像，在偏离星下点 30 度时为 12.4 厘米。热红外（LWIR）成像仪在星下点提供地面采样距离为 2 米的影像。
faq_zh.q: Albedo Space 的 Clarity 卫星搭载的仪器分辨率是多少？

faq_zh.a: Albedo Space 采用定制的云分发流程，可在数据采集后一小时内向用户提供影像。客户可以通过用户友好的应用程序编程接口（API）获取可直接用于分析的数据和影像。
faq_zh.q: Albedo Space 如何向客户提供其影像？

faq_en.a: The aim of the Clarity constellation is to provide high-resolution satellite imagery, previously only obtainable by aircraft imaging, which is less efficient and more expensive to maintain.
faq_en.q: What is the purpose of the Clarity constellation?

faq_en.a: The optical imager can capture imagery with Ground Sample Distance (GSD) of 10 cm at Nadir and 12.4 cm 30° off Nadir. The thermal infrared (LWIR) imager provides imagery with a GSD of 2 m at Nadir.
faq_en.q: What are the resolutions of the instruments onboard the Clarity satellites from Albedo Space?

faq_en.a: Albedo Space utilises a custom cloud delivery pipeline to provide imagery to users within an hour of its collection. Customers can access analysis-ready data and imagery through a user-friendly Application Programming Interface (API).
faq_en.q: How does Albedo Space deliver its imagery to customers?

eoportal.name: CLARREO (Climate Absolute Radiance and Refractivity Observatory)
eoportal.summary: CLARREO is expected to launch in the late 2020s, with a smaller pathfinder mission expected to launch to the International Space Station in late 2023.

faq.a: CLARREO is expected to launch in the late 2020s.
faq.q: When is CLARREO expected to launch?

faq.a: NASA will oversee the launch of CLARREO.

faq.a: The primary objective of CLARREO is to improve humankind's understanding of climate change through the measurement of the Earth’s infrared emission spectrum at very high accuracy and traceable to SI units and radiometric standards.

eoportal.name: Cluster (Four Spacecraft Constellation in Concert with SOHO)

eoportal.name: Copernicus Anthropogenic Carbon Dioxide Monitoring (CO2M) Mission
taxonomy: Cloud type, amount and cloud top temperature
Sea ice cover, edge and thickness
Downward short-wave irradiance at Earth surface
eoportal.summary: CO2M consists of three platforms, and is one of six Copernicus Sentinel Expansion missions, which ESA are developing with the European Commission.

faq.a: To monitor anthropogenic carbon dioxide (CO2) emissions with high precision, supporting climate policy implementation and tracking global carbon reduction efforts.
faq.q: What is the main objective of the CO2M mission?

faq.a: Each satellite carries a combined CO2 and NO2 Imaging Spectrometer (CO2I/NO2I), a Multi-Angle Polarimeter (MAP), and a CLoud IMager (CLIM).
faq.q: What instruments are onboard CO2M?

faq.a: One satellite revisits every 11 days at the equator. With two satellites, the revisit time drops to 6 days, and all three together achieve global coverage roughly every 3.5 days.
faq.q: How often will CO2M satellites cover the Earth?

eoportal.name_zh: 哥白尼人为二氧化碳监测 (CO2M) 任务
eoportal.agency_zh: ESA（欧洲航天局）、EUMETSAT（欧洲气象卫星应用组织）、COM（欧盟委员会）
eoportal.summary_zh: CO2M任务由三颗卫星平台组成，是哥白尼哨兵扩展任务之一，由ESA（欧洲航天局）与欧盟委员会共同开发。
eoportal.summary_en: CO2M consists of three platforms, and is one of six Copernicus Sentinel Expansion missions, which ESA are developing with the European Commission.

faq_zh.a: 高精度监测人为二氧化碳 (CO2) 排放，以支持气候政策的实施并追踪全球碳减排努力。
faq_zh.q: CO2M任务的主要目标是什么？

faq_zh.a: 每颗卫星都搭载了一个组合式CO2和NO2成像光谱仪 (CO2I/NO2I)、一个多角度偏振仪 (MAP) 和一个云成像仪 (CLIM)。
在赤道处，单颗卫星的重访周期为11天。使用两颗卫星时，重访周期可缩短至6天，而三颗卫星协同工作则可实现大约每3.5天一次的全球覆盖。
faq_zh.q: CO2M卫星的覆盖周期是多久？

faq_en.a: To monitor anthropogenic carbon dioxide (CO2) emissions with high precision, supporting climate policy implementation and tracking global carbon reduction efforts.
faq_en.q: What is the main objective of the CO2M mission?

faq_en.a: Each satellite carries a combined CO2 and NO2 Imaging Spectrometer (CO2I/NO2I), a Multi-Angle Polarimeter (MAP), and a CLoud IMager (CLIM).
faq_en.q: What instruments are onboard CO2M?

faq_en.a: One satellite revisits every 11 days at the equator. With two satellites, the revisit time drops to 6 days, and all three together achieve global coverage roughly every 3.5 days.
faq_en.q: How often will CO2M satellites cover the Earth?

eoportal.name: Copernicus Space Programme
eoportal.agency: ESA, EUMETSAT, COM, ECMWF, Mercator Océan International, SatCen, FRONTEX, EMSA, EEA, EU
eoportal.summary: The space component consists of the Sentinel missions, as well as the Copernicus Contributing Missions, and is supported by the Copernicus Ground Segment.

faq.a: Copernicus is the Earth observation component of the European Union's Space programme. It offers information services that draw from space-based and in-situ data.
faq.q: What is the Copernicus Programme?

faq.a: The EU Copernicus programme is co-funded by the European Union and ESA, with a minor contribution from EUMETSAT.
faq.q: Who funds the Copernicus programme?

faq.a: ESA is the European Union’s main partner and co-funder of the programme, and has the mandate to coordinate the space component, also acting as system architect, and serving users with satellite data available through the dedicated satellite missions, the Sentinel families, and through the Copernicus Contributing Missions.
faq.q: What’s ESA’s role on Copernicus?

faq.a: Copernicus primarily builds on the data collected from dedicated EO satellites built for the programme, the Sentinels, and complemented by other missions, the Copernican Contributing Missions, from which part of the data is used by Copernicus, all of them making up, along with an integrated ground infrastructure capable of delivering all the data produced to the users, the Copernicus Space Component.
faq.q: What is the Copernicus Space component?

faq.a: The Sentinel family of satellites form the backbone of the space component of the European Union’s Copernicus programme. They have been developed by ESA specifically to answer users’ needs and collect data on a range of Earth system parameters.
faq.q: What are the Sentinel satellites?

faq.a: The Copernicus Space Segment includes the six Sentinel missions of the first generation (Sentinel-1, 2, 3, 4 , 5P/5 and 6), as well as the six future Copernicus Expansion Missions (CHIME, CIMR, CO2M, CRISTAL, LSTM and ROSE-L), the Next Generation Sentinels (Sentinel-1 Next Gen, Sentinel-2 Next Gen, Sentinel-3 Next Gen Optical, Sentinel-3 Next Gen Topographic and Sentinel-6 Next Gen) and Copernicus Contributing Missions.
faq.q: How many Copernicus satellite missions are there?

eoportal.name_zh: 哥白尼空间计划 (Copernicus Space Programme)
eoportal.agency_zh: ESA, EUMETSAT, COM, ECMWF, Mercator Océan International, SatCen, FRONTEX, EMSA, EEA, EU
eoportal.summary_zh: 哥白尼空间部分由哨兵（Sentinel）系列任务以及哥白尼贡献任务构成，并由哥白尼地面段提供支持。
eoportal.summary_en: The space component consists of the Sentinel missions, as well as the Copernicus Contributing Missions, and is supported by the Copernicus Ground Segment.

faq_zh.a: 哥白尼是欧盟空间计划中的地球观测组成部分。它通过整合基于空间和现场数据，提供各类信息服务。
欧盟哥白尼计划由欧盟和欧空局（ESA）共同资助，欧洲气象卫星应用组织（EUMETSAT）也有少量资金贡献。
faq_zh.q: 谁为哥白尼计划提供资金？

faq_zh.a: 欧空局（ESA）是欧盟的主要合作伙伴和计划的共同资助方，并受委托协调空间部分，同时担任系统架构师，通过专属的卫星任务（哨兵系列）以及哥白尼贡献任务，为用户提供卫星数据服务。
faq_zh.q: 欧空局在哥白尼计划中扮演什么角色？

faq_zh.a: 哥白尼主要依托为该计划专门建造的地球观测卫星——哨兵（Sentinel）系列采集的数据，并由其他任务（哥白尼贡献任务）的数据进行补充，哥白尼会使用其中部分数据。所有这些卫星，连同能够向用户传送所有产出数据的综合地面基础设施，共同构成了哥白尼空间组成部分。
faq_zh.q: 什么是哥白尼空间组成部分？

faq_zh.a: 哨兵卫星系列是欧盟哥白尼计划空间部分的骨干。它们由欧空局（ESA）专门为满足用户需求而开发，用于收集一系列地球系统参数的数据。
哥白尼空间段包括第一代的六个哨兵任务（哨兵-1、2、3、4、5P/5和6），以及未来的六个哥白尼扩展任务（CHIME、CIMR、CO2M、CRISTAL、LSTM和ROSE-L），下一代哨兵系列（哨兵-1 Next Gen、哨兵-2 Next Gen、哨兵-3 Next Gen Optical、哨兵-3 Next Gen Topographic和哨兵-6 Next Gen），以及哥白尼贡献任务。
faq_zh.q: 哥白尼计划共有多少个卫星任务？

faq_en.a: Copernicus is the Earth observation component of the European Union's Space programme. It offers information services that draw from space-based and in-situ data.
faq_en.q: What is the Copernicus Programme?

faq_en.a: The EU Copernicus programme is co-funded by the European Union and ESA, with a minor contribution from EUMETSAT.
faq_en.q: Who funds the Copernicus programme?

faq_en.a: ESA is the European Union’s main partner and co-funder of the programme, and has the mandate to coordinate the space component, also acting as system architect, and serving users with satellite data available through the dedicated satellite missions, the Sentinel families, and through the Copernicus Contributing Missions.
faq_en.q: What’s ESA’s role on Copernicus?

faq_en.a: Copernicus primarily builds on the data collected from dedicated EO satellites built for the programme, the Sentinels, and complemented by other missions, the Copernican Contributing Missions, from which part of the data is used by Copernicus, all of them making up, along with an integrated ground infrastructure capable of delivering all the data produced to the users, the Copernicus Space Component.
faq_en.q: What is the Copernicus Space component?

faq_en.a: The Sentinel family of satellites form the backbone of the space component of the European Union’s Copernicus programme. They have been developed by ESA specifically to answer users’ needs and collect data on a range of Earth system parameters.
faq_en.q: What are the Sentinel satellites?

faq_en.a: The Copernicus Space Segment includes the six Sentinel missions of the first generation (Sentinel-1, 2, 3, 4 , 5P/5 and 6), as well as the six future Copernicus Expansion Missions (CHIME, CIMR, CO2M, CRISTAL, LSTM and ROSE-L), the Next Generation Sentinels (Sentinel-1 Next Gen, Sentinel-2 Next Gen, Sentinel-3 Next Gen Optical, Sentinel-3 Next Gen Topographic and Sentinel-6 Next Gen) and Copernicus Contributing Missions.

eoportal.name: Copernicus: Sentinel-1
taxonomy: Ocean imagery and water leaving spectral radiance
Sea ice cover, edge and thickness
eoportal.summary: The first satellite, Sentinel-1A, launched in April 2014.

faq.a: The Sentinel data policy, established by the European Space Agency and European Commission, provides full and open access to the data. The data can be accessed on the Copernicus Hub, free of charge.
faq.q: Can I access Sentinel-1 data?

faq.a: Sentinel-1 satellites are one of the few Sentinel satellites to have an imaging microwave radar onboard, in order to suit the Sentinel-1 objectives. Whilst Sentinel-1 is focused on continuously providing radar mapping of land and sea, other Sentinel missions are concerned with optical observations of land and coastlines, air quality, and sea-surface topography and temperature.
faq.q: How does Sentinel-1 differ from other Sentinel missions?

faq_en.a: Sentinel-1 satellites are one of the few Sentinel satellites to have an imaging microwave radar onboard, in order to suit the Sentinel-1 objectives. Whilst Sentinel-1 is focused on continuously providing radar mapping of land and sea, other Sentinel missions are concerned with optical observations of land and coastlines, air quality, and sea-surface topography and temperature.

eoportal.name: Copernicus: Sentinel-2
taxonomy: Ocean imagery and water leaving spectral radiance
eoportal.summary: The orbits of the satellites are coordinated to optimise coverage and revisit time.

faq.a: The Sentinel data policy, established by the European Space Agency and European Commission, provides full and open access to the data. The data can be accessed on the Copernicus Hub, free of charge.
faq.q: Can I access Sentinel-2 data?

faq.a: The satellite lifespan is 7.25 years, which includes a 3 month in-orbit commissioning phase. Batteries and propellants have been provided to accommodate 12 years of operations, including end of life de-orbiting manoeuvres.
faq.q: What is the design lifetime of Sentinel-2 missions?

faq.a: Sentinel-2 satellites are the only Sentinel satellites to have a high-resolution optical imager onboard, in order to suit the Sentinel-2 objectives. Whilst Sentinel-2 is focused on continuously providing optical observations of land and coastlines, other Sentinel missions are concerned with radar mapping of land and sea, air quality, and sea-surface topography and temperature.
faq.q: How does Sentinel-2 differ from other Sentinel missions?

eoportal.agency_zh: ESA（欧洲航天局）、COM（欧盟委员会）
eoportal.summary_zh: 卫星轨道经过协调优化，以提升覆盖范围和重访周期。

faq_zh.a: 由欧洲航天局和欧洲委员会制定的哨兵数据政策，提供完全公开的数据访问。数据可在哥白尼数据访问中心免费获取。
