faq_zh.a: 卫星设计寿命为7.25年，其中包括3个月的在轨调试期。配备了足够的电池和推进剂以支持长达12年的运行，其中包括退役时的离轨机动。
faq_zh.q: 哨兵-2任务的设计寿命是多少？

faq_zh.a: 哨兵-2卫星是唯一搭载高分辨率光学成像仪的哨兵卫星，以满足其任务目标。哨兵-2专注于持续提供陆地和海岸线的光学观测数据，而其他哨兵任务则涉及陆地和海洋的雷达成像、空气质量监测以及海面地形和温度测量。
faq_zh.q: 哨兵-2与其他哨兵系列任务有何不同？

faq_en.a: The Sentinel data policy, established by the European Space Agency and European Commission, provides full and open access to the data. The data can be accessed on the Copernicus Hub, free of charge.
faq_en.q: Can I access Sentinel-2 data?

faq_en.a: The satellite lifespan is 7.25 years, which includes a 3 month in-orbit commissioning phase. Batteries and propellants have been provided to accommodate 12 years of operations, including end of life de-orbiting manoeuvres.
faq_en.q: What is the design lifetime of Sentinel-2 missions?

faq_en.a: Sentinel-2 satellites are the only Sentinel satellites to have a high-resolution optical imager onboard, in order to suit the Sentinel-2 objectives. Whilst Sentinel-2 is focused on continuously providing optical observations of land and coastlines, other Sentinel missions are concerned with radar mapping of land and sea, air quality, and sea-surface topography and temperature.
faq_en.q: How does Sentinel-2 differ from other Sentinel missions?

eoportal.name: Copernicus: Sentinel-3
taxonomy: Cloud type, amount and cloud top temperature
Ocean imagery and water leaving spectral radiance
Sea ice cover, edge and thickness
eoportal.summary: Both S3A, launched in February 2016, and S3B, launched in April 2018, are part of Copernicus - the European Union’s Earth observation program, managed by the European Commission (COM).

faq.a: The Sentinel data policy, established by ESA and COM, provides full and open access to the data.
faq.q: Can I access Sentinel-3 data?

faq.a: Sentinel-3C is expected to launch in October 2024 and Sentinel-3B in November 2028. This is designed to coincide (with the addition of some extra time in case issues arise) with the end of life of Sentinel-3A and 3B, expected in 2026 and 2028 respectively.
faq.q: When will Sentinel-3C and Sentinel-3D be launched?

faq.a: Whilst Sentinel-3 is focused on measuring sea-surface topography, and the colour and temperature of ocean and land surfaces; other Sentinel missions are concerned with radar mapping of land and sea, monitoring air quality, and providing optical observations of land and coastlines.
faq.q: How does Sentinel-3 differ from other Sentinel missions?

eoportal.summary_zh: 哨兵-3A（于2016年2月发射）和哨兵-3B（于2018年4月发射）均属于哥白尼计划——欧盟由欧洲委员会（COM）管理的地球观测项目。
eoportal.summary_en: Both S3A, launched in February 2016, and S3B, launched in April 2018, are part of Copernicus - the European Union’s Earth observation program, managed by the European Commission (COM).

faq_zh.a: 由ESA和COM制定的哨兵数据政策确保数据的完全开放和免费获取。
faq_zh.q: 我可以获取哨兵-3的数据吗？

faq_zh.a: 哨兵-3C预计于2024年10月发射，哨兵-3D预计于2028年11月发射。这一安排旨在与哨兵-3A和3B的预期寿命终结时间（分别预计在2026年和2028年）相衔接（并预留了应对可能出现问题的额外时间）。
哨兵-3专注于测量海面地形以及海洋和陆地表面的颜色与温度；而其他哨兵任务则涉及陆地和海洋的雷达测绘、空气质量监测，以及对陆地和海岸线的光学观测。
faq_zh.q: 哨兵-3与其他哨兵任务有何不同？

faq_en.a: The Sentinel data policy, established by ESA and COM, provides full and open access to the data.
faq_en.q: Can I access Sentinel-3 data?

faq_en.a: Sentinel-3C is expected to launch in October 2024 and Sentinel-3B in November 2028. This is designed to coincide (with the addition of some extra time in case issues arise) with the end of life of Sentinel-3A and 3B, expected in 2026 and 2028 respectively.
faq_en.q: When will Sentinel-3C and Sentinel-3D be launched?

faq_en.a: Whilst Sentinel-3 is focused on measuring sea-surface topography, and the colour and temperature of ocean and land surfaces; other Sentinel missions are concerned with radar mapping of land and sea, monitoring air quality, and providing optical observations of land and coastlines.
faq_en.q: How does Sentinel-3 differ from other Sentinel missions?

eoportal.name: Copernicus: Sentinel-4
taxonomy: Cloud type, amount and cloud top temperature
eoportal.summary: The instruments will be hosted onboard the Meteosat Third Generation-Sounder (MTG-S) platforms, with Sentinel-4A on MTG-S1, and Sentinel-4B on MTG-S2.

faq.a: The aim of the Sentinel-4 mission is to provide measurements of the diurnal cycle of the tropospheric composition over Europe and North Africa. It is able to monitor the daily cycle as it is in a geostationary orbit locked above Europe.
faq.q: What is the aim of the Sentinel-4 mission?

faq.a: Sentinel-4’s UVN (Ultraviolet-Visible-Near-Infrared) spectrometer images in three spectrometric bands: Ultraviolet (305 - 400 nm), Visible (400 - 500 nm) and near infrared (750 - 775 nm).
faq.q: What range of wavelengths does the Sentinel-4 images in?

eoportal.agency_zh: ESA（欧空局）、EUMETSAT（欧洲气象卫星开发组织）、COM
eoportal.summary_zh: 该仪器将搭载于第三代气象卫星探测器（Meteosat Third Generation-Sounder, MTG-S）平台上，哨兵-4A搭载于MTG-S1，哨兵-4B搭载于MTG-S2。
eoportal.summary_en: The instruments will be hosted onboard the Meteosat Third Generation-Sounder (MTG-S) platforms, with Sentinel-4A on MTG-S1, and Sentinel-4B on MTG-S2.

faq_zh.a: 哨兵-4任务的目标是提供对欧洲和北非上空对流层成分昼夜变化的测量。由于其位于锁定在欧洲上空的地球静止轨道上，它能够监测该成分的日变化周期。
faq_zh.q: 哨兵-4任务的目标是什么？

faq_zh.a: 哨兵-4的UVN（紫外-可见光-近红外）光谱仪在三个光谱波段成像：紫外（305 - 400纳米）、可见光（400 - 500纳米）和近红外（750 - 775纳米）。
faq_zh.q: 哨兵-4的成像波长范围是多少？

faq_en.a: The aim of the Sentinel-4 mission is to provide measurements of the diurnal cycle of the tropospheric composition over Europe and North Africa. It is able to monitor the daily cycle as it is in a geostationary orbit locked above Europe.
faq_en.q: What is the aim of the Sentinel-4 mission?

faq_en.a: Sentinel-4’s UVN (Ultraviolet-Visible-Near-Infrared) spectrometer images in three spectrometric bands: Ultraviolet (305 - 400 nm), Visible (400 - 500 nm) and near infrared (750 - 775 nm).
faq_en.q: What range of wavelengths does the Sentinel-4 images in?

eoportal.name: Copernicus: Sentinel-5
taxonomy: Cloud type, amount and cloud top temperature
Downward short-wave irradiance at Earth surface
eoportal.summary: The instrument, launched in August 2025, it is jointly operated by the European Space Agency (ESA) and the European Commission (COM).

faq.a: The Sentinel-5 program consists of a single instrument, the Sentinel 5 UNVS (Ultraviolet/Visible/Near Infrared/SWIR), a passive pushbroom spectrometer developed by Airbus Defence and Space.
faq.q: What is Sentinel-5?

faq.a: Sentinel-5 is part of the European Copernicus program (formerly the Global Monitoring for Environment and Security program (GMES)) and is operated by the European Space Agency and the European Commission.
faq.q: Which organisation operates Sentinel-5?

faq.a: Sentinel-5 has a maximum spectral resolution of 0.25 nm, and a minimum spectral resolution of 1 nm.
faq.q: What is the spectral resolution of Copernicus mission Sentinel-5?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该仪器于2025年8月发射，由欧洲航天局（ESA）和欧洲委员会（COM）联合运营。
eoportal.summary_en: The instrument, launched in August 2025, it is jointly operated by the European Space Agency (ESA) and the European Commission (COM).

faq_zh.a: 哨兵-5计划包含一个单一仪器，即哨兵-5 UNVS（紫外/可见光/近红外/短波红外），这是一款由空客防务与航天公司开发的被动式推扫式光谱仪。
哨兵-5是欧洲哥白尼计划（前身为全球环境与安全监测计划 GMES）的一部分，由欧洲航天局和欧洲委员会运营。
哨兵-5的最大光谱分辨率为0.25纳米，最小光谱分辨率为1纳米。
faq_zh.q: 哥白尼任务哨兵-5的光谱分辨率是多少？

faq_en.a: The Sentinel-5 program consists of a single instrument, the Sentinel 5 UNVS (Ultraviolet/Visible/Near Infrared/SWIR), a passive pushbroom spectrometer developed by Airbus Defence and Space.
faq_en.q: What is Sentinel-5?

faq_en.a: Sentinel-5 is part of the European Copernicus program (formerly the Global Monitoring for Environment and Security program (GMES)) and is operated by the European Space Agency and the European Commission.
faq_en.q: Which organisation operates Sentinel-5?

faq_en.a: Sentinel-5 has a maximum spectral resolution of 0.25 nm, and a minimum spectral resolution of 1 nm.
faq_en.q: What is the spectral resolution of Copernicus mission Sentinel-5?

eoportal.name: Copernicus: Sentinel-5P
taxonomy: Cloud type, amount and cloud top temperature
Downward short-wave irradiance at Earth surface
eoportal.summary: The satellite launched in October 2017.

faq.a: Yes, Sentinel-5P is operational. It was launched in 2017 and has a design life of 7 years.
faq.q: Is Sentinel-5P currently operational?

faq.a: Yes, Sentinel-5p has a ground segment that is broken into three different segments: the core ground segment, the collaborative ground segment and the Copernicus missions’ ground segment.
faq.q: Does Sentinel-5P have a ground segment?

faq.a: The Sentinel-5P satellite records data via its instrument the Tropospheric Monitoring Instrument (TROPOMI) which is a high-resolution nadir-scanning spectrometer.
faq.q: How does Sentinel-5P record its data?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该卫星于2017年10月发射。
eoportal.summary_en: The satellite launched in October 2017.

faq_zh.a: 是的，哨兵-5P卫星正在运行。它于2017年发射，设计寿命为7年。
faq_zh.q: 哨兵-5P卫星目前是否在运行？

faq_zh.a: 是的，哨兵-5P卫星拥有一个地面段，它被分为三个不同的部分：核心地面段、协作地面段和哥白尼任务地面段。
faq_zh.q: 哨兵-5P卫星是否有地面段？

faq_zh.a: 哨兵-5P卫星通过其搭载的仪器——对流层监测仪（TROPOMI）记录数据，这是一种高分辨率天底观测光谱仪。
faq_zh.q: 哨兵-5P卫星是如何记录数据的？

faq_en.a: Yes, Sentinel-5P is operational. It was launched in 2017 and has a design life of 7 years.
faq_en.q: Is Sentinel-5P currently operational?

faq_en.a: Yes, Sentinel-5p has a ground segment that is broken into three different segments: the core ground segment, the collaborative ground segment and the Copernicus missions’ ground segment.
faq_en.q: Does Sentinel-5P have a ground segment?

faq_en.a: The Sentinel-5P satellite records data via its instrument the Tropospheric Monitoring Instrument (TROPOMI) which is a high-resolution nadir-scanning spectrometer.
faq_en.q: How does Sentinel-5P record its data?

eoportal.name: Copernicus: Sentinel-6
eoportal.summary: A joint ESA, EUMETSAT, NASA, and NOAA venture under the Copernicus programme, it includes Sentinel-6A Michael Freilich launched in November 2020, and Sentinel-6B launched in November 2025, providing continuous high-precision measurements of sea-surface height, as well as troposphere and stratosphere temperature and humidity profiles.

faq.a: The main purpose of Sentinel-6 is to measure sea-surface heights of the ocean. This is a key component to understanding how Earth’s climate is changing.
faq.q: What is the purpose of Sentinel-6?

faq.a: The Sentinel-6 satellite was built by Airbus Defence and Space in Friedrichshafen, Germany. It was commissioned by the European Space Agency (ESA),  the European Organisation for the Exploitation of Meteorological Satellites (EUMETSAT), the National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA), and the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA).
faq.q: Who built Sentinel-6?

faq.a: The first Sentinel-6 satellite, Sentinel-6A Michael Freilich launched onboard a SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket on November 21, 2020 from Space Launch Complex 4 at Vandenberg Air Force Base in California. A continuation of this mission, Sentinel-6B, was launched on November 17, 2025.
faq.q: When was the first Sentinel-6 satellite launched?

eoportal.summary_zh: 哨兵-6 是 ESA、EUMETSAT、NASA 和 NOAA 在哥白尼计划下的联合项目，包括 2020 年 11 月发射的哨兵-6A Michael Freilich 和 2025 年 11 月发射的哨兵-6B，提供连续的高精度海面高度测量，以及对流层和平流层温湿廓线数据。
eoportal.summary_en: A joint ESA, EUMETSAT, NASA, and NOAA venture under the Copernicus programme, it includes Sentinel-6A Michael Freilich launched in November 2020, and Sentinel-6B launched in November 2025, providing continuous high-precision measurements of sea-surface height, as well as troposphere and stratosphere temperature and humidity profiles.

faq_zh.a: 哨兵-6 的主要用途是测量海洋的海面高度。这是理解地球气候变化的关键环节。
faq_zh.q: 哨兵-6 的主要用途是什么？

faq_zh.a: 哨兵-6 卫星由位于德国腓特烈港的 Airbus Defence and Space 公司建造。它受欧洲空间局（ESA）、欧洲气象卫星应用组织（EUMETSAT）、美国国家航空航天局（NASA）和美国国家海洋和大气管理局（NOAA）委托制造。
faq_zh.q: 谁建造了哨兵-6 卫星？

faq_zh.a: 第一颗哨兵-6 卫星，即哨兵-6A Michael Freilich，于 2020 年 11 月 21 日搭载 SpaceX Falcon 9 火箭从加利福尼亚范登堡空军基地的 4 号航天发射场发射升空。该任务的延续型号哨兵-6B 则于 2025 年 11 月 17 日发射。
faq_zh.q: 第一颗哨兵-6 卫星是什么时候发射的？

faq_en.a: The main purpose of Sentinel-6 is to measure sea-surface heights of the ocean. This is a key component to understanding how Earth’s climate is changing.
faq_en.q: What is the purpose of Sentinel-6?

faq_en.a: The Sentinel-6 satellite was built by Airbus Defence and Space in Friedrichshafen, Germany. It was commissioned by the European Space Agency (ESA),  the European Organisation for the Exploitation of Meteorological Satellites (EUMETSAT), the National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA), and the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA).
faq_en.q: Who built Sentinel-6?

faq_en.a: The first Sentinel-6 satellite, Sentinel-6A Michael Freilich launched onboard a SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket on November 21, 2020 from Space Launch Complex 4 at Vandenberg Air Force Base in California. A continuation of this mission, Sentinel-6B, was launched on November 17, 2025.
faq_en.q: When was the first Sentinel-6 satellite launched?

eoportal.name: COSMO-SkyMed
taxonomy: Sea ice cover, edge and thickness
eoportal.summary: Starting in 2018, COSMO-SkyMed Second Generation (CSG) has provided continuity for COSMO-SkyMed.

faq.a: The first COSMO-SkyMed second generation satellite was launched in December 2019, followed by the second satellite launched in January 2022. An additional two satellites are to be added to complete the constellation, with launch dates set for 2024 and 2025.
faq.q: What is the COSMO-SkyMed second generation launch date?

faq.a: The COSMO-SkyMed mission is a 4 spacecraft constellation conceived by the ASI (Agenzia Spaziale Italiana) and funded by the Italian Ministry of Defence and the Italian Ministry of Research. It provides Earth observation data used for military, research and commercial purposes.
faq.q: What is the COSMO-SkyMed mission?

faq.a: COSMO-SkyMed satellites allow for multimodal acquisition. The ‘Spotlight’ operational mode has a spatial resolution of  less than 1m, whereas the ‘Stripmap’ mode has a spatial resolution of 3-15m. The two ScanSAR modes (Wideregion and Hugeregion) have a lower spatial resolution of 30m and 100m respectively.
faq.q: What is the resolution of COSMO-SkyMed spacecraft?

eoportal.agency_zh: 意大利国防部 (MoD)，意大利航天局 (ASI)
eoportal.summary_zh: 自2018年起，COSMO-SkyMed第二代（CSG）卫星为第一代COSMO-SkyMed星座提供了任务延续。
eoportal.summary_en: Starting in 2018, COSMO-SkyMed Second Generation (CSG) has provided continuity for COSMO-SkyMed.

faq_zh.a: 首颗COSMO-SkyMed第二代卫星于2019年12月发射，第二颗于2022年1月发射。另外两颗卫星计划于2024年和2025年发射，以完成整个星座组网。
faq_zh.q: COSMO-SkyMed第二代卫星的发射日期是什么时候？

faq_zh.a: COSMO-SkyMed任务是由意大利航天局（ASI）构想，并由意大利国防部和意大利研究部资助的4颗卫星星座。它提供的地球观测数据用于军事、科研和商业目的。
COSMO-SkyMed卫星支持多种成像模式。其中，“聚束”工作模式空间分辨率优于1米，“条带”模式空间分辨率为3-15米。两种扫描SAR模式（宽幅区域和超宽幅区域）的空间分辨率较低，分别为30米和100米。

faq_en.a: The first COSMO-SkyMed second generation satellite was launched in December 2019, followed by the second satellite launched in January 2022. An additional two satellites are to be added to complete the constellation, with launch dates set for 2024 and 2025.
faq_en.q: What is the COSMO-SkyMed second generation launch date?

faq_en.a: The COSMO-SkyMed mission is a 4 spacecraft constellation conceived by the ASI (Agenzia Spaziale Italiana) and funded by the Italian Ministry of Defence and the Italian Ministry of Research. It provides Earth observation data used for military, research and commercial purposes.
faq_en.q: What is the COSMO-SkyMed mission?

faq_en.a: COSMO-SkyMed satellites allow for multimodal acquisition. The ‘Spotlight’ operational mode has a spatial resolution of  less than 1m, whereas the ‘Stripmap’ mode has a spatial resolution of 3-15m. The two ScanSAR modes (Wideregion and Hugeregion) have a lower spatial resolution of 30m and 100m respectively.
faq_en.q: What is the resolution of COSMO-SkyMed spacecraft?

faq.a: The aim of the COSMO-SkyMed Second Generation is to enhance the capabilities of the first generation constellation. Its implementation grants greater operative versatility, imaging quality and performance capabilities, leading to a greater variety of user applications.
faq.q: Why was the second generation of the COSMO-SkyMed developed?

faq.a: The first satellite of the COSMO-SkyMed Second Generation was launched in December 2019 and the second satellite was launched in January 2022. The remaining two satellites are due to be launched in February 2024 and February 2025.

eoportal.name: COSPAS-SARSAT
eoportal.summary: There are currently 62 operational satellites within the program.

faq.a: COSPAS translates to Space System for the Search of Distressed Vessels, while SARSAT stands for Search and Rescue Satellite Aided Tracking System.
faq.q: What does COSPAS-SARSAT stand for?

faq.a: COSPAS-SARSAT is an intergovernmental and internationally operated series of satellites for the location of associated emergency beacons to aid in and initiate search and rescue operations.
faq.q: What does COSPAS-SARSAT do?

faq.a: The COSPAS-SARSAT is an intergovernmental initiative first started by Canada, France, the United States and the Soviet Union, but now has 45 participant nations.

COSPAS-SARSAT是一项由加拿大、法国、美国和苏联最初发起的政府间倡议，目前已有45个参与国。

eoportal.name: CryoSat (Earth Explorer Opportunity Mission-1)
taxonomy: Sea ice cover, edge and thickness
eoportal.summary: CryoSat was supposed to be the first Earth Explorer Opportunity mission in ESA’s Living Planet program, but was lost in a launch failure on 8 October 2005, prompting the development of a successor mission, CryoSat-2.

faq.a: CryoSat was owned and operated by ESA, as the first Earth Explorer Opportunity Mission in its Living Planet Program.
faq.q: Who owned CryoSat?

faq.a: CryoSat was lost in a launch failure on 8 October 2005, prompting the development of a successor mission, CryoSat-2.
faq.q: What happened to CryoSat?

faq.a: The CryoSat sensor complement consisted of three instruments, the Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) Interferometer Radar Altimeter (SIRAL) (a Ku-band radar altimeter), the Doppler Orbitography and Radio Positioning Integration by Satellite (DORIS) instrument, which would have measured the Doppler frequency shifts of both VHF and S-Band signals transmitted by ground beacons, and a Laser Retroreflector (LRR) (a passive optical sensor to be used as a secondary method of orbit determination).
faq.q: What instruments did CryoSat carry?

eoportal.summary_zh: CryoSat 卫星本应是 ESA“生命行星计划”中的首个地球探索者机遇任务，但在2005年10月8日发射失败中丢失，这促使其后续任务 CryoSat-2 的研发。
eoportal.summary_en: CryoSat was supposed to be the first Earth Explorer Opportunity mission in ESA’s Living Planet program, but was lost in a launch failure on 8 October 2005, prompting the development of a successor mission, CryoSat-2.

faq_zh.a: CryoSat 由 ESA 拥有和运营，是其“生命行星计划”中的首个地球探索者机遇任务。
faq_zh.q: CryoSat 卫星由谁拥有和运营？

faq_zh.a: CryoSat 在2005年10月8日的发射失败中丢失，这促使其后续任务 CryoSat-2 的研发。
CryoSat 的传感器套件包含三台仪器：合成孔径雷达干涉雷达高度计（SIRAL，一台 Ku 波段雷达高度计）、通过卫星进行多普勒定轨和无线电定位集成仪器（DORIS，用于测量地面信标发射的 VHF 和 S 波段信号的多普勒频移）以及激光后向反射器（LRR，一种被动光学传感器，作为轨道测定的辅助方法）。

faq_en.a: CryoSat was owned and operated by ESA, as the first Earth Explorer Opportunity Mission in its Living Planet Program.
faq_en.q: Who owned CryoSat?

faq_en.a: CryoSat was lost in a launch failure on 8 October 2005, prompting the development of a successor mission, CryoSat-2.
faq_en.q: What happened to CryoSat?

faq_en.a: The CryoSat sensor complement consisted of three instruments, the Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) Interferometer Radar Altimeter (SIRAL) (a Ku-band radar altimeter), the Doppler Orbitography and Radio Positioning Integration by Satellite (DORIS) instrument, which would have measured the Doppler frequency shifts of both VHF and S-Band signals transmitted by ground beacons, and a Laser Retroreflector (LRR) (a passive optical sensor to be used as a secondary method of orbit determination).
faq_en.q: What instruments did CryoSat carry?

eoportal.name: CryoSat-2 (Earth Explorer Opportunity Mission-2)
taxonomy: Sea ice cover, edge and thickness
eoportal.summary: It monitors changes in polar ice sheets.

faq.a: CryoSat-2 measures land and sea ice sheet thickness to help explain the connection between the melting of polar ice and the rise in sea levels.
faq.q: What does CryoSat-2 measure?

faq.a: CryoSat-2 was launched in April 2010 from the Baikonur Cosmodrome in Kazakhstan aboard a Dnepr launch vehicle provided by the International Space Company Kosmotras.
faq.q: What were the launch details of CryoSat-2?

faq.a: ESA’s Earth Explorer Missions are a set of missions dedicated to addressing major challenges identified by society. Five missions have been or are in operation (GOCE, SMOS, CryoSat, Swarm, Aeolus) and five further missions are approved for development  (EarthCARE, Biomass, FLEX, FORUM, Harmony).
faq.q: What are ESA’s Earth Explorer Missions?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该卫星用于监测极地冰盖的变化。

CryoSat-2 于 2010 年 4 月从哈萨克斯坦拜科努尔航天发射场，搭乘由国际航天公司 Kosmotras 提供的第聂伯运载火箭发射升空。
faq_zh.q: CryoSat-2 的发射细节是什么？

faq_zh.q: ESA 的地球探测者任务是什么？

eoportal.name: CSES (China Seismo-Electromagnetic Satellite) / Zhangheng
taxonomy: Electron Energy and Pitch Angle Distribution
eoportal.summary: The first satellite, CSES-1, was launched in February 2018, while CSES-02 was launched in June 2025.

faq.a: The ionosphere exists in the upper radius of Earth’s atmosphere and interacts with the magnetosphere. It is named after the presence of ionised particles that result from solar radiation. The CSES mission studies the lithosphere-atmosphere-ionosphere pairing due to its under-exploited influence upon Earth’s seismic activities. Patterns of ionosphere/lithosphere disturbances have previously been observed during and after earthquake activity, and thus it is theorised that improved monitoring of the charged particles in these regions can aid in preparation and response to earthquakes.
faq.q: Why does the CSES mission study the ionosphere?

faq.a: CSES-02 was launched in June 2025 to complement the already present CSES-01, decreasing net revisit time and improving the quality of data gathered. With both satellites orbiting out of phase by 180 degrees, the constellation’s revisit time has been reduced from 5 to 2.5 days.
faq.q: Has the CSES-02 mission replaced the CSES-01 mission?

faq.a: CSES-01 houses additional instruments from CSES-01: the Medium Energy Electron Detector (MEED) and the Ionospheric Photometer (IP). MEED is a dedicated particle detector covering electrons, identifying their charge, orientation and particle type. The existing instruments also have improved accuracy and sensitivity to fluctuations of their respective measurement.
faq.q: What are the main differences between CSES-01 and CSES-02?

eoportal.name_zh: 张衡（CSES，中国地震电磁监测试验卫星）
eoportal.agency_zh: CNSA（中国国家航天局）、ASI（意大利航天局）
eoportal.summary_zh: 首颗卫星CSES-1于2018年2月发射升空，而CSES-02于2025年6月发射。
eoportal.summary_en: The first satellite, CSES-1, was launched in February 2018, while CSES-02 was launched in June 2025.

faq_zh.a: 电离层位于地球大气层上部，与磁层相互作用，因太阳辐射产生的电离粒子而得名。CSES任务研究岩石圈-大气层-电离层耦合体系，因其对地球地震活动的影响尚未得到充分探索。此前已在地震活动期间及之后观测到电离层/岩石圈扰动模式，因此理论上认为，加强对这些区域带电粒子的监测有助于地震的预防与应急响应。
CSES-02于2025年6月发射，旨在补充已在轨的CSES-01，缩短重访周期并提升数据质量。两颗卫星以180度相位差运行，星座的重访时间已从5天缩短至2.5天。
CSES-02搭载了CSES-01所没有的附加仪器：中能电子探测器（MEED）和电离层光度计（IP）。MEED是专用的粒子探测器，用于识别电子的电荷、方向和粒子类型。此外，CSES-02上原有仪器的测量精度和对波动的敏感性也得到了提升。

faq_en.a: The ionosphere exists in the upper radius of Earth’s atmosphere and interacts with the magnetosphere. It is named after the presence of ionised particles that result from solar radiation. The CSES mission studies the lithosphere-atmosphere-ionosphere pairing due to its under-exploited influence upon Earth’s seismic activities. Patterns of ionosphere/lithosphere disturbances have previously been observed during and after earthquake activity, and thus it is theorised that improved monitoring of the charged particles in these regions can aid in preparation and response to earthquakes.
faq_en.q: Why does the CSES mission study the ionosphere?

faq_en.a: CSES-02 was launched in June 2025 to complement the already present CSES-01, decreasing net revisit time and improving the quality of data gathered. With both satellites orbiting out of phase by 180 degrees, the constellation’s revisit time has been reduced from 5 to 2.5 days.
faq_en.q: Has the CSES-02 mission replaced the CSES-01 mission?

faq_en.a: CSES-01 houses additional instruments from CSES-01: the Medium Energy Electron Detector (MEED) and the Ionospheric Photometer (IP). MEED is a dedicated particle detector covering electrons, identifying their charge, orientation and particle type. The existing instruments also have improved accuracy and sensitivity to fluctuations of their respective measurement.
faq_en.q: What are the main differences between CSES-01 and CSES-02?

eoportal.name: CYGNSS (Cyclone Global Navigation Satellite System)
eoportal.summary: It aims to determine how  tropical cyclones form and evolve.

faq.a: GPS satellites reflect radio signals off the ocean surface towards CYGNSS satellites. When wind speeds are high, the ocean becomes rougher which scatters the signal and makes the received signal much weaker than the transmitted signal. The exact ocean surface wind speed is calculated from the received signal strength by the CYGNSS wind speed retrieval algorithm.
faq.q: How does CYGNSS measure ocean surface wind speed?

faq.a: Eight satellites make up CYGNSS. They follow the same orbit with each pair being separated by 12 minutes.
faq.q: How many satellites make up CYGNSS?

faq.a: In 2019, CYGNSS mission was extended to 2023. It is expected to be extended once more, to 2026.
faq.q: What is the expected end of life date of CYGNSS?

eoportal.name_zh: CYGNSS（飓风全球导航卫星系统）
eoportal.summary_zh: 该任务旨在探究热带气旋的形成与演变过程。
eoportal.summary_en: It aims to determine how  tropical cyclones form and evolve.

faq_zh.a: GPS卫星将无线电信号从海面反射至CYGNSS卫星。当风速较高时，海面变得粗糙，导致信号散射，使得接收信号远弱于发射信号。CYGNSS风速反演算法通过接收到的信号强度来精确计算海面风速。
CYGNSS由八颗卫星组成。它们遵循相同的轨道，每对卫星间隔12分钟。
2019年，CYGNSS任务被延长至2023年。预计还将再次延长至2026年。
faq_zh.q: CYGNSS的预期任务结束日期是什么时候？

faq_en.a: GPS satellites reflect radio signals off the ocean surface towards CYGNSS satellites. When wind speeds are high, the ocean becomes rougher which scatters the signal and makes the received signal much weaker than the transmitted signal. The exact ocean surface wind speed is calculated from the received signal strength by the CYGNSS wind speed retrieval algorithm.
faq_en.q: How does CYGNSS measure ocean surface wind speed?

faq_en.a: Eight satellites make up CYGNSS. They follow the same orbit with each pair being separated by 12 minutes.

eoportal.name: Daqi-1/-2 (DQ-1/-2)
eoportal.summary: Data transmission uses a 1553B + Space Wire interface, and the power system provides 12-20 kW of power to the payloads.

faq.a: The DQ-1 and DQ-2 satellites aim to monitor the Earth's atmospheric environment by measuring aerosols, clouds, and greenhouse gases such as carbon dioxide and methane. The missions provide data for studies of air quality, climate change, and atmospheric composition.
faq.q: What is the purpose of the DQ-1 and DQ-2 satellites?

faq.a: The DQ-1 satellite was launched on April 15, 2022, aboard a Long March 4C rocket from the Taiyuan Satellite Launch Centre.
faq.q: When was DQ-1 launched?

faq.a: The DQ-1 and DQ-2 satellites carry multiple instruments. DQ-1 carries the Aerosol and Carbon Detection Lidar (ACDL), Directional Polarisation Camera-2 (DPC-2), Particulate Observing Scanning Polarimeter (POSP), Environment Monitoring Instrument-2 (EMI-2), while DQ-2 will carry the Greenhouse-gases Absorption Spectrometer-2 (GAS-2) and ACDL.
faq.q: What instruments are carried onboard the DQ satellites?

eoportal.summary_zh: 数据传输采用1553B + SpaceWire接口，电源系统为有效载荷提供12至20千瓦的电力。
eoportal.summary_en: Data transmission uses a 1553B + Space Wire interface, and the power system provides 12-20 kW of power to the payloads.

faq_zh.a: DQ-1和DQ-2卫星旨在通过测量气溶胶、云和温室气体（如二氧化碳和甲烷）来监测地球大气环境。这些任务为研究空气质量、气候变化和大气成分提供数据。
DQ-1卫星于2022年4月15日搭载长征四号丙运载火箭从太原卫星发射中心发射升空。
DQ-1和DQ-2卫星搭载了多种仪器。DQ-1搭载了气溶胶和碳探测激光雷达（ACDL）、多角度偏振成像仪-2（DPC-2）、颗粒物观测扫描偏振仪（POSP）和环境监测仪-2（EMI-2），而DQ-2将搭载温室气体吸收光谱仪-2（GAS-2）和ACDL。
faq_zh.q: DQ系列卫星搭载了哪些仪器？

faq_en.a: The DQ-1 and DQ-2 satellites aim to monitor the Earth's atmospheric environment by measuring aerosols, clouds, and greenhouse gases such as carbon dioxide and methane. The missions provide data for studies of air quality, climate change, and atmospheric composition.
faq_en.q: What is the purpose of the DQ-1 and DQ-2 satellites?

faq_en.a: The DQ-1 satellite was launched on April 15, 2022, aboard a Long March 4C rocket from the Taiyuan Satellite Launch Centre.
faq_en.q: When was DQ-1 launched?

faq_en.a: The DQ-1 and DQ-2 satellites carry multiple instruments. DQ-1 carries the Aerosol and Carbon Detection Lidar (ACDL), Directional Polarisation Camera-2 (DPC-2), Particulate Observing Scanning Polarimeter (POSP), Environment Monitoring Instrument-2 (EMI-2), while DQ-2 will carry the Greenhouse-gases Absorption Spectrometer-2 (GAS-2) and ACDL.
faq_en.q: What instruments are carried onboard the DQ satellites?

eoportal.name: DarkCarb
eoportal.summary:

EO

SSTL

DarkCarb is a commercial Earth observation (EO) satellite developed by Surrey Satellite Technology Ltd (SSTL) to collect high resolution Mid-Wave Infrared (MWIR) imagery and videos from low Earth orbit, supporting applications such as urban heat mapping, disaster response, and defence and security.

faq.a: DarkCarb is a commercial Earth observation mission developed by SSTL to demonstrate high-resolution MWIR imaging and video from Low Earth Orbit.
faq.q: What is DarkCarb?

faq.a: The mission was designed and built by SSTL, a UK-based small satellite manufacturer and operator established in 1985. SSTL is now a wholly owned subsidiary of Airbus Defence and Space and is well known for pioneering affordable, rapid-deployment small satellites.
faq.q: Who developed DarkCarb?

faq.a: DarkCarb carries a MWIR imager with a cooled Mercury Cadmium Telluride (MCT) detector, capable of 4 m ground sampling distance (GSD) from a 500 km orbit. The instrument can capture both still images and video, allowing observation of rapidly changing events day or night.
faq.q: What is the primary payload onboard DarkCarb?

eoportal.summary_zh: DarkCarb 是由萨里卫星技术有限公司 (SSTL) 开发的一颗商业地球观测卫星，旨在从低地球轨道收集高分辨率的中波红外 (MWIR) 图像和视频，支持城市热图绘制、灾害响应以及国防与安全等应用。

SSTL

DarkCarb is a commercial Earth observation (EO) satellite developed by Surrey Satellite Technology Ltd (SSTL) to collect high resolution Mid-Wave Infrared (MWIR) imagery and videos from low Earth orbit, supporting applications such as urban heat mapping, disaster response, and defence and security.

faq_zh.a: DarkCarb 是 SSTL 开发的一项商业地球观测任务，旨在演示从低地球轨道进行高分辨率中波红外成像和视频拍摄的能力。
该任务由英国的小型卫星制造商和运营商 SSTL 设计并建造。SSTL 成立于 1985 年，现为空客防务与航天公司的全资子公司，以其在经济实惠、快速部署的小型卫星领域的开创性工作而闻名。
DarkCarb 携带一台配备冷却型碲镉汞 (MCT) 探测器的中波红外成像仪，在 500 公里轨道上可实现 4 米的地面采样距离 (GSD)。该仪器可拍摄静态图像和视频，无论昼夜均可观测快速变化的事件。

faq_en.q: What is DarkCarb?

faq_en.a: DarkCarb carries a MWIR imager with a cooled Mercury Cadmium Telluride (MCT) detector, capable of 4 m ground sampling distance (GSD) from a 500 km orbit. The instrument can capture both still images and video, allowing observation of rapidly changing events day or night.

Non-EO

NASA’s mission DART (Double Asteroid Redirection Test), which was launched on November 24, 2021, was the first-ever mission dedicated to investigating and demonstrating one method of asteroid deflection by changing an asteroid’s motion in space through kinetic impact.
eoportal.summary_zh: NASA的DART（双小行星重定向测试）任务于2021年11月24日发射，是首个致力于研究和演示小行星偏转方法的任务，该方法通过动能撞击改变小行星在空间中的运动轨迹。

eoportal.name: Deimos-1/GEOSAT-1
eoportal.summary: The satellite was launched in July 2009 and joined 3 other existing satellites in the second generation of DMC satellites (DMC-2G), providing commercial imaging to support rapid-response, large-area mapping for a range of applications.

faq.a: There are four satellites in DMC-2G, including Deimos-1 (launched July 2009), UK-DMC-2 (July 2009), NigeriaSat-2 (August 2011) and NigeriaSat-X (August 2011).
faq.q: How many satellites are in the second generation DMC constellation?

faq.a: Deimos-1 has a wide range of applications, including  rapid-response disaster monitoring, and land and vegetation cover research.
faq.q: What is Deimos-1 used for?

faq.a: Deimos-1 (now GEOSAT-1) was launched in July 2009.
faq.q: When was Deimos-1 (GEOSAT-1) launched?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该卫星于2009年7月发射，加入了DMC第二代星座（DMC-2G）中已有的3颗卫星行列，提供商业成像服务，以支持快速响应和大范围测绘，适用于多种应用领域。
eoportal.summary_en: The satellite was launched in July 2009 and joined 3 other existing satellites in the second generation of DMC satellites (DMC-2G), providing commercial imaging to support rapid-response, large-area mapping for a range of applications.

faq_zh.a: DMC-2G包含四颗卫星，分别是Deimos-1（2009年7月发射）、UK-DMC-2（2009年7月）、NigeriaSat-2（2011年8月）和NigeriaSat-X（2011年8月）。
faq_zh.q: 第二代DMC星座包含多少颗卫星？

faq_zh.a: Deimos-1的应用范围广泛，包括快速响应灾害监测以及土地和植被覆盖研究。
Deimos-1（现称GEOSAT-1）于2009年7月发射。

faq_en.q: How many satellites are in the second generation DMC constellation?

faq_en.a: Deimos-1 has a wide range of applications, including  rapid-response disaster monitoring, and land and vegetation cover research.

eoportal.name: Deimos-2/GeoSat-2
eoportal.summary: In 2021, the company GEOSAT acquired Deimos-1 & 2, and renamed them to GEOSAT-1 & 2, respectively.

faq.a: The satellite functions as a multi-purpose land imager that has specific applications in the measurement of land processes such as biomass, land cover and vegetation properties.
faq.q: What is the purpose of Deimos-2?

faq.a: The central instrument for the Deimos-2 mission is the High Resolution Advanced Imaging System (HiRAS) which is a pushbroom type imager that conducts vegetation measurements.
faq.q: What kind of instrument does Deimos-2 have?

faq.a: Deimos-2 was launched in June 2019 from the Yasny Cosmodrome of the Dombarovsky region in Russia.
faq.q: When and where did Deimos-2 launch?

eoportal.summary_zh: 2021年，GEOSAT公司收购了Deimos-1与Deimos-2卫星，并将其分别更名为GEOSAT-1与GEOSAT-2。
eoportal.summary_en: In 2021, the company GEOSAT acquired Deimos-1 & 2, and renamed them to GEOSAT-1 & 2, respectively.

faq_zh.a: 该卫星是一颗多用途陆地成像卫星，专门应用于测量生物质、土地覆盖和植被特性等陆地过程。
Deimos-2任务的核心仪器是高分辨率先进成像系统（HiRAS），这是一款推扫式成像仪，用于进行植被测量。
faq_zh.q: Deimos-2搭载了什么类型的仪器？

faq_zh.a: Deimos-2卫星于2019年6月从俄罗斯Dombarovsky地区的Yasny航天发射场发射升空。
faq_zh.q: Deimos-2卫星是何时何地发射的？

faq_en.a: The satellite functions as a multi-purpose land imager that has specific applications in the measurement of land processes such as biomass, land cover and vegetation properties.
faq_en.q: What is the purpose of Deimos-2?

faq_en.a: The central instrument for the Deimos-2 mission is the High Resolution Advanced Imaging System (HiRAS) which is a pushbroom type imager that conducts vegetation measurements.
faq_en.q: What kind of instrument does Deimos-2 have?

faq_en.a: Deimos-2 was launched in June 2019 from the Yasny Cosmodrome of the Dombarovsky region in Russia.
faq_en.q: When and where did Deimos-2 launch?

eoportal.name: Dellingr CubeSat Demonstration Mission
eoportal.summary: The instruments being tested focus on measurements of the Earth’s magnetosphere and composition of the Earth’s atmosphere.

faq.a: Dellingr is operated by NASA and uses the Wallops Flight Facility UHF ground station and Goddard Space Flight Center MOC (Mission Operation Centre).
faq.q: Who operates Dellingr?

faq.a: Dellingr is used for measurements of the Earth’s magnetic field and of the composition of the Earth’s atmosphere. Its main purpose, however, was to test processes and technologies for producing efficient, low-cost satellites.
faq.q: What is the use of Dellingr?

faq.a: CubeSats are small and low-cost, and can be used to test new instruments, technologies, and materials for their suitability for integration into more complex space missions.
faq.q: What does the CubeSat do?

eoportal.summary_zh: 正在测试的仪器专注于测量地球磁层和大气成分。
eoportal.summary_en: The instruments being tested focus on measurements of the Earth’s magnetosphere and composition of the Earth’s atmosphere.

faq_zh.a: Dellingr 由 NASA 运营，并使用 Wallops Flight Facility 的 UHF 地面站和 Goddard Space Flight Center 的任务运行中心（MOC）。
Dellingr 用于测量地球磁场和大气成分。然而，其主要目的是测试制造高效、低成本卫星的流程和技术。
立方星体积小、成本低，可用于测试新仪器、技术和材料，以评估其是否适合集成到更复杂的太空任务中。

faq_en.a: Dellingr is operated by NASA and uses the Wallops Flight Facility UHF ground station and Goddard Space Flight Center MOC (Mission Operation Centre).
faq_en.q: Who operates Dellingr?

faq_en.a: Dellingr is used for measurements of the Earth’s magnetic field and of the composition of the Earth’s atmosphere. Its main purpose, however, was to test processes and technologies for producing efficient, low-cost satellites.
faq_en.q: What is the use of Dellingr?

faq_en.a: CubeSats are small and low-cost, and can be used to test new instruments, technologies, and materials for their suitability for integration into more complex space missions.
faq_en.q: What does the CubeSat do?

eoportal.name: Deloitte-1
eoportal.summary: The satellite functions as an on-orbit cyber security environment to test and evaluate Deloitte’s Cyber Intrusion Detection System (IDS) against real-world and simulated threats.

faq.a: The Deloitte-1 mission's primary purpose is to provide an on-orbit cyber security testbed to validate Deloitte’s Silent Shield Cyber Intrusion Detection System (IDS), enabling onboard detection and analysis of cyber anomalies in low Earth orbiting satellite systems.
faq.q: What is the primary purpose of the Deloitte-1 satellite mission?

faq.a: The main feature of the Silent Shield IDS is its out-of-band architecture, which is enforced by a hardware-level one-way diode. This design ensures the IDS can monitor data from the satellite bus but is not capable of transmitting any commands back into the spacecraft's software.
faq.q: What is the purpose of the Deloitte-1 Silent Shield payload?

faq.a: Deloitte-1 was successfully launched on March 15, 2025, aboard a SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket as part of the Transporter-13 rideshare mission.
faq.q: When was Deloitte-1 launched?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该卫星作为在轨网络安全环境，用于测试和评估Deloitte公司的网络入侵检测系统（IDS）面对真实世界及模拟威胁时的性能。
eoportal.summary_en: The satellite functions as an on-orbit cyber security environment to test and evaluate Deloitte’s Cyber Intrusion Detection System (IDS) against real-world and simulated threats.

faq_zh.a: Deloitte-1任务的主要目的是提供一个在轨网络安全测试平台，以验证Deloitte公司的“静默之盾”网络入侵检测系统（IDS），实现对低地球轨道卫星系统内网络异常的在轨检测与分析。
faq_zh.q: Deloitte-1卫星任务的主要目的是什么？

faq_zh.a: “静默之盾”IDS的主要特点是其带外架构，该架构通过硬件级单向二极管来强制执行。这种设计确保IDS能够监控来自卫星平台的数据，但无法向航天器的软件回传任何指令。
faq_zh.q: “静默之盾”载荷的用途是什么？

faq_zh.a: Deloitte-1于2025年3月15日，搭载SpaceX猎鹰9号火箭，作为Transporter-13共享发射任务的一部分成功发射。

faq_en.a: The Deloitte-1 mission's primary purpose is to provide an on-orbit cyber security testbed to validate Deloitte’s Silent Shield Cyber Intrusion Detection System (IDS), enabling onboard detection and analysis of cyber anomalies in low Earth orbiting satellite systems.
faq_en.q: What is the primary purpose of the Deloitte-1 satellite mission?

faq_en.a: The main feature of the Silent Shield IDS is its out-of-band architecture, which is enforced by a hardware-level one-way diode. This design ensures the IDS can monitor data from the satellite bus but is not capable of transmitting any commands back into the spacecraft's software.
faq_en.q: What is the purpose of the Deloitte-1 Silent Shield payload?

faq_en.a: Deloitte-1 was successfully launched on March 15, 2025, aboard a SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket as part of the Transporter-13 rideshare mission.
faq_en.q: When was Deloitte-1 launched?

eoportal.name: Diamant-1 programme - Diapason, Diadème-1, and Diadème-2
eoportal.summary: Diapason, launched in February 1966, and the Diadème dual satellite system, launched a week apart in February 1967, allowed for geodetic measurements using the Doppler delay of radio signals and laser telemetry techniques.

faq.a: Diadème-1 (D-1C) and 2 (D-1D) were the second and third satellites in the Diamant-1 (D-1) programme, following Diapason (D-1A). After the success of D-1A, the backup satellite D-1B was not launched, and officials instead decided to proceed directly to D-1C and D-1D to continue the geodetic measurements of D-1A.
faq.q: How do Diadème-1 and 2 relate to CNES’ Diapason (D-1A) satellite?

faq.a: The launch of Diadème-1 was a partial failure as the third stage produced less thrust than expected, resulting in a lower-than-planned orbit. Despite this, the satellite was still able to fulfill its mission.
faq.q: Was the launch of Diadème-1 a failure?

faq.a: Diadème-1 and 2 both carried dual-frequency Doppler transmitters and a retroreflector array for the collection of geodetic measurements, as well as two radio transmitters for the transmission of telemetry data.
faq.q: What instruments were carried by Diadème-1 and 2?

eoportal.agency_zh: CNES（法国国家空间研究中心）
eoportal.summary_zh: Diapason卫星于1966年2月发射，Diadème双卫星系统于1967年2月间隔一周相继发射。这些卫星利用无线电信号的多普勒延迟和激光测距技术进行大地测量。
eoportal.summary_en: Diapason, launched in February 1966, and the Diadème dual satellite system, launched a week apart in February 1967, allowed for geodetic measurements using the Doppler delay of radio signals and laser telemetry techniques.

faq_zh.a: Diadème-1（D-1C）和Diadème-2（D-1D）是钻石-1（D-1）计划中的第二和第三颗卫星，继承自Diapason（D-1A）。在D-1A成功后，备用卫星D-1B未被发射，任务方决定直接推进D-1C和D-1D的发射，以延续D-1A的大地测量任务。
Diadème-1的发射属于部分失败。其第三级火箭产生的推力低于预期，导致卫星入轨高度低于计划值。尽管如此，该卫星仍能执行其任务。
Diadème-1和2均搭载了用于收集大地测量数据的双频多普勒发射器和后向反射器阵列，以及两个用于传输遥测数据的无线电发射器。

faq_en.a: Diadème-1 (D-1C) and 2 (D-1D) were the second and third satellites in the Diamant-1 (D-1) programme, following Diapason (D-1A). After the success of D-1A, the backup satellite D-1B was not launched, and officials instead decided to proceed directly to D-1C and D-1D to continue the geodetic measurements of D-1A.
faq_en.q: How do Diadème-1 and 2 relate to CNES’ Diapason (D-1A) satellite?

faq_en.a: The launch of Diadème-1 was a partial failure as the third stage produced less thrust than expected, resulting in a lower-than-planned orbit. Despite this, the satellite was still able to fulfill its mission.
faq_en.q: Was the launch of Diadème-1 a failure?

faq_en.a: Diadème-1 and 2 both carried dual-frequency Doppler transmitters and a retroreflector array for the collection of geodetic measurements, as well as two radio transmitters for the transmission of telemetry data.
faq_en.q: What instruments were carried by Diadème-1 and 2?

diamant-1 programme - diapason, diadème-.display: Diamant-1 programme - Diapason, Diadème-1, and Diadème-2

eoportal.name: DICE (Dynamic Ionosphere CubeSat Experiment)
eoportal.summary: Its objective is the measurement of ionosphere plasma density and its effects on communication, surveillance and navigation systems.

faq.a: DICE was launched by NASA(National Aeronautics and Space Administration), but developed by a collaboration between many organisations like Utah State University/Space Dynamics Laboratory (USU/SDL), Astra LLC, and Embry-Riddle Universities, together under the DICE consortium umbrella.
faq.q: Which organisation developed the DICE mission?

faq.a: DICE satellites measure plasma density distribution and electric fields. This data can convey how SED phenomena form and evolves.
faq.q: What measurements does DICE provide?

faq.a: CubeSat provides many advantages to this mission. The ability to launch multiple satellites in one payload, low-cost access to space for implementation of multi-point measurements. DICE has also demonstrated the development of a high-speed communications link for CubeSats with unprecedented data rates for this class of spacecraft.
faq.q: Why does DICE employ CubeSat form factor?

eoportal.name_zh: DICE (动态电离层立方星实验)
eoportal.summary_zh: 该任务的目标是测量电离层等离子体密度及其对通信、监视和导航系统的影响。
eoportal.summary_en: Its objective is the measurement of ionosphere plasma density and its effects on communication, surveillance and navigation systems.

faq_zh.a: DICE由NASA发射，但由犹他州立大学/空间动力学实验室（USU/SDL）、Astra LLC和安柏瑞德航空大学等多家机构在DICE联盟框架下合作开发。
faq_zh.q: DICE任务是由哪个机构开发的？

faq_zh.a: DICE卫星测量等离子体密度分布和电场。这些数据可以揭示SED现象的形成和演化过程。
立方星为这项任务提供了诸多优势。它能够在一个载荷中发射多颗卫星，以低成本进入太空并实现多点测量。DICE还演示了为立方星开发的高速通信链路，其数据传输率在同类航天器中前所未有。
faq_zh.q: 为什么DICE采用立方星形态？

faq_en.a: DICE was launched by NASA(National Aeronautics and Space Administration), but developed by a collaboration between many organisations like Utah State University/Space Dynamics Laboratory (USU/SDL), Astra LLC, and Embry-Riddle Universities, together under the DICE consortium umbrella.
faq_en.q: Which organisation developed the DICE mission?

faq_en.a: DICE satellites measure plasma density distribution and electric fields. This data can convey how SED phenomena form and evolves.
faq_en.q: What measurements does DICE provide?

faq_en.a: CubeSat provides many advantages to this mission. The ability to launch multiple satellites in one payload, low-cost access to space for implementation of multi-point measurements. DICE has also demonstrated the development of a high-speed communications link for CubeSats with unprecedented data rates for this class of spacecraft.
faq_en.q: Why does DICE employ CubeSat form factor?

eoportal.name: Dione CubeSat
eoportal.summary: Dione will provide data to improve space weather forecasts to help satellites avoid early reentry.

faq.a: Dione could be the most densely packed CubeSat ever flown with four instruments and one experiment inside a 6U CubeSat.
faq.q: Why is Dione considered ‘interesting’ in terms of its instruments?

faq.a: Dione has completed its instrument and mission preliminary design review and is looking at maturing its overall design.
faq.q: What is the current status of Dione?

faq.a: The primary challenge for Dione is fitting and packaging its instruments and experiment into the 6U form.
faq.q: What challenges face Dione?

eoportal.summary_zh: Dione 将提供数据，以改进空间天气预报，帮助卫星避免过早再入大气层。
eoportal.summary_en: Dione will provide data to improve space weather forecasts to help satellites avoid early reentry.

faq_zh.a: Dione 可能是有史以来载荷密度最高的 CubeSat，在一个 6U 立方星内集成了四台仪器和一个实验装置。
faq_zh.q: 为什么 Dione 在仪器方面被认为很'有趣'？

faq_zh.a: Dione 已完成其仪器和任务初步设计评审，目前正致力于使其整体设计趋于成熟。
Dione 面临的主要挑战是如何将其仪器和实验装置适配并封装到 6U 的外形中。

faq_en.a: Dione could be the most densely packed CubeSat ever flown with four instruments and one experiment inside a 6U CubeSat.
faq_en.q: Why is Dione considered ‘interesting’ in terms of its instruments?

faq_en.a: Dione has completed its instrument and mission preliminary design review and is looking at maturing its overall design.
faq_en.q: What is the current status of Dione?

faq_en.a: The primary challenge for Dione is fitting and packaging its instruments and experiment into the 6U form.
faq_en.q: What challenges face Dione?

eoportal.name: Diwata-1 / PHL-Microsat-1 (Philippine Scientific Earth Observation Microsatellite-1)
eoportal.summary: It completed its mission on 5 April, 2020, burning up in Earth’s atmosphere.

faq.a: Diwata-1 has the objectives of Earth observation, climate monitoring and the development of Philippine human resources.
faq.q: What is the purpose of Diwata-1?

faq.a: Diwata-1 became inactive on 5 April, 2020, after 4 years of operation, and burned up in Earth’s atmosphere.
faq.q: Is Diwata-1 still active?

faq.a: Diwata-1 was owned and operated by the Philippine DOST (Department of Science and Technology), in collaboration with the Japanese Hokkaido and Tohoku universities.
faq.q: Who ran Diwata-1?

eoportal.name_zh: Diwata-1 / PHL-Microsat-1 (菲律宾科学地球观测微卫星-1)
eoportal.summary_zh: 该卫星于2020年4月5日完成任务，在再入地球大气层时烧毁。
eoportal.summary_en: It completed its mission on 5 April, 2020, burning up in Earth’s atmosphere.

faq_zh.a: Diwata-1的目标是进行地球观测、气候监测以及发展菲律宾的人力资源。
Diwata-1在运行4年后于2020年4月5日停止工作，并在再入地球大气层时烧毁。
Diwata-1由菲律宾科技部（DOST）拥有并运营，并与日本北海道大学和东北大学合作。

faq_en.a: Diwata-1 has the objectives of Earth observation, climate monitoring and the development of Philippine human resources.
faq_en.q: What is the purpose of Diwata-1?

faq_en.a: Diwata-1 became inactive on 5 April, 2020, after 4 years of operation, and burned up in Earth’s atmosphere.
faq_en.q: Is Diwata-1 still active?

faq_en.a: Diwata-1 was owned and operated by the Philippine DOST (Department of Science and Technology), in collaboration with the Japanese Hokkaido and Tohoku universities.
faq_en.q: Who ran Diwata-1?

eoportal.name: Diwata-2
taxonomy: Philippine Department of Science and Technology (DOST)
eoportal.agency: Philippine Department of Science and Technology (DOST), University of the Philippines Diliman (UPD)
eoportal.summary: The mission was developed by the Philippine Department of Science and Technology (DOST) and University of the Philippines Diliman (UPD), in collaboration with Japanese partners Tohoku University and Hokkaido University.

faq.a: Diwata-2's main purpose is to capture remote sensing data for applications including determining disaster damage, monitoring natural resources and environmental changes, and observing cloud patterns and weather.
faq.q: What is the main purpose of the Diwata-2 mission?

faq.a: Diwata-2's main instruments include the High Precision Telescope (HPT), which provides high-resolution imagery at a GSD 5 m, and the Space-borne Multispectral Imager (SMI) with a Liquid Crystal Tunable Filter (LCTF).
faq.q: What are the main instruments onboard Diwata-2?

faq.a: The Diwata-2 microsatellite was launched into a Sun Synchronous Orbit (SSO) on October 29, 2018 onboard the JAXA H-IIA Launch Vehicle 40 from the Tanegashima Space Center in Kagoshima Prefecture, Japan.
faq.q: When was Diwata-2 launched?

eoportal.agency_zh: 菲律宾科学技术部（DOST）、菲律宾大学迪利曼分校（UPD）
eoportal.summary_zh: 该任务由菲律宾科学技术部（DOST）和菲律宾大学迪利曼分校（UPD）开发，并与日本合作伙伴东北大学和北海道大学合作完成。
eoportal.summary_en: The mission was developed by the Philippine Department of Science and Technology (DOST) and University of the Philippines Diliman (UPD), in collaboration with Japanese partners Tohoku University and Hokkaido University.

faq_zh.a: Diwata-2的主要目的是获取遥感数据，用于包括评估灾害损失、监测自然资源与环境变化以及观测云型和天气等应用。
faq_zh.q: Diwata-2任务的主要目的是什么？

faq_zh.a: Diwata-2的主要仪器包括提供地面采样距离为5米的高分辨率图像的高精度望远镜（HPT），以及搭载液晶可调谐滤光片（LCTF）的星载多光谱成像仪（SMI）。
faq_zh.q: Diwata-2搭载的主要仪器有哪些？

faq_zh.a: Diwata-2微卫星于2018年10月29日搭载日本宇宙航空研究开发机构（JAXA）的H-IIA运载火箭40号机，从日本鹿儿岛县的种子岛航天中心发射，进入太阳同步轨道（SSO）。

faq_en.a: Diwata-2's main purpose is to capture remote sensing data for applications including determining disaster damage, monitoring natural resources and environmental changes, and observing cloud patterns and weather.
faq_en.q: What is the main purpose of the Diwata-2 mission?

faq_en.a: Diwata-2's main instruments include the High Precision Telescope (HPT), which provides high-resolution imagery at a GSD 5 m, and the Space-borne Multispectral Imager (SMI) with a Liquid Crystal Tunable Filter (LCTF).
faq_en.q: What are the main instruments onboard Diwata-2?

faq_en.a: The Diwata-2 microsatellite was launched into a Sun Synchronous Orbit (SSO) on October 29, 2018 onboard the JAXA H-IIA Launch Vehicle 40 from the Tanegashima Space Center in Kagoshima Prefecture, Japan.
faq_en.q: When was Diwata-2 launched?

eoportal.name: DMC-1G (Disaster Monitoring Constellation- First Generation)
eoportal.summary: This combines the benefits of a file delivery protocol designed for space, with the use of low cost commercial off-the-shelf (COTS) products for the data link layer to provide a robust and flexible communications system.
eoportal.summary_zh: 该系统结合了为太空设计的文件传输协议的优势，以及在数据链路层使用低成本商业现货（COTS）产品，从而提供了一个稳健且灵活的通信系统。
eoportal.summary_en: This combines the benefits of a file delivery protocol designed for space, with the use of low cost commercial off-the-shelf (COTS) products for the data link layer to provide a robust and flexible communications system.

eoportal.name: DMC-3 (Disaster Monitoring Constellation-3)
eoportal.summary: The constellation has a design life of seven years.

faq.a: The spatial resolution of the DMC-3 satellites are 1 m Ground Sample Distance (GSD) when imaged in panchromatic bands and 4 m GSD when imaged in the multispectral band.
faq.q: What is the spatial resolution of DMC-3?

faq.a: The DMC-3 constellation, consisting of three identical satellites, was launched on board a PSLV-C28 launch vehicle  from SDSC (Satish Dhawan Space Centre) in India  in July 2015.
faq.q: When was DMC-3 launched?

faq.a: DMC-3 is designed as a high-resolution (both spatial and temporal) imager in a small-satellite form factor with the aim to provide service to various clients on a needs basis. This service is operated and owned by DMCii (DMC International Imaging Ltd.), based in Surrey, UK.
faq.q: What does DMC-3 aim to accomplish?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该星座的设计寿命为七年。
eoportal.summary_en: The constellation has a design life of seven years.

faq_zh.a: DMC-3卫星在全色波段成像时的地面采样距离（GSD）为1米，在多光谱波段成像时为4米。
DMC-3星座由三颗相同的卫星组成，于2015年7月搭载印度SDSC（萨迪什·达万航天中心）的PSLV-C28运载火箭发射升空。
DMC-3被设计为一种小型卫星形式的高分辨率（空间和时间分辨率）成像仪，旨在根据需求为各类客户提供服务。该服务由位于英国萨里的DMCii（DMC国际成像有限公司）运营和拥有。

faq_en.a: The spatial resolution of the DMC-3 satellites are 1 m Ground Sample Distance (GSD) when imaged in panchromatic bands and 4 m GSD when imaged in the multispectral band.
faq_en.q: What is the spatial resolution of DMC-3?

faq_en.a: The DMC-3 constellation, consisting of three identical satellites, was launched on board a PSLV-C28 launch vehicle  from SDSC (Satish Dhawan Space Centre) in India  in July 2015.
faq_en.q: When was DMC-3 launched?

faq_en.a: DMC-3 is designed as a high-resolution (both spatial and temporal) imager in a small-satellite form factor with the aim to provide service to various clients on a needs basis. This service is operated and owned by DMCii (DMC International Imaging Ltd.), based in Surrey, UK.
faq_en.q: What does DMC-3 aim to accomplish?

eoportal.name: DMSP (Defense Meteorological Satellite Program) Block 5D
taxonomy: Cloud type, amount and cloud top temperature
Precipitation intensity at the surface (liquid or solid)
Ion Density, Drift Velocity, and Temperature
eoportal.summary: There are three satellites currently operational in the series, DMSP F-16, DMSP, F-17, and DMSP F-18.

faq.a: The National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) operates the Defence Meteorological Satellite Program (DMSP) in partnership with the United States Air Force (USAF), with NOAA responsible for operating the ground systems development and overseeing the satellites daily operation.
faq.q: Who operates DMSP?

faq.a: All of the Defence Meteorological Satellite Program (DMSP) satellites were built by  Lockheed Martin Space Systems Company (LMSSC) under contract from the United States Air Force (USAF).
faq.q: Who built the DMSP satellites?

faq.a: The Defence Meteorological Satellite Program (DMSP) has been in operation for over 50 years, however, after the failure of DMSP F-19, DMSP F-20 was scrapped due to limited funding from the United States Congress who ordered the United States Air Force (USAF) to move on to a next-generation system.
faq.q: Is the DMSP program near its end?

eoportal.name_zh: 国防气象卫星计划 Block 5D (DMSP Block 5D)
eoportal.agency_zh: 美国国家海洋和大气管理局 (NOAA)、美国太空军 (USSF)、美国空军 (USAF)
eoportal.summary_zh: 目前该系列有三颗卫星在轨运行，分别是 DMSP F-16、DMSP F-17 和 DMSP F-18。
eoportal.summary_en: There are three satellites currently operational in the series, DMSP F-16, DMSP, F-17, and DMSP F-18.

所有国防气象卫星计划 (DMSP) 的卫星均由洛克希德·马丁空间系统公司 (LMSSC) 根据美国空军 (USAF) 的合同建造。

faq_zh.a: 国防气象卫星计划 (DMSP) 已运行超过 50 年。然而，在 DMSP F-19 卫星失效后，由于美国国会资金有限，并指示美国空军 (USAF) 转向开发下一代系统，DMSP F-20 已被废弃。
faq_zh.q: DMSP 计划是否接近尾声？

faq_en.a: The National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) operates the Defence Meteorological Satellite Program (DMSP) in partnership with the United States Air Force (USAF), with NOAA responsible for operating the ground systems development and overseeing the satellites daily operation.
faq_en.q: Who operates DMSP?

faq_en.a: All of the Defence Meteorological Satellite Program (DMSP) satellites were built by  Lockheed Martin Space Systems Company (LMSSC) under contract from the United States Air Force (USAF).
faq_en.q: Who built the DMSP satellites?

faq_en.a: The Defence Meteorological Satellite Program (DMSP) has been in operation for over 50 years, however, after the failure of DMSP F-19, DMSP F-20 was scrapped due to limited funding from the United States Congress who ordered the United States Air Force (USAF) to move on to a next-generation system.

eoportal.name: Dove-1 and Dove-2 Nanosatellites
eoportal.summary: The purpose of this mission was to prove that using commercial off-the-shelf (COTS) components to create a low-cost bus to fit in a 3U CubeSat form factor was feasible and could operate on short schedules and low costs.

faq.a: Dove-1 and Dove-2 were manufactured and operated by Cosmogia Inc., (now Planet Labs Inc.) based in California, USA.
faq.q: Who owns Dove-1 and Dove-2?

faq.a: Dove-1 and Dove-2 were designed as a technological demonstration mission, utilising low-cost Commercial Off-the-Shelf parts to manufacture the satellite bus.
faq.q: What was the purpose of Dove-1 and Dove-2?

faq.a: Dove-1 and Dove-2 carried an optical telescope and a high-resolution camera.
faq.q: What instruments were on Dove-1 and Dove-2?

eoportal.name_zh: Dove-1 和 Dove-2 纳米卫星
eoportal.summary_zh: 本次任务旨在证明，使用商用现货（COTS）组件构建一个能适配3U CubeSat外形的低成本卫星平台是可行的，并且能够以较短的周期和较低的成本投入运行。
eoportal.summary_en: The purpose of this mission was to prove that using commercial off-the-shelf (COTS) components to create a low-cost bus to fit in a 3U CubeSat form factor was feasible and could operate on short schedules and low costs.

faq_zh.a: Dove-1 和 Dove-2 由美国加利福尼亚州的 Cosmogia 公司（现为 Planet Labs 公司）制造并运营。
faq_zh.q: Dove-1 和 Dove-2 归谁所有？

faq_zh.a: Dove-1 和 Dove-2 被设计为技术验证任务，利用低成本的商用现货部件来制造卫星平台。
faq_zh.q: Dove-1 和 Dove-2 的目的是什么？

faq_zh.a: Dove-1 和 Dove-2 搭载了一台光学望远镜和一台高分辨率相机。
faq_zh.q: Dove-1 和 Dove-2 上搭载了哪些仪器？

faq_en.a: Dove-1 and Dove-2 were manufactured and operated by Cosmogia Inc., (now Planet Labs Inc.) based in California, USA.
faq_en.q: Who owns Dove-1 and Dove-2?

faq_en.a: Dove-1 and Dove-2 were designed as a technological demonstration mission, utilising low-cost Commercial Off-the-Shelf parts to manufacture the satellite bus.

faq_en.a: Dove-1 and Dove-2 carried an optical telescope and a high-resolution camera.

eoportal.name: Drag-free CubeSat
eoportal.summary: Orbit: A sun-synchronous orbit is preferred (due to its constant solar illumination), the preferred altitude is 400 km.
eoportal.summary_zh: 轨道：优选太阳同步轨道（因其具有持续的日照条件），优选轨道高度为400公里。
eoportal.summary_en: Orbit: A sun-synchronous orbit is preferred (due to its constant solar illumination), the preferred altitude is 400 km.

eoportal.name: Drishti Constellation (GalaxEye)
eoportal.summary: The first satellite was launched in May 2026.

faq.a: GalaxEye provides a commercially available, high-resolution satellite dataset with high coverage for application in disaster management, marine, insurance, energy and utility, mining, and defense sectors.
faq.q: What does GalaxEye Space Solutions offer?

faq.a: Yes, GalaxEye is building a multi-sensor imaging satellite called Drishti. The onboard SyncFusion Imaging System is the world’s first combination of SAR and optical imaging on the same platform, which was successfully tested on GalaxEye’s ‘tech demo’ GLX-SQ, launched on 31 December 2024.
faq.q: Is GalaxEye Space Solutions developing the world’s first SAR and optical sensors?

faq.a: In February 2025, GalaxEye announced a partnership with Florida-based impulso.space to launch its multi-sensor EO satellite, Drishiti, through SpaceX.
faq.q: Is GalaxEye Space Solutions collaborating with SpaceX to launch the Drishti Mission?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该卫星的首颗星于2026年5月发射。
eoportal.summary_en: The first satellite was launched in May 2026.

faq_zh.a: GalaxEye提供商业可用、高分辨率、高覆盖率的卫星数据集，可应用于灾害管理、海洋、保险、能源与公用事业、采矿和国防等领域。
是的，GalaxEye正在建造一颗名为Drishti的多传感器成像卫星。其搭载的SyncFusion成像系统是全球首个在同一平台上集成SAR与光学成像的技术，该系统已于2024年12月31日发射的GalaxEye‘技术验证星’GLX-SQ上成功完成测试。
faq_zh.q: GalaxEye Space Solutions是否正在开发世界上首个同时搭载SAR和光学传感器的卫星？

faq_zh.a: 是的，在2025年2月，GalaxEye宣布与位于佛罗里达州的impulso.space公司建立合作伙伴关系，将通过SpaceX发射其多传感器对地观测卫星Drishti。
faq_zh.q: GalaxEye Space Solutions是否与SpaceX合作发射Drishti任务？

faq_en.a: GalaxEye provides a commercially available, high-resolution satellite dataset with high coverage for application in disaster management, marine, insurance, energy and utility, mining, and defense sectors.
faq_en.q: What does GalaxEye Space Solutions offer?

faq_en.a: Yes, GalaxEye is building a multi-sensor imaging satellite called Drishti. The onboard SyncFusion Imaging System is the world’s first combination of SAR and optical imaging on the same platform, which was successfully tested on GalaxEye’s ‘tech demo’ GLX-SQ, launched on 31 December 2024.
faq_en.q: Is GalaxEye Space Solutions developing the world’s first SAR and optical sensors?

faq_en.a: In February 2025, GalaxEye announced a partnership with Florida-based impulso.space to launch its multi-sensor EO satellite, Drishiti, through SpaceX.
faq_en.q: Is GalaxEye Space Solutions collaborating with SpaceX to launch the Drishti Mission?

eoportal.name: DSCOVR (Deep Space Climate Observatory)
taxonomy: Cloud type, amount and cloud top temperature
Upwelling (Outgoing) long-wave radiation at Earth surface
Upwelling (Outgoing) Short-wave Radiation at the Earth Surface
eoportal.summary: It aims to monitor space weather and climate, as well as provide deep space imagery of the Earth’s surface.

faq.a: DSCOVR aims to monitor space weather and climate, as well as provide deep space imagery of the Earth’s surface.
faq.q: What is the aim of DSCOVR?

faq.a: DSCOVR is owned and operated by NOAA (National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration), with data processed by NASA (National Aeronautics and Space Administration).
faq.q: Who owns DSCOVR?

faq.a: DSCOVR operates in a Lissajous orbit about L1 (Lagrangian Point 1) in the Earth-Sun system, approximately 1,500,000 km from Earth, in the direction of the sun.
faq.q: Where is DSCOVR?

eoportal.name: EagleEye
eoportal.summary: Space Research Center of the Polish Academy of Sciences (CBK PAN), and funded by the European Union and the European Regional Development Fund of the Smart Growth Development Operational Programme 2014-2020.

faq.a: EagleEye is a Polish Earth observation mission that serves to showcase Polish space capabilities to a global stage and provide support for military and agricultural applications. The project began in 2020 and launched early 2024, carrying the largest optical space telescope designed and constructed in Poland.
faq.q: What is the EagleEye mission?

faq.a: The SOP200 telescope has images in the visible near-infrared (VNIR) spectrum at a 1 m resolution from a 350 km orbit.
faq.q: What is the resolution of the EagleEye satellite?

faq.a: The EagleEye mission is led by Creotech Instruments S.A., and developed with support from Firma Scanway S.A. and the Space Research Centre of the Polish Academy of Sciences.
faq.q: Who developed the EagleEye mission

eoportal.summary_zh: EagleEye 卫星是波兰科学院太空研究中心（CBK PAN）负责的地球观测任务，由欧盟及欧洲区域发展基金（2014-2020年智慧增长发展运营计划）资助。
eoportal.summary_en: Space Research Center of the Polish Academy of Sciences (CBK PAN), and funded by the European Union and the European Regional Development Fund of the Smart Growth Development Operational Programme 2014-2020.

faq_zh.a: EagleEye 是一项波兰地球观测任务，旨在向全球展示波兰的航天能力，并为军事和农业应用提供支持。该项目始于2020年，于2024年初发射，搭载了波兰设计和建造的最大光学太空望远镜。
SOP200 望远镜在350公里轨道上，可见光近红外（VNIR）谱段的成像分辨率为1米。
faq_zh.q: EagleEye 卫星的分辨率是多少？

faq_zh.a: EagleEye 任务由 Creotech Instruments S.A. 牵头，并与 Firma Scanway S.A. 和波兰科学院太空研究中心合作开发。
faq_zh.q: EagleEye 任务是由谁开发的？

faq_en.a: EagleEye is a Polish Earth observation mission that serves to showcase Polish space capabilities to a global stage and provide support for military and agricultural applications. The project began in 2020 and launched early 2024, carrying the largest optical space telescope designed and constructed in Poland.
faq_en.q: What is the EagleEye mission?

faq_en.a: The SOP200 telescope has images in the visible near-infrared (VNIR) spectrum at a 1 m resolution from a 350 km orbit.
faq_en.q: What is the resolution of the EagleEye satellite?

faq_en.a: The EagleEye mission is led by Creotech Instruments S.A., and developed with support from Firma Scanway S.A. and the Space Research Centre of the Polish Academy of Sciences.

faq.a: EarthCARE uses a radar, an imager, and a radiometer to observe clouds and aerosols as well as measure solar and thermal radiation. This will develop our understanding of the role that clouds and aerosols play in reflecting the sun's radiation and trapping the infrared radiation emitted from the Earth’s surface.
faq.q: What does EarthCARE do?

faq.a: The Cloud Profiling Radar (CPR) aboard EarthCARE uses the Doppler effect implementing radio waves to measure the vertical velocity of cloud particles.
faq.q: How will EarthCARE measure cloud velocity?

faq.a: ESA’s Earth Explorer Missions are a set of missions dedicated to addressing major challenges identified by society. EarthCARE搭载的云廓线雷达（CPR）利用多普勒效应，通过无线电波测量云粒子的垂直速度。

faq.a: The EarthDaily Constellation optical imagers developed by the Canadian-based space company ABB, are designed to observe over 22 spectral bands.

eoportal.summary: Launched in December 2023, the mission aims to train students and researchers in satellite development, promote STEM education, and advance Irish space technology capabilities.

eoportal.agency_zh: EIRSAT-1 由爱尔兰都柏林大学（University College Dublin）主导，作为 ESA Academy 的“飞你的卫星！”项目的一部分。
eoportal.summary_zh: EIRSAT-1（爱尔兰教育研究卫星1号）于2023年12月发射，其主要任务是培训卫星开发领域的学生和研究人员，推动STEM教育，并提升爱尔兰的空间技术能力。
eoportal.summary_en: Launched in December 2023, the mission aims to train students and researchers in satellite development, promote STEM education, and advance Irish space technology capabilities.

eoportal.name: Electro-L / GOMS (Geostationary Operational Meteorological Satellite)
taxonomy: Cloud type, amount and cloud top temperature
Downward long-wave irradiance at Earth surface
Precipitation intensity at the surface (liquid or solid)
eoportal.summary: Electro-L N1, the first satellite of the series, was launched in January 2011.

faq.a: Electro-L’s sensors provide data that can be used for the monitoring and forecasting of atmospheric, magnetospheric, ionospheric, and solar fluctuations, as well as hydro-meteorological, heliospheric, ionospheric, and magnetospheric measurements of the Earth’s atmosphere and land and Ocean surface.
faq.q: What is the use of Electro-L?

faq.a: Electro-L is operated by Roshydromet and ROSKOSMOS (Russian Federal Space Agency).
faq.q: Who operates Electro-L?

faq.a: Electro-L contained instruments classified as space environment, imaging multispectral radiometers (vis/IR), and data collection. These would take measurements relating to the atmosphere, gravitational and magnetic fields, the Ocean, land, and snow & ice.
faq.q: What instrument types are on board Electro-L?

eoportal.name_zh: Electro-L / GOMS（地球静止轨道业务气象卫星）
eoportal.agency_zh: ROSKOSMOS（俄罗斯国家航天集团），ROSHYDROMET（俄罗斯联邦水文气象和环境监测局）
eoportal.summary_zh: Electro-L系列的首颗卫星Electro-L N1于2011年1月发射。
eoportal.summary_en: Electro-L N1, the first satellite of the series, was launched in January 2011.

faq_zh.a: Electro-L的传感器提供的数据可用于监测和预报大气、磁层、电离层和太阳活动的波动，以及进行水文气象、日球层、电离层和磁层的测量，以获取地球大气、陆地和海洋表面的信息。
Electro-L卫星由ROSHYDROMET（俄罗斯联邦水文气象和环境监测局）和ROSKOSMOS（俄罗斯国家航天集团）负责运营。
Electro-L卫星搭载的仪器包括空间环境监测仪、成像多光谱辐射计（可见光/红外）以及数据收集系统。这些仪器用于测量与大气、重力场和磁场、海洋、陆地、冰雪相关的参数。
faq_zh.q: Electro-L卫星搭载了哪些类型的仪器？

faq_en.a: Electro-L’s sensors provide data that can be used for the monitoring and forecasting of atmospheric, magnetospheric, ionospheric, and solar fluctuations, as well as hydro-meteorological, heliospheric, ionospheric, and magnetospheric measurements of the Earth’s atmosphere and land and Ocean surface.
faq_en.q: What is the use of Electro-L?

faq_en.a: Electro-L is operated by Roshydromet and ROSKOSMOS (Russian Federal Space Agency).
faq_en.q: Who operates Electro-L?

faq_en.a: Electro-L contained instruments classified as space environment, imaging multispectral radiometers (vis/IR), and data collection. These would take measurements relating to the atmosphere, gravitational and magnetic fields, the Ocean, land, and snow & ice.

eoportal.name: EnMAP (Environmental Monitoring and Analysis Program)
eoportal.summary: Launched  in April 2022, the mission is managed by the German Aerospace Centre (DLR), with the German Research Centre for Geosciences (GFZ) leading the associated scientific research.

faq.a: EnMAP is a hyperspectral German satellite, commissioned to take high quality, global photos to track both atmospheric aerosol composition and variation in land vegetation coverage.
faq.q: What is EnMAP used for?

faq.a: EnMAP is a hyperspectral imaging minisatellite, carrying out Earth observation.
faq.q: What type of satellite is EnMAP?

faq.a: EnMAP traverses the Earth on a near-polar orbit, taking images across a wide-range of spectral bands. Information is then transmitted to the ground  using  X-band radio frequency and algorithmically developed into images, using software at the German Research Centre for Geosciences (GFZ).
faq.q: How does EnMAP work?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该任务于2022年4月发射，由德国宇航中心（DLR）管理，德国地球科学研究中心（GFZ）负责牵头开展相关的科学研究。
eoportal.summary_en: Launched  in April 2022, the mission is managed by the German Aerospace Centre (DLR), with the German Research Centre for Geosciences (GFZ) leading the associated scientific research.

faq_zh.a: EnMAP是一颗德国高光谱卫星，旨在获取高质量的全球图像，用于追踪大气气溶胶成分和地表植被覆盖的变化。
EnMAP是一颗进行对地观测的高光谱成像小卫星。
EnMAP在近极地轨道上运行，覆盖广泛的光谱波段进行成像。然后，信息通过X波段无线电频率传输到地面，并由德国地球科学研究中心（GFZ）的软件进行算法处理，生成图像。

faq_en.a: EnMAP is a hyperspectral German satellite, commissioned to take high quality, global photos to track both atmospheric aerosol composition and variation in land vegetation coverage.
faq_en.q: What is EnMAP used for?

faq_en.a: EnMAP is a hyperspectral imaging minisatellite, carrying out Earth observation.
faq_en.q: What type of satellite is EnMAP?

faq_en.a: EnMAP traverses the Earth on a near-polar orbit, taking images across a wide-range of spectral bands. Information is then transmitted to the ground  using  X-band radio frequency and algorithmically developed into images, using software at the German Research Centre for Geosciences (GFZ).
faq_en.q: How does EnMAP work?

eoportal.name: EnviSat (Environmental Satellite)
taxonomy: Cloud type, amount and cloud top temperature
Ocean imagery and water leaving spectral radiance
Precipitation intensity at the surface (liquid or solid)
Sea ice cover, edge and thickness
eoportal.summary: EnviSat application areas included meteorology, climatology, environment, atmospheric chemistry, vegetation, hydrology, land use, and ocean and ice processes.

faq.a: EnviSat was an Earth observation mission whose data was used for studying and monitoring the Earth's environment at various scales, from local through regional to global. It was also used for monitoring and management of the Earth's renewable and nonrenewable resources.
faq.q: What was EnviSat used for?

faq.a: EnviSat was in a sun-synchronous polar orbit at an altitude of 800 km and an inclination of 98.55°. The Local Time on Descending Node (LTDN) was at 1000 hours with an orbit period of 100.6 minutes and an exact repeat cycle of 35 days.
faq.q: What orbit was EnviSat in?

faq.a: EnviSat was about 9 metres long and 5 metres wide.
faq.q: How big was EnviSat?

eoportal.summary_zh: 环境卫星（EnviSat）的应用领域涵盖气象学、气候学、环境科学、大气化学、植被、水文学、土地利用以及海洋和冰川过程。
eoportal.summary_en: EnviSat application areas included meteorology, climatology, environment, atmospheric chemistry, vegetation, hydrology, land use, and ocean and ice processes.

faq_zh.a: 环境卫星（EnviSat）是一项地球观测任务，其数据用于从局部到区域再到全球不同尺度上研究和监测地球环境。它还用于监测和管理地球上的可再生与不可再生资源。
环境卫星（EnviSat）运行在高度为800公里、倾角为98.55°的太阳同步极地轨道上。其降交点地方时（LTDN）为上午10点，轨道周期为100.6分钟，精确重访周期为35天。
faq_zh.q: 环境卫星（EnviSat）运行在什么轨道上？

faq_zh.a: 环境卫星（EnviSat）长约9米，宽约5米。

faq_en.a: EnviSat was an Earth observation mission whose data was used for studying and monitoring the Earth's environment at various scales, from local through regional to global. It was also used for monitoring and management of the Earth's renewable and nonrenewable resources.
faq_en.q: What was EnviSat used for?

faq_en.a: EnviSat was in a sun-synchronous polar orbit at an altitude of 800 km and an inclination of 98.55°. The Local Time on Descending Node (LTDN) was at 1000 hours with an orbit period of 100.6 minutes and an exact repeat cycle of 35 days.
faq_en.q: What orbit was EnviSat in?

faq_en.a: EnviSat was about 9 metres long and 5 metres wide.
faq_en.q: How big was EnviSat?

eoportal.name: EO-1 (Earth Observing-1)
taxonomy: Sea ice cover, edge and thickness
eoportal.summary: Due to its numerous valuable contributions, NASA approved the extension of the satellite until it was finally decommissioned in March 2017.

faq.a: The EO-1 spacecraft was developed by NASA for its New Millennium Program. It was operated and managed by NASA’s Goddard Space Flight Centre.
faq.q: What agency operated EO-1?

faq.a: The EO-1 satellite was launched on 21 November 2000 and decommissioned on 30 March, 2017.
faq.q: When was EO-1 launched?

faq.a:
Hyperion is a hyperspectral instrument that enables scientists to understand the chemical constituents of Earth’s surface in fine detail. It is capable of resolving 220 spectral bands from a range of 0.4 to 2.5 μm with a 30m spatial resolution and a swath width of 7.5 km. The continuous spectral coverage in the VNIR and SWIR spectrum is defined by a spectral resolution of 10 nanometers.
The data has been used to identify specific minerals, track vegetation type and vigour of forests and monitor volcanic activity.
faq.q: What imaging does Hyperion do?

eoportal.summary_zh: 由于其众多宝贵的贡献，NASA批准延长了该卫星的服役期限，直至2017年3月最终退役。
eoportal.summary_en: Due to its numerous valuable contributions, NASA approved the extension of the satellite until it was finally decommissioned in March 2017.

faq_zh.a: EO-1航天器是NASA为其“新千年计划”开发的，由NASA的戈达德太空飞行中心负责运营和管理。
EO-1卫星于2000年11月21日发射，并于2017年3月30日退役。
Hyperion是一种高光谱仪器，使科学家能够精细了解地球表面的化学成分。它能够分辨0.4至2.5微米范围内的220个光谱波段，空间分辨率为30米，幅宽为7.5公里。在可见光近红外（VNIR）和短波红外（SWIR）光谱范围内，其连续光谱覆盖的光谱分辨率为10纳米。该数据已被用于识别特定矿物、追踪森林植被类型和活力以及监测火山活动。

faq_en.a: The EO-1 spacecraft was developed by NASA for its New Millennium Program. It was operated and managed by NASA’s Goddard Space Flight Centre.
faq_en.q: What agency operated EO-1?

faq_en.a: The EO-1 satellite was launched on 21 November 2000 and decommissioned on 30 March, 2017.
faq_en.q: When was EO-1 launched?

faq_en.a:
Hyperion is a hyperspectral instrument that enables scientists to understand the chemical constituents of Earth’s surface in fine detail. It is capable of resolving 220 spectral bands from a range of 0.4 to 2.5 μm with a 30m spatial resolution and a swath width of 7.5 km. The continuous spectral coverage in the VNIR and SWIR spectrum is defined by a spectral resolution of 10 nanometers.
The data has been used to identify specific minerals, track vegetation type and vigour of forests and monitor volcanic activity.
faq_en.q: What imaging does Hyperion do?

eoportal.name: EOS SAT-1 / AgriSat-1
eoportal.summary: Commissioned by EOS Data Analytics (EOSDA), the satellite uses AI-powered data analytics and satellite imagery to support sustainable agriculture and environmental monitoring.

faq.a: The EOS SAT-1 is the first of seven satellites forming a constellation developed by South African company Dragonfly Aerospace EOS Sat-1 aims to address important issues in farming and land management, including soil moisture, biodiversity impacted by fertilisers and pesticides, the food crisis and water scarcity.
faq.q: What is the EOS Sat-1 mission?

faq.a: EOS Sat-1 targets large-scale issues like climate change, deforestation, soil degradation, food crisis, and water scarcity. All collected data feeds into solving these issues from an agriculture perspective.
faq.q: What is the satellite’s mission?

faq.a: Processed data can be attained through EOS Data Analytics (EOSDA) for implementation in environmental management and research.
faq.q: Where can EOS Sat-1 data be accessed?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该卫星由 EOS Data Analytics (EOSDA) 委托建造，采用人工智能数据分析和卫星影像技术，旨在支持可持续农业和环境监测。
eoportal.summary_en: Commissioned by EOS Data Analytics (EOSDA), the satellite uses AI-powered data analytics and satellite imagery to support sustainable agriculture and environmental monitoring.

faq_zh.a: EOS SAT-1 是南非公司 Dragonfly Aerospace 开发的由七颗卫星组成的星座中的首颗。该卫星旨在解决农业和土地管理中的重要问题，包括土壤湿度、受化肥和农药影响的生物多样性、粮食危机和水资源短缺。
EOS SAT-1 旨在应对气候变化、森林砍伐、土壤退化、粮食危机和水资源短缺等大规模问题。所有收集的数据都将用于从农业角度解决这些问题。
可通过 EOS Data Analytics (EOSDA) 获取处理后的数据，用于环境管理和研究。
faq_zh.q: 如何访问 EOS SAT-1 的数据？

faq_en.a: The EOS SAT-1 is the first of seven satellites forming a constellation developed by South African company Dragonfly Aerospace EOS Sat-1 aims to address important issues in farming and land management, including soil moisture, biodiversity impacted by fertilisers and pesticides, the food crisis and water scarcity.
faq_en.q: What is the EOS Sat-1 mission?

faq_en.a: EOS Sat-1 targets large-scale issues like climate change, deforestation, soil degradation, food crisis, and water scarcity. All collected data feeds into solving these issues from an agriculture perspective.
faq_en.q: What is the satellite’s mission?

faq_en.a: Processed data can be attained through EOS Data Analytics (EOSDA) for implementation in environmental management and research.
faq_en.q: Where can EOS Sat-1 data be accessed?
