eoportal.name: EPICHyper / Dragonette
eoportal.summary: The final satellite in the constellation, EPICHyper-3, was launched on 11 November 2023.

faq.a: Yes, EPICHyper-1 is the name given by the manufacturers of the satellite, AAC Clyde Space, whereas Dragonette-001 is the name given to the same satellite by Wyvern. Wyvern is a Canadian-based geospatial data provider that will have exclusive access to the data collected by the Dragonette constellation. According to CEO Chris Robson, “Dragonette is a baby dragon. And so because it's our first one, we thought it'd be cute to give that name to the satellite”.
faq.q: Are EPICHyper-1 and Dragonette-001 the same satellite?

faq.a: All three satellites host hyperspectral imaging payloads. EPICHyper-2 and 3 are able to image a wider spectral range, with more spectral bands.
faq.q: What are the differences between the three EPICHyper Satellites?

faq.a: Dragonette-4 is a hyperspectral imaging satellite planned to launch in 2024 and will be based on Loft Orbital's Longbow satellite bus platform.
faq.q: Are there plans for more satellite launches as part of the Dragonette constellation?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该星座的最后一颗卫星EPICHyper-3于2023年11月11日发射。
eoportal.summary_en: The final satellite in the constellation, EPICHyper-3, was launched on 11 November 2023.

faq_zh.a: 是的，EPICHyper-1是由该卫星的制造商AAC Clyde Space所取的名称，而Dragonette-001则是由Wyvern为同一颗卫星所取的名称。Wyvern是一家总部位于加拿大的地理空间数据提供商，将独家访问Dragonette星座所收集的数据。据其首席执行官Chris Robson所述：“Dragonette意为‘幼龙’。因为这是我们发射的第一颗卫星，所以我们觉得给它取这个名字很可爱。”
三颗卫星都搭载了高光谱成像载荷。EPICHyper-2和EPICHyper-3能够覆盖更宽的光谱范围，并具备更多的光谱波段。
faq_zh.q: 三颗EPICHyper卫星之间有何不同？

faq_zh.a: Dragonette-4是一颗计划于2024年发射的高光谱成像卫星，它将基于Loft Orbital公司的Longbow卫星平台。
faq_zh.q: 是否有计划发射更多卫星以扩充Dragonette星座？

faq_en.a: Yes, EPICHyper-1 is the name given by the manufacturers of the satellite, AAC Clyde Space, whereas Dragonette-001 is the name given to the same satellite by Wyvern. Wyvern is a Canadian-based geospatial data provider that will have exclusive access to the data collected by the Dragonette constellation. According to CEO Chris Robson, “Dragonette is a baby dragon. And so because it's our first one, we thought it'd be cute to give that name to the satellite”.
faq_en.q: Are EPICHyper-1 and Dragonette-001 the same satellite?

faq_en.a: All three satellites host hyperspectral imaging payloads. EPICHyper-2 and 3 are able to image a wider spectral range, with more spectral bands.
faq_en.q: What are the differences between the three EPICHyper Satellites?

faq_en.a: Dragonette-4 is a hyperspectral imaging satellite planned to launch in 2024 and will be based on Loft Orbital's Longbow satellite bus platform.
faq_en.q: Are there plans for more satellite launches as part of the Dragonette constellation?

eoportal.name: EQUULEUS (EQUilibriUm Lunar-Earth point 6U Spacecraft) and OMOTENASHI
eoportal.summary: OMOTENASHI aimed to demonstrate technology for deployment of a lunar nano-lander.

faq.a: Both missions were amongst the 10 CubeSats flying in NASA’s Artemis 1 mission. Artemis 1 was delayed on multiple occasions from August 2022, eventually launching in November 2022.
faq.q: How were EQUULEUS and OMOTENASHI launched?

faq.a: EQUULEUS will be the first mission to measure the LIF at the far side of the moon from EML2 and the first nano-spacecraft to control trajectory within the Sun-Earth-Moon region.
faq.q: What makes the EQUULEUS mission unique?

faq.a: OMOTENASHI was set to perform two deterministic manoeuvres which would have made it the first CubeSat satellite to perform a semi-hard moon landing, at approximately 30 m/s.
faq.q: How would OMOTENASHI have landed on the moon?

eoportal.name_zh: EQUULEUS (地球-月球平衡点6U航天器) 和 OMOTENASHI
eoportal.summary_zh: OMOTENASHI旨在演示月球纳米着陆器的部署技术。
eoportal.summary_en: OMOTENASHI aimed to demonstrate technology for deployment of a lunar nano-lander.

faq_zh.a: 这两项任务是NASA阿尔忒弥斯1号任务中搭载的10颗立方星之一。阿尔忒弥斯1号从2022年8月起多次推迟，最终于2022年11月发射。
EQUULEUS将成为首个在月球远端EML2点测量月球间辐射的第一项任务，也是首个在日-地-月区域内控制轨道的纳米航天器。
faq_zh.q: EQUULEUS任务的独特之处是什么？

faq_zh.a: OMOTENASHI原计划执行两次确定性机动，这将使其成为首个以约30米/秒的速度进行半硬着陆的立方星卫星。
faq_zh.q: OMOTENASHI原计划如何在月球着陆？

faq_en.a: Both missions were amongst the 10 CubeSats flying in NASA’s Artemis 1 mission. Artemis 1 was delayed on multiple occasions from August 2022, eventually launching in November 2022.
faq_en.q: How were EQUULEUS and OMOTENASHI launched?

faq_en.a: EQUULEUS will be the first mission to measure the LIF at the far side of the moon from EML2 and the first nano-spacecraft to control trajectory within the Sun-Earth-Moon region.
faq_en.q: What makes the EQUULEUS mission unique?

faq_en.a: OMOTENASHI was set to perform two deterministic manoeuvres which would have made it the first CubeSat satellite to perform a semi-hard moon landing, at approximately 30 m/s.
faq_en.q: How would OMOTENASHI have landed on the moon?

eoportal.name: EROS-A (Earth Remote Observation System-A)
eoportal.summary: EROS-A launched in December 2000 and completed its mission in July 2016.

faq.a: EROS-A was the first satellite launched on 5 December 2000, followed by EROS-B which launched on 25 April 2006. Both were launched from Svobodny Cosmodrome, Launch Complex-5 in eastern Siberia. EROS-C was cancelled after being postponed several times.
faq.q: Which was the first EROS satellite?

faq.a: EROS-C was cancelled because of the development of the latest EROS satellite, the EROS-NG.
faq.q: Why was EROS-C cancelled?

faq.a: Depending on quality and quantity, the price could vary.
faq.q: How much would customers pay for EROS-A images?

eoportal.summary_zh: EROS-A 卫星于2000年12月发射，并于2016年7月完成任务。
eoportal.summary_en: EROS-A launched in December 2000 and completed its mission in July 2016.

faq_zh.a: EROS-A 是第一颗于2000年12月5日发射的卫星，随后是2006年4月25日发射的 EROS-B。两者均从西伯利亚东部的 Svobodny 航天发射场 LC-5 发射。EROS-C 在多次推迟后被取消。
faq_zh.q: 哪颗是第一颗 EROS 卫星？

faq_zh.a: EROS-C 因最新一代 EROS 卫星 EROS-NG 的研发而被取消。
价格会根据影像质量和数量而有所不同。
faq_zh.q: 客户购买 EROS-A 影像需要支付多少费用？

faq_en.a: EROS-A was the first satellite launched on 5 December 2000, followed by EROS-B which launched on 25 April 2006. Both were launched from Svobodny Cosmodrome, Launch Complex-5 in eastern Siberia. EROS-C was cancelled after being postponed several times.
faq_en.q: Which was the first EROS satellite?

faq_en.a: EROS-C was cancelled because of the development of the latest EROS satellite, the EROS-NG.
faq_en.q: Why was EROS-C cancelled?

faq_en.a: Depending on quality and quantity, the price could vary.

eoportal.name: EROS-B (Earth Remote Observation System-B)
eoportal.summary:
Table of contents
EROS-B (Earth Remote Observation System-B)
Last updated:
Feb 27, 2026

EO

|

Operational (extended)

|

ISI

The Earth Remote Observation System-B (EROS-B) is the second satellite in the EROS series, and provides high-resolution imagery used for emergency monitoring, illegal construction supervision, agricultural monitoring, oil and gas exploration and infrastructure monitoring.

faq.a: EROS-B, as well as the wider EROS satellite series, are owned and operated by ImageSat International N.V. (ISI). ISI is a joint venture between Israel Aircraft Industries Ltd (IAI), El-Op (the company that supplied the optical payload for EROS-B), and Core Software Technology (CST).
faq.q: Who operates the EROS-B satellite?

faq.a:
EROS-B obtains high-resolution panchromatic imagery of the Earth’s surface. This imagery has many applications including emergency monitoring, illegal construction supervision, agricultural monitoring, oil and gas exploration and infrastructure monitoring.

In the 2016 Olympic and Paralympic Games, Rio, EROS-B provided high-resolution images (up to 50 cm over a 450 km area) which were used for monitoring and security purposes.
faq.q: What is the purpose of the EROS-B satellite?

faq.a: EROS-B was launched on 25 April, 2006 from the Svobodny Cosmodrome Launch Complex-5 in eastern Siberia on a Start-1 launch vehicle.
faq.q: When was EROS-B launched?

eoportal.summary_zh: EROS-B（地球遥感观测系统-B）是EROS系列的第二颗卫星，提供高分辨率图像，可用于应急监测、违章建筑监督、农业监测、油气勘探和基础设施监测。
eoportal.summary_en:
Table of contents
EROS-B (Earth Remote Observation System-B)

ISI

The Earth Remote Observation System-B (EROS-B) is the second satellite in the EROS series, and provides high-resolution imagery used for emergency monitoring, illegal construction supervision, agricultural monitoring, oil and gas exploration and infrastructure monitoring.

faq_zh.a: EROS-B以及更广泛的EROS卫星系列均由ImageSat International N.V. (ISI)拥有和运营。ISI是以色列飞机工业有限公司（IAI）、El-Op（为EROS-B提供光学载荷的公司）和Core Software Technology (CST)的合资企业。

EROS-B获取地球表面的高分辨率全色图像。这些图像具有多种应用，包括应急监测、违章建筑监督、农业监测、油气勘探和基础设施监测。

在2016年里约奥运会和残奥会期间，EROS-B提供了高分辨率图像（在450公里区域内分辨率高达50厘米），用于监测和安保目的。
EROS-B于2006年4月25日从西伯利亚东部的Svobodny航天发射场第5号发射综合体使用Start-1运载火箭发射升空。

faq_en.a: EROS-B, as well as the wider EROS satellite series, are owned and operated by ImageSat International N.V. (ISI). ISI is a joint venture between Israel Aircraft Industries Ltd (IAI), El-Op (the company that supplied the optical payload for EROS-B), and Core Software Technology (CST).
faq_en.q: Who operates the EROS-B satellite?

faq_en.a:
EROS-B obtains high-resolution panchromatic imagery of the Earth’s surface. This imagery has many applications including emergency monitoring, illegal construction supervision, agricultural monitoring, oil and gas exploration and infrastructure monitoring.

In the 2016 Olympic and Paralympic Games, Rio, EROS-B provided high-resolution images (up to 50 cm over a 450 km area) which were used for monitoring and security purposes.
faq_en.q: What is the purpose of the EROS-B satellite?

faq_en.a: EROS-B was launched on 25 April, 2006 from the Svobodny Cosmodrome Launch Complex-5 in eastern Siberia on a Start-1 launch vehicle.
faq_en.q: When was EROS-B launched?

eoportal.name: ERS-1 (European Remote-Sensing Satellite-1)
taxonomy: Cloud type, amount and cloud top temperature
Ocean imagery and water leaving spectral radiance
Precipitation intensity at the surface (liquid or solid)
Sea ice cover, edge and thickness
eoportal.summary: The mission detected land and ocean surface change and provided observation data on oceans, polar ice, vegetation, geology, meteorology and ecology.

faq.a: ERS-1 was launched in July 1991 on an Ariarne-4 launch vehicle. Operations ceased in March 2000 after an onboard attitude control system, where the gyro failed which led to an inability to manoeuvre the aircraft into emergency acquisition mode.
faq.q: When was ERS-1 operational?

faq.a: ERS-1 had the purpose of providing various forms of environmental monitoring data. This includes monitoring land, sea, atmosphere, snow and ice, as well as gravitational and magnetic fields, to provide valuable insights into several disciplines of research, including climate change.
faq.q: What is the purpose of ERS-1?

faq.a: ERS-1 included an Active Microwave Instrument (AMI), a Radar Altimeter-1 (RA-1), an Along-Track Scanning Radiometer and Microwave Sounder-1 (ATSR-1), a Laser Retro-Reflector (LRR) and a Precise Range and Range-Rate Equipment (PRARE) which were used together to generate various different forms of data.
faq.q: What sensors are on board of ERS-1?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该任务探测了陆地和海洋表面变化，并提供了海洋、极地冰盖、植被、地质、气象和生态方面的观测数据。
eoportal.summary_en: The mission detected land and ocean surface change and provided observation data on oceans, polar ice, vegetation, geology, meteorology and ecology.

faq_zh.a: ERS-1 于 1991 年 7 月由阿里安-4 运载火箭发射升空。由于星上姿态控制系统的陀螺仪发生故障，导致卫星无法机动进入应急数据采集模式，其运行于 2000 年 3 月停止。
faq_zh.q: ERS-1 何时处于运行状态？

faq_zh.a: ERS-1 的目的是提供多种形式的环境监测数据。这包括对陆地、海洋、大气、冰雪以及重力和磁场的监测，为气候变化等多个研究领域提供宝贵信息。
ERS-1 包括一台主动微波仪器（AMI）、一台雷达高度计（RA-1）、一台沿轨扫描辐射计和微波探测器（ATSR-1）、一台激光后向反射器（LRR）以及一台精确测距和测速设备（PRARE），这些仪器协同工作以生成多种不同形式的数据。
faq_zh.q: ERS-1 上搭载了哪些传感器？

faq_en.a: ERS-1 was launched in July 1991 on an Ariarne-4 launch vehicle. Operations ceased in March 2000 after an onboard attitude control system, where the gyro failed which led to an inability to manoeuvre the aircraft into emergency acquisition mode.
faq_en.q: When was ERS-1 operational?

faq_en.a: ERS-1 had the purpose of providing various forms of environmental monitoring data. This includes monitoring land, sea, atmosphere, snow and ice, as well as gravitational and magnetic fields, to provide valuable insights into several disciplines of research, including climate change.
faq_en.q: What is the purpose of ERS-1?

faq_en.a: ERS-1 included an Active Microwave Instrument (AMI), a Radar Altimeter-1 (RA-1), an Along-Track Scanning Radiometer and Microwave Sounder-1 (ATSR-1), a Laser Retro-Reflector (LRR) and a Precise Range and Range-Rate Equipment (PRARE) which were used together to generate various different forms of data.
faq_en.q: What sensors are on board of ERS-1?

eoportal.name: ERS-2 (European Remote-Sensing Satellite-2)
taxonomy: Cloud type, amount and cloud top temperature
Ocean imagery and water leaving spectral radiance
Precipitation intensity at the surface (liquid or solid)
Sea ice cover, edge and thickness
eoportal.summary: ERS-2 provided microwave spectrum environmental monitoring across a range of disciplines (oceans, polar ice, forestry).

faq.a: The key difference between ERS-1 and ERS-2 was in the sensor complement on board the spacecraft. ERS-2 utilised an updated Along-Track Scanning Radiometer and Microwave Sounder (ATSR-2) as well as the addition of a Global Ozone Monitoring Experiment (GOME) which in tandem provided greater data coverage than its predecessor.
faq.q: What were the key differences between ERS-1 and ERS-2?

faq.a: The purpose of ERS-2 was to continue the environmental monitoring across various disciplines which ERS-1 was able to achieve. This included land, sea, atmospheric, snow and ice, vegetation and gravitational and magnetic field data to provide valuable insights into the research of Earth processes, particularly in the field of climate change.
faq.q: What was the purpose of ERS-2?

faq.a: ERS-2 included an Active Microwave Instrument (AMI), a Radar Altimeter-1 (RA-1), an Along-Track Scanning Radiometer and Microwave Sounder-2 (ATSR-2), a Laser Retro-Reflector (LRR), Precise Range and Range-Rate Equipment (PRARE) and a Global Ozone Monitoring Experiment (GOME).
faq.q: What sensors are found on board of ERS-2?

eoportal.summary_zh: ERS-2卫星提供了跨多个学科（海洋、极地冰盖、林业）的微波频谱环境监测。
eoportal.summary_en: ERS-2 provided microwave spectrum environmental monitoring across a range of disciplines (oceans, polar ice, forestry).

faq_zh.a: ERS-1和ERS-2之间的主要区别在于星上搭载的传感器组合。ERS-2使用了更新版的沿轨扫描辐射计和微波探测器（ATSR-2），并新增了全球臭氧监测实验仪（GOME），两者协同工作，提供了比前身更广泛的数据覆盖范围。
faq_zh.q: ERS-1和ERS-2之间的主要区别是什么？

faq_zh.a: ERS-2的目的是延续ERS-1所实现的跨学科环境监测。这包括陆地、海洋、大气、冰雪、植被以及重力场和磁场数据，为研究地球过程，特别是在气候变化领域，提供了宝贵的见解。
ERS-2搭载了主动微波仪器（AMI）、雷达高度计-1（RA-1）、沿轨扫描辐射计和微波探测器-2（ATSR-2）、激光后向反射器（LRR）、精确测距和测距率设备（PRARE）以及全球臭氧监测实验仪（GOME）。

faq_en.a: The key difference between ERS-1 and ERS-2 was in the sensor complement on board the spacecraft. ERS-2 utilised an updated Along-Track Scanning Radiometer and Microwave Sounder (ATSR-2) as well as the addition of a Global Ozone Monitoring Experiment (GOME) which in tandem provided greater data coverage than its predecessor.
faq_en.q: What were the key differences between ERS-1 and ERS-2?

faq_en.a: The purpose of ERS-2 was to continue the environmental monitoring across various disciplines which ERS-1 was able to achieve. This included land, sea, atmospheric, snow and ice, vegetation and gravitational and magnetic field data to provide valuable insights into the research of Earth processes, particularly in the field of climate change.
faq_en.q: What was the purpose of ERS-2?

faq_en.a: ERS-2 included an Active Microwave Instrument (AMI), a Radar Altimeter-1 (RA-1), an Along-Track Scanning Radiometer and Microwave Sounder-2 (ATSR-2), a Laser Retro-Reflector (LRR), Precise Range and Range-Rate Equipment (PRARE) and a Global Ozone Monitoring Experiment (GOME).
faq_en.q: What sensors are found on board of ERS-2?

eoportal.summary_en: Launched in September 2020, its mission was to improve ship tracking and maritime traffic monitoring through advanced AIS (Automatic Identification System) data collection.

eoportal.name: EscaPADE (Escape and Plasma Acceleration and Dynamics Explorers)
eoportal.agency: NASA, University of California, Berkeley (UC Berkeley)
eoportal.summary: EscaPADE consists of two identical spacecraft, nicknamed ‘Blue’ and ‘Gold’, both provided by Rocket Lab.

faq.a: EscaPADE (Escape and Plasma Acceleration and Dynamics Explorers) is a dual-spacecraft Martian mission of NASA and the University of California Berkeley that aims to investigate Mars’ hybrid magnetosphere and atmospheric escape.
faq.q: What is the EscaPADE mission?

faq.a: Rocket Lab constructed the spacecraft for EscaPADE, utilising the Photon platform modified for the Martian mission.
faq.q: Who built the EscaPADE spacecraft?

faq.a: The primary science mission duration will last 11 months, which is split into two science campaigns lasting six and five months each.
faq.q: How long is the EscaPADE mission duration?

eoportal.name_zh: EscaPADE（逃逸与等离子体加速及动力学探测器）
eoportal.agency_zh: NASA、加州大学伯克利分校 (UC Berkeley)
eoportal.summary_zh: EscaPADE 由两艘完全相同的航天器组成，分别昵称为“蓝色”和“金色”，均由火箭实验室 (Rocket Lab) 提供。
eoportal.summary_en: EscaPADE consists of two identical spacecraft, nicknamed ‘Blue’ and ‘Gold’, both provided by Rocket Lab.

faq_zh.a: EscaPADE（逃逸与等离子体加速及动力学探测器）是 NASA 与加州大学伯克利分校联合开展的双航天器火星任务，旨在研究火星的混合磁层和大气逃逸现象。
火箭实验室 (Rocket Lab) 为 EscaPADE 制造了航天器，使用了为火星任务改进的光子 (Photon) 平台。
faq_zh.q: 谁制造了 EscaPADE 航天器？

faq_zh.a: 主要科学任务阶段将持续 11 个月，分为两次科学活动，每次分别持续 6 个月和 5 个月。
faq_zh.q: EscaPADE 任务的持续时间是多久？

faq_en.a: EscaPADE (Escape and Plasma Acceleration and Dynamics Explorers) is a dual-spacecraft Martian mission of NASA and the University of California Berkeley that aims to investigate Mars’ hybrid magnetosphere and atmospheric escape.
faq_en.q: What is the EscaPADE mission?

faq_en.a: Rocket Lab constructed the spacecraft for EscaPADE, utilising the Photon platform modified for the Martian mission.
faq_en.q: Who built the EscaPADE spacecraft?

faq_en.a: The primary science mission duration will last 11 months, which is split into two science campaigns lasting six and five months each.
faq_en.q: How long is the EscaPADE mission duration?

The European Space Agency's Euclid mission, launched in July 2023, aims to explore the mysteries of dark energy and dark matter by analyzing the shapes and distributions of galaxies, to create the most detailed 3D map of the universe to date, enhancing our understanding of cosmic expansion and the large-scale structure of the cosmos.

欧洲航天局（ESA）的Euclid任务于2023年7月发射，旨在通过分析星系的形状和分布来探索暗能量和暗物质的奥秘，从而绘制迄今为止最详细的宇宙三维地图，增进我们对宇宙膨胀和宇宙大尺度结构的理解。

• Demonstrate two-way intersatellite crosslink with verified data exchange

• Demonstrate autonomous thruster operation using single-antenna on-orbit real-time GPS attitude determination.

FLEX is a fluorescence imaging satellite operated by ESA as an Earth Explorer mission, which will run in tandem with Sentinel-3 to map global photosynthetic activity.

faq.a: FLEX has onboard an instrument called FLuORescence Imaging Spectrometer (FLORIS) which measures how fluorescent plants are, which is directly related to their health and stress.
faq.q: What does FLEX measure?

faq.a: Plants absorb energy from the sun to perform photosynthesis (the process by which plants convert water and carbon dioxide to create oxygen and energy). A portion of this energy is then re-emitted as invisible but measurable fluorescence.
faq.q: Why are plants fluorescent?

faq.a: ESA’s Earth Explorer Missions are a set of missions dedicated to addressing major challenges identified by society. Five missions have been or are in operation (GOCE, SMOS, CryoSat, Swarm, Aeolus) and five further missions are approved for development  (EarthCARE, Biomass, FLEX, FORUM, Harmony).
faq.q: What are ESA's Earth Explorer Missions

eoportal.summary_zh: FLEX（荧光探测器）是由ESA运营的一项地球探测者任务，是一颗用于测量植物荧光的成像卫星。该卫星将与哨兵-3（Sentinel-3）协同运行，旨在绘制全球光合作用活动图。

FLEX is a fluorescence imaging satellite operated by ESA as an Earth Explorer mission, which will run in tandem with Sentinel-3 to map global photosynthetic activity.

faq_zh.a: FLEX搭载了一台名为荧光成像光谱仪（FLORIS）的仪器，用于测量植物的荧光强度。这种荧光强度与植物的健康状况及胁迫程度直接相关。
植物吸收太阳能量进行光合作用（即植物将水和二氧化碳转化为氧气和能量的过程）。其中一部分能量会以不可见但可测量的荧光形式重新发射出来。
faq_zh.q: 植物为什么会发出荧光？

faq_zh.a: ESA的地球探测者任务是一系列致力于解决社会所关注重大挑战的专项任务。目前已有五项任务已经或正在运行（GOCE、SMOS、CryoSat、Swarm、Aeolus），另有五项任务已获批进行开发（EarthCARE、Biomass、FLEX、FORUM、Harmony）。
faq_zh.q: ESA的地球探测者任务是什么？

faq_en.a: FLEX has onboard an instrument called FLuORescence Imaging Spectrometer (FLORIS) which measures how fluorescent plants are, which is directly related to their health and stress.
faq_en.q: What does FLEX measure?

faq_en.a: Plants absorb energy from the sun to perform photosynthesis (the process by which plants convert water and carbon dioxide to create oxygen and energy). A portion of this energy is then re-emitted as invisible but measurable fluorescence.
faq_en.q: Why are plants fluorescent?

faq_en.a: ESA’s Earth Explorer Missions are a set of missions dedicated to addressing major challenges identified by society. Five missions have been or are in operation (GOCE, SMOS, CryoSat, Swarm, Aeolus) and five further missions are approved for development  (EarthCARE, Biomass, FLEX, FORUM, Harmony).
faq_en.q: What are ESA's Earth Explorer Missions

eoportal.name: Flying Laptop
eoportal.summary: Additionally, FLP carries scientific instruments for multispectral Earth observation as well as spaceborne maritime ship surveillance.

faq.a: Flying Laptop is equipped with ten new technologies, including a new Lithium iron phosphate battery, a high precision altitude control system (ACS) and an experimental laser downlink system, OSIRISv1.
faq.q: What microsatellite technologies is the Flying Laptop testing?

faq.a: The Flying Laptop is operated by the IRS (Institute of Space Systems - Institut für Raumfahrtsysteme) at the University of Stuttgart, Germany.
faq.q: Who operates the Flying Laptop satellite?

faq.a: OSIRISv1 was designed by the DLR Institute of Communications and Navigation, with assistance from the Institute of Space Systems at the University of Stuttgart.
faq.q: Who designed the OSIRIS laser system?

eoportal.summary_zh: 此外，Flying Laptop 还搭载了用于多光谱地球观测的科学仪器，以及用于星基海事船舶监视的载荷。
eoportal.summary_en: Additionally, FLP carries scientific instruments for multispectral Earth observation as well as spaceborne maritime ship surveillance.

faq_zh.a: Flying Laptop 测试了十项新技术，包括新型磷酸铁锂电池、高精度姿态控制系统（ACS）以及一个实验性激光下行链路系统 OSIRISv1。
faq_zh.q: Flying Laptop 正在测试哪些微卫星技术？

faq_zh.a: Flying Laptop 由德国斯图加特大学空间系统研究所（IRS - Institut für Raumfahrtsysteme）运营。
faq_zh.q: 谁运营 Flying Laptop 卫星？

faq_zh.a: OSIRISv1 由德国宇航中心（DLR）通信与导航研究所设计，斯图加特大学空间系统研究所提供了协助。
faq_zh.q: OSIRIS 激光系统由谁设计？

faq_en.a: Flying Laptop is equipped with ten new technologies, including a new Lithium iron phosphate battery, a high precision altitude control system (ACS) and an experimental laser downlink system, OSIRISv1.
faq_en.q: What microsatellite technologies is the Flying Laptop testing?

faq_en.a: The Flying Laptop is operated by the IRS (Institute of Space Systems - Institut für Raumfahrtsysteme) at the University of Stuttgart, Germany.
faq_en.q: Who operates the Flying Laptop satellite?

faq_en.a: OSIRISv1 was designed by the DLR Institute of Communications and Navigation, with assistance from the Institute of Space Systems at the University of Stuttgart.
faq_en.q: Who designed the OSIRIS laser system?

eoportal.name: FOREST Satellite Series (OroraTech)
eoportal.summary: The series first launched with FOREST-1 in January 2022.

faq.a: The FOREST satellites utilize thermal infrared imaging and AI-powered onboard processing to detect fires in near real-time, significantly reducing response times compared to traditional satellite systems.
faq.q: How does the FOREST satellite constellation improve wildfire detection?

faq.a: FOREST-3 features a three-channel thermal infrared system, an optical imaging instrument, and AI-powered real-time data processing.
faq.q: What makes FOREST-3 more advanced than its predecessors?

faq.a: OroraTech, a Munich-based New Space intelligence operates the FOREST satellites, which served as technology demonstrators for their fire monitoring satellite constellation.
faq.q: Who operates the FOREST satellites?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该系列于2022年1月首次发射了FOREST-1卫星。
eoportal.summary_en: The series first launched with FOREST-1 in January 2022.

faq_zh.a: FOREST 卫星利用热红外成像和人工智能驱动的星上处理技术，实现近实时的火点探测，与传统卫星系统相比，显著缩短了响应时间。
faq_zh.q: FOREST 卫星星座如何改进野火探测？

faq_zh.a: FOREST-3 配备了三通道热红外系统、光学成像仪以及人工智能驱动的实时数据处理能力。
faq_zh.q: 是什么让 FOREST-3 比前代卫星更先进？

faq_zh.a: 位于慕尼黑的太空新创情报公司 OroraTech 运营着 FOREST 卫星，这些卫星是其火灾监测卫星星座的技术验证平台。

faq_en.a: The FOREST satellites utilize thermal infrared imaging and AI-powered onboard processing to detect fires in near real-time, significantly reducing response times compared to traditional satellite systems.
faq_en.q: How does the FOREST satellite constellation improve wildfire detection?

faq_en.a: FOREST-3 features a three-channel thermal infrared system, an optical imaging instrument, and AI-powered real-time data processing.
faq_en.q: What makes FOREST-3 more advanced than its predecessors?

faq_en.a: OroraTech, a Munich-based New Space intelligence operates the FOREST satellites, which served as technology demonstrators for their fire monitoring satellite constellation.
faq_en.q: Who operates the FOREST satellites?

faq.q: Who built the components for the FormoSat-5 mission?

faq.a: The FormoSat-5 spacecraft was launched in August 2017 on a SpaceX vehicle.
faq.q: How was FormoSat-5 launched?

faq.a: FormoSat-5 uses a Remote Sensing Imager (RSI) and an Advanced Ionospheric Probe (AIP).

faq_en.a: NSPO took full responsibility for the design and analysis of the satellite’s systems and subsystems, component development, as well as payload integration and test.
faq_en.q: Who built the components for the FormoSat-5 mission?

faq_en.a: The FormoSat-5 spacecraft was launched in August 2017 on a SpaceX vehicle.
faq_en.q: How was FormoSat-5 launched?

faq_en.a: FormoSat-5 uses a Remote Sensing Imager (RSI) and an Advanced Ionospheric Probe (AIP).

eoportal.summary: The series objectives are to provide global measurements of 3-D temperature and moisture soundings, cloud and precipitation parameters, meteorological and hydrological events, biosphere anomalies and geophysical parameters in support of global change and climate monitoring.

faq.a: FenYung-3 (FY-3) series is the second generation of Chinese polar-orbiting meteorological satellites, following on from the FY-1 series.
faq.q: What is the FY-3 series?

faq.a: The FY-3 series consists of 10 satellites: FY-3A to -3J. FY-3A has been decommissioned, FY-3B, FY-3C, FY-3D, FY-3E, FY-3F and FY-3G are currently operational and, FY-3H, FY-3I and FY-3J are planned to be launched in the coming years.
faq.q: How many satellites are in the FY-3 series?

faq.a: The FY-3 series objectives are to provide global measurements of 3-D temperature and moisture soundings, cloud and precipitation parameters, meteorological and hydrological events, biosphere anomalies and geophysical parameters in support of global change and climate monitoring.
faq.q: What are FY-3 satellites used for?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该系列卫星的目标是提供全球性的三维温度与湿度廓线、云和降水参数、气象与水文事件、生物圈异常及地球物理参数的测量数据，以支持全球变化和气候监测。
eoportal.summary_en: The series objectives are to provide global measurements of 3-D temperature and moisture soundings, cloud and precipitation parameters, meteorological and hydrological events, biosphere anomalies and geophysical parameters in support of global change and climate monitoring.

faq_zh.a: 风云-3（FY-3）系列是中国第二代极轨气象卫星，是风云-1系列的后续。
风云-3系列包含10颗卫星：FY-3A至FY-3J。其中，FY-3A已退役；FY-3B、FY-3C、FY-3D、FY-3E、FY-3F和FY-3G目前在轨运行；FY-3H、FY-3I和FY-3J计划在未来几年发射。
faq_zh.q: 风云-3系列包含多少颗卫星？

faq_zh.a: 风云-3系列卫星的目标是提供全球性的三维温度与湿度廓线、云和降水参数、气象与水文事件、生物圈异常及地球物理参数的测量数据，以支持全球变化和气候监测。
faq_zh.q: 风云-3卫星的用途是什么？

faq_en.a: FenYung-3 (FY-3) series is the second generation of Chinese polar-orbiting meteorological satellites, following on from the FY-1 series.
faq_en.q: What is the FY-3 series?

faq_en.a: The FY-3 series consists of 10 satellites: FY-3A to -3J. FY-3A has been decommissioned, FY-3B, FY-3C, FY-3D, FY-3E, FY-3F and FY-3G are currently operational and, FY-3H, FY-3I and FY-3J are planned to be launched in the coming years.
faq_en.q: How many satellites are in the FY-3 series?

faq_en.a: The FY-3 series objectives are to provide global measurements of 3-D temperature and moisture soundings, cloud and precipitation parameters, meteorological and hydrological events, biosphere anomalies and geophysical parameters in support of global change and climate monitoring.
faq_en.q: What are FY-3 satellites used for?

eoportal.name: GEISAT Constellation
eoportal.summary: The mission’s predecessor, GEISAT Precursor, was launched in June 2023 and is planned to be joined by the GEISAT Constellation.

faq.a: The GEISAT Constellation is under development by the Spanish private space company SATLANTIS.
faq.q: Who is developing the GEISAT Constellation?

faq.a: There are three planned satellites in the GEISAT Constellation, plus the GEISAT Precursor satellite launched in June 2023.
faq.q: How many satellites are in the GEISAT Constellation?

faq.a: GEISAT is aimed at observing and quantifying methane (CH4) on the point-source level from the oil & gas industry with Very High Resolution (VHR) instrumentation capable of pinpointing the exact location of emissions.
faq.q: What will the GEISAT satellites be used for?

eoportal.name: GeoCARB (Geostationary Carbon Cycle Observatory)
eoportal.summary: As the second airborne investigation in NASA’s Earth Venture-Mission series, the mission aimed to measure the concentrations of carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide and methane in the air above South and North America, and to assess vegetation health levels, ultimately advancing the current understanding of Earth's natural exchanges of carbon between land, atmosphere and ocean.

faq.a: GeoCARB would have made daily measurements of the carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, methane and Solar-Induced Fluorescence quantities in the American atmosphere under all weather conditions, allowing scientists to gain a better understanding of the carbon cycle and to monitor the vegetation health in regions of interest.
faq.q: What would GeoCARB have done?

faq.a: The carbon cycle describes how carbon transfers between different reservoirs located on Earth. This cycle is important for maintaining a stable climate and carbon balance on Earth.
faq.q: What is the role of the carbon cycle?

faq.a: Though the instruments are similar in terms of the technology used to determine the greenhouse gases concentrations, the difference in orbit is crucial. OCO-2 samples a narrow 10 km track over much of the globe on a 16 day repeat cycle, while GeoCarb would have looked at the terrestrial Western Hemisphere continuously from a fixed position, scanning most of this land mass at least once per day.
faq.q: How was GeoCARB different from the mission where its technology was pioneered, OCO-2?

eoportal.name_zh: GeoCARB（地球静止轨道碳循环观测卫星）
eoportal.summary_zh: 作为NASA地球探索任务系列中的第二次机载研究，该任务旨在测量南、北美洲上空的二氧化碳、一氧化碳和甲烷浓度，并评估植被健康水平，最终增进当前对地球陆地、大气和海洋之间自然碳交换的理解。
eoportal.summary_en: As the second airborne investigation in NASA’s Earth Venture-Mission series, the mission aimed to measure the concentrations of carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide and methane in the air above South and North America, and to assess vegetation health levels, ultimately advancing the current understanding of Earth's natural exchanges of carbon between land, atmosphere and ocean.

faq_zh.a: GeoCARB本应在所有天气条件下，每日对美洲大气中的二氧化碳、一氧化碳、甲烷和太阳诱导荧光量进行测量，使科学家能够更好地了解碳循环并监测感兴趣区域的植被健康状况。
碳循环描述了碳如何在地球上不同的储存库之间转移。这一循环对于维持地球稳定的气候和碳平衡至关重要。
尽管在确定温室气体浓度的技术方面仪器相似，但轨道的差异至关重要。OCO-2在全球大部分地区以16天的重复周期对狭窄的10公里宽幅进行采样，而GeoCARB本应从固定位置持续观测西半球陆地，每天至少对这片陆地的大部分区域进行一次扫描。
faq_zh.q: GeoCARB与其技术奠基任务OCO-2有何不同？

faq_en.a: GeoCARB would have made daily measurements of the carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, methane and Solar-Induced Fluorescence quantities in the American atmosphere under all weather conditions, allowing scientists to gain a better understanding of the carbon cycle and to monitor the vegetation health in regions of interest.
faq_en.q: What would GeoCARB have done?

faq_en.a: The carbon cycle describes how carbon transfers between different reservoirs located on Earth. This cycle is important for maintaining a stable climate and carbon balance on Earth.
faq_en.q: What is the role of the carbon cycle?

faq_en.a: Though the instruments are similar in terms of the technology used to determine the greenhouse gases concentrations, the difference in orbit is crucial. OCO-2 samples a narrow 10 km track over much of the globe on a 16 day repeat cycle, while GeoCarb would have looked at the terrestrial Western Hemisphere continuously from a fixed position, scanning most of this land mass at least once per day.
faq_en.q: How was GeoCARB different from the mission where its technology was pioneered, OCO-2?

eoportal.name: GeoEye-1 (OrbView-5)
eoportal.summary: GeoEye-1 imagery is used for various applications, including but not limited to air and marine transportation, defence, disaster response, oil and gas exploration, mining exploration and production, mapping of remote regions, insurance and risk management, location-based services and agricultural crop management.

faq.a: GeoEye-1 satellite imagery is used for various applications, including but not limited to defence, disaster response, air and marine transportation, oil and gas exploration, mining production and exploration, mapping of remote regions, location based services, insurance and risk management, and agricultural crop management.
faq.q: What is GeoEye-1 used for?

faq.a: Maxar Technologies currently owns and operates GeoEye-1. GeoEye-1 was originally designed and built by a commercial company named GeoEye, which was merged with DigitalGlobe, which has since been acquired by Maxar.
faq.q: Who owns GeoEye-1?

faq.a: GeoEye-1 is capable of acquiring image data at 0.50 m panchromatic (B&W) and 1.84 m multispectral resolution. It has the ability to locate an object within just three metres of its physical location and features a revisit time of fewer than three days.
faq.q: What does GeoEye-1 do?

eoportal.summary_zh: GeoEye-1卫星影像被广泛应用于多种领域，包括但不限于空中与海上交通、国防、灾害响应、石油与天然气勘探、采矿勘探与生产、偏远地区制图、保险与风险管理、基于位置的服务以及农业作物管理。
eoportal.summary_en: GeoEye-1 imagery is used for various applications, including but not limited to air and marine transportation, defence, disaster response, oil and gas exploration, mining exploration and production, mapping of remote regions, insurance and risk management, location-based services and agricultural crop management.

faq_zh.a: GeoEye-1卫星影像可用于多种应用，包括但不限于国防、灾害响应、空中与海上交通、石油与天然气勘探、采矿生产与勘探、偏远地区制图、基于位置的服务、保险与风险管理以及农业作物管理。
Maxar Technologies目前拥有并运营GeoEye-1卫星。该卫星最初由一家名为GeoEye的商业公司设计和建造，后与DigitalGlobe合并，而DigitalGlobe已被Maxar收购。
GeoEye-1能够以0.50米的全色（黑白）分辨率和1.84米的多光谱分辨率采集图像数据。它能在目标物体实际位置仅三米的范围内定位目标，并且重访周期不到三天。

faq_en.a: GeoEye-1 satellite imagery is used for various applications, including but not limited to defence, disaster response, air and marine transportation, oil and gas exploration, mining production and exploration, mapping of remote regions, location based services, insurance and risk management, and agricultural crop management.
faq_en.q: What is GeoEye-1 used for?

faq_en.a: Maxar Technologies currently owns and operates GeoEye-1. GeoEye-1 was originally designed and built by a commercial company named GeoEye, which was merged with DigitalGlobe, which has since been acquired by Maxar.
faq_en.q: Who owns GeoEye-1?

faq_en.a: GeoEye-1 is capable of acquiring image data at 0.50 m panchromatic (B&W) and 1.84 m multispectral resolution. It has the ability to locate an object within just three metres of its physical location and features a revisit time of fewer than three days.
faq_en.q: What does GeoEye-1 do?

eoportal.name: GEOS (Geodetic Earth Orbiting Satellite) NASA
eoportal.summary: The mission consisted of three spacecraft: GEOS-1 (Geodetic Earth Orbiting Satellite-1) which operated from November 1965 to January 1968, GEOS-2 (Geodetic Earth Orbiting Satellite-2) which operated from January 1968 to January 1970, and GEOS-3 (Geodynamic Experimental Ocean Satellite) which operated from April 1975 to July 1979.

faq.a: The GEOS mission consisted of three satellites, GEOS-1 (Geodetic Earth Orbiting Satellite-1), GEOS-2 (Geodetic Earth Orbiting Satellite-2), and GEOS-3 (Geodynamic Experimental Ocean Satellite).
faq.q: How many GEOS satellites were there?

faq.a: GEOS-1 was launched on 6 November 1965 from Cape Canaveral, Florida, with a Thor-Delta E launch vehicle and ended operations in January 1968. GEOS-2 was launched on 11 January 1968 from Vandenberg Air Force Base, with a Thor-Delta E1 launch vehicle and ended operations in January 1970. GEOS-3 was launched on 9 April 1975 at Vandenberg Air Force Base with a Delta 1410 launch vehicle and operated until July 1979.
faq.q: When did GEOS satellites operate?

faq.a: Data from the GEOS-1 and GEOS-2 satellites helped to define the structure of the Earth's irregular gravitational field and refine the locations and magnitudes of the large gravity anomalies. GEOS-3 provided the first comprehensive coverage of ocean height over most areas of the world's oceans, providing a better understanding of the ocean geoid.
faq.q: What data did the GEOS missions provide?

eoportal.name_zh: GEOS (地球大地测量轨道卫星) NASA
eoportal.summary_zh: 该任务包含三颗航天器：GEOS-1（地球大地测量轨道卫星-1号），于1965年11月至1968年1月运行；GEOS-2（地球大地测量轨道卫星-2号），于1968年1月至1970年1月运行；以及GEOS-3（地球动力学实验海洋卫星），于1975年4月至1979年7月运行。
eoportal.summary_en: The mission consisted of three spacecraft: GEOS-1 (Geodetic Earth Orbiting Satellite-1) which operated from November 1965 to January 1968, GEOS-2 (Geodetic Earth Orbiting Satellite-2) which operated from January 1968 to January 1970, and GEOS-3 (Geodynamic Experimental Ocean Satellite) which operated from April 1975 to July 1979.

faq_zh.a: GEOS任务包含三颗卫星：GEOS-1（地球大地测量轨道卫星-1号）、GEOS-2（地球大地测量轨道卫星-2号）和GEOS-3（地球动力学实验海洋卫星）。
GEOS-1于1965年11月6日从佛罗里达州卡纳维拉尔角发射，使用Thor-Delta E运载火箭，并于1968年1月结束运行。GEOS-2于1968年1月11日从范登堡空军基地发射，使用Thor-Delta E1运载火箭，并于1970年1月结束运行。GEOS-3于1975年4月9日在范登堡空军基地发射，使用Delta 1410运载火箭，运行至1979年7月。
faq_zh.q: GEOS卫星的运行时间是多久？

faq_zh.a: GEOS-1和GEOS-2卫星的数据帮助定义了地球不规则重力场的结构，并精化了大型重力异常的位置和强度。GEOS-3首次提供了对世界大部分海洋区域的海洋高度综合覆盖，从而增进了对海洋大地水准面的理解。

faq_en.a: The GEOS mission consisted of three satellites, GEOS-1 (Geodetic Earth Orbiting Satellite-1), GEOS-2 (Geodetic Earth Orbiting Satellite-2), and GEOS-3 (Geodynamic Experimental Ocean Satellite).
faq_en.q: How many GEOS satellites were there?

faq_en.a: GEOS-1 was launched on 6 November 1965 from Cape Canaveral, Florida, with a Thor-Delta E launch vehicle and ended operations in January 1968. GEOS-2 was launched on 11 January 1968 from Vandenberg Air Force Base, with a Thor-Delta E1 launch vehicle and ended operations in January 1970. GEOS-3 was launched on 9 April 1975 at Vandenberg Air Force Base with a Delta 1410 launch vehicle and operated until July 1979.
faq_en.q: When did GEOS satellites operate?

faq_en.a: Data from the GEOS-1 and GEOS-2 satellites helped to define the structure of the Earth's irregular gravitational field and refine the locations and magnitudes of the large gravity anomalies. GEOS-3 provided the first comprehensive coverage of ocean height over most areas of the world's oceans, providing a better understanding of the ocean geoid.
faq_en.q: What data did the GEOS missions provide?

eoportal.name: GEOS (GEOstationary Scientific Satellite), ESA
eoportal.summary: They monitored Earth’s magnetic field until 1978 and 1983 respectively.

faq.a: ESA-GEOS 2 was the first geostationary satellite dedicated completely to scientific measurements.
faq.q: What was so significant about ESA-GEOS 2?

faq.a: Magnetic fields, electric fields, plasma interactions with the magnetic field, and electron pitch-angles.
faq.q: What did the ESA-GEOS satellites measure?

faq.a: The launch vehicle experienced a separation problem between stages two and three and as such wasn’t able to obtain a geostationary orbit as planned.
faq.q: Why did ESA-GEOS 1 enter an elliptical orbit?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该卫星分别监测地球磁场至1978年和1983年。

faq_zh.a: ESA-GEOS 2是首颗完全致力于科学测量的地球同步卫星。
磁场、电场、等离子体与磁场的相互作用以及电子投掷角。
运载火箭在第二级和第三级之间出现分离问题，因此未能按计划进入地球同步轨道。

eoportal.name: GEOSAT (Geodetic/Geophysical Satellite)
eoportal.summary: GEOSAT launched in 1985 and ended in 1990.

faq.a: Though initially generating classified information for the US Navy, GEOSAT was obtained by NASA and used for precise measurements of the ocean surface, allowing for long-term observations and studies of fronts and eddies, winds, waves, and ocean surface/ice topography. This provided valuable contributions to physical oceanography, geodesy, geophysics, and glaciology.
faq.q: What is GEOSAT used for?

faq.a: GEOSAT was initially operated by the US Navy for its period of life referred to as its Geodetic Mission (GM), where it provided precise oceanic topography data for use by submarine commanders. Later, the satellite was obtained and operated by NASA for its Exact Repeat Mission (ERM), where it was used for long-term global observations of oceanic topography for use in scientific research.
faq.q: Who operated GEOSAT?

faq.a: The GEOSAT spacecraft was able to maintain its orientation so that it was pointing directly towards the ground to within 1⁰ 98% of the time. Its orbit generated a grid of observation of 4 km width
faq.q: How accurate is GEOSAT data?

eoportal.agency_zh: NASA, 美国海军研究实验室 (US Naval Research Lab)
eoportal.summary_zh: GEOSAT 于 1985 年发射，1990 年结束任务。
eoportal.summary_en: GEOSAT launched in 1985 and ended in 1990.

faq_zh.a: 尽管最初为美国海军生成机密信息，但 GEOSAT 后来被 NASA 获取并用于对海面进行精确测量，从而能够长期观测和研究锋面与涡旋、风、浪以及海面/冰盖地形。这为物理海洋学、大地测量学、地球物理学和冰川学做出了宝贵贡献。
GEOSAT 最初由美国海军在其被称为“大地测量任务”(GM) 的寿命周期内操作，为潜艇指挥官提供精确的海洋地形数据。后来，该卫星被 NASA 获取并操作，用于其“精确重复任务”(ERM)，进行全球海洋地形的长期观测以供科学研究使用。
GEOSAT 航天器能够保持其方向，在 98% 的时间内精确指向地面，偏差在 1° 以内。其轨道生成的观测网格宽度为 4 公里。

faq_en.a: Though initially generating classified information for the US Navy, GEOSAT was obtained by NASA and used for precise measurements of the ocean surface, allowing for long-term observations and studies of fronts and eddies, winds, waves, and ocean surface/ice topography. This provided valuable contributions to physical oceanography, geodesy, geophysics, and glaciology.
faq_en.q: What is GEOSAT used for?

faq_en.a: GEOSAT was initially operated by the US Navy for its period of life referred to as its Geodetic Mission (GM), where it provided precise oceanic topography data for use by submarine commanders. Later, the satellite was obtained and operated by NASA for its Exact Repeat Mission (ERM), where it was used for long-term global observations of oceanic topography for use in scientific research.
faq_en.q: Who operated GEOSAT?

faq_en.a: The GEOSAT spacecraft was able to maintain its orientation so that it was pointing directly towards the ground to within 1⁰ 98% of the time. Its orbit generated a grid of observation of 4 km width
faq_en.q: How accurate is GEOSAT data?

taxonomy: Cloud type, amount and cloud top temperature

eoportal.summary: Designed to provide continuity for the GOES-R series, GeoXO will provide enhanced weather, air quality, and ocean monitoring to address threats from extreme weather and climate change, benefiting public safety and environmental protection.

faq.a: The GeoXO constellation is planned to replace the current GOES-R series and will enhance monitoring capabilities for weather, oceans, and air quality.
faq.q: Will GeoXO replace GOES-R?

faq.a: GeoXO will be operated by NOAA, with support from NASA during the development phase.
faq.q: Who operates GeoXO?

faq.a: Three satellites are planned for the GeoXO Constellation, Geo-West, Geo-East, and Geo-Central, each positioned at different locations over the Americas in geostationary orbit.
faq.q: How many satellites are in the GeoXO Constellation?

eoportal.name_zh: GeoXO（地球静止轨道扩展观测卫星）
eoportal.agency_zh: NOAA（美国国家海洋和大气管理局）
eoportal.summary_zh: GeoXO旨在为GOES-R系列卫星提供延续性，将提供增强的天气、空气质量和海洋监测能力，以应对极端天气和气候变化带来的威胁，从而惠及公共安全与环境保护。
eoportal.summary_en: Designed to provide continuity for the GOES-R series, GeoXO will provide enhanced weather, air quality, and ocean monitoring to address threats from extreme weather and climate change, benefiting public safety and environmental protection.

faq_zh.a: GeoXO星座计划取代当前的GOES-R系列，并将增强对天气、海洋和空气质量的监测能力。
GeoXO将由NOAA运营，在开发阶段由NASA提供支持。
GeoXO星座计划部署三颗卫星：Geo-West、Geo-East和Geo-Central，每颗卫星都将定位在美洲上空地球静止轨道的不同位置。

faq_en.a: The GeoXO constellation is planned to replace the current GOES-R series and will enhance monitoring capabilities for weather, oceans, and air quality.

faq_en.a: GeoXO will be operated by NOAA, with support from NASA during the development phase.

faq_en.a: Three satellites are planned for the GeoXO Constellation, Geo-West, Geo-East, and Geo-Central, each positioned at different locations over the Americas in geostationary orbit.

eoportal.name: GESat (Gas à Effet de Serre Satellite)
eoportal.summary: The first generation GESat mission, GESat Gen1, launched in January 2025 and serves as a demonstration satellite.

faq.a: The GESat constellation is designed to collect facility-scale data on CH₄ and CO₂ emissions, providing valuable insights for environmental scientists, regulatory bodies enforcing the European Climate Law, and environmental agencies.
faq.q: What is the objective of the GESat Constellation?

faq.a: GESat Gen1 uses a SWIR Fizeau interferometer, a high resolution spectrometer that scans the atmosphere to detect and measure methane emissions. The GESat Gen2 satellite will be equipped with an hyperspectral SWIR instrument capable of detecting both methane and carbon dioxide emissions.
faq.q: How do the GESat Gen1 and GESat Gen2 satellites detect greenhouse gas emissions?

faq.a: GESat Gen2 will feature a next-generation shortwave infrared spectrometer instrument, the HSI SWIR, which features a cryo-cooled Mercury Cadmium Telluride (MCT) sensor. This next-generation technology allows GESat Gen2 to detect both methane and carbon dioxide emissions across a wider range of wavelengths and swaths.
faq.q: How does GESat Gen2 differ from GESat Gen1?

eoportal.summary_zh: 第一代 GESat 任务 GESat Gen1 于 2025 年 1 月发射，是一颗演示验证卫星。
eoportal.summary_en: The first generation GESat mission, GESat Gen1, launched in January 2025 and serves as a demonstration satellite.

faq_zh.a: GESat 星座旨在收集设施尺度的甲烷和二氧化碳排放数据，为环境科学家、执行欧洲气候法的监管机构以及环境机构提供有价值的见解。
faq_zh.q: GESat 星座的目标是什么？

faq_zh.a: GESat Gen1 使用 SWIR 斐索干涉仪，这是一种高分辨率光谱仪，可扫描大气层以探测和测量甲烷排放。GESat Gen2 卫星将配备高光谱 SWIR 仪器，能够同时探测甲烷和二氧化碳排放。
faq_zh.q: GESat Gen1 和 GESat Gen2 卫星如何探测温室气体排放？

faq_zh.a: GESat Gen2 将搭载下一代短波红外光谱仪仪器 HSI SWIR，该仪器采用低温冷却的碲镉汞传感器。这项下一代技术使 GESat Gen2 能够在更宽的波长范围和扫描幅宽内探测甲烷和二氧化碳排放。
faq_zh.q: GESat Gen2 与 GESat Gen1 有何不同？

faq_en.a: The GESat constellation is designed to collect facility-scale data on CH₄ and CO₂ emissions, providing valuable insights for environmental scientists, regulatory bodies enforcing the European Climate Law, and environmental agencies.
faq_en.q: What is the objective of the GESat Constellation?

faq_en.a: GESat Gen1 uses a SWIR Fizeau interferometer, a high resolution spectrometer that scans the atmosphere to detect and measure methane emissions. The GESat Gen2 satellite will be equipped with an hyperspectral SWIR instrument capable of detecting both methane and carbon dioxide emissions.
faq_en.q: How do the GESat Gen1 and GESat Gen2 satellites detect greenhouse gas emissions?

faq_en.a: GESat Gen2 will feature a next-generation shortwave infrared spectrometer instrument, the HSI SWIR, which features a cryo-cooled Mercury Cadmium Telluride (MCT) sensor. This next-generation technology allows GESat Gen2 to detect both methane and carbon dioxide emissions across a wider range of wavelengths and swaths.
faq_en.q: How does GESat Gen2 differ from GESat Gen1?

faq.a: Gaofen-1 was launched on 26 April 2013 and, after an upgrade in 2016, is still in operation as of June 2022.

faq.a: Gaofen-1 is used specifically for detailed vegetation and forest monitoring, parameter generation, inland water monitoring and mineral resources exploration. It also has applications in flood mapping and management, observation of coastal zones, monitoring of land coverage, and agricultural applications.

eoportal.name: GF-3 (Gaofen-3)
eoportal.summary: Developed by the China National Space Administration (CNSA), it is a civilian satellite with primary users being the State Oceanic Administration (SOA), the China Meteorological Administration (CMA), the Ministry of Civil Affairs and the Ministry of Water Resources.

faq.a: The China National Space Administration (CNSA) has developed seven Gaofen satellites for the China High-Resolution Earth Observation System (CHEOS) series.
faq.q: How many Gaofen satellites are there?

faq.a: Gaofen-3, developed by the China National Space Administration (CNSA), was launched on 9 August 2016 from the Taiyuan Satellite Launch Center in China.
faq.q: Which country launched Earth Observation satellite Gaofen-3?

faq.a: Gaofen-3 was developed with the primary objective of presenting a preliminary quantitative result for wetland classification, but has broader applications in disaster monitoring, water conservancy, maritime and meteorology. Specific applications in these fields include shallow sea topography, extent of sea ice, drought scope, geological disaster prediction and warning, regional surface soil moisture monitoring, surface water distribution and characteristics of river basin systems.
faq.q: What is Gaofen-3 used for?

eoportal.summary_en: Developed by the China National Space Administration (CNSA), it is a civilian satellite with primary users being the State Oceanic Administration (SOA), the China Meteorological Administration (CMA), the Ministry of Civil Affairs and the Ministry of Water Resources.

由中国国家航天局（CNSA）研制的高分-3卫星，于2016年8月9日从中国太原卫星发射中心发射升空。
faq_zh.q: 哪个国家发射了对地观测卫星高分-3？

faq_zh.a: 高分-3卫星的研制初衷是为湿地分类提供初步的定量结果，但其应用范围更广，涵盖灾害监测、水利、海洋和气象等领域。在这些领域的具体应用包括浅海地形测量、海冰范围监测、干旱范围评估、地质灾害预测预警、区域地表土壤湿度监测、地表水分布以及流域系统特征分析。
faq_zh.q: 高分-3卫星是做什么用的？

faq_en.a: The China National Space Administration (CNSA) has developed seven Gaofen satellites for the China High-Resolution Earth Observation System (CHEOS) series.
faq_en.q: How many Gaofen satellites are there?

faq_en.a: Gaofen-3, developed by the China National Space Administration (CNSA), was launched on 9 August 2016 from the Taiyuan Satellite Launch Center in China.
faq_en.q: Which country launched Earth Observation satellite Gaofen-3?

faq_en.a: Gaofen-3 was developed with the primary objective of presenting a preliminary quantitative result for wetland classification, but has broader applications in disaster monitoring, water conservancy, maritime and meteorology. Specific applications in these fields include shallow sea topography, extent of sea ice, drought scope, geological disaster prediction and warning, regional surface soil moisture monitoring, surface water distribution and characteristics of river basin systems.
faq_en.q: What is Gaofen-3 used for?

eoportal.name: GHGSat Constellation
eoportal.summary: The first satellite, Iris (GHGSat-C1), launched in September 2020.

faq.a: The first satellite of the GHGSat constellation was launched in September 2020 and its name is  GHGSat-C1 (Iris).
faq.q: What is the name of the first GHGSat satellite to be launched?

faq.a: The GHGSat constellation is able to measure methane emissions to a high accuracy against the background. GHGSat has been able to measure methane from leaks and spills in oil and gas wells as well as methane emitted by cows in a paddock.
faq.q: What does GHGSat measure?

faq.a: The GHGSat constellation is owned and operated by GHGSat Inc which was founded in 2011 by Stephane Germain.
faq.q: Who operates the GHGSat constellation?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该星座的首颗卫星“Iris”（GHGSat-C1）于2020年9月发射。
eoportal.summary_en: The first satellite, Iris (GHGSat-C1), launched in September 2020.

faq_zh.a: GHGSat 星座的第一颗卫星于2020年9月发射，其名称为 GHGSat-C1（Iris）。
faq_zh.q: GHGSat 星座发射的第一颗卫星叫什么名字？

faq_zh.a: GHGSat 星座能够在背景中高精度测量甲烷排放。它已能够测量油气井泄漏和溢出的甲烷，以及牧场中奶牛排放的甲烷。
GHGSat 星座由 GHGSat Inc. 所有并运营，该公司由 Stephane Germain 于 2011 年创立。

faq_en.a: The first satellite of the GHGSat constellation was launched in September 2020 and its name is  GHGSat-C1 (Iris).
faq_en.q: What is the name of the first GHGSat satellite to be launched?

faq_en.a: The GHGSat constellation is able to measure methane emissions to a high accuracy against the background. GHGSat has been able to measure methane from leaks and spills in oil and gas wells as well as methane emitted by cows in a paddock.
faq_en.q: What does GHGSat measure?

faq_en.a: The GHGSat constellation is owned and operated by GHGSat Inc which was founded in 2011 by Stephane Germain.
faq_en.q: Who operates the GHGSat constellation?

eoportal.name: GHGSat-D (Greenhouse Gas Satellite - Demonstrator) / Claire
taxonomy: Cloud type, amount and cloud top temperature
eoportal.summary: The satellite completed  its mission in December 2024.

faq.a: GHGSat-D was a high-resolution remote sensing commercial microsatellite that detected and measured greenhouse gases from space. It provided unique emissions imagery and data to businesses, governments, regulators and investors worldwide to optimise their performance and uphold environmental standards.
faq.q: What did GHGSat-D do?

faq.a: Greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions are the sum of emissions of various gases that influence the greenhouse effect, including carbon dioxide (CO2), methane (CH4), nitrous oxide (N2O), and smaller trace gases such as hydrofluorocarbons (HFCs) and sulphur hexafluoride (SF6). GHGSat-D specifically measured carbon dioxide and methane emissions.
faq.q: What are GHG emissions and which GHG did GHGSat-D measure?

faq.a: GHGSat-D was launched on June 22, 2016 from Satish Dhawan Space Center (SDSC) in India as a secondary payload on the Indian Space Research Organisation‘s (ISRO's) launch of CartoSat-2C.
faq.q: When was GHGSat-D launched?

eoportal.name_zh: GHGSat-D (温室气体卫星 - 验证星) / Claire
eoportal.summary_zh: 该卫星于2024年12月完成了其任务。
eoportal.summary_en: The satellite completed  its mission in December 2024.

faq_zh.a: GHGSat-D 是一颗高分辨率的商业遥感微卫星，用于从太空探测和测量温室气体。它为全球的企业、政府、监管机构和投资者提供了独特的排放影像和数据，以优化其运营并维护环境标准。
温室气体排放是指各种影响温室效应的气体排放总和，包括二氧化碳、甲烷、一氧化二氮，以及氢氟碳化物和六氟化硫等微量气体。GHGSat-D 具体测量了二氧化碳和甲烷的排放。
faq_zh.q: 什么是温室气体排放？GHGSat-D 测量了哪些气体？

faq_zh.a: GHGSat-D 于2016年6月22日从印度萨迪什·达万航天中心发射，作为印度空间研究组织发射 CartoSat-2C 的次级载荷。

faq_en.a: GHGSat-D was a high-resolution remote sensing commercial microsatellite that detected and measured greenhouse gases from space. It provided unique emissions imagery and data to businesses, governments, regulators and investors worldwide to optimise their performance and uphold environmental standards.
faq_en.q: What did GHGSat-D do?

faq_en.a: Greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions are the sum of emissions of various gases that influence the greenhouse effect, including carbon dioxide (CO2), methane (CH4), nitrous oxide (N2O), and smaller trace gases such as hydrofluorocarbons (HFCs) and sulphur hexafluoride (SF6). GHGSat-D specifically measured carbon dioxide and methane emissions.
faq_en.q: What are GHG emissions and which GHG did GHGSat-D measure?

faq_en.a: GHGSat-D was launched on June 22, 2016 from Satish Dhawan Space Center (SDSC) in India as a secondary payload on the Indian Space Research Organisation‘s (ISRO's) launch of CartoSat-2C.
faq_en.q: When was GHGSat-D launched?

eoportal.name: GHOSt (Global Hyperspectral Observation Satellite)
eoportal.summary: GHOSt aims to build on its predecessor Aurora to provide enhanced commercial data sets used in industrial, government, agriculture, environmental and defence sectors.

faq.a: GHOSt constellation is planned to comprise six technically identical microsatellites.
faq.q: How many GHOSt satellites are there?

faq.a: GHOSt is a planned hyperspectral constellation by Orbital Sidekick Inc. (OSK), with the spacecraft to be built by Astro Digital.
faq.q: Who is GHOSt developed by?

faq.a: GHOSt aims to provide enhanced hyperspectral imagery for use in industrial, government, agriculture, environmental and defence sectors. Specific key applications of its use include capturing critical environmental and situational awareness information for defence, detecting minute real-time changes in physical infrastructure, monitoring energy infrastructure and regulatory compliance, detecting environmental erosion and degradation, and monitoring crop yield and agriculture.
faq.q: What is the purpose of GHOSt?

eoportal.summary_zh: GHOSt旨在其前身Aurora的基础上，提供用于工业、政府、农业、环境和国防领域的增强型商业数据集。
eoportal.summary_en: GHOSt aims to build on its predecessor Aurora to provide enhanced commercial data sets used in industrial, government, agriculture, environmental and defence sectors.

faq_zh.a: GHOSt星座计划由六颗技术相同的微型卫星组成。
GHOSt是由Orbital Sidekick Inc. (OSK)规划的高光谱星座，其航天器由Astro Digital制造。
GHOSt旨在为工业、政府、农业、环境和国防领域提供增强的高光谱图像。其关键应用包括：为国防捕捉关键的环境和态势感知信息、检测物理基础设施的微小实时变化、监测能源基础设施和法规遵从性、探测环境侵蚀和退化、以及监测作物产量和农业。

faq_en.a: GHOSt constellation is planned to comprise six technically identical microsatellites.
faq_en.q: How many GHOSt satellites are there?

faq_en.a: GHOSt is a planned hyperspectral constellation by Orbital Sidekick Inc. (OSK), with the spacecraft to be built by Astro Digital.
faq_en.q: Who is GHOSt developed by?

faq_en.a: GHOSt aims to provide enhanced hyperspectral imagery for use in industrial, government, agriculture, environmental and defence sectors. Specific key applications of its use include capturing critical environmental and situational awareness information for defence, detecting minute real-time changes in physical infrastructure, monitoring energy infrastructure and regulatory compliance, detecting environmental erosion and degradation, and monitoring crop yield and agriculture.
faq_en.q: What is the purpose of GHOSt?

eoportal.name: GLIMR (Geostationary Littoral Imaging and Monitoring Radiometer)
taxonomy: Ocean imagery and water leaving spectral radiance
eoportal.summary: GLIMR will be the first Hyperspectral ocean colour sensor in the Western Hemisphere to monitor phytoplankton evolution, harmful algal blooms, coral bleaching, and oil spills.

faq.a: The GLIMR satellite will most frequently image the Gulf of Mexico. It will also target the United States coastal waters, the Amazon River Plume, as well as regions of interest in the Caribbean Sea, Hawaii and South Pacific waters. Adaptive scheduling will allow GLIMR to target episodic events & hazards.
faq.q: What regions will the GLIMR satellite observe?

faq.a: The GLIMR project has built-in flexibility to adapt as scientists learn how best to use geostationary hyperspectral imagery to benefit science. GLIMR will play an important role in preparing the ocean colour scientific community for future operational geostationary hyperspectral capability such as the GeoXO Ocean Color Instrument (OCX). The data products from GLIMR will also have synergies with Hyperspectral PACE & SBG Missions.
faq.q: Will GLIMR be useful for other Earth observation satellite missions?

faq.a: The GLIMR satellite has the potential for land and atmospheric applications such as measuring land surface reflectance and Air Quality Index. The GLIMR satellite may also be used to validate regional weather/climate models.
faq.q: Will the GLIMR satellite have other multidisciplinary applications?

eoportal.name_zh: GLIMR (地球静止轨道海岸带成像与监测辐射计)
eoportal.summary_zh: GLIMR将是西半球首颗用于监测浮游植物演化、有害藻华、珊瑚白化和石油泄漏的超光谱海洋水色传感器。
eoportal.summary_en: GLIMR will be the first Hyperspectral ocean colour sensor in the Western Hemisphere to monitor phytoplankton evolution, harmful algal blooms, coral bleaching, and oil spills.

faq_zh.a: GLIMR卫星最频繁观测的区域将是墨西哥湾。它还将以美国沿海水域、亚马逊河羽流以及加勒比海、夏威夷和南太平洋水域的感兴趣区域为目标。自适应调度将使GLIMR能够针对偶发事件和灾害进行观测。
GLIMR项目具有内在的灵活性，可以随着科学家们了解如何最好地利用地球静止轨道超光谱图像来服务科学而进行调整。GLIMR将在为海洋水色科学界准备未来运行的地球静止轨道超光谱能力（如GeoXO海洋水色仪器OCX）方面发挥重要作用。GLIMR的数据产品还将与PACE和SBG超光谱任务产生协同效应。
faq_zh.q: GLIMR对其他对地观测卫星任务有用吗？

faq_zh.a: GLIMR卫星在陆地和大气应用方面具有潜力，例如测量地表反射率和空气质量指数。GLIMR卫星还可用于验证区域天气/气候模型。
faq_zh.q: GLIMR卫星还会有其他多学科应用吗？

faq_en.a: The GLIMR satellite will most frequently image the Gulf of Mexico. It will also target the United States coastal waters, the Amazon River Plume, as well as regions of interest in the Caribbean Sea, Hawaii and South Pacific waters. Adaptive scheduling will allow GLIMR to target episodic events & hazards.
faq_en.q: What regions will the GLIMR satellite observe?

faq_en.a: The GLIMR project has built-in flexibility to adapt as scientists learn how best to use geostationary hyperspectral imagery to benefit science. GLIMR will play an important role in preparing the ocean colour scientific community for future operational geostationary hyperspectral capability such as the GeoXO Ocean Color Instrument (OCX). The data products from GLIMR will also have synergies with Hyperspectral PACE & SBG Missions.
faq_en.q: Will GLIMR be useful for other Earth observation satellite missions?

faq_en.a: The GLIMR satellite has the potential for land and atmospheric applications such as measuring land surface reflectance and Air Quality Index. The GLIMR satellite may also be used to validate regional weather/climate models.
faq_en.q: Will the GLIMR satellite have other multidisciplinary applications?

eoportal.summary: ITAR (International Traffic in Arms Regulations) and information proprietary to the companies involved.
eoportal.summary_zh: 该卫星的信息受到《国际武器贸易条例》（ITAR）及相关公司专有信息的限制。
eoportal.summary_en: ITAR (International Traffic in Arms Regulations) and information proprietary to the companies involved.

eoportal.name: GLOWS (Global L-band Observatory for Water Cycle Studies)

faq.a: The objective of GLOWS is to develop a new deployable meta-lens antenna that will continue the time series of L-band observations of soil moisture and ocean salinity using an Earth Venture class satellite.
faq.q: What are the objectives of the GLOWS mission?

faq.a: Preliminary results of this novel antenna design are very encouraging, and further validation of this technology opens the possibilities of further use of scalable, large aperture meta-lens antennas for other missions. Reduced costs and simplification of satellite bus requirements are the major advantages of this technology.
faq.q: What are the implications of the meta-lens reflectarray antenna technology used by the GLOWS mission?

faq.a: The major challenge of future low frequency microwave missions is to strike the correct balance between antenna size/design, performance, and cost/efficiency of operations.
faq.q: What are the major challenges of the GLOWS mission?

eoportal.name_zh: GLOWS（全球L波段水循环观测站）
eoportal.summary_zh: GLOWS旨在满足未来L波段任务的需求，以保持与欧洲空间局（ESA）的土壤湿度和海洋盐度（SMOS）卫星以及土壤湿度主被动（SMAP）卫星相同分辨率和精度的数据连续性。

faq_zh.a: GLOWS的目标是开发一种新型可展开超透镜天线，利用地球探测级卫星延续对土壤湿度和海洋盐度的L波段观测时间序列。
这项新颖天线设计的初步结果非常鼓舞人心，该技术的进一步验证为其他任务使用可扩展的大口径超透镜天线开辟了可能性。降低成本和简化卫星平台需求是该技术的主要优势。
faq_zh.q: GLOWS任务采用的超透镜反射阵列天线技术有何意义？

faq_zh.a: 未来低频微波任务的主要挑战在于如何在天线尺寸/设计、性能以及运营成本/效率之间取得正确的平衡。
faq_zh.q: GLOWS任务面临的主要挑战是什么？

faq_en.a: The objective of GLOWS is to develop a new deployable meta-lens antenna that will continue the time series of L-band observations of soil moisture and ocean salinity using an Earth Venture class satellite.
faq_en.q: What are the objectives of the GLOWS mission?

faq_en.a: Preliminary results of this novel antenna design are very encouraging, and further validation of this technology opens the possibilities of further use of scalable, large aperture meta-lens antennas for other missions. Reduced costs and simplification of satellite bus requirements are the major advantages of this technology.
faq_en.q: What are the implications of the meta-lens reflectarray antenna technology used by the GLOWS mission?

faq_en.a: The major challenge of future low frequency microwave missions is to strike the correct balance between antenna size/design, performance, and cost/efficiency of operations.
faq_en.q: What are the major challenges of the GLOWS mission?

eoportal.name: GOCE  (Gravity field and steady-state Ocean Circulation Explorer)
eoportal.summary: The mission ended in October 2013 after it ran out of fuel.

faq.a: GOCE ended its service on October 21, 2013, after it ran out of fuel.
faq.q: When did GOCE complete its mission?

faq.a: GOCE stands for Gravity Field and Steady-State Ocean Circulation Explorer.
faq.q: What does GOCE stand for?

faq.a: GOCE employed a Drag-Free and Attitude Control System (DFACS) and an Attitude and Orbit Control System (AOCS) using an ion propulsion engine for continuous, closed-loop counteraction of the drag caused by Earth's atmosphere to map the details of Earth’s gravity field.
faq.q: How did GOCE work?

eoportal.name_zh: GOCE（重力场与稳态海洋环流探测卫星）
eoportal.summary_zh: 该任务于2013年10月因燃料耗尽而结束。
eoportal.summary_en: The mission ended in October 2013 after it ran out of fuel.

faq_zh.a: GOCE于2013年10月21日因燃料耗尽而结束服务。
GOCE代表重力场与稳态海洋环流探测卫星。
GOCE采用无阻力与姿态控制系统（DFACS）以及姿态与轨道控制系统（AOCS），并利用离子推进发动机，持续、闭环地抵消地球大气层引起的阻力，以详细测绘地球重力场。

faq_en.a: GOCE ended its service on October 21, 2013, after it ran out of fuel.
faq_en.q: When did GOCE complete its mission?

faq_en.a: GOCE stands for Gravity Field and Steady-State Ocean Circulation Explorer.
faq_en.q: What does GOCE stand for?

faq_en.a: GOCE employed a Drag-Free and Attitude Control System (DFACS) and an Attitude and Orbit Control System (AOCS) using an ion propulsion engine for continuous, closed-loop counteraction of the drag caused by Earth's atmosphere to map the details of Earth’s gravity field.
faq_en.q: How did GOCE work?

eoportal.name: GOES (Geostationary Operational Environmental Satellite) 2nd Generation
taxonomy: Cloud type, amount and cloud top temperature
Ocean imagery and water leaving spectral radiance
Precipitation intensity at the surface (liquid or solid)
eoportal.summary: All five satellites were launched between 1994 and 2001, with a design lifetime of 7 years.

faq.a: GOES-8 was launched in April 1994, with GOES-9 in May 1995, GOES-10 in April 1997, GOES-11 in May 2000 and finally GOES-12 in July 2001.
faq.q: When were the GOES 2nd Generation satellites launched?

faq.a: The GOES 2nd Generation satellites were able to provide continuous and reliable storm warning systems, monitor Earth’s surface and space environment and provide distress signal detection.
faq.q: What did the GOES 2nd Generation satellites achieve?

faq.a: The GOES 2nd Generation Satellites were operated jointly by the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) and the National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA).
faq.q: Which agencies operated the GOES 2nd Generation Satellites?

eoportal.name_zh: 地球静止轨道环境业务卫星（GOES）第二代
eoportal.summary_zh: 所有五颗卫星在1994年至2001年间发射，设计寿命为7年。
eoportal.summary_en: All five satellites were launched between 1994 and 2001, with a design lifetime of 7 years.

faq_zh.a: GOES-8于1994年4月发射，GOES-9于1995年5月，GOES-10于1997年4月，GOES-11于2000年5月，最后是GOES-12于2001年7月发射。

faq_zh.q: GOES第二代卫星取得了什么成就？

faq_zh.a: GOES第二代卫星由美国国家海洋和大气管理局（NOAA）和美国国家航空航天局（NASA）联合运营。

faq_en.q: When were the GOES 2nd Generation satellites launched?

faq_en.a: The GOES 2nd Generation satellites were able to provide continuous and reliable storm warning systems, monitor Earth’s surface and space environment and provide distress signal detection.
faq_en.q: What did the GOES 2nd Generation satellites achieve?

faq_en.a: The GOES 2nd Generation Satellites were operated jointly by the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) and the National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA).

eoportal.name: GOES-N, O, P
taxonomy: Cloud type, amount and cloud top temperature
Ocean imagery and water leaving spectral radiance
Precipitation intensity at the surface (liquid or solid)
eoportal.summary: The missions launched in May 2006, June 2009 and March 2010 respectively, with GOES-N operations managed by the United States Space Force (USSF), with GOES-O and -P operations managed by the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA).

faq.a: GOES-N is managed by the United States Space Force (USSF), whilst GOES-O and GOES-P are managed by the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA), with partial mid-mission management by the National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA).
faq.q: Which agencies are responsible for GOES -N,-O and -P operations?

faq.a: The main goals of the GOES N-P missions are to maintain continuous and reliable storm warning systems to protect life and property, as well as monitor Earth’s surface and space environment with improved capabilities.
faq.q: What is the main purpose of the GOES N-P missions?

faq.a: GOES-N was launched on 24 May 2006, with GOES-O launched on 27 June 2009 and GOES-P on 4 March 2010.
faq.q: When were the satellites launched?

eoportal.summary_zh: 这些任务分别于2006年5月、2009年6月和2010年3月发射。其中，GOES-N的运营由美国太空军（USSF）管理，而GOES-O和GOES-P的运营则由美国国家海洋和大气管理局（NOAA）负责。
eoportal.summary_en: The missions launched in May 2006, June 2009 and March 2010 respectively, with GOES-N operations managed by the United States Space Force (USSF), with GOES-O and -P operations managed by the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA).

faq_zh.a: GOES-N由美国太空军（USSF）管理，而GOES-O和GOES-P由美国国家海洋和大气管理局（NOAA）管理，美国国家航空航天局（NASA）在任务中期会参与部分管理工作。
faq_zh.q: 哪些机构负责GOES-N、-O和-P的运营？

faq_zh.a: GOES N-P任务的主要目标是维护连续可靠的风暴预警系统以保护生命和财产，并利用增强的能力监测地球表面和空间环境。
faq_zh.q: GOES N-P任务的主要目的是什么？

faq_zh.a: GOES-N于2006年5月24日发射，GOES-O于2009年6月27日发射，GOES-P于2010年3月4日发射。
faq_zh.q: 这些卫星是什么时候发射的？

faq_en.a: GOES-N is managed by the United States Space Force (USSF), whilst GOES-O and GOES-P are managed by the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA), with partial mid-mission management by the National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA).
faq_en.q: Which agencies are responsible for GOES -N,-O and -P operations?

faq_en.a: The main goals of the GOES N-P missions are to maintain continuous and reliable storm warning systems to protect life and property, as well as monitor Earth’s surface and space environment with improved capabilities.
faq_en.q: What is the main purpose of the GOES N-P missions?

faq_en.a: GOES-N was launched on 24 May 2006, with GOES-O launched on 27 June 2009 and GOES-P on 4 March 2010.
faq_en.q: When were the satellites launched?

eoportal.name: GOES-R (Geostationary Operational Environmental Satellite-R)
taxonomy: Cloud type, amount and cloud top temperature
Downward long-wave irradiance at Earth surface
Precipitation intensity at the surface (liquid or solid)
Downward short-wave irradiance at Earth surface
eoportal.summary: The satellites launched for this series are GOES-R, launched in November 2016, GOES-S in March 2018, GOES-T in March 2022 and GOES-U, in June 2024.

faq.a: GOES-R stands for Geostationary Operational Environmental Satellite-R, and was subsequently designated GOES-16 after its launch.
faq.q: What does GOES-R stand for?

faq.a: GOES-R is designed to be operational for 10 years, with 5 years as an on-orbit spare.
faq.q: How long is GOES-R scheduled to run?

faq.a: GOES-R is primarily used as a geostationary meteorological imager, which is able to detect weather events on Earth, including lightning detection through cloud cover.
faq.q: What is the purpose of GOES-R?

eoportal.name_zh: GOES-R (地球静止轨道环境卫星-R)
eoportal.summary_zh: 该系列已发射的卫星包括：2016年11月发射的GOES-R、2018年3月发射的GOES-S、2022年3月发射的GOES-T以及2024年6月发射的GOES-U。
eoportal.summary_en: The satellites launched for this series are GOES-R, launched in November 2016, GOES-S in March 2018, GOES-T in March 2022 and GOES-U, in June 2024.

faq_zh.a: GOES-R是地球静止轨道环境卫星-R的缩写，发射后被重新编号为GOES-16。
GOES-R的设计运行寿命为10年，并有5年的在轨备份期。
faq_zh.q: GOES-R的设计运行寿命是多久？

faq_zh.a: GOES-R主要用作地球静止轨道气象成像仪，能够探测地球上的天气事件，包括穿透云层进行闪电探测。

faq_en.a: GOES-R stands for Geostationary Operational Environmental Satellite-R, and was subsequently designated GOES-16 after its launch.
faq_en.q: What does GOES-R stand for?

faq_en.a: GOES-R is designed to be operational for 10 years, with 5 years as an on-orbit spare.
faq_en.q: How long is GOES-R scheduled to run?

faq_en.a: GOES-R is primarily used as a geostationary meteorological imager, which is able to detect weather events on Earth, including lightning detection through cloud cover.

eoportal.name: Gokturk-1 Imaging Mission
eoportal.summary: The satellite has a wide range of military and civilian applications including reconnaissance, environmental monitoring, crop management, cadastral surveys, natural disaster damage assessment, and management of natural resources.

faq.a: Göktürk-1 is operated by the Turkish Ministry of National Defense and built by Telescpazio.
faq.q: Who operates Göktürk-1?

faq.a: Göktürk-1 was launched on December 5, 2016 from Kourou on the Vega VV08 vehicle of Arianespace.
faq.q: When was Göktürk-1 launched?

faq.a: Göktürk-1 has a wide range of military and civilian applications, including reconnaissance, homeland surveillance, intelligence, environmental monitoring, forest control, cadastral surveys, identifying illegal construction sites, crop management, damage assessment after natural disasters, and management of natural resources.
faq.q: What is Göktürk-1 used for?

eoportal.agency_zh: TUBITAK（土耳其科学技术研究委员会）
eoportal.summary_zh: 该卫星拥有广泛的军民两用功能，可用于侦察、环境监测、作物管理、地籍测绘、自然灾害损害评估以及自然资源管理。
eoportal.summary_en: The satellite has a wide range of military and civilian applications including reconnaissance, environmental monitoring, crop management, cadastral surveys, natural disaster damage assessment, and management of natural resources.

faq_zh.a: Göktürk-1 由土耳其国防部运营，由 Telespazio 公司建造。
Göktürk-1 于2016年12月5日由 Arianespace（阿丽亚娜航天公司）的 Vega VV08 火箭从库鲁发射升空。
Göktürk-1 用途广泛，涵盖军事与民用领域，包括侦察、国土监视、情报获取、环境监测、森林管理、地籍测绘、非法建筑识别、作物管理、自然灾害后损害评估以及自然资源管理。

faq_en.a: Göktürk-1 is operated by the Turkish Ministry of National Defense and built by Telescpazio.
faq_en.q: Who operates Göktürk-1?

faq_en.a: Göktürk-1 was launched on December 5, 2016 from Kourou on the Vega VV08 vehicle of Arianespace.
faq_en.q: When was Göktürk-1 launched?

faq_en.a: Göktürk-1 has a wide range of military and civilian applications, including reconnaissance, homeland surveillance, intelligence, environmental monitoring, forest control, cadastral surveys, identifying illegal construction sites, crop management, damage assessment after natural disasters, and management of natural resources.
faq_en.q: What is Göktürk-1 used for?
