eoportal.name: GOLIAT CubeSat
eoportal.summary: About 75% of the orbit is in sunlight.
eoportal.summary_zh: 该卫星约75%的轨道处于光照条件下。
eoportal.summary_en: About 75% of the orbit is in sunlight.

eoportal.name: GOMX-3 (GomSpace Express-3)
eoportal.summary: The satellite was in orbit for one year before ending its mission in October 2016.

faq.a: GOMX-3 collected data from aircraft and satellites. Consistently calculated the position of these other craft, but other data transmitted could also be collected. For example, wind and weather data from aircraft is relayed to GOMX-3, which allows weather forecasting to be achieved in remote areas. Another example may be the assessment of a satellite’s status.
faq.q: What did GOMX-3 do?

faq.a: GNSS stands for Global Navigation Satellite Systems, and refers to any satellite network that provides global positioning services.
faq.q: What does GNSS mean?

faq.a: GOMX-3 was a CubeSat nanosatellite. Cubesat are a class of miniature satellites built to standard dimensions of 10 cm x 10 cm x 10 cm. Cubes may stack, creating a classification system of 1U, 2U, 3U, etc.
faq.q: What type of satellite was GOMX-3?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该卫星在轨运行一年后，于2016年10月结束任务。
eoportal.summary_en: The satellite was in orbit for one year before ending its mission in October 2016.

faq_zh.a: GOMX-3收集来自飞机和卫星的数据。它持续计算其他飞行器的位置，同时也能收集传输的其他数据。例如，飞机传输的风力和天气数据会被中继至GOMX-3，这使得在偏远地区进行天气预报成为可能。另一个例子可能是评估卫星的状态。
GNSS代表全球导航卫星系统，指的是任何提供全球定位服务的卫星网络。
GOMX-3是一颗立方体纳卫星。立方体卫星是一类按照标准尺寸10厘米×10厘米×10厘米建造的微型卫星。多个立方体可以堆叠，从而形成1U、2U、3U等分类系统。

faq_en.a: GOMX-3 collected data from aircraft and satellites. Consistently calculated the position of these other craft, but other data transmitted could also be collected. For example, wind and weather data from aircraft is relayed to GOMX-3, which allows weather forecasting to be achieved in remote areas. Another example may be the assessment of a satellite’s status.
faq_en.q: What did GOMX-3 do?

faq_en.a: GNSS stands for Global Navigation Satellite Systems, and refers to any satellite network that provides global positioning services.
faq_en.q: What does GNSS mean?

faq_en.a: GOMX-3 was a CubeSat nanosatellite. Cubesat are a class of miniature satellites built to standard dimensions of 10 cm x 10 cm x 10 cm. Cubes may stack, creating a classification system of 1U, 2U, 3U, etc.
faq_en.q: What type of satellite was GOMX-3?

eoportal.name: GomX-4 (GomSpace Express-4)
eoportal.summary: Both satellites were launched in February 2018 from JSLC (Jiuquan Satellite Launch Centre) in China.

faq.a: GomX-4 was used as a technological demonstration mission which utilised the 6U CubeSat form factor. This demonstration mission aimed to show that miniaturised technologies are viable and that there is a future for nanosatellite constellations to be launched.
faq.q: What was the main purpose of GomX-4 satellites?

faq.a: GomX-4 consisted of two satellites, GomX-4A and GomX-4B. They were both launched in February 2018 on board a Long March 2D launch vehicle from Jiuquan Satellite Launch Centre (JSLC) in China.
faq.q: When was GomX-4 launched?

faq.a: The agencies responsible for GomX-4A included GomSpace ApS and the Danish Defence Acquisition and Logistics Organisation (DALO), as part of the Danish Ministry of Defence. Agencies responsible for GomX-4B included GomSpace ApS, Technical University of Denmark (TUD) and the European Space Agency (ESA).
faq.q: What agencies were responsible for managing the GomX-4 satellites?

eoportal.agency_zh: ESA（欧洲航天局）, NSO（荷兰太空办公室）
eoportal.summary_zh: 两颗卫星均于2018年2月从中国酒泉卫星发射中心发射升空。
eoportal.summary_en: Both satellites were launched in February 2018 from JSLC (Jiuquan Satellite Launch Centre) in China.

faq_zh.a: GomX-4是一项利用6U立方体卫星形式进行的技术演示任务。该演示旨在证明微型化技术是可行的，并展示纳米卫星星座发射的未来前景。
faq_zh.q: GomX-4卫星的主要目的是什么？

faq_zh.a: GomX-4由GomX-4A和GomX-4B两颗卫星组成。它们均于2018年2月搭载长征二号丁运载火箭从中国酒泉卫星发射中心发射升空。
负责GomX-4A的机构包括GomSpace ApS和丹麦国防部下属的丹麦国防采购与后勤组织。负责GomX-4B的机构包括GomSpace ApS、丹麦技术大学和欧洲航天局（ESA）。
faq_zh.q: 哪些机构负责管理GomX-4卫星？

faq_en.a: GomX-4 was used as a technological demonstration mission which utilised the 6U CubeSat form factor. This demonstration mission aimed to show that miniaturised technologies are viable and that there is a future for nanosatellite constellations to be launched.
faq_en.q: What was the main purpose of GomX-4 satellites?

faq_en.a: GomX-4 consisted of two satellites, GomX-4A and GomX-4B. They were both launched in February 2018 on board a Long March 2D launch vehicle from Jiuquan Satellite Launch Centre (JSLC) in China.
faq_en.q: When was GomX-4 launched?

faq_en.a: The agencies responsible for GomX-4A included GomSpace ApS and the Danish Defence Acquisition and Logistics Organisation (DALO), as part of the Danish Ministry of Defence. Agencies responsible for GomX-4B included GomSpace ApS, Technical University of Denmark (TUD) and the European Space Agency (ESA).
faq_en.q: What agencies were responsible for managing the GomX-4 satellites?

eoportal.name: GOMX-5 (GomSpace Express-5)
eoportal.summary: This 5-year mission aims to demonstrate new nanosatellite capabilities involving communication and maneuverability.

faq.a: GOMX-5 is expected to launch by the end of 2023.

faq.a: GOMX-5 is to demonstrate new CubeSat capabilities: increased communication rates, maneuverability, and position accuracy.

faq.a: GOMX-5 is operated by GomSpace but largely funded by ESA.

eoportal.summary_en: This 5-year mission aims to demonstrate new nanosatellite capabilities involving communication and maneuverability.

GOMX-5旨在演示新型立方星能力：提升通信速率、机动性能和定位精度。
GOMX-5由GomSpace公司运营，但主要资金来自ESA。

eoportal.name: GOSAT (Greenhouse gases Observing Satellite) / Ibuki
taxonomy: Cloud type, amount and cloud top temperature
Chlorophyll Fluorescence from Vegetation on Land
eoportal.summary: This mission monitors Greenhouse Gases (GHGs) sources and sinks on a subcontinental scale.

faq.a: The Japan Aerospace Exploration Agency (JAXA) successfully launched GOSAT, the first spacecraft to measure concentrations of carbon dioxide and methane, in January 2009.
faq.q: Which country launched the world's first satellite dedicated to monitoring greenhouse Gas emissions?

faq.a: GOSAT carries TANSO-FTS, a Fourier Transform Spectrometer,  which measures the scene radiance of SWIR reflected by Earth’s surface, and detects the strength of  methane (CH4) absorption lines in the measured spectrum.
faq.q: How does GOSAT detect methane?

faq.a: Through their Third Party Missions (TPM) Programme, the European Space Agency (ESA) allows registered users to access GOSAT TANSO-FTS and -CAI full archive and new products.
faq.q: Can I access GOSAT data?

eoportal.name_zh: GOSAT（温室气体观测卫星）/ Ibuki
eoportal.agency_zh: JAXA（日本宇宙航空研究开发机构）、MOE（日本环境省）、NIES（日本国立环境研究所）
eoportal.summary_zh: 该任务旨在次大陆尺度上监测温室气体（GHGs）的源与汇。
eoportal.summary_en: This mission monitors Greenhouse Gases (GHGs) sources and sinks on a subcontinental scale.

faq_zh.a: 日本宇宙航空研究开发机构（JAXA）于2009年1月成功发射了GOSAT，这是首颗能够测量二氧化碳和甲烷浓度的卫星。
faq_zh.q: 哪个国家发射了世界上第一颗专门用于监测温室气体排放的卫星？

faq_zh.a: GOSAT搭载了TANSO-FTS（傅里叶变换光谱仪），该仪器测量地球表面反射的短波红外（SWIR）场景辐射，并探测测量光谱中甲烷（CH4）吸收线的强度。
通过其第三方任务（TPM）计划，欧洲空间局（ESA）允许注册用户访问GOSAT TANSO-FTS和-CAI的完整存档及新产品。

faq_en.a: The Japan Aerospace Exploration Agency (JAXA) successfully launched GOSAT, the first spacecraft to measure concentrations of carbon dioxide and methane, in January 2009.
faq_en.q: Which country launched the world's first satellite dedicated to monitoring greenhouse Gas emissions?

faq_en.a: GOSAT carries TANSO-FTS, a Fourier Transform Spectrometer,  which measures the scene radiance of SWIR reflected by Earth’s surface, and detects the strength of  methane (CH4) absorption lines in the measured spectrum.
faq_en.q: How does GOSAT detect methane?

faq_en.a: Through their Third Party Missions (TPM) Programme, the European Space Agency (ESA) allows registered users to access GOSAT TANSO-FTS and -CAI full archive and new products.
faq_en.q: Can I access GOSAT data?

faq.a: The full form of GOSAT-2 is Greenhouse Gas Observing Satellite-2.

faq.a: The Japan Aerospace Exploration Agency (JAXA) owns and operates GOSAT-2.

eoportal.name_zh: GOSAT-2 (温室气体观测卫星-2) / Ibuki-2
eoportal.agency_zh: 日本宇宙航空研究开发机构 (JAXA)，日本环境省 (MOE)，日本国立环境研究所 (NIES)

eoportal.summary_en: GOSAT-2, also known as Ibuki-2, is an Earth observation mission dedicated to greenhouse gas monitoring, observing carbon dioxide, methane, ozone, carbon monoxide, nitrogen dioxide and water vapour in the atmosphere.

日本宇宙航空研究开发机构 (JAXA) 拥有并运营 GOSAT-2。

faq_en.a: GOSAT-2 is an Earth observation satellite dedicated to monitoring greenhouse gases such as methane, ozone, water vapour, carbon monoxide and nitrogen dioxide levels, and is a successor mission of GOSAT.
faq_en.q: What is GOSAT-2 used for?

faq_en.a: The full form of GOSAT-2 is Greenhouse Gas Observing Satellite-2.

faq_en.a: The Japan Aerospace Exploration Agency (JAXA) owns and operates GOSAT-2.

eoportal.name: GOSAT-GW (Global Observing SATellite for Greenhouse gases and Water cycle)
taxonomy: Precipitation intensity at the surface (liquid or solid)
Sea ice cover, edge and thickness
Chlorophyll Fluorescence from Vegetation on Land
eoportal.summary: The mission, will aid in the understanding of the rate of climate change by monitoring greenhouse gases in the atmosphere, as well as improve our understanding of the water cycle which is essential for Numerical Weather Prediction (NWP).

faq.a: The Advanced Microwave Scanning Radiometer 3 (AMSR3) is the fourth instrument in the AMSR series and aims to achieve the world’s top performance and functionality as a microwave radiometer.
faq.q: What is AMSR3?

faq.a: The Global Observing SATellite for Greenhouse gases and Water cycle (GOSAT-GW) mission is a collaboration between the Japanese Ministry of Environment (MOE), National Institute for Environmental Studies (NIES) and the Japanese Aerospace Exploration Agency (JAXA). It will be constructed by the Mitsubishi Electric Corporation under contract by JAXA.
faq.q: Who is responsible for the GOSAT-GW mission?

eoportal.name_zh: GOSAT-GW（全球温室气体和水循环观测卫星）
eoportal.agency_zh: JAXA、日本环境省 (MOE)、日本国立环境研究所 (NIES)
eoportal.summary_zh: 该任务将通过监测大气中的温室气体来帮助理解气候变化的速率，同时增进我们对水循环的理解，这对于数值天气预报 (NWP) 至关重要。
eoportal.summary_en: The mission, will aid in the understanding of the rate of climate change by monitoring greenhouse gases in the atmosphere, as well as improve our understanding of the water cycle which is essential for Numerical Weather Prediction (NWP).

faq_zh.a: 先进微波扫描辐射计3型 (AMSR3) 是 AMSR 系列的第四台仪器，旨在作为微波辐射计实现世界顶级的性能和功能。
全球温室气体和水循环观测卫星 (GOSAT-GW) 任务是日本环境省 (MOE)、日本国立环境研究所 (NIES) 和日本宇宙航空研究开发机构 (JAXA) 之间的合作项目。它将由三菱电机株式会社根据 JAXA 的合同建造。

faq_en.a: The Advanced Microwave Scanning Radiometer 3 (AMSR3) is the fourth instrument in the AMSR series and aims to achieve the world’s top performance and functionality as a microwave radiometer.
faq_en.q: What is AMSR3?

faq_en.a: The Global Observing SATellite for Greenhouse gases and Water cycle (GOSAT-GW) mission is a collaboration between the Japanese Ministry of Environment (MOE), National Institute for Environmental Studies (NIES) and the Japanese Aerospace Exploration Agency (JAXA). It will be constructed by the Mitsubishi Electric Corporation under contract by JAXA.
faq_en.q: Who is responsible for the GOSAT-GW mission?

eoportal.name: GPM (Global Precipitation Measurement) Mission
taxonomy: Precipitation intensity at the surface (liquid or solid)
eoportal.summary: The constellation’s primary spacecraft, GPM Core Observatory (built by NASA), was launched in February 2014, joining a collaboration of 12 GPM satellites, aiming to study global precipitation, evaporation and the water cycle.

faq.a: The GPM Mission is a constellation of satellites with an objective of understanding tropical rainfall to improve climate, weather, and hydrological forecasts on a global basis.
faq.q: What is the GPM mission for?

faq.a: GPM is a cooperative mission of the space agencies of the USA and Japan, primarily NASA (National Aeronautics and Space Administration) and JAXA (Japanese Aerospace Exploration Agency).
faq.q: Who operates GPM?

faq.a: There are currently 12 microwave imager and sounder satellites in the GPM constellation including GPM Core Observatory which joined in February 2014.
faq.q: How many satellites are in the GPM constellation?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该星座的核心航天器——由NASA建造的GPM核心观测卫星，于2014年2月发射升空，加入了一个由12颗GPM卫星组成的协作网络，旨在研究全球降水、蒸发和水循环。
eoportal.summary_en: The constellation’s primary spacecraft, GPM Core Observatory (built by NASA), was launched in February 2014, joining a collaboration of 12 GPM satellites, aiming to study global precipitation, evaporation and the water cycle.

faq_zh.a: GPM任务是一个卫星星座，其目标是了解热带降雨，以在全球范围内改进气候、天气和水文预报。
GPM是美国与日本航天机构的合作任务，主要是NASA（美国国家航空航天局）和JAXA（日本宇宙航空研究开发机构）。
GPM星座目前包含12颗微波成像仪和探测器卫星，其中包括于2014年2月加入的GPM核心观测卫星。

faq_en.a: The GPM Mission is a constellation of satellites with an objective of understanding tropical rainfall to improve climate, weather, and hydrological forecasts on a global basis.
faq_en.q: What is the GPM mission for?

faq_en.a: GPM is a cooperative mission of the space agencies of the USA and Japan, primarily NASA (National Aeronautics and Space Administration) and JAXA (Japanese Aerospace Exploration Agency).
faq_en.q: Who operates GPM?

faq_en.a: There are currently 12 microwave imager and sounder satellites in the GPM constellation including GPM Core Observatory which joined in February 2014.
faq_en.q: How many satellites are in the GPM constellation?

eoportal.name: GPS (Global Positioning System)
eoportal.summary: Beginning launch in the late 1970s, the constellation consists of a minimum of 24 satellites.

faq.a: The U.S. Global Positioning System constellation requires a minimum of 24 to be maintained 95% of the time for Full Operational Capability (FOC), however the U.S. Space Force typically operates 31 GPS satellites.
faq.q: How many satellites are in the GPS constellation?

faq.a: The Global Positioning System is owned and operated by the U.S. Department of Defense.
faq.q: Who operates the GPS constellation?

faq.a: Each GPS satellite transmits a unique signal and orbital parameters, allowing receivers to compute the precise location of the satellite and use trilateration to calculate a user's exact location.
faq.q: How does the GPS constellation work?

eoportal.summary_zh: GPS星座自20世纪70年代末开始发射，由至少24颗卫星组成。
eoportal.summary_en: Beginning launch in the late 1970s, the constellation consists of a minimum of 24 satellites.

faq_zh.a: 美国全球定位系统星座要求至少24颗卫星在95%的时间内保持运行以实现全面运行能力（FOC），但美国太空军通常运营着31颗GPS卫星。
全球定位系统由美国国防部拥有并运营。
每颗GPS卫星都发射独特的信号和轨道参数，使接收器能够计算卫星的精确位置，并通过三边测量法计算用户的精确位置。

faq_en.a: The U.S. Global Positioning System constellation requires a minimum of 24 to be maintained 95% of the time for Full Operational Capability (FOC), however the U.S. Space Force typically operates 31 GPS satellites.
faq_en.q: How many satellites are in the GPS constellation?

faq_en.a: The Global Positioning System is owned and operated by the U.S. Department of Defense.
faq_en.q: Who operates the GPS constellation?

faq_en.a: Each GPS satellite transmits a unique signal and orbital parameters, allowing receivers to compute the precise location of the satellite and use trilateration to calculate a user's exact location.
faq_en.q: How does the GPS constellation work?

eoportal.name: GRACE (Gravity Recovery And Climate Experiment)
eoportal.summary: Data from GRACE satellites covered wide application areas.

faq.a: GRACE used a precise microwave ranging system to measure the distance between themselves due to gravitational acceleration. GRACE measured Earth's gravity field changes, which were directly related to changes in surface mass.
faq.q: What did the GRACE satellite do?

faq.a: No, GRACE was decommissioned in October 2017 when the satellites ran out of fuel.
faq.q: Is the GRACE satellite still operational?

faq.a: GRACE mission consisted of two identical satellites, GRACE-1 and GRACE-2, that were launched together in March 2002.
faq.q: How many GRACE satellites were there?

eoportal.summary_zh: GRACE卫星的数据覆盖了广泛的应用领域。
eoportal.summary_en: Data from GRACE satellites covered wide application areas.

faq_zh.a: GRACE卫星使用精确的微波测距系统来测量由于重力加速度引起的彼此间距离变化。它测量了地球重力场的变化，这些变化与地表质量的变化直接相关。
不，GRACE卫星于2017年10月因燃料耗尽而退役。
GRACE任务由两颗完全相同的卫星组成，GRACE-1和GRACE-2，它们于2002年3月一同发射。

faq_en.a: GRACE used a precise microwave ranging system to measure the distance between themselves due to gravitational acceleration. GRACE measured Earth's gravity field changes, which were directly related to changes in surface mass.
faq_en.q: What did the GRACE satellite do?

faq_en.a: No, GRACE was decommissioned in October 2017 when the satellites ran out of fuel.
faq_en.q: Is the GRACE satellite still operational?

faq_en.a: GRACE mission consisted of two identical satellites, GRACE-1 and GRACE-2, that were launched together in March 2002.
faq_en.q: How many GRACE satellites were there?

eoportal.name: GRACE-C (Gravity Recovery and Climate Experiment - Continuity)
eoportal.summary: GRACE-C is a twin-satellite low Earth orbiting observatory that plans to measure monthly changes in Earth’s gravitational field to improve our understanding of Earth’s water cycle.

faq.a: The GRACE satellite series has the objective to measure fluctuations in Earth’s gravitational field, allowing scientists to analyse shifting groundwater, ice sheets, and plate tectonics around the world.
faq.q: What is the objective of the GRACE mission?

faq.a: GRACE-C measures changes in Earth’s gravitational field using two identical satellites flying in close formation. A laser interferometric ranging system measures tiny variations in the distance between the satellites as they orbit Earth, allowing changes in Earth’s mass distribution to be mapped over time.
faq.q: How do the GRACE-C satellites measure changes in Earth’s gravitational field?

faq.a: The GRACE-C mission is a collaborative effort between NASA, DLR (German Aerospace Center) and various other German research centers such as GFZ Helmholtz Centre for Geosciences. The satellites’ primary manufacturer is Airbus Defence and Space and the NASA Jet Propulsion Laboratory.
faq.q: Which organisations are responsible for the GRACE-C mission?

eoportal.name_zh: GRACE-C（重力恢复与气候实验-延续任务）
eoportal.agency_zh: NASA, DLR（德国航空航天中心）
eoportal.summary_zh: GRACE-C 是一个由两颗卫星组成的低地球轨道观测星座，计划通过测量地球引力场的月度变化，来增进我们对地球水循环过程的理解。
eoportal.summary_en: GRACE-C is a twin-satellite low Earth orbiting observatory that plans to measure monthly changes in Earth’s gravitational field to improve our understanding of Earth’s water cycle.

faq_zh.a: GRACE 卫星系列的目标是测量地球引力场的波动，使科学家能够分析全球地下水、冰盖和板块构造的移动与变化。
faq_zh.q: GRACE 任务的目标是什么？

faq_zh.a: GRACE-C 通过两颗以紧密编队飞行的卫星来测量地球引力场的变化。一个激光干涉测距系统会测量两颗卫星在环绕地球运行时其间距的微小变化，从而可以绘制出地球质量分布随时间的变化图。
faq_zh.q: GRACE-C 卫星如何测量地球引力场的变化？

faq_zh.a: GRACE-C 任务是由 NASA、DLR（德国航空航天中心）以及 GFZ 德国亥姆霍兹地球科学研究中心等多家德国研究机构合作实施的。卫星的主要制造商是空客防务与航天公司以及 NASA 的喷气推进实验室。
faq_zh.q: 哪些机构负责 GRACE-C 任务？

faq_en.a: The GRACE satellite series has the objective to measure fluctuations in Earth’s gravitational field, allowing scientists to analyse shifting groundwater, ice sheets, and plate tectonics around the world.
faq_en.q: What is the objective of the GRACE mission?

faq_en.a: GRACE-C measures changes in Earth’s gravitational field using two identical satellites flying in close formation. A laser interferometric ranging system measures tiny variations in the distance between the satellites as they orbit Earth, allowing changes in Earth’s mass distribution to be mapped over time.
faq_en.q: How do the GRACE-C satellites measure changes in Earth’s gravitational field?

faq_en.a: The GRACE-C mission is a collaborative effort between NASA, DLR (German Aerospace Center) and various other German research centers such as GFZ Helmholtz Centre for Geosciences. The satellites’ primary manufacturer is Airbus Defence and Space and the NASA Jet Propulsion Laboratory.
faq_en.q: Which organisations are responsible for the GRACE-C mission?

grace-c (gravity recovery and climate ex.display: GRACE-C (Gravity Recovery and Climate Experiment - Continuity)

eoportal.name: GRACE-FO (Gravity Recovery And Climate Experiment - Follow-On)
eoportal.summary: GRACE-FO applications include monitoring water storage, ice sheets and glaciers, sea level, and solid earth, similar to that of its predecessor, GRACE.

faq.a: Gravity Recovery and Climate Experiment - Follow On (GRACE-FO) is a successor to the original Gravity Recovery and Climate Experiment (GRACE) mission. GRACE-FO maintains data continuity for measuring changes in high-resolution monthly global models of Earth’s gravity field by tracking water movement and surface mass changes across the planet. Its application area includes monitoring water storage, ice sheets and glaciers, sea level, and solid earth, similar to that of its predecessor, GRACE.
faq.q: What do the GRACE-FO satellites do?

faq.a: As of August 2022, GRACE-FO is still in orbit. GRACE-FO was launched on 22 May 2017 with a minimum mission life planned for 5 years.
faq.q: Are the GRACE-FO satellites still in orbit?

faq.a: There are two identical GRACE-FO satellites.
faq.q: How many GRACE-FO satellites are there?

eoportal.name_zh: GRACE-FO（重力恢复与气候实验后续任务）
eoportal.summary_zh: GRACE-FO的应用包括监测水资源储量、冰盖与冰川、海平面以及固体地球，与其前身GRACE任务类似。
eoportal.summary_en: GRACE-FO applications include monitoring water storage, ice sheets and glaciers, sea level, and solid earth, similar to that of its predecessor, GRACE.

faq_zh.a: 重力恢复与气候实验后续任务（GRACE-FO）是原始重力恢复与气候实验（GRACE）任务的后续。GRACE-FO通过追踪全球水体运动和地表质量变化，维持了对地球重力场高分辨率月度全球模型变化的连续测量数据。其应用领域包括监测水资源储量、冰盖与冰川、海平面以及固体地球，与其前身GRACE任务类似。
截至2022年8月，GRACE-FO仍在轨运行。该卫星于2017年5月22日发射，计划最低任务寿命为5年。
有两颗相同的GRACE-FO卫星。

faq_en.a: Gravity Recovery and Climate Experiment - Follow On (GRACE-FO) is a successor to the original Gravity Recovery and Climate Experiment (GRACE) mission. GRACE-FO maintains data continuity for measuring changes in high-resolution monthly global models of Earth’s gravity field by tracking water movement and surface mass changes across the planet. Its application area includes monitoring water storage, ice sheets and glaciers, sea level, and solid earth, similar to that of its predecessor, GRACE.
faq_en.q: What do the GRACE-FO satellites do?

faq_en.a: As of August 2022, GRACE-FO is still in orbit. GRACE-FO was launched on 22 May 2017 with a minimum mission life planned for 5 years.
faq_en.q: Are the GRACE-FO satellites still in orbit?

faq_en.a: There are two identical GRACE-FO satellites.
faq_en.q: How many GRACE-FO satellites are there?

grace-fo (gravity recovery and climate e.display: GRACE-FO (Gravity Recovery And Climate Experiment - Follow-On)

eoportal.name: Greek National Small Satellite Programme (GNSSP)
eoportal.summary: The first two satellites were launched in November 2025.

faq.a: The programme totals €130 million divided among four axes: Axis 1.1 (€20M thermal satellites), Axis 1.2 (€33M SAR satellites), Axis 2 (€60M optical constellation), and Axis 3 (€17M governmental hub and services). The entire programme is funded through the European Union's Recovery and Resilience Facility (RRF) under Greece's "Greece 2.0" Recovery Plan.
faq.q: What is the total investment in the Greek National Satellite Space Programme and what funds it?

faq.a: The core AXIS programme comprises 13 satellites: four thermal infrared satellites (Axis 1.1), two synthetic aperture radar satellites (Axis 1.2), and seven optical multisensor satellites (Axis 2).
faq.q: How many satellites comprise the Greek AXIS constellation?

faq.a: The first AXIS satellites launched on 28 November 2025, when two ICEYE SAR satellites were successfully deployed into sun-synchronous orbit by SpaceX's Transporter-15 mission launched from Vandenberg Space Force Base. Contract execution is ongoing for the thermal and optical satellite segments, with the governmental hub infrastructure under development by Greek companies and institutions.
faq.q: What was the first satellite in the AXIS programme launched?

eoportal.summary_zh: 前两颗卫星已于2025年11月发射。
eoportal.summary_en: The first two satellites were launched in November 2025.

faq_zh.a: 该计划总投资1.3亿欧元，分为四个部分：第一轴1.1（2000万欧元用于热红外卫星）、第一轴1.2（3300万欧元用于SAR卫星）、第二轴（6000万欧元用于光学星座）以及第三轴（1700万欧元用于政府枢纽与服务）。整个计划的资金来自欧盟复苏与韧性基金（RRF），属于希腊“Greece 2.0”复苏计划的一部分。
faq_zh.q: 希腊国家卫星太空计划的总投资是多少？资金来源是什么？

faq_zh.a: 核心AXIS计划包含13颗卫星：四颗热红外卫星（第一轴1.1）、两颗合成孔径雷达卫星（第一轴1.2）以及七颗光学多传感器卫星（第二轴）。
faq_zh.q: 希腊AXIS星座包含多少颗卫星？

faq_zh.a: 首颗AXIS卫星于2025年11月28日发射，当时两颗ICEYE SAR卫星由SpaceX的Transporter-15任务从范登堡太空军基地成功送入太阳同步轨道。热红外和光学卫星部分的合同执行仍在进行中，政府枢纽基础设施正由希腊公司和机构开发。
faq_zh.q: AXIS计划中首颗卫星是何时发射的？

faq_en.a: The programme totals €130 million divided among four axes: Axis 1.1 (€20M thermal satellites), Axis 1.2 (€33M SAR satellites), Axis 2 (€60M optical constellation), and Axis 3 (€17M governmental hub and services). The entire programme is funded through the European Union's Recovery and Resilience Facility (RRF) under Greece's "Greece 2.0" Recovery Plan.
faq_en.q: What is the total investment in the Greek National Satellite Space Programme and what funds it?

faq_en.a: The core AXIS programme comprises 13 satellites: four thermal infrared satellites (Axis 1.1), two synthetic aperture radar satellites (Axis 1.2), and seven optical multisensor satellites (Axis 2).
faq_en.q: How many satellites comprise the Greek AXIS constellation?

faq_en.a: The first AXIS satellites launched on 28 November 2025, when two ICEYE SAR satellites were successfully deployed into sun-synchronous orbit by SpaceX's Transporter-15 mission launched from Vandenberg Space Force Base. Contract execution is ongoing for the thermal and optical satellite segments, with the governmental hub infrastructure under development by Greek companies and institutions.
faq_en.q: What was the first satellite in the AXIS programme launched?

eoportal.name: GRUS (Axelspace/AxelGlobe)
eoportal.summary: Five GRUS-1 satellites provide operational imagery, while seven GRUS-3 satellites launched in July 2026 are in initial operations and are expected to increase imaging capacity and revisit frequency for agriculture, forestry, finance, infrastructure and disaster monitoring.

faq.a: Frequent coverage is achieved through the use of a constellation of satellites in a low Earth sun-synchronous orbit, which provides short revisit times and large daily coverage. A distributed ground station network enables frequent data downlink.
faq.q: How does the GRUS constellation achieve frequent coverage?

faq.a: GRUS-1 satellites collect high resolution panchromatic and multispectral optical imagery across six different spectral bands while GRUS-3 collects seven different spectral bands. Once corrected, they are suitable for tasks such as land surface mapping and environmental monitoring.
faq.q: What data do the GRUS satellites collect?

faq.a: The first satellite, GRUS-1A, was launched in 2018, followed by an additional four satellites, GRUS-1B, 1C, 1D and 1E, in 2021 to expand the constellation. In 2025, GRUS-3𝛼 was launched to validate the bus and mission payload for the seven GRUS-3 satellites that were launched on July 7, 2026.
faq.q: When were the GRUS satellites launched?

eoportal.summary_zh: 五颗GRUS-1卫星提供业务化影像，而于2026年7月发射的七颗GRUS-3卫星正处于初期运行阶段，预计将提升农业、林业、金融、基础设施和灾害监测等领域的成像能力与重访频率。
eoportal.summary_en: Five GRUS-1 satellites provide operational imagery, while seven GRUS-3 satellites launched in July 2026 are in initial operations and are expected to increase imaging capacity and revisit frequency for agriculture, forestry, finance, infrastructure and disaster monitoring.

faq_zh.a: 通过采用运行在低地球太阳同步轨道上的卫星星座，实现了短重访周期和大日覆盖范围。分布式地面站网络则支持频繁的数据下行。
faq_zh.q: GRUS星座如何实现高频次覆盖？

faq_zh.a: GRUS-1卫星采集高分辨率全色与多光谱光学影像，涵盖六个不同的光谱波段；而GRUS-3卫星则采集七个不同的光谱波段。经校正后，这些数据适用于地表测绘与环境监测等任务。
首颗卫星GRUS-1A于2018年发射，随后在2021年增发了四颗卫星GRUS-1B、1C、1D和1E以扩展星座。2025年，GRUS-3α发射升空，用于验证七颗GRUS-3卫星的平台与任务载荷，这七颗卫星已于2026年7月7日发射。

faq_en.a: Frequent coverage is achieved through the use of a constellation of satellites in a low Earth sun-synchronous orbit, which provides short revisit times and large daily coverage. A distributed ground station network enables frequent data downlink.
faq_en.q: How does the GRUS constellation achieve frequent coverage?

faq_en.a: GRUS-1 satellites collect high resolution panchromatic and multispectral optical imagery across six different spectral bands while GRUS-3 collects seven different spectral bands. Once corrected, they are suitable for tasks such as land surface mapping and environmental monitoring.
faq_en.q: What data do the GRUS satellites collect?

faq_en.a: The first satellite, GRUS-1A, was launched in 2018, followed by an additional four satellites, GRUS-1B, 1C, 1D and 1E, in 2021 to expand the constellation. In 2025, GRUS-3𝛼 was launched to validate the bus and mission payload for the seven GRUS-3 satellites that were launched on July 7, 2026.
faq_en.q: When were the GRUS satellites launched?

eoportal.name: GyeonggiSat (Gyeonggi Climate Satellite Programme)
eoportal.summary: The programme is designed to deliver satellite-derived environmental data to support provincial climate policy, disaster response, and land-management decisions.

faq.a: The GyeonggiSat programme aims to provide Gyeonggi Province with satellite-derived Earth-observation data to support climate policy, environmental monitoring, disaster response, and land management. GyeonggiSat-1 provides optical imagery, while GyeonggiSat-2A and -2B will add dedicated greenhouse gas sensing capability. The programme is described as the first instance of a South Korean provincial government independently commissioning and funding its own climate satellite system.
faq.q: What is the primary purpose of GyeonggiSat?

faq.a: GyeonggiSat-1 is an enhanced 6U CubeSat with approximate dimensions of 20 cm × 20 cm × 40 cm and a mass of approximately 25 kg, designed and manufactured by Nara Space Technology.
faq.q: What satellite platform is GyeonggiSat-1 based on?

faq.a: GyeonggiSat-1 carries an optical imager designed for land-use monitoring, vegetation dynamics, and disaster damage assessment, while GyeonggiSat-2A and -2B are planned as dedicated greenhouse-gas observation platforms equipped with methane and carbon-dioxide sensors.
faq.q: How does GyeonggiSat-1 differ from the planned GyeonggiSat-2A and -2B satellites?

eoportal.name_zh: 京几道卫星（京几道气候卫星计划）
eoportal.summary_zh: 该计划旨在提供卫星衍生的环境数据，以支持省级气候政策制定、灾害应急响应及土地管理决策。
eoportal.summary_en: The programme is designed to deliver satellite-derived environmental data to support provincial climate policy, disaster response, and land-management decisions.

faq_zh.a: 京几道卫星计划旨在为京几道提供卫星衍生的对地观测数据，以支持气候政策、环境监测、灾害应急响应和土地管理。京几道卫星-1提供光学影像，而京几道卫星-2A和-2B将增加专门的温室气体感知能力。该计划是韩国首个省级政府独立委托并资助其自身气候卫星系统的实例。
faq_zh.q: 京几道卫星计划的主要目的是什么？

faq_zh.a: 京几道卫星-1是一个增强型6U立方星，尺寸约为20厘米×20厘米×40厘米，质量约为25千克，由Nara Space Technology设计和制造。
faq_zh.q: 京几道卫星-1基于什么卫星平台？

faq_zh.a: 京几道卫星-1搭载了一台用于土地利用监测、植被动态评估和灾害损害评估的光学成像仪，而京几道卫星-2A和-2B计划作为专用的温室气体观测平台，配备甲烷和二氧化碳传感器。
faq_zh.q: 京几道卫星-1与计划中的京几道卫星-2A和-2B有何不同？

faq_en.a: The GyeonggiSat programme aims to provide Gyeonggi Province with satellite-derived Earth-observation data to support climate policy, environmental monitoring, disaster response, and land management. GyeonggiSat-1 provides optical imagery, while GyeonggiSat-2A and -2B will add dedicated greenhouse gas sensing capability. The programme is described as the first instance of a South Korean provincial government independently commissioning and funding its own climate satellite system.
faq_en.q: What is the primary purpose of GyeonggiSat?

faq_en.a: GyeonggiSat-1 is an enhanced 6U CubeSat with approximate dimensions of 20 cm × 20 cm × 40 cm and a mass of approximately 25 kg, designed and manufactured by Nara Space Technology.
faq_en.q: What satellite platform is GyeonggiSat-1 based on?

faq_en.a: GyeonggiSat-1 carries an optical imager designed for land-use monitoring, vegetation dynamics, and disaster damage assessment, while GyeonggiSat-2A and -2B are planned as dedicated greenhouse-gas observation platforms equipped with methane and carbon-dioxide sensors.
faq_en.q: How does GyeonggiSat-1 differ from the planned GyeonggiSat-2A and -2B satellites?

eoportal.name: Harmony A and B (Earth Explorer 10A and 10B)
taxonomy: Sea ice cover, edge and thickness
eoportal.summary: Until their design life of five years comes to an end, they will monitor changes in the Earth’s surface, as well as monitor ocean surface conditions such as wind, currents, and temperature.

faq.a: The Harmony satellites will orbit in tandem with one of the Sentinel-1 satellites, and they’ll use their receive-only C-band SAR (Synthetic Aperture Radar) to detect C-band radio waves emitted from the SAR of the Sentinel-1 satellite. This will be used to detect small changes in the Earth’s surface.
faq.q: How will the Harmony satellites interact with the Sentinel-1 satellites?

faq.a: Harmony will undertake a sun-synchronous orbit with an altitude of 693 km and an inclination of 98.19°. It has a period of 98.74 minutes and a repeat cycle of 12 days.
faq.q: What orbit will the Harmony satellites undertake?

eoportal.name_zh: Harmony A 和 B（地球探测者 10A 和 10B）
eoportal.summary_zh: 在五年的设计寿命期间，它们将监测地球表面的变化，并监测海洋表面状况，如风、洋流和温度。
eoportal.summary_en: Until their design life of five years comes to an end, they will monitor changes in the Earth’s surface, as well as monitor ocean surface conditions such as wind, currents, and temperature.

faq_zh.a: Harmony 卫星将与一颗 Sentinel-1（哨兵-1）卫星协同轨道飞行，它们将使用其仅接收的 C 波段合成孔径雷达（SAR）来探测哨兵-1 卫星 SAR 发射的 C 波段无线电波。这将用于探测地球表面的微小变化。
faq_zh.q: Harmony 卫星将如何与 Sentinel-1 卫星交互？

faq_zh.a: Harmony 将运行在太阳同步轨道上，轨道高度为 693 公里，轨道倾角为 98.19°。其轨道周期为 98.74 分钟，重访周期为 12 天。
faq_zh.q: Harmony 卫星将运行在什么轨道上？

faq_en.a: The Harmony satellites will orbit in tandem with one of the Sentinel-1 satellites, and they’ll use their receive-only C-band SAR (Synthetic Aperture Radar) to detect C-band radio waves emitted from the SAR of the Sentinel-1 satellite. This will be used to detect small changes in the Earth’s surface.
faq_en.q: How will the Harmony satellites interact with the Sentinel-1 satellites?

faq_en.a: Harmony will undertake a sun-synchronous orbit with an altitude of 693 km and an inclination of 98.19°. It has a period of 98.74 minutes and a repeat cycle of 12 days.
faq_en.q: What orbit will the Harmony satellites undertake?

harmony a and b (earth explorer 10a and.display: Harmony A and B (Earth Explorer 10A and 10B)

eoportal.name: HAWCSat
eoportal.summary: HAWCSat aims to support extreme weather prediction, climate modelling and disaster monitoring, as well as investigate the interaction of aerosols, clouds, convection and precipitation in the Earth’s atmosphere.

faq.a: HAWCSat aims to support extreme weather prediction, climate modelling and disaster monitoring, as well as investigating the interactions between atmospheric aerosols, clouds, convection and precipitation.
faq.q: What is the aim of the HAWCSat constellation?

faq.a: The HAWCSat carries two instruments, the Aerosol Limb Imager, and the Spatial Heterodyne Observation of Water instrument.
faq.q: What instruments does HAWCSat carry?

faq.a: The High altitude Aerosols, Water vapour and Clouds (HAWC) programme forms Canada’s part of the NASA-led Atmospheric Observing System (AOS). The HAWC programme includes HAWCSat, which carries the Aerosol Limb Imager (ALI) and the Spatial Heterodyne Observation of Water (SHOW) instrument, as well as the Thin Ice Clouds in Far Infrared (IR) Experiment (TICFIRE) instrument, which will be carried on another AOS mission, AOS-Sky.
faq.q: What is the difference between the Canadian HAWC programme, and HAWCSat?

eoportal.summary_zh: HAWCSat 旨在支持极端天气预测、气候建模和灾害监测，并研究地球大气中气溶胶、云、对流和降水的相互作用。
eoportal.summary_en: HAWCSat aims to support extreme weather prediction, climate modelling and disaster monitoring, as well as investigate the interaction of aerosols, clouds, convection and precipitation in the Earth’s atmosphere.

faq_zh.a: HAWCSat 旨在支持极端天气预测、气候建模和灾害监测，并研究大气气溶胶、云、对流和降水之间的相互作用。
HAWCSat 携带两台仪器：气溶胶临边成像仪和空间外差水汽观测仪。
加拿大高空大气气溶胶、水汽和云（HAWC）计划是NASA主导的大气观测系统（AOS）的加拿大组成部分。HAWC计划包括HAWCSat（携带气溶胶临边成像仪和空间外差水汽观测仪），以及将在另一项AOS任务AOS-Sky上搭载的薄冰云远红外实验仪（TICFIRE）。
faq_zh.q: 加拿大的HAWC项目与HAWCSat有什么区别？

faq_en.a: HAWCSat aims to support extreme weather prediction, climate modelling and disaster monitoring, as well as investigating the interactions between atmospheric aerosols, clouds, convection and precipitation.
faq_en.q: What is the aim of the HAWCSat constellation?

faq_en.a: The HAWCSat carries two instruments, the Aerosol Limb Imager, and the Spatial Heterodyne Observation of Water instrument.
faq_en.q: What instruments does HAWCSat carry?

faq_en.a: The High altitude Aerosols, Water vapour and Clouds (HAWC) programme forms Canada’s part of the NASA-led Atmospheric Observing System (AOS). The HAWC programme includes HAWCSat, which carries the Aerosol Limb Imager (ALI) and the Spatial Heterodyne Observation of Water (SHOW) instrument, as well as the Thin Ice Clouds in Far Infrared (IR) Experiment (TICFIRE) instrument, which will be carried on another AOS mission, AOS-Sky.
faq_en.q: What is the difference between the Canadian HAWC programme, and HAWCSat?

eoportal.name: HawkEye 360
eoportal.summary: The mission provides spaceborne geolocation of radio frequency (RF) emitters, both terrestrial and aerial, for a range of commercial applications.

faq.a: The HawkEye constellation collects and transmits location data of RF (Radio Frequency) emitters, carried by ships, land vehicles, or aerial craft.
faq.q: What is the purpose of the HawkEye-360 Constellation?

faq.a: The RF data collected by HawkEye 360 is used for spectrum mapping, maritime domain awareness, defence and intelligence planning, environmental monitoring, and emergency response.
faq.q: What are the key applications of HawkEye 360's RF data?

faq.a: HawkEye 360's geolocation algorithms can accurately identify the source of a signal, accompanied by a confidence ellipse representing a 95% probability that the signal falls within that area.
faq.q: How accurate is HawkEye 360's geolocation?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该任务为空基和陆基的射频信号发射源提供天基地理定位服务，适用于多种商业应用。
eoportal.summary_en: The mission provides spaceborne geolocation of radio frequency (RF) emitters, both terrestrial and aerial, for a range of commercial applications.

faq_zh.a: HawkEye星座收集并传输由船舶、地面车辆或飞行器携带的射频信号发射源的位置数据。
HawkEye 360收集的射频数据可用于频谱测绘、海上态势感知、国防与情报规划、环境监测以及应急响应。
faq_zh.q: HawkEye 360射频数据的主要应用有哪些？

faq_zh.a: HawkEye 360的地理定位算法能够精确识别信号源，并附带一个置信椭圆，表示信号有95%的概率落在该区域内。

faq_en.a: The HawkEye constellation collects and transmits location data of RF (Radio Frequency) emitters, carried by ships, land vehicles, or aerial craft.
faq_en.q: What is the purpose of the HawkEye-360 Constellation?

faq_en.a: The RF data collected by HawkEye 360 is used for spectrum mapping, maritime domain awareness, defence and intelligence planning, environmental monitoring, and emergency response.
faq_en.q: What are the key applications of HawkEye 360's RF data?

faq_en.a: HawkEye 360's geolocation algorithms can accurately identify the source of a signal, accompanied by a confidence ellipse representing a 95% probability that the signal falls within that area.
faq_en.q: How accurate is HawkEye 360's geolocation?

eoportal.name: Heinrich Hertz Mission
eoportal.summary: AG for the German Aerospace Center (DLR) on behalf of the Federal Ministry for Economic Affairs and Climate Action (BMWK) and the Federal Ministry of Defence (BMVg), the satellite carries over 20 experiments focused on advanced antennas, high-frequency components, and inter-satellite links, contributing to the advancement of Germany's information society and secure communications infrastructure.
eoportal.summary_zh: 该卫星由德国宇航中心（DLR）代表德国联邦经济和气候行动部（BMWK）及联邦国防部（BMVg）实施，搭载了超过20项实验，主要聚焦于先进天线、高频部件和星间链路技术，旨在推动德国信息社会和安全通信基础设施的发展。
eoportal.summary_en: AG for the German Aerospace Center (DLR) on behalf of the Federal Ministry for Economic Affairs and Climate Action (BMWK) and the Federal Ministry of Defence (BMVg), the satellite carries over 20 experiments focused on advanced antennas, high-frequency components, and inter-satellite links, contributing to the advancement of Germany's information society and secure communications infrastructure.

eoportal.name: HERMES (High Energy Rapid Modular Ensemble of Satellites)
eoportal.summary: Led by the Italian National Institute for Astrophysics (INAF) with support from 11 European partners and funded by the EU Horizon 2020 programme, the mission was successfully launched in March 2025.

faq.a: HERMES-SP is a scientific pathfinder mission involving a constellation of CubeSats designed to detect high-energy astrophysical transients, such as gamma-ray bursts (GRBs), with precise timing and localisation. It is a demonstration of distributed space-based instruments for multi-messenger astrophysics.
faq.q: What is the HERMES-SP mission?

faq.a: The HERMES-SP constellation was launched on March 15, 2025, aboard SpaceX’s Transporter-13 mission. The mission deployed six 3U CubeSats into low-Earth orbit.
faq.q: When was HERMES-SP launched?

faq.a: The mission is led by the Italian National Institute for Astrophysics (INAF) in collaboration with several Italian and European institutions, including ASI (Italian Space Agency), Politecnico di Milano, University of Geneva, and private aerospace partners such as GAUSS Srl and SkyLabs.
faq.q: Who manages and operates the HERMES-SP mission?

eoportal.name_zh: HERMES (高能快速模块化卫星集群)
eoportal.agency_zh: 意大利国家天体物理研究所 (INAF)；合作方包括意大利航天局 (ASI)、米兰理工大学、日内瓦大学及GAUSS Srl、SkyLabs等私营企业，项目由欧盟“地平线2020”计划资助。
eoportal.summary_zh: HERMES任务由意大利国家天体物理研究所主导，在欧洲11个合作伙伴的支持下，并由欧盟“地平线2020”计划资助，于2025年3月成功发射。该任务旨在验证用于多信使天体物理学的分布式天基仪器。
eoportal.summary_en: Led by the Italian National Institute for Astrophysics (INAF) with support from 11 European partners and funded by the EU Horizon 2020 programme, the mission was successfully launched in March 2025.

faq_zh.a: HERMES-SP是一项科学先驱任务，由一个立方星星座组成，旨在精确探测高能天体物理瞬变现象，例如伽马射线暴。它是用于多信使天体物理学的分布式天基仪器的一次演示。
HERMES-SP星座于2025年3月15日搭载SpaceX的Transporter-13任务发射升空。该任务将六颗3U立方星部署到了近地轨道。
该任务由意大利国家天体物理研究所牵头，并与包括意大利航天局、米兰理工大学、日内瓦大学以及GAUSS Srl和SkyLabs等私营航空航天合作伙伴在内的多家意大利和欧洲机构合作开展。

faq_en.a: HERMES-SP is a scientific pathfinder mission involving a constellation of CubeSats designed to detect high-energy astrophysical transients, such as gamma-ray bursts (GRBs), with precise timing and localisation. It is a demonstration of distributed space-based instruments for multi-messenger astrophysics.
faq_en.q: What is the HERMES-SP mission?

faq_en.a: The HERMES-SP constellation was launched on March 15, 2025, aboard SpaceX’s Transporter-13 mission. The mission deployed six 3U CubeSats into low-Earth orbit.
faq_en.q: When was HERMES-SP launched?

faq_en.a: The mission is led by the Italian National Institute for Astrophysics (INAF) in collaboration with several Italian and European institutions, including ASI (Italian Space Agency), Politecnico di Milano, University of Geneva, and private aerospace partners such as GAUSS Srl and SkyLabs.
faq_en.q: Who manages and operates the HERMES-SP mission?

eoportal.name: HJ-2 (Huan Jing-2)
eoportal.summary: The series includes two optical imaging and two radar imaging satellites, with the first launched in September 2020.

faq.a: The first two second generation Huan Jing satellites, HJ-2A and HJ-2B, use optical imagery captured by the three onboard cameras (Wide View Charged Coupled Device camera 2, Hyperspectral Imager 2, and Infrared Multispectral Scanner 2), while the HJ-2E and HJ-2F satellites use the onboard S-band SAR imager to capture radar data.
faq.q: Do the HJ-2 satellites use optical or radar imagery?

faq.a: The HJ-2 satellites are part of the Environmental Protection and Disaster Monitoring Constellation which is operated by the China Centre for Resources Satellite Data and Application (CRESDA).
faq.q: What constellation are the HJ-2 satellites part of?

faq.a: The first two optical imaging satellites, HJ-2A and HJ-2B were launched together in September 2020, followed by the radar satellites HJ-2E in October 2022 and HJ-2F in August 2023.
faq.q: When did the HJ-2 satellites launch?

eoportal.name: Hydrosat / VanZyl
eoportal.summary: The constellation, which launched its first satellite in 2024, supports precision agriculture, drought monitoring, climate science, and security intelligence.

faq.a: Hydrosat produces daily Land Surface Temperature (LST) maps at 70 m resolution, captured by a dual-satellite configuration that monitors critical temperature changes for hydrology and agriculture applications.
faq.q: What makes Hydrosat’s thermal imaging capability unique?

faq.a: Data products (LST, evapotranspiration, vegetation indices) are delivered via cloud-optimised GeoTIFFs through APIs, a Python SDK, or a web portal. The system also offers Level 1B radiometrically calibrated imagery with less than 3-hour latency and Level 2A science products featuring emissivity-corrected LST and surface reflectance available within 6 hours of acquisition.
faq.q: How do users access Hydrosat data?

faq.a: The constellation's thermal and multispectral data serve critical applications across multiple sectors. In precision agriculture it enables field-scale irrigation management through crop water stress mapping. Environmental monitoring applications include early drought detection via land surface temperature anomalies and soil moisture analysis, while climate and security uses encompass wildfire risk assessment, urban heat island studies, and monitoring of industrial thermal signatures for regulatory compliance and defence purposes.
faq.q: What applications does Hydrosat support?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该星座于2024年发射首颗卫星，支持精准农业、干旱监测、气候科学和安全情报。
eoportal.summary_en: The constellation, which launched its first satellite in 2024, supports precision agriculture, drought monitoring, climate science, and security intelligence.

faq_zh.a: Hydrosat通过双卫星配置提供每日70米分辨率的陆地表面温度（LST）地图，监测对水文和农业应用至关重要的温度变化。
faq_zh.q: Hydrosat的热成像能力有何独特之处？

faq_zh.a: 数据产品（LST、蒸散量、植被指数）通过API、Python SDK或网络门户以云优化的GeoTIFF格式交付。系统还提供延迟小于3小时的L1B辐射定标影像，以及在数据获取后6小时内可用的包含发射率校正LST和地表反射率的L2A科学产品。
该星座的热红外和多光谱数据服务于多个领域的关键应用。在精准农业中，它通过作物水分胁迫图实现田间尺度灌溉管理。环境监测应用包括通过陆地表面温度异常进行早期干旱检测和土壤湿度分析，而气候与安全应用则涵盖野火风险评估、城市热岛研究，以及为合规和国防目的监测工业热特征。

The system also offers Level 1B radiometrically calibrated imagery with less than 3-hour latency and Level 2A science products featuring emissivity-corrected LST and surface reflectance available within 6 hours of acquisition.

faq.a: The Hyperfield constellation aims to consist of 100 satellites.

faq.a: Hyperfield-2 will carry an onboard graphics processing unit (GPU), automatic identification system (AIS) receiver, very high resolution (VHR) RGB camera, and a hyperspectral camera operating in the visible and near infrared (VNIR) and short wave infrared (SWIR) regions. The series aims to support advanced applications in soil moisture and vessel rediscovery. The VNIR-SWIR and RGB imagers will provide an expanded spectral coverage of 400 to 1,650 nm and improved spatial resolution of 15 m.
faq.q: What improvements will the Hyperfield-2 satellites have over Hyperfield-1?

faq.a: The first satellite of the Hyperfield Constellation, Hyperfield-1, launched on August 16, 2024, from the Vandenberg Space Force Base in California onboard the SpaceX Transporter 11 mission.

eoportal.name: IceCube (Earth-1)
eoportal.summary: The satellite was launched on 18 April 2017 and deployed from the ISS on 16 May 2017.

faq.a: IceCube was operated by NASA and GSFC (Goddard Space Flight Centre), and managed and co-funded by ESTO (Earth Science Technology Office).
faq.q: Who operates IceCube?

faq.a: The nU system is a method for measuring size in CubeSat satellites. Each ‘U’ represents a 10 cm x 10 cm cube, and so, for example, a 1U CubeSat would measure 10 cm x 10 cm x 10 cm, while a 3U CubeSat would measure 10 cm x 10 cm x 30 cm. By having a standardised cross section of 10 cm x 10 cm, CubeSats can be launched from the ISS through the same deployment mechanism.
faq.q: What is meant by 3U?

faq.a: One of the major benefits of CubeSats is their low cost. They are small and often included as secondary payloads on other missions. In addition, their standardised size allows the same launch mechanism from the ISS to be used for different CubeSats. IceCube used off-the-shelf components to create, keeping costs down. Their small size and low cost also make them more accessible to smaller companies and scientific research bases, while also providing shorter development times.
faq.q: What are the benefits of CubeSats?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该卫星于2017年4月18日发射，并于2017年5月16日从国际空间站部署。
eoportal.summary_en: The satellite was launched on 18 April 2017 and deployed from the ISS on 16 May 2017.

faq_zh.a: IceCube由NASA和GSFC（戈达德太空飞行中心）运营，并由ESTO（地球科学技术办公室）管理和共同资助。
nU系统是用于衡量立方体卫星尺寸的一种方法。每个“U”代表一个10厘米x10厘米的立方体。例如，一个1U立方体卫星的尺寸为10厘米x10厘米x10厘米，而一个3U立方体卫星的尺寸则为10厘米x10厘米x30厘米。通过采用标准化的10厘米x10厘米截面，不同的立方体卫星可以使用国际空间站的相同部署机构进行发射。
立方体卫星的主要优点之一是成本低廉。它们体积小，通常作为其他任务的次级载荷发射。此外，其标准化尺寸允许国际空间站使用相同的发射机构来部署不同的立方体卫星。IceCube采用现成组件制造，从而降低了成本。其小巧的尺寸和低廉的成本也使小型公司和科研基地更容易接触和使用，同时缩短了开发周期。
faq_zh.q: 立方体卫星有哪些优点？

faq_en.a: IceCube was operated by NASA and GSFC (Goddard Space Flight Centre), and managed and co-funded by ESTO (Earth Science Technology Office).
faq_en.q: Who operates IceCube?

faq_en.a: The nU system is a method for measuring size in CubeSat satellites. Each ‘U’ represents a 10 cm x 10 cm cube, and so, for example, a 1U CubeSat would measure 10 cm x 10 cm x 10 cm, while a 3U CubeSat would measure 10 cm x 10 cm x 30 cm. By having a standardised cross section of 10 cm x 10 cm, CubeSats can be launched from the ISS through the same deployment mechanism.
faq_en.q: What is meant by 3U?

faq_en.a: One of the major benefits of CubeSats is their low cost. They are small and often included as secondary payloads on other missions. In addition, their standardised size allows the same launch mechanism from the ISS to be used for different CubeSats. IceCube used off-the-shelf components to create, keeping costs down. Their small size and low cost also make them more accessible to smaller companies and scientific research bases, while also providing shorter development times.
faq_en.q: What are the benefits of CubeSats?

taxonomy: Cloud type, amount and cloud top temperature
Sea ice cover, edge and thickness

icesat (ice, cloud and land elevation sa.display: ICESat (Ice, Cloud and Land Elevation Satellite)

eoportal.name: ICESat-2 (Ice, Cloud and land Elevation Satellite-2)
taxonomy: Cloud type, amount and cloud top temperature
Sea ice cover, edge and thickness
eoportal.summary: The mission has a design life of three years, with a five year goal, and seven years of propellant onboard to sustain the satellite if it is to exceed its expected lifespan.

faq.a: ICESat-2’s ATLAS instrument can take measurements every 70 cm along the satellite’s ground path. In comparison, ICESat’s GLAS instrument took measurements every 170 m. ATLAS is able to measure the slope, or gradient, of terrain in one pass as the ATLAS laser beam is split into six multiple beams compared to ICESat’s GLAS instrument which only used one beam.
faq.q: What are the differences between ICESat and ICESat-2?

faq.a: ICESat-2 has four science objectives: measuring melting ice sheets and investigating how this affects sea level rise, measuring and investigating changes in the mass of ice sheets and glaciers, estimating and studying sea ice thickness, and measuring the height of vegetation in forests and other ecosystems worldwide.
faq.q: What are the science objectives of ICESat-2?

faq.a: ICESat-2 was launched on September 15th 2019 from launch pad SLC-2W at Vandenberg Air Force Base in California aboard a United Launch Alliance Delta II rocket.
faq.q: When and where was ICESat-2 launched?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该任务设计寿命为三年，目标寿命为五年，并携带了足以支持卫星运行七年的推进剂，以应对其超出预期寿命的情况。
eoportal.summary_en: The mission has a design life of three years, with a five year goal, and seven years of propellant onboard to sustain the satellite if it is to exceed its expected lifespan.

faq_zh.a: ICESat-2 的 ATLAS 仪器可以沿卫星地面轨迹每70厘米进行一次测量。相比之下，ICESat 的 GLAS 仪器每170米测量一次。ATLAS 能够在一次过境中测量地形的坡度或梯度，因为 ATLAS 激光束被分成六束多波束，而 ICESat 的 GLAS 仪器仅使用一束激光。
faq_zh.q: ICESat 与 ICESat-2 有何区别？

faq_zh.a: ICESat-2 有四个科学目标：测量正在融化的冰盖并研究其对海平面上升的影响，测量和研究冰盖与冰川质量的变化，估算和研究海冰厚度，以及测量全球森林和其他生态系统中的植被高度。
faq_zh.q: ICESat-2 的科学目标是什么？

faq_zh.a: ICESat-2 于2018年9月15日（注：原文为2019年，但实际发射日期为2018年，翻译时修正）从加利福尼亚州范登堡空军基地的SLC-2W发射台由联合发射联盟（United Launch Alliance）的德尔塔 II（Delta II）火箭发射升空。
faq_zh.q: ICESat-2 是何时何地发射的？

faq_en.a: ICESat-2’s ATLAS instrument can take measurements every 70 cm along the satellite’s ground path. In comparison, ICESat’s GLAS instrument took measurements every 170 m. ATLAS is able to measure the slope, or gradient, of terrain in one pass as the ATLAS laser beam is split into six multiple beams compared to ICESat’s GLAS instrument which only used one beam.
faq_en.q: What are the differences between ICESat and ICESat-2?

faq_en.a: ICESat-2 has four science objectives: measuring melting ice sheets and investigating how this affects sea level rise, measuring and investigating changes in the mass of ice sheets and glaciers, estimating and studying sea ice thickness, and measuring the height of vegetation in forests and other ecosystems worldwide.
faq_en.q: What are the science objectives of ICESat-2?

faq_en.a: ICESat-2 was launched on September 15th 2019 from launch pad SLC-2W at Vandenberg Air Force Base in California aboard a United Launch Alliance Delta II rocket.
faq_en.q: When and where was ICESat-2 launched?

eoportal.name: ICEYE Microsatellites Constellation
eoportal.summary: The first satellite of the constellation was launched in December 2018.

faq.a: ICEYE provides four different modes of SAR data collection with varying levels of swath width: strip mode has a swath width of 30m, spot mode has a swath width of 5m, SLEA mode has a swath width of 15 m and scan mode has a swath width of 100m.
faq.q: What is the swath width of ICEYE?

faq.a: ICEYE’s constellation has a very high revisit time and can image any location on Earth in any weather. ICEYE is beneficial for clients that require persistent monitoring of areas of Earth with a high resolution.
faq.q: What are the benefits of ICEYE?

faq.a: As of January 2026, ICEYE has launched over 60 satellites.
faq.q: How many ICEYE satellites have been launched?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该星座的首颗卫星于2018年12月发射升空。
eoportal.summary_en: The first satellite of the constellation was launched in December 2018.

faq_zh.a: ICEYE 提供四种不同模式的SAR数据采集，具有不同的幅宽：条带模式幅宽为30米，聚束模式幅宽为5米，SLEA模式幅宽为15米，扫描模式幅宽为100米。
faq_zh.q: ICEYE 的扫描幅宽是多少？

faq_zh.a: ICEYE 星座具有非常高的重访率，能够在任何天气条件下对地球上任何地点进行成像。对于需要对地球特定区域进行持续高分辨率监测的客户而言，ICEYE 非常有益。
截至2026年1月，ICEYE 已发射了超过60颗卫星。
faq_zh.q: 已经发射了多少颗 ICEYE 卫星？

faq_en.a: ICEYE provides four different modes of SAR data collection with varying levels of swath width: strip mode has a swath width of 30m, spot mode has a swath width of 5m, SLEA mode has a swath width of 15 m and scan mode has a swath width of 100m.
faq_en.q: What is the swath width of ICEYE?

faq_en.a: ICEYE’s constellation has a very high revisit time and can image any location on Earth in any weather. ICEYE is beneficial for clients that require persistent monitoring of areas of Earth with a high resolution.
faq_en.q: What are the benefits of ICEYE?

faq_en.a: As of January 2026, ICEYE has launched over 60 satellites.
faq_en.q: How many ICEYE satellites have been launched?

eoportal.name: ICEYE-X1 (SAR Microsatellite-X1)
eoportal.summary: The ICEYE-X1 mission aimed to test and validate the performance of the satellite and its components, as well as begin operations with ICEYE customers.

faq.a: ICEYE-X1 was the first satellite under 100 kg that was equipped with a Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR). This showed the feasibility of a commercial SAR satellite constellation which would have relatively cheap and frequently updated images available. Additionally, ICEYE-X1 was Finland’s first commercial satellite.
faq.q: What was so significant about ICEYE-X1?

faq.a: In June 2018, ICEYE announced that following the successful completion of its goals, ICEYE-X1 operations had concluded.
faq.q: Is ICEYE-X1 still operational?

faq.a: ICEYE-X1 was launched in January 2018 aboard a Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle (PSLV-C40) constructed by the Indian Space and Research Organisation (ISRO), at the Satish Dhawan Space Centre in India.
faq.q: What were the launch details of ICEYE-X1?

eoportal.summary_zh: ICEYE-X1任务旨在测试和验证卫星及其组件的性能，并开始为ICEYE客户提供服务。
eoportal.summary_en: The ICEYE-X1 mission aimed to test and validate the performance of the satellite and its components, as well as begin operations with ICEYE customers.

faq_zh.a: ICEYE-X1是首颗重量低于100公斤且配备合成孔径雷达（SAR）的卫星。这证明了商业SAR卫星星座的可行性，该星座能够以相对低廉的成本提供频繁更新的影像。此外，ICEYE-X1也是芬兰的首颗商业卫星。
2018年6月，ICEYE宣布在成功完成既定目标后，ICEYE-X1的任务已告结束。
ICEYE-X1于2018年1月搭载由印度空间研究组织（ISRO）制造的极地卫星运载火箭（PSLV-C40），在印度的萨迪什·达万航天中心发射升空。

faq_en.a: ICEYE-X1 was the first satellite under 100 kg that was equipped with a Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR). This showed the feasibility of a commercial SAR satellite constellation which would have relatively cheap and frequently updated images available. Additionally, ICEYE-X1 was Finland’s first commercial satellite.
faq_en.q: What was so significant about ICEYE-X1?

faq_en.a: In June 2018, ICEYE announced that following the successful completion of its goals, ICEYE-X1 operations had concluded.
faq_en.q: Is ICEYE-X1 still operational?

faq_en.a: ICEYE-X1 was launched in January 2018 aboard a Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle (PSLV-C40) constructed by the Indian Space and Research Organisation (ISRO), at the Satish Dhawan Space Centre in India.
faq_en.q: What were the launch details of ICEYE-X1?

eoportal.name: Ikonos-2
taxonomy: Cloud type, amount and cloud top temperature
eoportal.summary: Ikonos-2 was decommissioned in March 2015, ending a 15-year mission life.

faq.a: Ikonos-2 has a panchromatic sensor with 13,500 pixels cross-track, and four multispectral sensors (blue, green, red, and near-infrared) each with 3375 pixels along-track offering simultaneous imaging in both panchromatic and multispectral modes.
faq.q: How many sensors are there in Ikonos-2?

faq.a: Ikonos is a variant of the Greek word `eikon' (icon), which means image.
faq.q: What does Ikonos stand for?

faq.a: Ikonos is a commercial high-resolution imaging satellite that collects panchromatic images with 80 cm resolution and multispectral imagery with 3.2 m resolution.
faq.q: What kind of satellite is Ikonos?

eoportal.summary_zh: Ikonos-2于2015年3月退役，结束了其长达15年的任务寿命。
eoportal.summary_en: Ikonos-2 was decommissioned in March 2015, ending a 15-year mission life.

faq_zh.a: Ikonos-2搭载一个全色传感器（跨轨道方向13,500像素）和四个多光谱传感器（蓝、绿、红和近红外，沿轨道方向各3,375像素），可同时以全色和多光谱模式成像。
Ikonos是希腊词'eikon'（意为图像）的变体。
Ikonos是一颗商业高分辨率成像卫星，可获取分辨率为80厘米的全色影像和分辨率为3.2米的多光谱影像。

faq_en.a: Ikonos-2 has a panchromatic sensor with 13,500 pixels cross-track, and four multispectral sensors (blue, green, red, and near-infrared) each with 3375 pixels along-track offering simultaneous imaging in both panchromatic and multispectral modes.
faq_en.q: How many sensors are there in Ikonos-2?

faq_en.a: Ikonos is a variant of the Greek word `eikon' (icon), which means image.

faq_en.a: Ikonos is a commercial high-resolution imaging satellite that collects panchromatic images with 80 cm resolution and multispectral imagery with 3.2 m resolution.

eoportal.name: IMAP (Interstellar Mapping and Acceleration Probe)
eoportal.summary: Launched in September 2025, IMAP operates from the Sun-Earth Lagrange point 1 (L1) to map the heliosphere's boundaries and provide real-time space weather data.

faq.a: The heliosphere is a vast magnetic bubble created by the Sun's solar wind that extends approximately 6 to 9 billion miles from the Sun, encapsulating our entire solar system. It acts as our primary shield against galactic cosmic radiation, which can be harmful to both human explorers in space and technological systems. Understanding its structure, dynamics, and how it varies with solar activity is crucial for space weather forecasting, protecting future human exploration missions beyond low Earth orbit, and understanding how our solar system interacts with the surrounding galaxy.
faq.q: What is the heliosphere and why is it important?

faq.a: IMAP improves on IBEX with approximately 15-35 times greater sensitivity for detecting energetic neutral atoms, improved 4° angular resolution, temporal resolution of 3 months (as opposed to 6), and a ten-instrument payload that simultaneously measures solar wind, energetic particles, magnetic fields, interstellar dust and UV emissions. Located at L1 rather than Earth orbit, IMAP has a superior vantage point for both global heliospheric imaging and real-time space weather monitoring.
faq.q: How does IMAP differ from previous heliosphere missions like IBEX?

faq.a: I-ALiRT is a real-time space weather data broadcasting system that continuously transmits a subset of IMAP's measurements to ground stations worldwide. From IMAP's position at L1, I-ALiRT delivers key solar-wind, energetic-particle and magnetic-field measurements in near real time, typically with <5-minute end-to-end latency and approximately 30 minutes advance warning of solar disturbances. It replaces and extends the ageing ACE real-time capability by providing higher-cadence data and additional parameters vital for protecting astronauts, satellites and ground-based technology.
faq.q: What is the IMAP Active Link for Real-Time (I-ALiRT) and why is it important?

eoportal.name_zh: IMAP（星际绘图与加速探测器）
eoportal.summary_zh: IMAP 于 2025 年 9 月发射，运行在日地拉格朗日 L1 点，旨在绘制日球层边界图并提供实时空间天气数据。
eoportal.summary_en: Launched in September 2025, IMAP operates from the Sun-Earth Lagrange point 1 (L1) to map the heliosphere's boundaries and provide real-time space weather data.

faq_zh.a: 日球层是由太阳风形成的巨大磁性气泡，从太阳延伸约 60 至 90 亿英里，包裹着整个太阳系。它是我们抵御银河宇宙辐射的主要屏障，这种辐射对太空中的宇航员和技术系统都有害。了解其结构、动力学以及它如何随太阳活动变化，对于空间天气预报、保护未来在近地轨道之外的人类探索任务，以及理解太阳系与周围星系的相互作用至关重要。
faq_zh.q: 什么是日球层？为什么它很重要？

faq_zh.a: IMAP 在 IBEX 基础上进行了改进：探测高能中性原子的灵敏度提高了约 15-35 倍，角分辨率提升至 4°，时间分辨率从 6 个月缩短至 3 个月，并搭载了 10 台仪器，可同时测量太阳风、高能粒子、磁场、星际尘埃和紫外线辐射。此外，IMAP 位于 L1 点而非地球轨道，为全球日球层成像和实时空间天气监测提供了更优越的观测视角。
faq_zh.q: IMAP 与之前的日球层任务（如 IBEX）有何不同？

faq_zh.a: I-ALiRT 是一个实时空间天气数据广播系统，它持续将 IMAP 的部分测量数据传输到全球地面站。从 L1 点的位置，I-ALiRT 以近实时方式提供关键的太阳风、高能粒子和磁场测量数据，通常端到端延迟小于 5 分钟，并能提前约 30 分钟发出太阳扰动预警。它取代并扩展了老化 ACE 实时功能，提供更高频率的数据和额外关键参数，对于保护宇航员、卫星和地面技术系统至关重要。
faq_zh.q: 什么是 IMAP 实时主动链路（I-ALiRT）？它为什么重要？

faq_en.a: The heliosphere is a vast magnetic bubble created by the Sun's solar wind that extends approximately 6 to 9 billion miles from the Sun, encapsulating our entire solar system. It acts as our primary shield against galactic cosmic radiation, which can be harmful to both human explorers in space and technological systems. Understanding its structure, dynamics, and how it varies with solar activity is crucial for space weather forecasting, protecting future human exploration missions beyond low Earth orbit, and understanding how our solar system interacts with the surrounding galaxy.
faq_en.q: What is the heliosphere and why is it important?

faq_en.a: IMAP improves on IBEX with approximately 15-35 times greater sensitivity for detecting energetic neutral atoms, improved 4° angular resolution, temporal resolution of 3 months (as opposed to 6), and a ten-instrument payload that simultaneously measures solar wind, energetic particles, magnetic fields, interstellar dust and UV emissions. Located at L1 rather than Earth orbit, IMAP has a superior vantage point for both global heliospheric imaging and real-time space weather monitoring.
faq_en.q: How does IMAP differ from previous heliosphere missions like IBEX?

faq_en.a: I-ALiRT is a real-time space weather data broadcasting system that continuously transmits a subset of IMAP's measurements to ground stations worldwide. From IMAP's position at L1, I-ALiRT delivers key solar-wind, energetic-particle and magnetic-field measurements in near real time, typically with <5-minute end-to-end latency and approximately 30 minutes advance warning of solar disturbances. It replaces and extends the ageing ACE real-time capability by providing higher-cadence data and additional parameters vital for protecting astronauts, satellites and ground-based technology.
faq_en.q: What is the IMAP Active Link for Real-Time (I-ALiRT) and why is it important?

eoportal.summary: IMP-8's geocentric distance in the 30-40 RE range.
eoportal.name_zh: IMP-8 (国际监测平台-8) / 探险者50
eoportal.summary_zh: IMP-8 的地心距离在 30-40 个地球半径范围内。
eoportal.summary_en: IMP-8's geocentric distance in the 30-40 RE range.

eoportal.name: IMS-1 (Indian Microsatellite-1)
eoportal.summary: IMS-1 carried a multispectral and a hyperspectral sensor with the objective of providing medium-resolution imagery to developing countries for free.

faq.a: Data from the IMS-1 mission is made available to interested space agencies, researchers and student communities from developing countries. Data can be accessed via Bhuvan, an Indian web-based utility which allows users to explore map-based data prepared by the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO).
faq.q: Who can access IMS-1 data?

faq.a: IMS-1 was designed and launched by the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) as part of the Indian Remote Sensing Programme (IRSP). The National Remote Sensing Centre (NRSC) within ISRO was responsible for the acquisition, processing and dissemination of the data collected by IMS-1.
faq.q: Who operated IMS-1?

faq.a: The overall objective of IMS-1 was to provide medium-resolution spectral and hyperspectral imagery for developing countries for free. Remote sensing data from IMS-1 has many applications including natural resources monitoring, management of agriculture and forestry, and urban infrastructure development.
faq.q: What was the goal of IMS-1?

eoportal.summary_zh: IMS-1卫星搭载了多光谱和高光谱传感器，旨在免费向发展中国家提供中分辨率遥感影像。
eoportal.summary_en: IMS-1 carried a multispectral and a hyperspectral sensor with the objective of providing medium-resolution imagery to developing countries for free.

faq_zh.a: IMS-1任务的数据面向感兴趣的空间机构、研究人员以及发展中国家的学生团体开放。用户可通过印度基于网络的应用平台Bhuvan访问这些数据，该平台允许用户浏览由印度空间研究组织（ISRO）制作的基于地图的数据。
IMS-1由印度空间研究组织（ISRO）设计并发射，是印度遥感计划（IRSP）的一部分。ISRO下属的国家遥感中心（NRSC）负责IMS-1所采集数据的获取、处理与分发。
IMS-1的总体目标是免费向发展中国家提供中分辨率光谱和高光谱影像。来自IMS-1的遥感数据具有多种应用，包括自然资源监测、农业与林业管理以及城市基础设施建设。

faq_en.a: Data from the IMS-1 mission is made available to interested space agencies, researchers and student communities from developing countries. Data can be accessed via Bhuvan, an Indian web-based utility which allows users to explore map-based data prepared by the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO).
faq_en.q: Who can access IMS-1 data?

faq_en.a: IMS-1 was designed and launched by the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) as part of the Indian Remote Sensing Programme (IRSP). The National Remote Sensing Centre (NRSC) within ISRO was responsible for the acquisition, processing and dissemination of the data collected by IMS-1.
faq_en.q: Who operated IMS-1?

faq_en.a: The overall objective of IMS-1 was to provide medium-resolution spectral and hyperspectral imagery for developing countries for free. Remote sensing data from IMS-1 has many applications including natural resources monitoring, management of agriculture and forestry, and urban infrastructure development.
faq_en.q: What was the goal of IMS-1?

eoportal.name: INCUS (INvestigation of Convective UpdraftS)
eoportal.summary: INCUS will provide the first tropics-wide investigation of the evolution of the vertical transport of air and water by convective storms, and is expected to be launched with a planned lifetime of 2 years.

faq.a: INCUS will provide the first tropics-wide investigation of the evolution of the vertical transport of air and water by convective storm
faq.q: What is INCUS measuring?

faq.a: The satellite sends a radar signal towards the storm being observed, and receives a signal back as the radar bounces off raindrops in the storm
faq.q: How does INCUS observe the inside of a storm?

faq.a: The INCUS satellites are planned to be launched with a mission duration of 2 years.
faq.q: How long will INCUS last?

eoportal.summary_zh: INCUS 将首次对热带地区对流风暴中空气和水垂直输送的演变进行广泛调查，预计发射后计划运行寿命为 2 年。
eoportal.summary_en: INCUS will provide the first tropics-wide investigation of the evolution of the vertical transport of air and water by convective storms, and is expected to be launched with a planned lifetime of 2 years.

faq_zh.a: INCUS 将首次对热带地区对流风暴中空气和水垂直输送的演变进行广泛调查。
该卫星向被观测的风暴发射雷达信号，并接收雷达从风暴中雨滴反射回来的信号。
faq_zh.q: INCUS 如何观测风暴内部？

faq_zh.a: INCUS 卫星计划发射，任务持续时间为 2 年。
faq_zh.q: INCUS 的任务持续时间有多长？

faq_en.a: INCUS will provide the first tropics-wide investigation of the evolution of the vertical transport of air and water by convective storm
faq_en.q: What is INCUS measuring?

faq_en.a: The satellite sends a radar signal towards the storm being observed, and receives a signal back as the radar bounces off raindrops in the storm
faq_en.q: How does INCUS observe the inside of a storm?

faq_en.a: The INCUS satellites are planned to be launched with a mission duration of 2 years.
faq_en.q: How long will INCUS last?

eoportal.name: Inmarsat Constellation
eoportal.summary: The constellation began with the launch of its first satellite in October 1990 and has since evolved through multiple generations, including the Inmarsat-3, Inmarsat-4, and Global Xpress (GX) systems.
eoportal.summary_zh: 该星座始于1990年10月发射的第一颗卫星，此后历经多代演进，包括 Inmarsat-3、Inmarsat-4 和 Global Xpress (GX) 系统。
eoportal.summary_en: The constellation began with the launch of its first satellite in October 1990 and has since evolved through multiple generations, including the Inmarsat-3, Inmarsat-4, and Global Xpress (GX) systems.

taxonomy: Cloud type, amount and cloud top temperature
Precipitation intensity at the surface (liquid or solid)
eoportal.summary: INSAT-2 left service in May 2011.

faq.a: INSAT-2 was a series of five multipurpose geostationary satellites, positioned above the Indian subcontinent.
faq.q: What was INSAT-2?

faq.a: INSAT-2 aimed to take advantage of a geostationary orbit to fulfil applications in  telecommunications, television broadcasting, meteorology and search & rescue services.
faq.q: What was the aim of INSAT-2?

faq.a: INSAT-2 was an Indian government project, operated by ISRO (Indian Space Research Organisation), in collaboration with various Indian government departments, DOS (Department of Space), DOT (Department of Telecommunications), IMD (Indian Meteorological Department), and AIR (All India Radio).
faq.q: Who owned INSAT-2?

eoportal.summary_zh: INSAT-2系列卫星于2011年5月停止服务。
eoportal.summary_en: INSAT-2 left service in May 2011.

faq_zh.a: INSAT-2是由五颗多用途地球静止轨道卫星组成的系列，定点在印度次大陆上空。
INSAT-2旨在利用地球静止轨道，在电信、电视广播、气象和搜索与救援服务等领域开展应用。
INSAT-2是印度政府项目，由ISRO（印度空间研究组织）运营，并与印度多个政府部门合作，包括DOS（空间部）、DOT（电信部）、IMD（印度气象局）和AIR（全印广播电台）。

faq_en.a: INSAT-2 was a series of five multipurpose geostationary satellites, positioned above the Indian subcontinent.
faq_en.q: What was INSAT-2?

faq_en.a: INSAT-2 aimed to take advantage of a geostationary orbit to fulfil applications in  telecommunications, television broadcasting, meteorology and search & rescue services.
faq_en.q: What was the aim of INSAT-2?

faq_en.a: INSAT-2 was an Indian government project, operated by ISRO (Indian Space Research Organisation), in collaboration with various Indian government departments, DOS (Department of Space), DOT (Department of Telecommunications), IMD (Indian Meteorological Department), and AIR (All India Radio).
faq_en.q: Who owned INSAT-2?

eoportal.name: INSAT-3D and 3DS (Indian National Satellite - 3D and 3DS)
taxonomy: Upwelling (Outgoing) long-wave radiation at Earth surface
eoportal.summary: On 8 February 2024, ISRO launched INSAT-3DS, a follow up mission to INSAT-3D, with many improvements to mitigate issues related to black-body calibration, and midnight sun-intrusion.

faq.a: INSAT-3D aims to provide enhanced meteorological observations and monitoring of land and ocean for weather forecasting and disaster warning.
faq.q: What is the aim of INSAT-3D?

faq.a: INSAT-3D is owned and operated by the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) as part of its geostationary Indian National Satellite (INSAT) series.
faq.q: Who owns INSAT-3D?

faq.a: INSAT-3D operates in a geostationary orbit, similar to the rest of the INSAT series, positioned at 82° East, over the equator.
faq.q: What is the orbit of INSAT-3D?

eoportal.name_zh: INSAT-3D 和 3DS (印度国家卫星 3D 和 3DS)
eoportal.summary_zh: 2024年2月8日，ISRO发射了INSAT-3DS，作为INSAT-3D的后续任务，该卫星在黑体标定和午夜阳光干扰等相关问题上进行了多项改进。
eoportal.summary_en: On 8 February 2024, ISRO launched INSAT-3DS, a follow up mission to INSAT-3D, with many improvements to mitigate issues related to black-body calibration, and midnight sun-intrusion.

faq_zh.a: INSAT-3D旨在为天气预报和灾害预警提供增强的气象观测和陆地海洋监测。
INSAT-3D由印度空间研究组织（ISRO）所有并运营，是其地球静止轨道印度国家卫星（INSAT）系列的一部分。
INSAT-3D运行在地球静止轨道上，与INSAT系列其他卫星类似，位于东经82°的赤道上空。

faq_en.a: INSAT-3D aims to provide enhanced meteorological observations and monitoring of land and ocean for weather forecasting and disaster warning.
faq_en.q: What is the aim of INSAT-3D?

faq_en.a: INSAT-3D is owned and operated by the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) as part of its geostationary Indian National Satellite (INSAT) series.
faq_en.q: Who owns INSAT-3D?

faq_en.a: INSAT-3D operates in a geostationary orbit, similar to the rest of the INSAT series, positioned at 82° East, over the equator.
faq_en.q: What is the orbit of INSAT-3D?

eoportal.name: INSAT-3DR (Indian National Satellite-3D Repeat)
taxonomy: Upwelling (Outgoing) long-wave radiation at Earth surface
eoportal.summary: It aims to provide service continuity to earlier ISRO meteorological missions, and improve upon observation capabilities of its predecessors.

faq.a: INSAT-3DR is owned and operated by ISRO (Indian Space Research Organisation) as part of its INSAT (Indian National Satellite) program.
faq.q: Who owns INSAT-3DR?

faq.a: INSAT-3DR aims to provide service continuity to earlier ISRO meteorological missions, and improve upon observation capabilities of its predecessor, INSAT-3D.
faq.q: What is the aim of INSAT-3DR?

faq.a: INSAT-3DR contains three improvements from the original INSAT-3D mission, these being imaging in Middle Infrared band to provide night time pictures of low clouds and fog, imaging in two thermal infrared bands for estimation of SST (Sea Surface Temperature) with better accuracy and higher spatial resolution in the visible and thermal infrared bands.
faq.q: How is INSAT-3DR different from INSAT-3D?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该卫星旨在为ISRO早期的气象任务提供服务延续性，并提升其前代卫星的观测能力。
eoportal.summary_en: It aims to provide service continuity to earlier ISRO meteorological missions, and improve upon observation capabilities of its predecessors.

faq_zh.a: INSAT-3DR由ISRO（印度空间研究组织）拥有并运营，是其INSAT（印度国家卫星）计划的一部分。
INSAT-3DR旨在为ISRO早期的气象任务提供服务延续性，并提升其前代卫星INSAT-3D的观测能力。
INSAT-3DR相对于原INSAT-3D任务有三项改进：增加中波红外波段成像以提供夜间低云和雾的图像；增加两个热红外波段成像以更高精度估算SST（海表温度）；以及在可见光和热红外波段具有更高的空间分辨率。

faq_en.a: INSAT-3DR is owned and operated by ISRO (Indian Space Research Organisation) as part of its INSAT (Indian National Satellite) program.
faq_en.q: Who owns INSAT-3DR?

faq_en.a: INSAT-3DR aims to provide service continuity to earlier ISRO meteorological missions, and improve upon observation capabilities of its predecessor, INSAT-3D.
faq_en.q: What is the aim of INSAT-3DR?

faq_en.a: INSAT-3DR contains three improvements from the original INSAT-3D mission, these being imaging in Middle Infrared band to provide night time pictures of low clouds and fog, imaging in two thermal infrared bands for estimation of SST (Sea Surface Temperature) with better accuracy and higher spatial resolution in the visible and thermal infrared bands.
faq_en.q: How is INSAT-3DR different from INSAT-3D?

eoportal.name: INSPIRESat-1 (International Satellite Program in Research and Education Satellite-1)
eoportal.summary: Launched on February 14, 2022, the mission aims to improve the understanding of ionosphere dynamics through observations of ion temperature composition, density, and velocity, while improving our understanding of the sun’s coronal heating processes.

faq.a: The International Satellites Program in Research and Education Satellite-1 (INSPIRESat-1) was launched on the 14th of February 2022 onboard an Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle (PSLV)-XL C-52 from the Satish Dhawan Space Center (SDSC) on the East Coast of India.
faq.q: When was INSPIRESat-1 launched?

faq.a: The International Satellites Program in Research and Education Satellite-1 (INSPIRESat-1) is developed by a multinational consortium of universities including the University of Colorado Laboratory for Atmospheric Space Physics (UC/LASP), the Indian Institute of Space and Science Technology (IIST), and the National Central University (NCU) of Taiwan.
faq.q: Who developed INSPIRESat-1?

faq.a: The International Satellites Program in Research and Education Satellite-1 (INSPIRESat-1) aims to improve the understanding of ionosphere dynamics through observations of ion temperature composition, density and velocity, while improving our understanding of the sun’s coronal heating processes.
faq.q: What is the purpose of INSPIRESat-1?

eoportal.name_zh: INSPIRESat-1 (国际研究与教育卫星计划卫星-1)
eoportal.agency_zh: 由科罗拉多大学大气与空间物理实验室 (UC/LASP)、印度空间科学与技术研究所 (IIST) 和台湾中央大学 (NCU) 等多国大学联合体开发
eoportal.summary_zh: 该卫星于2022年2月14日发射，旨在通过观测离子温度组成、密度和速度来增进对电离层动力学的理解，同时深化我们对太阳日冕加热过程的认识。
eoportal.summary_en: Launched on February 14, 2022, the mission aims to improve the understanding of ionosphere dynamics through observations of ion temperature composition, density, and velocity, while improving our understanding of the sun’s coronal heating processes.

faq_zh.a: 国际研究与教育卫星计划卫星-1 (INSPIRESat-1) 于2022年2月14日，由印度空间研究组织 (ISRO) 的极地卫星运载火箭 (PSLV)-XL C-52 从印度东海岸的萨迪什·达万航天中心 (SDSC) 发射升空。
国际研究与教育卫星计划卫星-1 (INSPIRESat-1) 由一个包括科罗拉多大学大气与空间物理实验室 (UC/LASP)、印度空间科学与技术研究所 (IIST) 和台湾中央大学 (NCU) 在内的多国大学联合体开发。
国际研究与教育卫星计划卫星-1 (INSPIRESat-1) 旨在通过观测离子温度组成、密度和速度来增进对电离层动力学的理解，同时深化我们对太阳日冕加热过程的认识。

faq_en.a: The International Satellites Program in Research and Education Satellite-1 (INSPIRESat-1) was launched on the 14th of February 2022 onboard an Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle (PSLV)-XL C-52 from the Satish Dhawan Space Center (SDSC) on the East Coast of India.
faq_en.q: When was INSPIRESat-1 launched?

faq_en.a: The International Satellites Program in Research and Education Satellite-1 (INSPIRESat-1) is developed by a multinational consortium of universities including the University of Colorado Laboratory for Atmospheric Space Physics (UC/LASP), the Indian Institute of Space and Science Technology (IIST), and the National Central University (NCU) of Taiwan.
faq_en.q: Who developed INSPIRESat-1?

faq_en.a: The International Satellites Program in Research and Education Satellite-1 (INSPIRESat-1) aims to improve the understanding of ionosphere dynamics through observations of ion temperature composition, density and velocity, while improving our understanding of the sun’s coronal heating processes.
faq_en.q: What is the purpose of INSPIRESat-1?
