eoportal.name: Intuition-1
eoportal.summary: It is a technology demonstration mission for a novel onboard data processing unit and an advanced, 192-band hyperspectral camera.

faq.a: Intuition-1 aims to demonstrate a novel data processing unit that will reduce that volume of transmitted data, allowing greater efficiency communications with ground stations.
faq.q: What is the aim of Intuition-1?

faq.a: Intuition-1 carries a single instrument, the Eagle hyperspectral camera.
faq.q: What is the instrument used by Intuition-1?

faq.a: Intuition-1 carries the Leopard data processing unit (DPU), which is capable of three trillion operations per second, and enables the reduction of data volume that the mission aims to achieve.
faq.q: What is the onboard data processing unit on Intuition-1?

eoportal.summary_zh: 这是一项技术验证任务，旨在测试一种新型星载数据处理单元和一台先进的192波段高光谱相机。
eoportal.summary_en: It is a technology demonstration mission for a novel onboard data processing unit and an advanced, 192-band hyperspectral camera.

faq_zh.a: Intuition-1 旨在验证一种新型数据处理单元，该单元将减少传输数据量，从而提高与地面站通信的效率。
faq_zh.q: Intuition-1 任务的目标是什么？

faq_zh.a: Intuition-1 搭载了单一仪器，即 Eagle 高光谱相机。
Intuition-1 搭载了 Leopard 数据处理单元，其运算能力达到每秒三万亿次，能够实现任务旨在达成的数据量缩减。
faq_zh.q: Intuition-1 上的星载数据处理单元是什么？

faq_en.a: Intuition-1 aims to demonstrate a novel data processing unit that will reduce that volume of transmitted data, allowing greater efficiency communications with ground stations.
faq_en.q: What is the aim of Intuition-1?

faq_en.a: Intuition-1 carries a single instrument, the Eagle hyperspectral camera.
faq_en.q: What is the instrument used by Intuition-1?

faq_en.a: Intuition-1 carries the Leopard data processing unit (DPU), which is capable of three trillion operations per second, and enables the reduction of data volume that the mission aims to achieve.
faq_en.q: What is the onboard data processing unit on Intuition-1?

eoportal.name: Intuitive Machines Nova-C
eoportal.summary: IM-1, launched in February 2024, and IM-2, launched in February 2025, lasted 14 and 8 days, respectively, carrying both CLPS and commercial payloads for scientific exploration of the Moon and technology validations.

faq.a: IM-1, the first mission in Intuitive Machines’ Lunar Payload and Data Services (LPDS) programme, successfully soft-landed a Nova-C lunar lander, named Odysseus, on the Moon on 22 February, 2024. The mission was successful despite some landing gear damage and landing with an unplanned tilt, lasting 14 days.
faq.q: What happened to the first Intuitive Machines mission?

faq.a: IM-2, the second mission in Intuitive Machine’s Lunar Payload and Data Services (LPDS) programme, landed a Nova-C lunar lander, named Athena, on the Moon on 6 March 2025 with an incorrect orientation that prevented the spacecraft from generating sufficient power. The mission ended on 7 March 2025, less than 13 hours after landing. The landing prevented the deployment of certain rovers and while some payloads were able to power on and transmit data, some were not able to become operational.
faq.q: What happened to the second Intuitive Machines mission?

faq.a: Intuitive Machines has been contracted under NASA’s Commercial Lunar Payload Services (CLPS) initiative for four lunar landing missions using the Nova-C lunar lander: IM-1, IM-2, IM-3, and IM-4, to deliver over 20 NASA payloads to the Moon.
faq.q: What is the NASA contract with Intuitive Machines?

eoportal.summary_zh: IM-1任务于2024年2月发射，IM-2任务于2025年2月发射，分别持续了14天和8天。两项任务均搭载了CLPS和商业载荷，用于月球科学探测和技术验证。
eoportal.summary_en: IM-1, launched in February 2024, and IM-2, launched in February 2025, lasted 14 and 8 days, respectively, carrying both CLPS and commercial payloads for scientific exploration of the Moon and technology validations.

faq_zh.a: IM-1是Intuitive Machines公司月球载荷与数据服务（LPDS）计划的首次任务，于2024年2月22日成功将名为Odysseus的Nova-C月球着陆器软着陆在月球上。尽管着陆支架受损且着陆时出现非计划倾斜，任务仍持续了14天，总体成功。
faq_zh.q: Intuitive Machines的首次任务发生了什么？

faq_zh.a: IM-2是Intuitive Machines公司月球载荷与数据服务（LPDS）计划的第二次任务，于2025年3月6日将名为Athena的Nova-C月球着陆器着陆在月球上。由于着陆器姿态错误导致供电不足，任务在着陆后不到13小时（即2025年3月7日）终止。着陆姿态问题影响了部分巡视器的部署；虽然部分载荷成功加电并传输数据，但未能全部正常工作。
faq_zh.q: Intuitive Machines的第二次任务发生了什么？

faq_zh.a: 根据NASA的商业月球载荷服务（CLPS）计划，Intuitive Machines公司获得了四次月球着陆任务的合同，将使用Nova-C月球着陆器执行IM-1、IM-2、IM-3和IM-4任务，向月球运送超过20个NASA载荷。
faq_zh.q: NASA与Intuitive Machines的合同内容是什么？

faq_en.a: IM-1, the first mission in Intuitive Machines’ Lunar Payload and Data Services (LPDS) programme, successfully soft-landed a Nova-C lunar lander, named Odysseus, on the Moon on 22 February, 2024. The mission was successful despite some landing gear damage and landing with an unplanned tilt, lasting 14 days.
faq_en.q: What happened to the first Intuitive Machines mission?

faq_en.a: IM-2, the second mission in Intuitive Machine’s Lunar Payload and Data Services (LPDS) programme, landed a Nova-C lunar lander, named Athena, on the Moon on 6 March 2025 with an incorrect orientation that prevented the spacecraft from generating sufficient power. The mission ended on 7 March 2025, less than 13 hours after landing. The landing prevented the deployment of certain rovers and while some payloads were able to power on and transmit data, some were not able to become operational.
faq_en.q: What happened to the second Intuitive Machines mission?

faq_en.a: Intuitive Machines has been contracted under NASA’s Commercial Lunar Payload Services (CLPS) initiative for four lunar landing missions using the Nova-C lunar lander: IM-1, IM-2, IM-3, and IM-4, to deliver over 20 NASA payloads to the Moon.
faq_en.q: What is the NASA contract with Intuitive Machines?

eoportal.name: Ionosphera-M / Ionosfera-M
eoportal.summary: The satellites were launched in pairs, in November 2024 and July 2025.

faq.a: Ionosphera-M consists of four Earth pointing Ionosphera satellites and a fifth, sun pointing Ionozond satellite.
faq.q: How many satellites are in the Ionosphera-M constellation?

faq.a: Ionosphera-M will observe the effects of solar and geomagnetic activity, including both natural and man-made irregularities and ionosphere disturbances.
faq.q: What is the aim of the Ionosphera-M mission?

faq.a: Each satellite carries the same eight instruments which include a suite of spectrometers and a radio occultation receiver.
faq.q: How many instruments does each Ionosphera-M satellite carry?

eoportal.summary_zh: 这些卫星以成对方式发射，分别在2024年11月和2025年7月升空。
eoportal.summary_en: The satellites were launched in pairs, in November 2024 and July 2025.

faq_zh.a: Ionosphera-M星座包括四颗对地观测的Ionosphera卫星和第五颗对日观测的Ionozond卫星。
faq_zh.q: Ionosphera-M星座由多少颗卫星组成？

faq_zh.a: Ionosphera-M将观测太阳和地磁活动的影响，包括自然和人为的不规则性以及电离层扰动。
每颗卫星携带相同的八台仪器，包括一套光谱仪和一个无线电掩星接收机。
faq_zh.q: 每颗Ionosphera-M卫星携带多少台仪器？

faq_en.a: Ionosphera-M consists of four Earth pointing Ionosphera satellites and a fifth, sun pointing Ionozond satellite.

faq_en.a: Ionosphera-M will observe the effects of solar and geomagnetic activity, including both natural and man-made irregularities and ionosphere disturbances.
faq_en.q: What is the aim of the Ionosphera-M mission?

faq_en.a: Each satellite carries the same eight instruments which include a suite of spectrometers and a radio occultation receiver.
faq_en.q: How many instruments does each Ionosphera-M satellite carry?

eoportal.name: IRAZU CubeSat Mission
eoportal.summary: UBAKUSAT, a joint Turkish and Japanese 3U Cubesat (4 kg) technology demonstration mission, built by ITU (Istanbul Technical University), Istanbul, Turkey in cooperation with JPF (Japan Space Forum), and KIT (Kyushu Institute of Technology).
eoportal.summary_zh: UBAKUSAT是一颗土耳其与日本合作的3U立方星（4公斤），属于技术验证任务。该卫星由土耳其伊斯坦布尔的伊斯坦布尔技术大学（ITU）与日本宇宙论坛（JPF）及九州工业大学（KIT）合作建造。
eoportal.summary_en: UBAKUSAT, a joint Turkish and Japanese 3U Cubesat (4 kg) technology demonstration mission, built by ITU (Istanbul Technical University), Istanbul, Turkey in cooperation with JPF (Japan Space Forum), and KIT (Kyushu Institute of Technology).

eoportal.name: Iridium NEXT
eoportal.summary: The satellites have been launched in eight stages, starting in January 2017 and finishing in January 2019, and have successfully replaced the previous generation Iridium satellites.

faq.a: Whilst the majority of satellite communication services implement a small number of geostationary satellites, Iridium NEXT is a large constellation of LEO (Low Earth Orbit) satellites. This proximity to Earth causes the transmitted signals to be stronger and faster.
faq.q: How does Iridium NEXT differ from other communication satellites?

faq.a: There are a total of 81 Iridium NEXT satellites: 66 in operation, nine spares in orbit, and six spares on the ground.
faq.q: How many Iridium NEXT satellites are there?

faq.a: The original number of satellites planned for the Iridium constellation was 77, and as the atomic number for the element Iridium is also 77, the name ‘Iridium’ was chosen.
faq.q: Why are the satellites called ‘Iridium’?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该卫星星座分八个阶段发射，从2017年1月开始至2019年1月完成，已成功替代了上一代铱星卫星。
eoportal.summary_en: The satellites have been launched in eight stages, starting in January 2017 and finishing in January 2019, and have successfully replaced the previous generation Iridium satellites.

faq_zh.a: 大多数卫星通信服务使用少量地球静止轨道卫星，而铱星 NEXT 是一个由众多低地球轨道（LEO）卫星组成的大型星座。由于距离地球更近，其传输信号更强、速度更快。
faq_zh.q: 铱星 NEXT 与其他通信卫星有何不同？

faq_zh.a: 铱星 NEXT 卫星共有81颗：66颗在轨运行，9颗在轨备用，6颗地面备用。
faq_zh.q: 有多少颗铱星 NEXT 卫星？

faq_zh.a: 最初计划的铱星星座卫星数量为77颗，而铱元素的原子序数也恰好是77，因此得名“铱星”。
faq_zh.q: 为什么这些卫星被称为“铱星”？

faq_en.a: Whilst the majority of satellite communication services implement a small number of geostationary satellites, Iridium NEXT is a large constellation of LEO (Low Earth Orbit) satellites. This proximity to Earth causes the transmitted signals to be stronger and faster.
faq_en.q: How does Iridium NEXT differ from other communication satellites?

faq_en.a: There are a total of 81 Iridium NEXT satellites: 66 in operation, nine spares in orbit, and six spares on the ground.
faq_en.q: How many Iridium NEXT satellites are there?

faq_en.a: The original number of satellites planned for the Iridium constellation was 77, and as the atomic number for the element Iridium is also 77, the name ‘Iridium’ was chosen.
faq_en.q: Why are the satellites called ‘Iridium’?

eoportal.name: IRS (Indian Remote Sensing Satellites)
taxonomy: Cloud type, amount and cloud top temperature
eoportal.summary: The first IRS was launched in 1988, and continued to observe and manage India’s natural resources until 2019.

faq.a: Previous IRSs had been launched via the Russian-built vehicle Vostok-2M. However, for the IRS-1E/P1 launch, ISRO used a self-developed launch vehicle: PSLV-D1 (Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle-Developmental flight 1). During the second stage of the launch, the software experienced an error preventing the satellite from achieving orbit.
faq.q: Why did the launch of IRS-1E/P1 fail?

faq.a: The IRS series observed and managed soil, crops, forests, and land use, as part of India’s National Natural Resources Management System (NNRMS).
faq.q: What did the IRS series do?

faq.a: There were a total of 10 satellites in the IRS series but only nine successfully achieved orbit; the IRS-1E/P1 launch vehicle experienced an error during launch.

IRS系列卫星对土壤、农作物、森林和土地利用进行观测和管理，是印度国家自然资源管理系统（NNRMS）的组成部分。

eoportal.name: ISS CLARREO Pathfinder
eoportal.summary: During the one-year mission, it aims to improve understanding of climate change by providing high accuracy measurements of solar irradiance, as well as demonstrate novel on-orbit and inter-calibration approaches for itself and other solar sensors.

faq.a: CLARREO Pathfinder is unique as its measurements will be tied directly to unchanging international standards, providing high accuracy in monitoring Earth’s climate.
faq.q: Why is CLARREO Pathfinder different from other climate missions?

faq.a: CLARREO Pathfinder (CPF) will produce raw image data as well as time, angle and space-matched inter-calibration data for CPF, CERES and VIIRS. This data will be made publicly available via Atmospheric Science Data Centre.
faq.q: What data will CLARREO Pathfinder produce?

faq.a: CPF is planned to take measurements for one year. An additional year will be required for data analysis.
faq.q: What will be the duration of the CLARREO Pathfinder mission?

eoportal.summary_zh: 在为期一年的任务期间，该仪器旨在通过提供高精度的太阳辐照度测量，增进对气候变化的理解，并演示其自身及其他太阳传感器的新型在轨与交叉定标方法。
eoportal.summary_en: During the one-year mission, it aims to improve understanding of climate change by providing high accuracy measurements of solar irradiance, as well as demonstrate novel on-orbit and inter-calibration approaches for itself and other solar sensors.

faq_zh.a: CLARREO Pathfinder 的独特之处在于其测量结果将直接与不变的国际标准挂钩，从而在监测地球气候时提供高精度。
faq_zh.q: CLARREO Pathfinder 与其他气候任务有何不同？

faq_zh.a: CLARREO Pathfinder (CPF) 将生成原始图像数据，以及 CPF、CERES 和 VIIRS 的时间、角度与空间匹配的交叉定标数据。这些数据将通过大气科学数据中心公开提供。
faq_zh.q: CLARREO Pathfinder 将产生什么数据？

faq_zh.a: CPF 计划进行为期一年的测量。数据分析将需要额外的一年时间。
faq_zh.q: CLARREO Pathfinder 任务的持续时间是多久？

faq_en.a: CLARREO Pathfinder is unique as its measurements will be tied directly to unchanging international standards, providing high accuracy in monitoring Earth’s climate.
faq_en.q: Why is CLARREO Pathfinder different from other climate missions?

faq_en.a: CLARREO Pathfinder (CPF) will produce raw image data as well as time, angle and space-matched inter-calibration data for CPF, CERES and VIIRS. This data will be made publicly available via Atmospheric Science Data Centre.
faq_en.q: What data will CLARREO Pathfinder produce?

faq_en.a: CPF is planned to take measurements for one year. An additional year will be required for data analysis.
faq_en.q: What will be the duration of the CLARREO Pathfinder mission?

eoportal.name: ISS Utilisation: AWE (Atmospheric Waves Experiment)
eoportal.summary: AWE will help scientists understand and forecast space weather which can have profound impacts on society due to its impacts on technology.

faq.a: The hydroxyl radical (OH) airglow layer is a region of the atmosphere where the light of electronically and/or vibration-rotationally excited atoms and molecules can be seen at night. OH radicals emit red and infrared-red in a narrow layer centred at 87 km.
faq.q: What is the OH airglow layer?

faq.a: The Atmospheric Waves Experiment (AWE) will, for the first time from space, measure Gravity Waves (GWs) in the thermosphere by using the OH airglow layer as a tracer.
faq.q: What is the aim of the AWE mission?

faq.a: The Atmospheric Waves Experiment (AWE) will use the Advanced Mesospheric Temperature Mapper (AMTM) which is developed by the Space Dynamics Laboratory (SDL). The AMTM for the AWE mission is based on the successful Utah State University AMTM which is currently deployed in two polar locations on Earth.
faq.q: What sensor will the AWE mission use?

eoportal.summary_zh: 大气波实验（AWE）将帮助科学家理解和预报空间天气，由于其对技术的影响，空间天气可能对社会产生深远影响。
eoportal.summary_en: AWE will help scientists understand and forecast space weather which can have profound impacts on society due to its impacts on technology.

faq_zh.a: 羟基（OH）气辉层是大气中的一个区域，夜间可以在该区域看到处于电子激发和/或振动-转动激发态的原子和分子的光。羟基自由基在以87公里为中心的狭窄层中发射红光和红外光。
大气波实验（AWE）将首次从太空中，利用羟基（OH）气辉层作为示踪物来测量热层中的重力波（GWs）。
大气波实验（AWE）将使用由空间动力学实验室（SDL）开发的先进中间层温度测绘仪（AMTM）。AWE任务所用的AMTM基于犹他州立大学成功开发的AMTM，该仪器目前已部署在地球的两个极地位置。
faq_zh.q: AWE任务将使用什么传感器？

faq_en.a: The hydroxyl radical (OH) airglow layer is a region of the atmosphere where the light of electronically and/or vibration-rotationally excited atoms and molecules can be seen at night. OH radicals emit red and infrared-red in a narrow layer centred at 87 km.
faq_en.q: What is the OH airglow layer?

faq_en.a: The Atmospheric Waves Experiment (AWE) will, for the first time from space, measure Gravity Waves (GWs) in the thermosphere by using the OH airglow layer as a tracer.
faq_en.q: What is the aim of the AWE mission?

faq_en.a: The Atmospheric Waves Experiment (AWE) will use the Advanced Mesospheric Temperature Mapper (AMTM) which is developed by the Space Dynamics Laboratory (SDL). The AMTM for the AWE mission is based on the successful Utah State University AMTM which is currently deployed in two polar locations on Earth.
faq_en.q: What sensor will the AWE mission use?

eoportal.name: ISS: ECOSTRESS (ECOsystem Spaceborne Thermal Radiometer Experiment on Space Station)
eoportal.summary: ECOSTRESS measures plant and soil temperature to improve understanding of plants' necessity for water.

faq.a: ECOSTRESS was launched in June 2018 onboard a Falcon 9 launch vehicle, manufactured by SpaceX, from the Kennedy Space Centre in the United States.
faq.q: What are the launch details of ECOSTRESS?

faq.a: As of November 2022, ECOSTRESS is still operational.
faq.q: Is ECOSTRESS still operational?

faq.a: ECOSTRESS is designed to monitor the temperature of soil and plants in order to measure plant evaporation and transpiration (evapotranspiration). From the data collected, we can better understand how much water plants require, how plants react to stress (due to an overabundance or lack of water), and how ecosystems are changing over time.
faq.q: What is the purpose of ECOSTRESS?

eoportal.name_zh: ISS: ECOSTRESS (国际空间站生态系统星载热辐射计实验)
eoportal.summary_zh: ECOSTRESS通过测量植物和土壤温度，以增进对植物水分需求的理解。
eoportal.summary_en: ECOSTRESS measures plant and soil temperature to improve understanding of plants' necessity for water.

faq_zh.a: ECOSTRESS于2018年6月在美国肯尼迪航天中心，由SpaceX公司制造的猎鹰9号运载火箭发射升空。
截至2022年11月，ECOSTRESS仍在轨运行。
ECOSTRESS旨在监测土壤和植物的温度，以测量植物的蒸发和蒸腾作用（蒸散作用）。通过收集的数据，我们可以更好地了解植物需要多少水分、植物如何应对胁迫（由于水分过多或缺乏），以及生态系统如何随时间变化。

faq_en.a: ECOSTRESS was launched in June 2018 onboard a Falcon 9 launch vehicle, manufactured by SpaceX, from the Kennedy Space Centre in the United States.
faq_en.q: What are the launch details of ECOSTRESS?

faq_en.a: As of November 2022, ECOSTRESS is still operational.
faq_en.q: Is ECOSTRESS still operational?

faq_en.a: ECOSTRESS is designed to monitor the temperature of soil and plants in order to measure plant evaporation and transpiration (evapotranspiration). From the data collected, we can better understand how much water plants require, how plants react to stress (due to an overabundance or lack of water), and how ecosystems are changing over time.
faq_en.q: What is the purpose of ECOSTRESS?

eoportal.name: ISS: EMIT (Earth surface Mineral dust source InvesTigation)
eoportal.summary: Hosted onboard the International Space Station  (ISS), the EMIT spectrometer collects data on the composition of minerals that become airborne dust with the aim to expand our knowledge on these fine particles from Earth’s deserts and ultimately how they affect climate.

faq.a: EMIT was launched to the International Space Centre on July 14, 2022 onboard a SpaceX resupply mission.
faq.q: When was ISS-EMIT launched?

faq.a: ISS-EMIT collects data on the composition of minerals that become airborne dust with the application of determining how dust interacts with Earth’s atmosphere, land, water and organisms. This includes how dust heats and cools the planet and how this may change under future climate scenarios as well as applications in the analysis of the correlation between dust and cloud formation, air quality, human health and blooms of microscopic algae.
faq.q: What is ISS-EMIT used for?

faq.a: EMIT is situated onboard the International Space Station’s (ISS) primary forward-facing hub for externally mounted instruments.
faq.q: Where is ISS-EMIT?

eoportal.name_zh: 国际空间站：EMIT (Earth surface Mineral dust source InvesTigation)
eoportal.summary_zh: EMIT 光谱仪搭载于国际空间站（ISS），用于收集会成为大气尘埃的矿物成分数据，旨在增进我们对来自地球沙漠的这些细微颗粒物的认识，以及最终了解它们如何影响气候。
eoportal.summary_en: Hosted onboard the International Space Station  (ISS), the EMIT spectrometer collects data on the composition of minerals that become airborne dust with the aim to expand our knowledge on these fine particles from Earth’s deserts and ultimately how they affect climate.

faq_zh.a: EMIT 于2022年7月14日搭载 SpaceX 补给任务发射至国际空间站。
ISS-EMIT 收集会成为大气尘埃的矿物成分数据，其应用包括确定尘埃与地球大气、陆地、水体和生物的相互作用方式。这包括尘埃对地球的加热和冷却效应，以及在未来气候情景下这些效应可能如何变化，同时也应用于分析尘埃与云的形成、空气质量、人类健康以及微型藻类水华之间的相关性。
EMIT 安装在国际空间站（ISS）主要的、面向前端的外部仪器安装枢纽上。

faq_en.a: EMIT was launched to the International Space Centre on July 14, 2022 onboard a SpaceX resupply mission.
faq_en.q: When was ISS-EMIT launched?

faq_en.a: ISS-EMIT collects data on the composition of minerals that become airborne dust with the application of determining how dust interacts with Earth’s atmosphere, land, water and organisms. This includes how dust heats and cools the planet and how this may change under future climate scenarios as well as applications in the analysis of the correlation between dust and cloud formation, air quality, human health and blooms of microscopic algae.
faq_en.q: What is ISS-EMIT used for?

faq_en.a: EMIT is situated onboard the International Space Station’s (ISS) primary forward-facing hub for externally mounted instruments.
faq_en.q: Where is ISS-EMIT?

eoportal.name: ISS: GEDI (Global Ecosystems Dynamics Investigation Lidar)
eoportal.summary: GEDI operations were paused in March 2023, and resumed nominal science operations with all three lasers on June 11, 2024.

faq.a: GEDI is used by NASA to track the vertical profile of Earth’s forests and to study the effect of atmospheric carbon dioxide on the associated biodiversity of these regions.
faq.q: What is GEDI used for?

faq.a: Firstly, to determine the above-ground carbon balance of the land surface. Secondly, ascertain what role the land surface will play in mitigating atmospheric carbon dioxide in the coming decades. Thirdly, to establish how ecosystem structure affects habitat quality and biodiversity.
faq.q: What are the GEDI science objectives?

faq.a: GEDI produces the vertical profile of Earth’s forests by measuring the reflected LIDAR waveforms off the surface of the planet. A machine learning algorithm is trained to correlate pixel brightness with canopy height.
faq.q: What does GEDI measure?

eoportal.name_zh: 国际空间站: GEDI（全球生态系统动力学调查激光雷达）
eoportal.summary_zh: GEDI的运行于2023年3月暂停，并于2024年6月11日恢复了使用全部三台激光器的正常科学观测。
eoportal.summary_en: GEDI operations were paused in March 2023, and resumed nominal science operations with all three lasers on June 11, 2024.

faq_zh.a: NASA利用GEDI来追踪地球森林的垂直结构，并研究大气二氧化碳对这些区域相关生物多样性的影响。
首先，确定地表以上的碳平衡。其次，确定在未来几十年中地表在缓解大气二氧化碳方面将扮演的角色。第三，建立生态系统结构如何影响栖息地质量和生物多样性的关系。
GEDI通过测量从地球表面反射的激光雷达波形来生成地球森林的垂直剖面图。它使用机器学习算法来关联像素亮度与树冠高度。

faq_en.a: GEDI is used by NASA to track the vertical profile of Earth’s forests and to study the effect of atmospheric carbon dioxide on the associated biodiversity of these regions.
faq_en.q: What is GEDI used for?

faq_en.a: Firstly, to determine the above-ground carbon balance of the land surface. Secondly, ascertain what role the land surface will play in mitigating atmospheric carbon dioxide in the coming decades. Thirdly, to establish how ecosystem structure affects habitat quality and biodiversity.
faq_en.q: What are the GEDI science objectives?

faq_en.a: GEDI produces the vertical profile of Earth’s forests by measuring the reflected LIDAR waveforms off the surface of the planet. A machine learning algorithm is trained to correlate pixel brightness with canopy height.
faq_en.q: What does GEDI measure?

iss: gedi (global ecosystems dynamics in.display: ISS: GEDI (Global Ecosystems Dynamics Investigation Lidar)

eoportal.name: ISS: LIS (Lightning Imaging Sensor)

faq.a: The LIS sensor contains a staring imager which is optimised to locate and detect lightning with a storm-scale resolution of 3-6 km over a large region of the Earth's surface.

faq.a: LIS data can be downloaded directly from ISS-LIS_NRT (https://www.earthdata.nasa.gov/learn/find-data/near-real-time/lis)

eoportal.name: ISS: RapidScat
eoportal.summary:
Post-launch

- Absolute calibration, slice balance, sigma0 from all slices match the sigma0 from the whole footprint, sigma0 vary appropriately along the scan (Amazon rain forest to determine scan bias)

- Use data to estimate pointing and update Doppler and range tables

- Use data to update gain and update calibration tables

- Solar array positions used to discard affected data (<8%).
eoportal.summary_zh:
发射后

- 进行了绝对定标，切片平衡，所有切片的后向散射系数σ0与整个足迹的σ0匹配，并且σ0沿扫描方向（利用亚马逊雨林来确定扫描偏差）适当变化。

- 利用数据估算指向，并更新多普勒和距离表。

- 利用数据更新增益，并更新定标表。

- 利用太阳能电池板位置丢弃受影响的数据（<8%）。
eoportal.summary_en:
Post-launch

- Absolute calibration, slice balance, sigma0 from all slices match the sigma0 from the whole footprint, sigma0 vary appropriately along the scan (Amazon rain forest to determine scan bias)

- Use data to estimate pointing and update Doppler and range tables

- Use data to update gain and update calibration tables

- Solar array positions used to discard affected data (<8%).

eoportal.summary: It is still operational, being operated by the ITA ground team in São José dos Campos.

faq.a: ITASat-1 is a university technological 6U CubeSat mission, funded by the Brazilian Space Agency (AEB) with a view to helping Brazilian university students gain valuable space technology experience.
faq.q: What is ITASat-1?

faq.a: The satellite was launched on an SSO-A rideshare mission of Spaceflight from the Vandenberg Air Force Base in California, alongside 63 other payloads.
faq.q: Where was ITASat-1 launched?

faq.a: The existence of two computers (one for ADCS, one for OBDH) offers the platform more flexibility in adapting to changes, as well as during tests, once the satellite was able to perform tests separately.
faq.q: What is the advantage of using two on-board computers?

faq_zh.a: ITASat-1是一颗大学技术验证型6U立方星任务，由巴西航天局（AEB）资助，旨在帮助巴西大学生获得宝贵的航天技术经验。
该卫星搭载Spaceflight公司的SSO-A共享任务，从加利福尼亚州的范登堡空军基地发射升空，与其他63个有效载荷一同上天。
两台计算机（一台用于姿态控制系统，另一台用于星务数据管理）为平台在适应变化以及测试期间提供了更大的灵活性，因为卫星能够独立执行测试。

eoportal.name: Jason-2/OSTM
eoportal.summary: Jason-2 successfully obtained a continuous record of observations in line with previous missions which included measurements of time-averaged ocean circulation, global sea-level change and improved open ocean tide models, until it was decommissioned in October 2019.

faq.a: Jason-2 was used to monitor ocean topography. This included measuring ocean circulation, the rise in sea level caused by global warming, the ocean’s role in Earth’s climate and improving global forecast and climate predictions.
faq.q: What was Jason-2 (OSTM) used for?

faq.a: Jason-2 (OTSM) was launched in June 2008, on a Delta launch vehicle from Vandenberg Air Force Base (VAFB), United States.
faq.q: When was Jason-2 launched?

faq.a: There are three Jason satellites: Jason-1, Jason-2 and Jason-3. Only Jason-3 is operational as of August 2022.
faq.q: How many Jason satellites are there?

eoportal.summary_zh: Jason-2 卫星成功延续了先前任务的观测记录，测量了时间平均海洋环流、全球海平面变化，并改进了开阔大洋潮汐模型，直至2019年10月退役。
eoportal.summary_en: Jason-2 successfully obtained a continuous record of observations in line with previous missions which included measurements of time-averaged ocean circulation, global sea-level change and improved open ocean tide models, until it was decommissioned in October 2019.

faq_zh.a: Jason-2 卫星用于监测海洋地形。具体包括测量海洋环流、由全球变暖引起的海平面上升、海洋在地球气候中的作用，并改进全球天气预报和气候预测。
Jason-2（OTSM）于2008年6月在美国范登堡空军基地（VAFB）由德尔塔（Delta）运载火箭发射升空。
目前共有三颗 Jason 系列卫星：Jason-1、Jason-2 和 Jason-3。截至2022年8月，只有 Jason-3 在轨运行。
faq_zh.q: 共有多少颗 Jason 系列卫星？

faq_en.a: Jason-2 was used to monitor ocean topography. This included measuring ocean circulation, the rise in sea level caused by global warming, the ocean’s role in Earth’s climate and improving global forecast and climate predictions.
faq_en.q: What was Jason-2 (OSTM) used for?

faq_en.a: Jason-2 (OTSM) was launched in June 2008, on a Delta launch vehicle from Vandenberg Air Force Base (VAFB), United States.

eoportal.summary: The mission has been extended past its three year nominal lifetime and is operational as of August 2022.

faq.a: Jason-3 aims to provide continuity to Poseidon/TOPEX, Jason-1 and Jason-2 missions which support applications related to extreme weather events, operational oceanography, climate and forecasting.
faq.q: What is the purpose of Jason-3?

faq.a: Yes, the Jason-3 mission has been extended past its three year nominal lifetime and remains in orbit and operational as of August 2022.
faq.q: Is Jason-3 still in orbit?

faq.a: Jason-3 was developed and led collaboratively by NOAA (National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration), EUMETSAT (European Organization for the Exploitation of Meteorological Satellites), CNES (Centre national d'études spatiales) and NASA. It was launched in January 2016 onboard a SpaceX Falcon 9 v1.1 vehicle from Vandenberg Air Force Base in California.
faq.q: Who launched Jason-3?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该任务已超过其三年的名义寿命，并在2022年8月仍处于在轨运行状态。

Jason-3由NOAA（美国国家海洋和大气管理局）、EUMETSAT（欧洲气象卫星应用组织）、CNES（法国国家空间研究中心）和NASA合作开发和领导。它于2016年1月搭载SpaceX Falcon 9 v1.1运载火箭从加利福尼亚州范登堡空军基地发射升空。

faq_zh.a: NASDA、MITI和STA是负责该项目的组织。2003年，这三个日本航天机构合并为日本宇宙航空研究开发机构（JAXA）。
数据可以在ESA Earth Online网站以及NASA Earthdata网站的OpenSearch数据集上找到。
faq_zh.q: 在哪里可以找到JERS-1产生的数据？

faq_zh.a: 任务运行中心是位于筑波航天中心的跟踪与控制中心（TACC）。

faq_en.a: NASDA, MITI and STA were the organisations in charge of the project. In 2003, the three Japanese space organisations were merged into Japan Aerospace Exploration Agency (JAXA).
faq_en.q: Which organisation was responsible for JERS-1?

faq_en.a: The data can be found on the ESA Earth Online website and the OpenSearch Datasets on NASA Earthdata website.
faq_en.q: Where can the data produced from JERS-1 be found?

faq_en.a: The mission operation centre was the Tracking and Control Center (TACC), located at the Tsukuba Space Center.
faq_en.q: Where was the control station for JERS-1?

eoportal.summary_en: The first set of satellites were launched by Long March 2D in Jiuquan Satellite Launch Center on 7 October 2015.

eoportal.summary: JPSS-1 (NOAA-20) and JPSS-2 (NOAA-21) were launched in November 2017 and 2022, respectively, and will be joined by JPSS-3 and -4.

faq.a: he Joint Polar Satellite System (JPSS) is the latest generation of polar-orbiting environmental satellites used for gathering data for the creation of weather prediction models for forecasts, climate monitoring, and much more.
faq.q: What is JPSS used for?

faq.a: A total of four JPSS missions have been planned.
faq.q: How many JPSS missions have been planned?

faq.a: Libera is a broad-band radiometer designed to capture readings of solar radiation, infrared radiation, and the total outgoing radiation from the Earth system. Libera will fly on JPSS-4.
faq.q: What is Libera?

eoportal.summary_zh: JPSS-1（NOAA-20）和 JPSS-2（NOAA-21）分别于 2017 年和 2022 年发射，后续还将加入 JPSS-3 和 JPSS-4。
eoportal.summary_en: JPSS-1 (NOAA-20) and JPSS-2 (NOAA-21) were launched in November 2017 and 2022, respectively, and will be joined by JPSS-3 and -4.

faq_zh.a: 联合极轨卫星系统 (JPSS) 是最新一代极轨环境卫星，用于收集数据以创建天气预报模型、进行气候监测等多项任务。
总共计划了四个 JPSS 任务。
faq_zh.q: 计划了多少个 JPSS 任务？

faq_zh.a: Libera 是一种宽带辐射计，旨在捕获太阳辐射、红外辐射和地球系统总外辐射的读数。Libera 将搭载在 JPSS-4 上飞行。

faq_en.a: A total of four JPSS missions have been planned.

faq_en.a: Libera is a broad-band radiometer designed to capture readings of solar radiation, infrared radiation, and the total outgoing radiation from the Earth system. Libera will fly on JPSS-4.

eoportal.name: Jugnu Nanosatellite
eoportal.summary: An indigenously built separation (ejection system), designed to release Jugnu, was developed by IIT students.

eoportal.summary_en: An indigenously built separation (ejection system), designed to release Jugnu, was developed by IIT students.

eoportal.summary_en: The mission launched in April 2023 to observe Jupiter and its moons.

eoportal.name: KaleidEO Constellation (SatSure)
eoportal.summary: The KaleidEO Constellation will deliver high-resolution multispectral imagery with onboard edge computing to support sectors such as agriculture, finance, infrastructure, and disaster response.

faq.a: KaleidEO is a fully owned subsidiary of the company SatSure. KaleidEO develops Earth observation payloads and will operate a planned constellation of microsatellites, expanding SatSure’s geospatial data capabilities.
faq.q: What is KaleidEO and how is it linked to SatSure?

faq.a: KaleidEO is developing a constellation of four microsatellites, each weighing around 175 kg. These satellites will be equipped with optical multispectral imagers and onboard edge processing systems that pre-process data before transmission to ground stations.
faq.q: What types of satellites is KaleidEO building?

faq.a: KaleidEO adopts a demand-led, vertically integrated model. It designs its systems to close specific performance gaps in Earth observation, tailoring solutions for diverse users, including commercial clients, government bodies, and research institutions.
faq.q: What is unique about KaleidEO’s approach to Earth observation?

eoportal.summary_zh: KaleidEO星座将提供高分辨率多光谱图像，并搭载星上边缘计算能力，以支持农业、金融、基础设施和灾害响应等领域。
eoportal.summary_en: The KaleidEO Constellation will deliver high-resolution multispectral imagery with onboard edge computing to support sectors such as agriculture, finance, infrastructure, and disaster response.

faq_zh.a: KaleidEO是SatSure公司的全资子公司。KaleidEO负责开发地球观测载荷，并将运营一个计划中的微小卫星星座，以扩展SatSure的地理空间数据能力。
KaleidEO正在开发一个由四颗微小卫星组成的星座，每颗卫星重约175公斤。这些卫星将配备光学多光谱成像仪和星上边缘处理系统，可在数据传输到地面站之前对其进行预处理。
faq_zh.q: KaleidEO正在建造什么类型的卫星？

faq_zh.a: KaleidEO采用需求驱动、垂直整合的模式。它设计其系统旨在弥合地球观测领域的特定性能差距，为包括商业客户、政府机构和研究机构在内的多样化用户量身定制解决方案。
faq_zh.q: KaleidEO的地球观测方法有何独特之处？

faq_en.a: KaleidEO is a fully owned subsidiary of the company SatSure. KaleidEO develops Earth observation payloads and will operate a planned constellation of microsatellites, expanding SatSure’s geospatial data capabilities.
faq_en.q: What is KaleidEO and how is it linked to SatSure?

faq_en.a: KaleidEO is developing a constellation of four microsatellites, each weighing around 175 kg. These satellites will be equipped with optical multispectral imagers and onboard edge processing systems that pre-process data before transmission to ground stations.
faq_en.q: What types of satellites is KaleidEO building?

faq_en.a: KaleidEO adopts a demand-led, vertically integrated model. It designs its systems to close specific performance gaps in Earth observation, tailoring solutions for diverse users, including commercial clients, government bodies, and research institutions.
faq_en.q: What is unique about KaleidEO’s approach to Earth observation?

eoportal.name: Kanopus-V 1 (Kanopus-Vulkan N1)
taxonomy: Sea ice cover, edge and thickness
eoportal.summary: The overall objective of the mission was to monitor Earth’s surface, the atmosphere, ionosphere, and magnetosphere to detect and study the probability of strong earthquake occurrence and other natural disasters.

faq.a: The Kanopus-V 1 minisatellite was launched on July 22, 2012 onboard a Soyuz FG rocket from the Baikonur Cosmodrome in Kazakhstan.
faq.q: When was Kanopus-V 1 launched?

faq.a: Kanopus-V 1 was built collaboratively by the Federal State Unitary Enterprise (FSUE) NPP VNIIEM of Moscow and Surrey Satellite Technology Ltd (SSTL) on behalf of ROSKOSMOS.
faq.q: Who built Kanopus-V 1?

faq.a: Kanopus-V 1 recorded multispectral and panchromatic imagery in the Visible and Near-Infrared ranges to monitor Earth’s surface, the atmosphere, ionosphere, and magnetosphere to detect and study the probability of strong Earthquake occurrence and other natural disasters.
faq.q: What did Kanopus-V 1 measure?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该任务的总体目标是监测地球表面、大气层、电离层和磁层，以探测和研究强震及其他自然灾害发生的可能性。
eoportal.summary_en: The overall objective of the mission was to monitor Earth’s surface, the atmosphere, ionosphere, and magnetosphere to detect and study the probability of strong earthquake occurrence and other natural disasters.

faq_zh.a: Kanopus-V 1 小型卫星于2012年7月22日搭载联盟号FG运载火箭，从哈萨克斯坦的拜科努尔航天发射场发射升空。
Kanopus-V 1 由位于莫斯科的联邦国家单一制企业 NPP VNIIEM 和萨里卫星技术有限公司 (SSTL) 代表 ROSKOSMOS 合作建造。
Kanopus-V 1 在可见光和近红外波段记录了多光谱和全色图像，用于监测地球表面、大气层、电离层和磁层，以探测和研究强震及其他自然灾害发生的可能性。

faq_en.a: The Kanopus-V 1 minisatellite was launched on July 22, 2012 onboard a Soyuz FG rocket from the Baikonur Cosmodrome in Kazakhstan.
faq_en.q: When was Kanopus-V 1 launched?

faq_en.a: Kanopus-V 1 was built collaboratively by the Federal State Unitary Enterprise (FSUE) NPP VNIIEM of Moscow and Surrey Satellite Technology Ltd (SSTL) on behalf of ROSKOSMOS.
faq_en.q: Who built Kanopus-V 1?

faq_en.a: Kanopus-V 1 recorded multispectral and panchromatic imagery in the Visible and Near-Infrared ranges to monitor Earth’s surface, the atmosphere, ionosphere, and magnetosphere to detect and study the probability of strong Earthquake occurrence and other natural disasters.
faq_en.q: What did Kanopus-V 1 measure?

eoportal.name: KhalifaSat Earth Observation Satellite Mission
eoportal.summary: Launched in October 2018, KhalifaSat obtains high-resolution optical imagery for various purposes, including urban planning, monitoring environmental change, and aiding relief efforts for natural disasters.

faq.a: The KhalifaSat spacecraft was launched on 29 October, 2018 from TNSC (Tanegashima Launch Center), Japan as a secondary payload on GOSAT-2 (Greenhouse gases Observing Satellite-2) of JAXA (Japan Aerospace Exploration Agency).
faq.q: When was KhalifaSat launched?

faq.a: KhalifaSat provides sub-meter resolution optical imagery for various purposes, including urban planning, monitoring environmental change, and aiding relief efforts for natural disasters. KhalifaSat is also part of a broader UAE objective to strengthen the standing of the Arab region in the competitive field of space science.
faq.q: What is the purpose of KhalifaSat?

faq.a: KhalifaSat is an Earth observation minisatellite that obtains multi-purpose high-resolution optical imagery of the land surface globally, in the visible light and near-infrared spectrum.
faq.q: What type of satellite is KhalifaSat?

eoportal.summary_zh: KhalifaSat于2018年10月发射，获取高分辨率光学图像，用于多种目的，包括城市规划、监测环境变化以及为自然灾害提供救援支持。
eoportal.summary_en: Launched in October 2018, KhalifaSat obtains high-resolution optical imagery for various purposes, including urban planning, monitoring environmental change, and aiding relief efforts for natural disasters.

faq_zh.a: KhalifaSat航天器于2018年10月29日作为日本宇宙航空研究开发机构（JAXA）的温室气体观测卫星-2（GOSAT-2）的搭载载荷，从日本的种子岛航天中心（TNSC）发射升空。
KhalifaSat提供亚米级分辨率的光学图像，用于多种目的，包括城市规划、监测环境变化以及为自然灾害提供救援支持。KhalifaSat也是阿联酋更广泛目标的一部分，旨在加强阿拉伯地区在竞争激烈的太空科学领域的地位。
KhalifaSat是一颗对地观测微型卫星，在可见光和近红外光谱范围内获取全球陆地表面的多用途高分辨率光学图像。

faq_en.a: The KhalifaSat spacecraft was launched on 29 October, 2018 from TNSC (Tanegashima Launch Center), Japan as a secondary payload on GOSAT-2 (Greenhouse gases Observing Satellite-2) of JAXA (Japan Aerospace Exploration Agency).
faq_en.q: When was KhalifaSat launched?

faq_en.a: KhalifaSat provides sub-meter resolution optical imagery for various purposes, including urban planning, monitoring environmental change, and aiding relief efforts for natural disasters. KhalifaSat is also part of a broader UAE objective to strengthen the standing of the Arab region in the competitive field of space science.
faq_en.q: What is the purpose of KhalifaSat?

faq_en.a: KhalifaSat is an Earth observation minisatellite that obtains multi-purpose high-resolution optical imagery of the land surface globally, in the visible light and near-infrared spectrum.

eoportal.name: KOMPSAT-1 (Korea Multi-Purpose Satellite-1) / Arirang-1
taxonomy: Ocean imagery and water leaving spectral radiance
eoportal.summary: Satellite operations ceased in December 2007 when the mission was completed.

faq.a: To conduct comprehensive imaging of the Korean peninsula and conduct various space physics experiments, including the detection of high energy electrons and protons in Low Earth Orbit.
faq.q: What was the purpose of KOMPSAT-1?

faq.a: Korean Aerospace Research Institute’s (KARI) owned KOMPSAT-1 and all subsequent satellites in the KOMPSAT program.
faq.q: Which organisation owned KOMPSAT-1?

faq.a: There are nine satellites in the KOMPSAT program, with the first launched in 1999 and the most recent in 2020.
faq.q: How many KOMPSAT satellites are there?

eoportal.name_zh: KOMPSAT-1（多用途卫星-1）/ 阿里郎-1
eoportal.summary_zh: 卫星于2007年12月停止运行，任务随之完成。
eoportal.summary_en: Satellite operations ceased in December 2007 when the mission was completed.

faq_zh.a: 对朝鲜半岛进行综合成像，并进行各种空间物理实验，包括探测近地轨道的高能电子和质子。
KOMPSAT-1及KOMPSAT项目后续的所有卫星均由韩国航空宇宙研究院（KARI）拥有。
KOMPSAT项目共有9颗卫星，第一颗于1999年发射，最近的一颗于2020年发射。

faq_en.a: To conduct comprehensive imaging of the Korean peninsula and conduct various space physics experiments, including the detection of high energy electrons and protons in Low Earth Orbit.

faq_en.a: Korean Aerospace Research Institute’s (KARI) owned KOMPSAT-1 and all subsequent satellites in the KOMPSAT program.

faq_en.a: There are nine satellites in the KOMPSAT program, with the first launched in 1999 and the most recent in 2020.

eoportal.name: KOMPSAT-2 (Korea Multi-Purpose Satellite-2) / Arirang-2
eoportal.summary: KOMPSAT-2 was retired in December 2024.

faq.a: KOMPSAT-2 was operated and developed by KARI (Korea Aerospace Research Institute), until its retirement in December 2024.
faq.q: Who operated KOMPSAT-2?

faq.a: KOMPSAT-2 aimed to provide surveillance capabilities for large-scale disasters by collecting high-resolution imagery with GIS (Geographic Information Systems) applications, and provided data continuity for KOMPSAT-1.
faq.q: What was the aim of KOMPSAT-2?

faq.a: Beginning in December 1999 with the launch of KOMPSAT-1, the KOMPSAT (Korea Multi-Purpose Satellite) program currently consists of seven satellites, of which six are operational after KOMPSAT-1 left service in January 2008.
faq.q: How many satellites are in the KOMPSAT program?

eoportal.agency_zh: KARI（韩国航空宇宙研究院）, E-LOP, ASTRIUM
eoportal.summary_zh: KOMPSAT-2卫星已于2024年12月退役。
eoportal.summary_en: KOMPSAT-2 was retired in December 2024.

faq_zh.a: KOMPSAT-2由KARI（韩国航空宇宙研究院）负责运营和开发，直至2024年12月退役。
KOMPSAT-2旨在通过采集高分辨率影像用于地理信息系统（GIS）应用，为大规模灾害提供监测能力，并为KOMPSAT-1提供数据连续性。
从1999年12月发射KOMPSAT-1开始，KOMPSAT（韩国多用途卫星）计划目前包含七颗卫星，在KOMPSAT-1于2008年1月结束服务后，其中六颗仍在运行。

faq_en.a: KOMPSAT-2 was operated and developed by KARI (Korea Aerospace Research Institute), until its retirement in December 2024.
faq_en.q: Who operated KOMPSAT-2?

faq_en.a: KOMPSAT-2 aimed to provide surveillance capabilities for large-scale disasters by collecting high-resolution imagery with GIS (Geographic Information Systems) applications, and provided data continuity for KOMPSAT-1.
faq_en.q: What was the aim of KOMPSAT-2?

faq_en.a: Beginning in December 1999 with the launch of KOMPSAT-1, the KOMPSAT (Korea Multi-Purpose Satellite) program currently consists of seven satellites, of which six are operational after KOMPSAT-1 left service in January 2008.
faq_en.q: How many satellites are in the KOMPSAT program?

eoportal.name: KOMPSAT-3 (Korea Multi-Purpose Satellite-3) / Arirang-3
eoportal.summary: The mission aims to meet Korea’s demand for high-resolution optical imagery, in the form of Geographical Information Systems (GIS), for applications in environmental, oceanographic and agricultural monitoring.

faq.a: KOMPSAT-3 was designed to meet Korea’s need for high-resolution optical imagery in the form of Geographical Information Systems that highlight environmental, agricultural and oceanographic monitoring.
faq.q: What is the purpose of KOMPSAT-3?

faq.a: KOMPSAT-3 is an observation mission of the Korea Aerospace Research Institute (KARI) that is funded by the Korean Ministry of Education, Science and Technology (MEST).
faq.q: Which organisation owns KOMPSAT-3?

faq.a: There are a total of five bands, one panchromatic band (450nm - 900 nm), one blue multispectral band (450 nm - 520 nm), one green multispectral band (520 nm - 600 nm), one red multispectral band (630 nm - 690 nm) and one near-infrared (NIR) band (760 nm - 900 nm).
faq.q: How many bands does the Advanced Electronic Image Scanning System (AEISS) on KOMPSAT-3 have?

eoportal.agency_zh: KARI（韩国航空宇宙研究院）, ASTRIUM
eoportal.summary_zh: 该任务旨在满足韩国对高分辨率光学影像的需求，以地理信息系统（GIS）形式，服务于环境、海洋和农业监测等应用领域。
eoportal.summary_en: The mission aims to meet Korea’s demand for high-resolution optical imagery, in the form of Geographical Information Systems (GIS), for applications in environmental, oceanographic and agricultural monitoring.

faq_zh.a: KOMPSAT-3 旨在满足韩国对高分辨率光学影像的需求，该影像以地理信息系统形式提供，重点关注环境、农业和海洋监测。
KOMPSAT-3 是韩国航空宇宙研究院（KARI）的一项观测任务，由韩国教育部、科技部（MEST）资助。
总共有五个波段：一个全色波段（450纳米 - 900纳米）、一个蓝色多光谱波段（450纳米 - 520纳米）、一个绿色多光谱波段（520纳米 - 600纳米）、一个红色多光谱波段（630纳米 - 690纳米）和一个近红外（NIR）波段（760纳米 - 900纳米）。
faq_zh.q: KOMPSAT-3 上的先进电子图像扫描系统（AEISS）有多少个波段？

faq_en.a: KOMPSAT-3 was designed to meet Korea’s need for high-resolution optical imagery in the form of Geographical Information Systems that highlight environmental, agricultural and oceanographic monitoring.
faq_en.q: What is the purpose of KOMPSAT-3?

faq_en.a: KOMPSAT-3 is an observation mission of the Korea Aerospace Research Institute (KARI) that is funded by the Korean Ministry of Education, Science and Technology (MEST).
faq_en.q: Which organisation owns KOMPSAT-3?

faq_en.a: There are a total of five bands, one panchromatic band (450nm - 900 nm), one blue multispectral band (450 nm - 520 nm), one green multispectral band (520 nm - 600 nm), one red multispectral band (630 nm - 690 nm) and one near-infrared (NIR) band (760 nm - 900 nm).
faq_en.q: How many bands does the Advanced Electronic Image Scanning System (AEISS) on KOMPSAT-3 have?

eoportal.name: KOMPSAT-3A (Korea Multi-Purpose Satellite-3A) / Arirang-3A
eoportal.summary: The mission aims to collect infrared (IR) and high resolution electro-optical imagery for Geographical Information Systems (GIS) applications, and is the successor to KOMPSAT-3.

faq.a: KOMPSAT-3A provides high spatial resolution panchromatic and multispectral imagery,  for environmental, agricultural and oceanographical applications.
faq.q: What does KOMPSAT-3A do?

faq.a: KOMPSAT-3A is a collaboration between the Korean government entity KARI (Korea Aerospace Research Institute) and two private enterprises, APA (Asia-Pacific Aerospace) and KAI (Korea Aerospace Industries).
faq.q: Who developed KOMPSAT-3A?

faq.a: KOMPSAT-3A data is obtainable for purchase through SIIS (SI Imaging Services), the exclusive provider of KOMPSAT constellation data.
faq.q: Is KOMPSAT-3A data accessible?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该任务旨在为地理信息系统（GIS）应用收集红外（IR）和高分辨率光学影像，是KOMPSAT-3卫星的后续型号。
eoportal.summary_en: The mission aims to collect infrared (IR) and high resolution electro-optical imagery for Geographical Information Systems (GIS) applications, and is the successor to KOMPSAT-3.

faq_zh.a: KOMPSAT-3A提供高空间分辨率的全色和多光谱影像，用于环境、农业和海洋学应用。
KOMPSAT-3A由韩国政府机构KARI（韩国航空宇宙研究院）与两家私营企业APA（亚太航空航天）和KAI（韩国航空宇宙产业）合作开发。
KOMPSAT-3A的数据可通过SIIS（SI影像服务）购买获取，该公司是KOMPSAT卫星星座数据的独家提供商。

faq_en.a: KOMPSAT-3A provides high spatial resolution panchromatic and multispectral imagery,  for environmental, agricultural and oceanographical applications.
faq_en.q: What does KOMPSAT-3A do?

faq_en.a: KOMPSAT-3A is a collaboration between the Korean government entity KARI (Korea Aerospace Research Institute) and two private enterprises, APA (Asia-Pacific Aerospace) and KAI (Korea Aerospace Industries).
faq_en.q: Who developed KOMPSAT-3A?

faq_en.a: KOMPSAT-3A data is obtainable for purchase through SIIS (SI Imaging Services), the exclusive provider of KOMPSAT constellation data.
faq_en.q: Is KOMPSAT-3A data accessible?

eoportal.summary: KOMPSAT-5 is often referred to as the GOLDEN mission, due to the characteristic aspects of the mission: GIS (Geographic Information System), Ocean, Land management, Disaster and Environmental monitoring.

faq.q: How many modes does the Corea SAR Instrument (COSI) on KOMPSAT-5 contain?

faq.a: The Korean Ministry of Education, Science and Technology and the Korea Aerospace Research institute (KARI) are responsible for the development of KOMPSAT-5.

eoportal.name: KOMPSAT-6 (Korea Multi-Purpose Satellite-6) / Arirang-6
eoportal.summary: KOMPSAT-6 aims to provide high resolution radar imagery required in GIS (Geographical Information Systems), disaster & environment monitoring and ocean & land management.

faq.a: KOMPSAT-6 aims to expedite the production of high resolution, spaceborne SAR (Synthetic Aperture Radar) imagery, providing continuity for KOMPSAT-5.
faq.q: What is the purpose of KOMPSAT-6?

faq.a: KOMPSAT-6 has been developed by, is owned by and will be operated by KARI (Korea Aerospace Research Institute), a Korean Government organisation.
faq.q: Who owns KOMPSAT-6?

faq.a: KOMPSAT-6 carries two instruments, XSAR (X-band Synthetic Aperture Radar), a high spatial resolution multi-purpose radar imager, and S-AIS (Satellite- Automatic Identification System), a wireless system that identifies the position, bearing, heading, destination and cargo of large vessels.
faq.q: What instruments does KOMPSAT-6 carry?

eoportal.name_zh: KOMPSAT-6（多用途卫星-6）/ 阿里郎-6
eoportal.summary_zh: KOMPSAT-6旨在提供高分辨率雷达图像，这些图像用于地理信息系统（GIS）、灾害与环境监测以及海洋与陆地管理。
eoportal.summary_en: KOMPSAT-6 aims to provide high resolution radar imagery required in GIS (Geographical Information Systems), disaster & environment monitoring and ocean & land management.

faq_zh.a: KOMPSAT-6旨在加速高分辨率星载合成孔径雷达（SAR）图像的生产，为KOMPSAT-5提供连续性。
KOMPSAT-6由韩国政府机构KARI（韩国航空宇宙研究院）开发、拥有并运营。
KOMPSAT-6搭载了两种仪器：XSAR（X波段合成孔径雷达），一种高空间分辨率的多用途雷达成像仪；以及S-AIS（卫星自动识别系统），一种用于识别大型船只位置、方位、航向、目的地和货物的无线系统。

faq_en.a: KOMPSAT-6 aims to expedite the production of high resolution, spaceborne SAR (Synthetic Aperture Radar) imagery, providing continuity for KOMPSAT-5.
faq_en.q: What is the purpose of KOMPSAT-6?

faq_en.a: KOMPSAT-6 has been developed by, is owned by and will be operated by KARI (Korea Aerospace Research Institute), a Korean Government organisation.
faq_en.q: Who owns KOMPSAT-6?

faq_en.a: KOMPSAT-6 carries two instruments, XSAR (X-band Synthetic Aperture Radar), a high spatial resolution multi-purpose radar imager, and S-AIS (Satellite- Automatic Identification System), a wireless system that identifies the position, bearing, heading, destination and cargo of large vessels.
faq_en.q: What instruments does KOMPSAT-6 carry?

Launched in December 2025, the Korean Multipurpose Satellite-7 (KOMPSAT-7), or Arirang 7, is an optical imaging satellite designed by the Korean Aerospace Research Institute (KARI) to support national applications in high resolution land and vegetation monitoring.

faq.a: KOMPSAT-7 aims to provide continuity to the Korean Multipurpose Satellite (KOMPSAT) programme by serving national demand for high resolution optical imagery over the Korean Peninsula.
faq.q: What is the purpose of KOMPSAT-7?

faq.a: KOMPSAT-7 provides continuity to KOMPSAT-3A’s observations with the High Resolution Advanced Electronic Image Scanning System (AEISS-HR), an updated model of KOMPSAT-3’s visible and near-infrared (VNIR) multispectral imager. Additionally, the satellite features an Advanced Infrared Module, capable of thermal signature detection, through day or night.
faq.q: What instruments are aboard KOMPSAT-7?

faq.a: KOMPSAT-7 was successfully launched by Arianespace on December 1, 2025 from Kourou, French into Sun-synchronous low-Earth orbit (LEO).
faq.q: When did KOMPSAT-7 launch?

eoportal.summary_zh: KOMPSAT-7（韩国多用途卫星-7）/ Arirang 7 是一颗光学成像卫星，由韩国航空宇宙研究院（KARI）设计，于2025年12月发射。该卫星旨在支持国家在高分辨率土地和植被监测方面的应用。

Launched in December 2025, the Korean Multipurpose Satellite-7 (KOMPSAT-7), or Arirang 7, is an optical imaging satellite designed by the Korean Aerospace Research Institute (KARI) to support national applications in high resolution land and vegetation monitoring.

faq_zh.a: KOMPSAT-7旨在通过满足韩国对朝鲜半岛高分辨率光学影像的国家需求，延续韩国多用途卫星（KOMPSAT）计划。
KOMPSAT-7搭载了先进高分辨率电子成像扫描系统（AEISS-HR），这是KOMPSAT-3可见光和近红外（VNIR）多光谱成像仪的升级版，可延续KOMPSAT-3A的观测。此外，该卫星还配备了一个先进的红外模块，能够全天时探测热特征。
KOMPSAT-7于2025年12月1日由Arianespace从法属圭亚那库鲁成功发射，进入太阳同步低地球轨道（LEO）。

faq_en.a: KOMPSAT-7 aims to provide continuity to the Korean Multipurpose Satellite (KOMPSAT) programme by serving national demand for high resolution optical imagery over the Korean Peninsula.
faq_en.q: What is the purpose of KOMPSAT-7?

faq_en.a: KOMPSAT-7 provides continuity to KOMPSAT-3A’s observations with the High Resolution Advanced Electronic Image Scanning System (AEISS-HR), an updated model of KOMPSAT-3’s visible and near-infrared (VNIR) multispectral imager. Additionally, the satellite features an Advanced Infrared Module, capable of thermal signature detection, through day or night.
faq_en.q: What instruments are aboard KOMPSAT-7?

faq_en.a: KOMPSAT-7 was successfully launched by Arianespace on December 1, 2025 from Kourou, French into Sun-synchronous low-Earth orbit (LEO).

eoportal.name: Kondor-FKA
eoportal.summary: Kondor-FKA was launched on May 26, 2023 by Roscosmos.

faq.a: Kondor-FKA, where FKA stands for Federal Space Agency, is a Russian hydrology and military reconnaissance satellite developed by NPO Mashinostroyeniya (NPOM) for ROSCOSMOS.
faq.q: What is Kondor-FKA?

faq.a: Kondor-FKA houses the Synthetic Aperture Radar-10 (SAR-10) radar imaging payload, which will support measurements of soil moisture, biomass, fractions of land vegetation and sea ice, and land cover and topography. This instrument can provide high-resolution imagery of ground features as small as 1 m in size.
faq.q: What instruments are onboard Kondor-FKA?

faq.a: “Kondor” refers to satellites built by and for the Russian military. The civilian Kondor-E stands for data export of soil and hydrology. While Kondor-FKA does hold hydrology capability, it is the military counterpart of the Kondor series, whose data is encrypted and maintained only for Russian military use.
faq.q: How does Kondor-FKA differ from the Kondor-E satellite?

eoportal.name: LAGEOS (Laser Geodynamics Satellite-I) / LAGEOS-II
eoportal.summary: The satellites  are designed to orbit the Earth for millions of years to come.

faq.a: LAGEOS acts as a permanent reference point. Being small, high density, and homogenous in design, in addition to having a highly stable orbit, the satellites can provide information about the Earth and universal systems via unexpected orbital deviations. For example, the data obtained have been used to measure movements in the Earth’s tectonic plates, gravitational phenomena, and irregularities in the rotation of the Earth.
faq.q: What does LAGEOS do?

faq.a: Satellite Laser Ranging (SLR) is a system whereby several laser-ranging stations are positioned at various locations around the globe to form a network. Each station can fire lasers at specific satellites that are designed to reflect laser pulses back to their source and time how long it takes the laser pulses to return to the station. These timings are highly precise, and when several stations fire simultaneously at such a satellite, its location relative to the Earth can be accurately determined. This method is so precise that positions can be determined down to within a few millimetres, as in the case of the LAGEOS satellites.
faq.q: What is SLR in geodesy?

faq.a: LAGEOS stands for LAser GEOdynamics Satellite.
faq.q: What does LAGEOS stand for?

eoportal.name_zh: LAGEOS (激光地球动力学卫星-I) / LAGEOS-II
eoportal.summary_zh: 这些卫星旨在环绕地球运行数百万年。
eoportal.summary_en: The satellites  are designed to orbit the Earth for millions of years to come.

faq_zh.a: LAGEOS卫星充当一个永久的参考点。由于其体积小、密度高、设计均匀，并且具有高度稳定的轨道，这些卫星能够通过异常的轨道偏移来提供关于地球和宇宙系统的信息。例如，获得的数据已被用于测量地球构造板块的运动、引力现象以及地球自转的不规则性。
卫星激光测距（SLR）是一个系统，其中多个激光测距站分布在全球各地形成网络。每个站点都可以向特定卫星发射激光，这些卫星被设计为将激光脉冲反射回其源点，并测量激光脉冲返回站点所需的时间。这些计时非常精确，当多个站点同时向同一颗卫星发射激光时，可以准确确定其相对于地球的位置。这种方法非常精确，位置确定精度可以达到几毫米以内，LAGEOS卫星的情况就是如此。
faq_zh.q: 在大地测量学中，SLR是什么？

faq_zh.a: LAGEOS代表LAser GEOdynamics Satellite（激光地球动力学卫星）。

faq_en.a: LAGEOS acts as a permanent reference point. Being small, high density, and homogenous in design, in addition to having a highly stable orbit, the satellites can provide information about the Earth and universal systems via unexpected orbital deviations. For example, the data obtained have been used to measure movements in the Earth’s tectonic plates, gravitational phenomena, and irregularities in the rotation of the Earth.
faq_en.q: What does LAGEOS do?

faq_en.a: Satellite Laser Ranging (SLR) is a system whereby several laser-ranging stations are positioned at various locations around the globe to form a network. Each station can fire lasers at specific satellites that are designed to reflect laser pulses back to their source and time how long it takes the laser pulses to return to the station. These timings are highly precise, and when several stations fire simultaneously at such a satellite, its location relative to the Earth can be accurately determined. This method is so precise that positions can be determined down to within a few millimetres, as in the case of the LAGEOS satellites.
faq_en.q: What is SLR in geodesy?

faq_en.a: LAGEOS stands for LAser GEOdynamics Satellite.
faq_en.q: What does LAGEOS stand for?

faq.a: Landsat 10 will carry a single imaging instrument suite known as the Landsat Instrument Suite (LandIS).
faq.q: What instrument will Landsat 10 carry?

faq.a: To accommodate Landsat 10's lower altitude and unique ground track, scientists created the Worldwide Reference System 3 (WRS-3).
faq.q: What is the WRS-3 grid system?

faq.a: Landsat 10 benefits from double the spectral bands from its predecessors, as well as increased spatial and temporal resolution.
faq.q: How does Landsat 10 improve upon Landsat 8 & 9?

eoportal.name: Landsat-1 to 3
eoportal.summary: The first three spacecraft of the series were launched in July 1972, January 1975 and March 1978, respectively, and were decommissioned in January 1978, February 1982 and March 1983, respectively.

faq.a: Yes, although it has been non-operational since January 1978 Landsat-1 remains in orbit.
faq.q: Is Landsat-1 still in orbit?

faq.a: Landsat-3 images in four spectral bands in the visible and near-infrared region (VNIR), the same as Landsat-1 and -2.
faq.q: How many bands does Landsat-3 have?

faq.a: The primary objective of the first Landsat satellites was to monitor Earth's resources via multispectral, high spatial resolution images of solar radiation reflected from Earth’s surface. The digital image data acquired by these satellites were used for a broad range of applications and scientific investigations.
faq.q: What is the purpose of the first Landsat satellites?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该系列的前三颗卫星分别于1972年7月、1975年1月和1978年3月发射，并分别于1978年1月、1982年2月和1983年3月退役。

faq_zh.a: 是的，尽管自1978年1月起已停止运行，但Landsat-1卫星仍留在轨道上。
Landsat-3卫星在可见光和近红外区域拥有四个光谱波段，与Landsat-1和Landsat-2相同。
第一代Landsat卫星的主要目标是通过获取地表反射太阳辐射的多光谱、高空间分辨率图像来监测地球资源。这些卫星获取的数字图像数据被用于广泛的应用和科学研究。
faq_zh.q: 第一代Landsat卫星的目的是什么？

faq_en.a: Yes, although it has been non-operational since January 1978 Landsat-1 remains in orbit.
faq_en.q: Is Landsat-1 still in orbit?

faq_en.q: Is Landsat-4 still operational?

faq_en.a: Landsat-4 utilised a Thematic Mapper which imaged in seven spectral bands with a spatial resolution of 80 m for visible and near infrared bands and 120 m for the thermal band.
faq_en.q: What is the resolution of Landsat-4?

faq_en.a: No, Landsat-5 was officially retired when deorbiting and decommissioning began in January 2013.
faq_en.q: Is Landsat-5 still operational?

eoportal.name: Landsat-8/LDCM
taxonomy: Cloud type, amount and cloud top temperature
Ocean imagery and water leaving spectral radiance
Sea ice cover, edge and thickness
eoportal.summary: Operated by the United States Geological Survey (USGS), this collaborative mission aims to collect and archive thermal and multispectral image data whilst ensuring consistency with previous Landsat mission data.

faq.a: Alongside seasonal changes, Landsat-8 observes changing areas of irrigated agriculture, systemic conversion of forest to pasture and urban, coastal changes and their causes including human or natural environmental pressures. The Landsat satellite series is the longest continuous Earth imaging program in history, used to detect and measure land cover/land use change, the health of ecosystems and water availability.
faq.q: What is Landsat-8 used for?

faq.a: LDCM distributes standard data products obtained from Landsat satellites to users on a non-discriminatory basis at no cost. The data can be found on the USGS Earth Explorer webpage.
faq.q: Is Landsat-8 data free?

faq.a: Landsat-8 has 11 image bands, including five in the visible and near infrared spectrum (NVIR), three in the short-wave infrared spectrum (SWIR), one panchromatic image (PAN) band for image sharpening, and two thermal imaging bands (TIR).
faq.q: What are the Landsat-8 bands?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该任务由美国地质调查局（USGS）运营，旨在采集并归档热红外与多光谱影像数据，同时确保与以往陆地卫星任务数据的一致性。
eoportal.summary_en: Operated by the United States Geological Survey (USGS), this collaborative mission aims to collect and archive thermal and multispectral image data whilst ensuring consistency with previous Landsat mission data.

faq_zh.a: 除季节性变化外，陆地卫星-8还用于观测灌溉农业区域的变化、森林向牧场和城市用地的系统性转化、海岸变化及其成因（包括人类或自然环境压力）。陆地卫星系列是历史上持续时间最长的地球成像项目，用于探测和测量土地覆盖/土地利用变化、生态系统健康状况及水资源可用性。
LDCM在无差别基础上免费向用户分发从陆地卫星获取的标准数据产品。相关数据可在USGS Earth Explorer网页上找到。
faq_zh.q: 陆地卫星-8的数据是免费的吗？

faq_zh.a: 陆地卫星-8拥有11个影像波段，包括5个可见光与近红外波段（VNIR）、3个短波红外波段（SWIR）、1个用于影像锐化的全色波段（PAN）以及2个热红外波段（TIR）。

faq_en.a: Alongside seasonal changes, Landsat-8 observes changing areas of irrigated agriculture, systemic conversion of forest to pasture and urban, coastal changes and their causes including human or natural environmental pressures. The Landsat satellite series is the longest continuous Earth imaging program in history, used to detect and measure land cover/land use change, the health of ecosystems and water availability.
faq_en.q: What is Landsat-8 used for?

faq_en.a: LDCM distributes standard data products obtained from Landsat satellites to users on a non-discriminatory basis at no cost. The data can be found on the USGS Earth Explorer webpage.
faq_en.q: Is Landsat-8 data free?

faq_en.a: Landsat-8 has 11 image bands, including five in the visible and near infrared spectrum (NVIR), three in the short-wave infrared spectrum (SWIR), one panchromatic image (PAN) band for image sharpening, and two thermal imaging bands (TIR).
faq_en.q: What are the Landsat-8 bands?

eoportal.name: LAPAN-A2 microsatellite of Indonesia
eoportal.summary: Launched in September 2015 and still operational, as a follow-on mission to LAPAN-TUBSAT, LAPAN-A2 aims to monitor the environment, disaster mitigation, land use and natural resources.

faq.a: LAPAN-A2 was launched on the south-east coast of India as a secondary payload to the AstroSat spacecraft of ISRO on the ISRO’s PSLV-C30 vehicle.
faq.q: How was LAPAN-A2 launched?

faq.a: The mission features a Video Camera assembly, an Automatic Identification System (AIS) and an Automatic Packet Reporting System (APRS).
faq.q: What are the instruments on LAPAN-A2?

faq.a: The mission objectives of LAPAN-A2 are to use the microsatellite for disaster mitigation monitoring by Earth observation, as well as for land use, natural resources and environment monitoring.
faq.q: What is the purpose of LAPAN-A2?

eoportal.summary_zh: LAPAN-A2 作为 LAPAN-TUBSAT 的后续任务，于 2015 年 9 月发射，目前仍在轨运行，旨在监测环境、减灾、土地利用和自然资源。
eoportal.summary_en: Launched in September 2015 and still operational, as a follow-on mission to LAPAN-TUBSAT, LAPAN-A2 aims to monitor the environment, disaster mitigation, land use and natural resources.

faq_zh.a: LAPAN-A2 作为次级载荷，在印度东南海岸搭载印度空间研究组织（ISRO）的极轨卫星运载火箭（PSLV-C30）与 AstroSat 航天器一同发射升空。
该任务配备了视频相机组件、自动识别系统（AIS）和自动数据包报告系统（APRS）。
LAPAN-A2 的任务目的是利用微卫星进行地球观测，以实现灾害减缓监测，以及土地利用、自然资源和环境监测。
faq_zh.q: LAPAN-A2 的任务目的是什么？

faq_en.a: LAPAN-A2 was launched on the south-east coast of India as a secondary payload to the AstroSat spacecraft of ISRO on the ISRO’s PSLV-C30 vehicle.
faq_en.q: How was LAPAN-A2 launched?

faq_en.a: The mission features a Video Camera assembly, an Automatic Identification System (AIS) and an Automatic Packet Reporting System (APRS).
faq_en.q: What are the instruments on LAPAN-A2?

faq_en.a: The mission objectives of LAPAN-A2 are to use the microsatellite for disaster mitigation monitoring by Earth observation, as well as for land use, natural resources and environment monitoring.
faq_en.q: What is the purpose of LAPAN-A2?

eoportal.name: LIBRA
eoportal.summary: The goal of CSRB is to launch an International System of Units (SI) traceable satellite named LIBRA to contribute to space-based climate studies via publicly available data.

faq.a: LIBRA, CLARREO and TRUTHS all offer SI traceability. The main difference between these missions is the instruments; LIBRA will have 4 instruments on board, whereas CLARREO and TRUTHS have 2 and 3 respectively. The instruments involved in each mission fall into four categories: instruments to measure spectrally resolved infrared radiance, spectrally resolved reflectance of solar radiation, total solar irradiance, and spectrally resolved solar irradiance. Although the instruments are similar, they differ in spectral coverage, spectral resolution, and measurement uncertainty.
faq.q: How does LIBRA differ from CLARREO and TRUTHS?

faq.a: Monthly lunar observations are major components of the on-orbit calibration strategies of LIBRA. The reference and target satellite observe the same lunar phase which is realised by attitude manoeuvres each month. Then, the intercalibration can be performed using a standard lunar radiation model, such as RObotic Lunar Observatory (ROLO).
faq.q: How will LIBRA use lunar observations for intercalibration?

faq.a: No, a prototype of the LIBRA satellite has not been built. The prototypes of the payloads have been built, including an absolute radiance standard IR calibrator, a RSB self-correction absolute calibrator based on Spontaneous Parametric Down-Conversion (SPDC), and a cavity-type absolute cryogenic radiometer (ACR) with 20 K operational temperature.
faq.q: Are there prototypes of the LIBRA satellite?

eoportal.summary_zh: CSRB 的目标是发射一颗可溯源至国际单位制（SI）的卫星 LIBRA，通过公开可用的数据为空间气候研究做出贡献。
eoportal.summary_en: The goal of CSRB is to launch an International System of Units (SI) traceable satellite named LIBRA to contribute to space-based climate studies via publicly available data.

faq_zh.a: LIBRA、CLARREO 和 TRUTHS 都提供 SI 可溯源性。这些任务之间的主要区别在于仪器；LIBRA 将搭载 4 台仪器，而 CLARREO 和 TRUTHS 分别搭载 2 台和 3 台。每个任务涉及的仪器可分为四类：测量光谱分辨率红外辐射亮度的仪器、太阳辐射光谱分辨率反射率的仪器、总太阳辐照度的仪器以及光谱分辨率太阳辐照度的仪器。虽然仪器相似，但它们在光谱覆盖范围、光谱分辨率和测量不确定性方面有所不同。
faq_zh.q: LIBRA 与 CLARREO 和 TRUTHS 有何不同？

faq_zh.a: 每月的月球观测是 LIBRA 轨道上定标策略的主要组成部分。参考卫星和目标卫星观测相同的月相，这是通过每月的姿态机动实现的。然后，可以使用标准月球辐射模型（如 ROLO 机器人月球观测站）进行相互定标。
faq_zh.q: LIBRA 将如何利用月球观测进行相互定标？

faq_zh.a: 没有，LIBRA 卫星的原型尚未建造。有效载荷的原型已经建造，包括绝对辐射标准红外定标器、基于自发参量下转换（SPDC）的 RSB 自校正绝对定标器，以及工作温度为 20 K 的腔型绝对低温辐射计（ACR）。
faq_zh.q: 是否有 LIBRA 卫星的原型？

faq_en.a: LIBRA, CLARREO and TRUTHS all offer SI traceability. The main difference between these missions is the instruments; LIBRA will have 4 instruments on board, whereas CLARREO and TRUTHS have 2 and 3 respectively. The instruments involved in each mission fall into four categories: instruments to measure spectrally resolved infrared radiance, spectrally resolved reflectance of solar radiation, total solar irradiance, and spectrally resolved solar irradiance. Although the instruments are similar, they differ in spectral coverage, spectral resolution, and measurement uncertainty.
faq_en.q: How does LIBRA differ from CLARREO and TRUTHS?

faq_en.a: Monthly lunar observations are major components of the on-orbit calibration strategies of LIBRA. The reference and target satellite observe the same lunar phase which is realised by attitude manoeuvres each month. Then, the intercalibration can be performed using a standard lunar radiation model, such as RObotic Lunar Observatory (ROLO).
faq_en.q: How will LIBRA use lunar observations for intercalibration?

faq_en.a: No, a prototype of the LIBRA satellite has not been built. The prototypes of the payloads have been built, including an absolute radiance standard IR calibrator, a RSB self-correction absolute calibrator based on Spontaneous Parametric Down-Conversion (SPDC), and a cavity-type absolute cryogenic radiometer (ACR) with 20 K operational temperature.
faq_en.q: Are there prototypes of the LIBRA satellite?

eoportal.name: Light-1
taxonomy: Downward short-wave irradiance at Earth surface
eoportal.summary: It monitors terrestrial gamma-ray flashes (TGFs), which are emitted during lightning storms, to provide insight into how TGFs affect aircraft, their passengers, and humans on the ground.

faq.a: Light-1 was launched to the ISS on December 21, 2021, and deployed into orbit on February 3, 2022.
faq.q: When was Light-1 launched?

faq.a: Light-1 monitors terrestrial gamma-ray flashes (TGFs) which are emitted during lightning storms.
faq.q: What does Light-1 do?

faq.a: The name ‘Light-1’ was inspired by His Majesty King Hama bin Isa Al Khalifa of Bahrain’s book, titled ‘The First Light’. The book recounts key cultural and scientific moments in Bahrain’s history.
faq.q: Why is Light-1 called ‘Light-1’?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该卫星监测在雷暴期间产生的地面伽马射线闪（TGFs），旨在深入了解TGFs如何影响飞机、机上乘客以及地面人员。
eoportal.summary_en: It monitors terrestrial gamma-ray flashes (TGFs), which are emitted during lightning storms, to provide insight into how TGFs affect aircraft, their passengers, and humans on the ground.

faq_zh.a: Light-1于2021年12月21日发射至国际空间站，并于2022年2月3日部署入轨。
Light-1监测在雷暴期间产生的地面伽马射线闪（TGFs）。
‘Light-1’的名称灵感来源于巴林国王哈马·本·伊萨·阿勒哈利法陛下的著作《第一道光》。该书回顾了巴林历史上的关键文化与科学时刻。

faq_en.a: Light-1 was launched to the ISS on December 21, 2021, and deployed into orbit on February 3, 2022.
faq_en.q: When was Light-1 launched?

faq_en.a: Light-1 monitors terrestrial gamma-ray flashes (TGFs) which are emitted during lightning storms.
faq_en.q: What does Light-1 do?

faq_en.a: The name ‘Light-1’ was inspired by His Majesty King Hama bin Isa Al Khalifa of Bahrain’s book, titled ‘The First Light’. The book recounts key cultural and scientific moments in Bahrain’s history.
faq_en.q: Why is Light-1 called ‘Light-1’?

eoportal.name: LISA Observatory
eoportal.summary: LPF has successfully demonstrated key technologies for measuring gravity in space for the first time, paving the way not only for gravitational wave detectors but also for next-generation Earth and planetary gravimetry.
eoportal.summary_zh: LISA探路者（LPF）任务首次成功演示了在空间测量引力的关键技术，这不仅为引力波探测器铺平了道路，也为下一代地球和行星重力测量奠定了基础。
eoportal.summary_en: LPF has successfully demonstrated key technologies for measuring gravity in space for the first time, paving the way not only for gravitational wave detectors but also for next-generation Earth and planetary gravimetry.

eoportal.name: LLITED (Low-Latitude Ionosphere/Thermosphere Enhancements in Density)
eoportal.summary: U CubeSats were launched on 15 April, 2023 on board a SpaceX Transporter-7.

faq.a: The data is used to research the Equatorial Ionisation Anomaly (EIA) and the Equatorial Temperature and Wind Anomaly (ETWA).
faq.q: What are the findings of the LLITED mission used for?

faq.a: LLITED is a 1.5U CubeSat so will be approximately 100 x 100 x 170 mm.
faq.q: What are the dimensions for the LLITED spacecraft?

faq.a: LLITED has a planned mission duration of one year.
faq.q: How long will the LLITED mission last?

LLITED是一颗1.5U立方卫星，尺寸约为100 x 100 x 170毫米。
LLITED计划任务周期为一年。

eoportal.name: LOTUSat-1 (JV-LOTUSat)
eoportal.summary: LOTUSat-1 will provide timely information to respond and minimise the impacts of natural disasters and climate change in Vietnam.

faq.a: According to Tetsuya Kawasaki, of the Space Product Department, NEC, one of the toughest challenges was finding suitable calibration sites. Identifying locations where equipment can be installed to calibrate and validate image data from the satellite proved difficult.
faq.q: What are the challenges facing the LOTUSat-1 project?

faq.a: There are plans for a second LOTUSat satellite, LOTUSat-2, to be manufactured in Vietnam in the future.
faq.q: Are there plans for more LOTUSat satellites?

faq.a: The LOTUSat-1 project receives Official Development Assistance (ODA) funds from the Japan International Cooperation Agency (JICA). The manufacture and launch of LOTUSat-1 combined with the development of a ground system is expected to be worth approximately 20 billion yen.
faq.q: How much funding is dedicated to the LOTUSat-1 project?

eoportal.summary_zh: LOTUSat-1 卫星将提供及时信息，以应对和减轻自然灾害及气候变化对越南的影响。
eoportal.summary_en: LOTUSat-1 will provide timely information to respond and minimise the impacts of natural disasters and climate change in Vietnam.

faq_zh.a: 据 NEC 公司空间产品部门的 Tetsuya Kawasaki 称，最艰巨的挑战之一是寻找合适的定标场。证明可以安装设备来定标和验证卫星图像数据的地点被发现是困难的。
faq_zh.q: LOTUSat-1 项目面临哪些挑战？

faq_zh.a: 有计划未来在越南制造第二颗 LOTUSat 卫星，即 LOTUSat-2。
faq_zh.q: 是否有计划发射更多 LOTUSat 卫星？

faq_zh.a: LOTUSat-1 项目获得了日本国际协力机构 (JICA) 的官方发展援助 (ODA) 资金。LOTUSat-1 的制造、发射以及地面系统的开发预计总价值约为 200 亿日元。
faq_zh.q: LOTUSat-1 项目获得了多少资金？

faq_en.a: According to Tetsuya Kawasaki, of the Space Product Department, NEC, one of the toughest challenges was finding suitable calibration sites. Identifying locations where equipment can be installed to calibrate and validate image data from the satellite proved difficult.
faq_en.q: What are the challenges facing the LOTUSat-1 project?

faq_en.a: There are plans for a second LOTUSat satellite, LOTUSat-2, to be manufactured in Vietnam in the future.
faq_en.q: Are there plans for more LOTUSat satellites?

faq_en.a: The LOTUSat-1 project receives Official Development Assistance (ODA) funds from the Japan International Cooperation Agency (JICA). The manufacture and launch of LOTUSat-1 combined with the development of a ground system is expected to be worth approximately 20 billion yen.
faq_en.q: How much funding is dedicated to the LOTUSat-1 project?

eoportal.name: Lunar IceCube
eoportal.summary: The secondary payloads, 13 CubeSats, were selected through a series of announcements of flight opportunities, a NASA challenge and negotiations with NASA’s international partners.
eoportal.summary_zh: Lunar IceCube是作为次级载荷发射的13颗立方体卫星之一。这些卫星是通过一系列飞行机会公告、一项NASA挑战以及与NASA国际合作伙伴的协商遴选而出的。
eoportal.summary_en: The secondary payloads, 13 CubeSats, were selected through a series of announcements of flight opportunities, a NASA challenge and negotiations with NASA’s international partners.

eoportal.name: Lunar Trailblazer
eoportal.summary: The mission,  managed by NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory (JPL), failed shortly after launch in February 2025.

faq.a: Lunar Trailblazer was managed by NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory (JPL), operated by the California Institute of Technology (CalTech), and built by Lockheed Martin Space.
faq.q: Who managed the Lunar Trailblazer mission?

faq.a: Lunar Trailblazer aimed to improve our understanding of the lunar water cycle, investigate the distribution and abundance of different forms of water, and gather data for future landed missions on the Moon.
faq.q: What was the goal of the Lunar Trailblazer mission?

faq.a: Lunar Trailblazer carried two science instruments: the High Resolution Volatiles and Minerals Moon Mapper (HVM3) and the Lunar Thermal Mapper (LTM), which aimed to characterise molecular water, water ice, and hydroxyls on the Moon.
faq.q: How many instruments did Lunar Trailblazer carry?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该任务由NASA喷气推进实验室（JPL）管理，于2025年2月发射后不久即宣告失败。
eoportal.summary_en: The mission,  managed by NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory (JPL), failed shortly after launch in February 2025.

faq_zh.a: Lunar Trailblazer任务由NASA喷气推进实验室（JPL）管理，由加州理工学院（CalTech）运营，并由洛克希德·马丁太空公司建造。
Lunar Trailblazer旨在增进我们对月球水循环的理解，调查不同形式水的分布与丰度，并为未来月球着陆任务收集数据。
faq_zh.q: Lunar Trailblazer任务的目标是什么？

faq_zh.a: Lunar Trailblazer搭载了两台科学仪器：高分辨率挥发物与矿物月球测绘仪（HVM3）和月球热测绘仪（LTM），旨在表征月球上的分子水、水冰和羟基。
faq_zh.q: Lunar Trailblazer搭载了多少台仪器？

faq_en.a: Lunar Trailblazer was managed by NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory (JPL), operated by the California Institute of Technology (CalTech), and built by Lockheed Martin Space.
faq_en.q: Who managed the Lunar Trailblazer mission?

faq_en.a: Lunar Trailblazer aimed to improve our understanding of the lunar water cycle, investigate the distribution and abundance of different forms of water, and gather data for future landed missions on the Moon.
faq_en.q: What was the goal of the Lunar Trailblazer mission?

faq_en.a: Lunar Trailblazer carried two science instruments: the High Resolution Volatiles and Minerals Moon Mapper (HVM3) and the Lunar Thermal Mapper (LTM), which aimed to characterise molecular water, water ice, and hydroxyls on the Moon.
faq_en.q: How many instruments did Lunar Trailblazer carry?
