eoportal.name: SAOCOM (SAR Observation & Communications Satellite)
taxonomy: Ocean imagery and water leaving spectral radiance
Sea ice cover, edge and thickness
eoportal.summary: SAOCOM-1A was launched in October 2018, SAOCOM-1B in August 2020, and their successors, SAOCOM-2A and -2B, are planned for launch in 2025 and 2026, respectively.

faq.a: SAOCOM-1A was launched in October 2018, SAOCOM-1B in August 2020, and two more missions, SAOCOM-2A and -2B planned for launch in 2025 and 2026, respectively.
faq.q: When was the SAOCOM constellation launched?

faq.a: The SAOCOM constellation aims to meet the goals of CONAE (Comision Nacional de Actividades Espaciales), the Argentinian Space Agency, with imagery and observations in the fields of agriculture, forestry, weather, hydrology, oceanography, natural resources of land and sea, cartography, geology, mining, soil exploitation and archeology.​
faq.q: What is the purpose of the SAOCOM constellation?

faq.a: The SAOCOM constellation is owned and operated by CONAE (Comision Nacional de Actividades Espaciales), the Argentinian Space Agency.
faq.q: Who owns the SAOCOM constellation?

eoportal.summary_zh: SAOCOM-1A于2018年10月发射，SAOCOM-1B于2020年8月发射，其后续任务SAOCOM-2A和-2B计划分别于2025年和2026年发射。
eoportal.summary_en: SAOCOM-1A was launched in October 2018, SAOCOM-1B in August 2020, and their successors, SAOCOM-2A and -2B, are planned for launch in 2025 and 2026, respectively.

faq_zh.a: SAOCOM-1A于2018年10月发射，SAOCOM-1B于2020年8月发射，另有两项任务SAOCOM-2A和-2B计划分别于2025年和2026年发射。
SAOCOM星座旨在实现阿根廷国家空间活动委员会（CONAE）的目标，在农业、林业、气象、水文、海洋学、陆地与海洋自然资源、制图、地质、采矿、土壤利用和考古等领域提供影像和观测数据。
SAOCOM星座由阿根廷国家空间活动委员会（CONAE）拥有并运营。

faq_en.a: SAOCOM-1A was launched in October 2018, SAOCOM-1B in August 2020, and two more missions, SAOCOM-2A and -2B planned for launch in 2025 and 2026, respectively.
faq_en.q: When was the SAOCOM constellation launched?

faq_en.a: The SAOCOM constellation aims to meet the goals of CONAE (Comision Nacional de Actividades Espaciales), the Argentinian Space Agency, with imagery and observations in the fields of agriculture, forestry, weather, hydrology, oceanography, natural resources of land and sea, cartography, geology, mining, soil exploitation and archeology.​
faq_en.q: What is the purpose of the SAOCOM constellation?

faq_en.a: The SAOCOM constellation is owned and operated by CONAE (Comision Nacional de Actividades Espaciales), the Argentinian Space Agency.
faq_en.q: Who owns the SAOCOM constellation?

eoportal.name: SAR-Lupe Constellation
eoportal.summary: This SAR (Synthetic Aperture Radar) project consists of five identical satellites, which are still operational after having been launched in 2006-2008 with the overall objective of providing high-resolution radar imagery to German defence forces for at least ten years.

faq.a: A synthetic aperture radar (SAR) is an active sensor that first transmits microwave signals and then receives back the signals that are returned, or backscattered , from the Earth's surface.
faq.q: What is a SAR satellite?

faq.a: In the German language, the word “Lupe” means "magnifying glass," a reference and connotation for high-resolution imagery.
faq.q: How was SAR-Lupe named?

faq.a: SARah-1 is a phased array radar satellite, part of the three-satellite constellation built by Airbus for the German government as a follow-up to SAR-Lupe.
faq.q: What is the SARah-1 satellite?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该项目由五颗相同的合成孔径雷达卫星组成，于2006年至2008年间发射，目前仍在轨运行。其总体目标是为德国国防军提供至少十年的高分辨率雷达图像。
eoportal.summary_en: This SAR (Synthetic Aperture Radar) project consists of five identical satellites, which are still operational after having been launched in 2006-2008 with the overall objective of providing high-resolution radar imagery to German defence forces for at least ten years.

faq_zh.a: 合成孔径雷达是一种主动式传感器，它首先发射微波信号，然后接收从地球表面返回或后向散射的信号。
在德语中，‘Lupe’一词意为‘放大镜’，这里引申和隐喻指代高分辨率图像。
SARah-1是一颗相控阵雷达卫星，是由空客公司为德国政府建造的三星星座的一部分，作为SAR-Lupe的后继任务。

faq_en.a: A synthetic aperture radar (SAR) is an active sensor that first transmits microwave signals and then receives back the signals that are returned, or backscattered , from the Earth's surface.
faq_en.q: What is a SAR satellite?

faq_en.a: In the German language, the word “Lupe” means "magnifying glass," a reference and connotation for high-resolution imagery.
faq_en.q: How was SAR-Lupe named?

faq_en.a: SARah-1 is a phased array radar satellite, part of the three-satellite constellation built by Airbus for the German government as a follow-up to SAR-Lupe.
faq_en.q: What is the SARah-1 satellite?

eoportal.name: SARah (Satellite-based Radar Reconnaissance System)
eoportal.summary: All three satellites feature advanced synthetic aperture radar (SAR) technology, providing high-resolution observations for intelligence applications.
eoportal.summary_zh: 这三颗卫星均配备了先进的合成孔径雷达（SAR）技术，为情报应用提供高分辨率观测。
eoportal.summary_en: All three satellites feature advanced synthetic aperture radar (SAR) technology, providing high-resolution observations for intelligence applications.

eoportal.name: SARAL (Satellite with ARgos and ALtiKa)
eoportal.summary: Launched in 2013, data gathered by SARAL has applications in oceanography and atmospheric studies.

faq.a: SARAL is an altimetry satellite with applications in oceanography, hydrology, cryospheric sciences and geodesy.
faq.q: What is SARAL used for?

faq.a: SARAL is a cooperative mission of ISRO (Indian Space Research Organisation) and CNES (Space Agency of France). CNES provides the payload module, while ISRO is responsible for the platform, launch and operations of the spacecraft.
faq.q: Who operates SARAL?

faq.a: The SARAL spacecraft was launched from Sriharikota, India on 25 February 2013 on ISRO’s PSLV-C20 launcher.
faq.q: Where was SARAL launched?

eoportal.name_zh: SARAL（搭载 ARgos 和 ALtiKa 的卫星）
eoportal.agency_zh: CNES（法国国家空间研究中心）、ISRO（印度空间研究组织）
eoportal.summary_zh: SARAL 卫星于 2013 年发射，其收集的数据在海洋学和大气研究领域具有广泛应用。
eoportal.summary_en: Launched in 2013, data gathered by SARAL has applications in oceanography and atmospheric studies.

faq_zh.a: SARAL 是一颗测高卫星，其应用领域涵盖海洋学、水文学、冰冻圈科学和大地测量学。
faq_zh.q: SARAL 的主要用途是什么？

faq_zh.a: SARAL 是 ISRO（印度空间研究组织）与 CNES（法国国家空间研究中心）的合作任务。CNES 负责提供有效载荷模块，而 ISRO 则负责航天器的平台、发射和运营。
SARAL 航天器于 2013 年 2 月 25 日在印度斯里赫里戈达发射场，使用 ISRO 的 PSLV-C20 运载火箭发射升空。

faq_en.a: SARAL is an altimetry satellite with applications in oceanography, hydrology, cryospheric sciences and geodesy.
faq_en.q: What is SARAL used for?

faq_en.a: SARAL is a cooperative mission of ISRO (Indian Space Research Organisation) and CNES (Space Agency of France). CNES provides the payload module, while ISRO is responsible for the platform, launch and operations of the spacecraft.
faq_en.q: Who operates SARAL?

faq_en.a: The SARAL spacecraft was launched from Sriharikota, India on 25 February 2013 on ISRO’s PSLV-C20 launcher.
faq_en.q: Where was SARAL launched?

eoportal.name: Sateliot_X Constellation
eoportal.summary: The first satellite in the constellation named Enxaneta was launched in March 2021.

faq.a: Sateliot plans to launch at least 250 satellites into the Sateliot_X Constellation.
faq.q: How many satellites will be in the Sateliot_X Constellation?

faq.a: There are currently six satellites in the Sateliot_X Constellation, the first satellite was launched in March 2021 and is a 3U CubeSat called Enxaneta.
faq.q: How many satellites are in the constellation currently?

faq.a: Sateliot has contracted Open Cosmos Ltd. (UK) to construct and operate the nanosatellite constellation.
faq.q: Who operates the constellation?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该星座的首颗卫星 Enxaneta 于 2021 年 3 月发射。
eoportal.summary_en: The first satellite in the constellation named Enxaneta was launched in March 2021.

faq_zh.a: Sateliot 计划向 Sateliot_X 星座发射至少 250 颗卫星。
faq_zh.q: Sateliot_X 星座将包含多少颗卫星？

faq_zh.a: Sateliot_X 星座目前共有六颗卫星，首颗卫星 Enxaneta 于 2021 年 3 月发射，是一颗 3U 立方体卫星。
faq_zh.q: 该星座目前有多少颗卫星？

faq_zh.a: Sateliot 已委托 Open Cosmos Ltd.（英国）建造和运营该纳米卫星星座。

faq_en.a: Sateliot plans to launch at least 250 satellites into the Sateliot_X Constellation.
faq_en.q: How many satellites will be in the Sateliot_X Constellation?

faq_en.a: There are currently six satellites in the Sateliot_X Constellation, the first satellite was launched in March 2021 and is a 3U CubeSat called Enxaneta.
faq_en.q: How many satellites are in the constellation currently?

faq_en.a: Sateliot has contracted Open Cosmos Ltd. (UK) to construct and operate the nanosatellite constellation.
faq_en.q: Who operates the constellation?

eoportal.name: SatLeo Labs Constellation
eoportal.summary: The constellation will capture temperature variations across mid- and long-wave infrared bands, complemented by visible imagery, to support applications such as agriculture, climate monitoring, disaster management, and urban planning.

faq.a: SatLeo Labs is a commercial space technology company based in Ahmedabad, India, founded in 2023. It develops microsatellites equipped with thermal imaging sensors for Earth observation.
faq.q: What is SatLeo Labs?

faq.a: Applications include agriculture, climate modelling, weather forecasting, renewable energy, natural disaster management, environmental conservation, archaeology and geology, and urban planning.
faq.q: What are the main applications of SatLeo Lab’s data?

faq.a: The satellites can detect temperature variations with an accuracy of about one Kelvin. They also support edge computing for real-time data processing onboard the satellites.
faq.q: What is unique about SatLeo Lab’s satellites?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该星座将捕获中波和长波红外波段的温度变化，并辅以可见光图像，以支持农业、气候监测、灾害管理和城市规划等应用。
eoportal.summary_en: The constellation will capture temperature variations across mid- and long-wave infrared bands, complemented by visible imagery, to support applications such as agriculture, climate monitoring, disaster management, and urban planning.

faq_zh.a: SatLeo Labs 是一家位于印度艾哈迈达巴德的商业太空技术公司，成立于2023年。该公司开发配备热成像传感器用于地球观测的微型卫星。
应用领域包括农业、气候建模、天气预报、可再生能源、自然灾害管理、环境保护、考古与地质以及城市规划。
faq_zh.q: SatLeo Labs 的数据有哪些主要应用？

faq_zh.a: 这些卫星能够以约1开尔文的精度检测温度变化，并支持在卫星上进行实时数据处理的边缘计算。
faq_zh.q: SatLeo Labs 的卫星有何独特之处？

faq_en.a: SatLeo Labs is a commercial space technology company based in Ahmedabad, India, founded in 2023. It develops microsatellites equipped with thermal imaging sensors for Earth observation.
faq_en.q: What is SatLeo Labs?

faq_en.a: Applications include agriculture, climate modelling, weather forecasting, renewable energy, natural disaster management, environmental conservation, archaeology and geology, and urban planning.
faq_en.q: What are the main applications of SatLeo Lab’s data?

faq_en.a: The satellites can detect temperature variations with an accuracy of about one Kelvin. They also support edge computing for real-time data processing onboard the satellites.
faq_en.q: What is unique about SatLeo Lab’s satellites?

eoportal.name: SC-ODIN (Space Concordia’s Orbital Dust Imaging Nanosatellite)
eoportal.summary: It was deployed from the International Space Station (ISS) on 6 July 2023.

faq.a: The SC-ODIN mission aims to capture RGB imaging data over Lake Colhue Huapi in Argentina and the Namibian coastal regions to identify the aerosol particles in the dust storms that frequent the area. As well as acquiring total ionising dose (TID) data to monitor radiation levels for future Space Concordia projects and to give a better understanding of the radiation levels in Low Earth Orbit (LEO).
faq.q: What are the objectives of the SC-ODIN mission?

faq.a: SC-ODIN carries a GomSpace NanoCam and two RADFET sensors, as well as an onboard computer to downlink the data from these instruments to the ground station.
faq.q: What instruments does the SC-ODIN mission carry?

faq.a: SC-ODIN was launched on 5 June 2023 from Cape Canaveral Space Force Station onboard a SpaceX Falcon-9 v1.2 (Block 5) launcher as a part of the Dragon CRS-28 resupply mission to the International Space Station (ISS).
faq.q: When was SC-ODIN launched?

eoportal.name_zh: SC-ODIN（Space Concordia 轨道尘埃成像纳米卫星）
eoportal.summary_zh: 该卫星于2023年7月6日从国际空间站 (ISS) 部署。
eoportal.summary_en: It was deployed from the International Space Station (ISS) on 6 July 2023.

faq_zh.a: SC-ODIN 任务旨在获取阿根廷科尔韦瓦皮湖和纳米比亚沿海地区的RGB成像数据，以识别该地区频发沙尘暴中的气溶胶粒子。同时，采集总电离剂量 (TID) 数据，用于监测未来Space Concordia项目的辐射水平，并加深对低地球轨道 (LEO) 辐射水平的了解。
faq_zh.q: SC-ODIN 任务的目标是什么？

faq_zh.a: SC-ODIN 搭载了一台 GomSpace NanoCam 相机、两个 RADFET 传感器以及一个星载计算机，用于将这些仪器的数据下传至地面站。
faq_zh.q: SC-ODIN 任务搭载了哪些仪器？

faq_zh.a: SC-ODIN 于2023年6月5日从卡纳维拉尔角太空军基地，搭载 SpaceX 猎鹰9号 v1.2 (Block 5) 运载火箭发射升空，作为国际空间站 (ISS) 龙飞船 CRS-28 补给任务的一部分。

faq_en.a: The SC-ODIN mission aims to capture RGB imaging data over Lake Colhue Huapi in Argentina and the Namibian coastal regions to identify the aerosol particles in the dust storms that frequent the area. As well as acquiring total ionising dose (TID) data to monitor radiation levels for future Space Concordia projects and to give a better understanding of the radiation levels in Low Earth Orbit (LEO).
faq_en.q: What are the objectives of the SC-ODIN mission?

faq_en.a: SC-ODIN carries a GomSpace NanoCam and two RADFET sensors, as well as an onboard computer to downlink the data from these instruments to the ground station.
faq_en.q: What instruments does the SC-ODIN mission carry?

faq_en.a: SC-ODIN was launched on 5 June 2023 from Cape Canaveral Space Force Station onboard a SpaceX Falcon-9 v1.2 (Block 5) launcher as a part of the Dragon CRS-28 resupply mission to the International Space Station (ISS).
faq_en.q: When was SC-ODIN launched?

sc-odin (space concordia’s orbital dust.display: SC-ODIN (Space Concordia’s Orbital Dust Imaging Nanosatellite)

eoportal.name: SciSat-1 / ACE (Science Satellite/Atmospheric Chemistry Experiment)
eoportal.summary: SciSat-1 measures over sixty atmospheric species at one of the world’s highest vertical resolutions.

faq.a: The Canadian Space Agency’s (CSA) Science Satellite-1 (SciSat-1) is designed to make observations of the Earth’s atmosphere to provide data for key climate action goals.
faq.q: What does SciSat-1 do?

faq.a: The Science Satellite-1 (SciSat-1) was launched by a Pegasus-XL rocket onboard a National Aerospace and Space Administration (NASA) Lockheed L-1011 carrier aircraft on August 12, 2003 from Vandenberg Air Force Base.
faq.q: What are the details of SciSat-1’s launch?

faq.a: The Atmospheric Chemistry Experiment (ACE) is an experiment onboard the Science Satellite-1 mission. ACE uses the Fourier Transform Spectrometer (FTS) onboard SciSat-1. The satellite is referred to as SciSat-1/ACE in most literature as ACE plays a major role in measurements onboard SciSat-1.
faq.q: What does ACE mean in SciSat-1/ACE?

eoportal.name_zh: SciSat-1 / ACE (科学卫星/大气化学实验)
eoportal.summary_zh: SciSat-1 以世界领先的高垂直分辨率，对超过六十种大气成分进行测量。
eoportal.summary_en: SciSat-1 measures over sixty atmospheric species at one of the world’s highest vertical resolutions.

faq_zh.a: 加拿大航天局 (CSA) 的科学卫星-1 (SciSat-1) 旨在对地球大气层进行观测，为实现关键的气候行动目标提供数据。
科学卫星-1 (SciSat-1) 于2003年8月12日从范登堡空军基地，搭载美国国家航空航天局 (NASA) 的洛克希德 L-1011 运载飞机，由飞马座-XL火箭发射升空。
faq_zh.q: SciSat-1 的发射细节是怎样的？

faq_zh.a: 大气化学实验 (ACE) 是科学卫星-1 任务上搭载的一个实验。ACE 使用了 SciSat-1 上的傅里叶变换光谱仪 (FTS)。由于 ACE 在该卫星的测量工作中扮演了重要角色，大多数文献将其称为 SciSat-1/ACE。
faq_zh.q: 在 SciSat-1/ACE 中，ACE 是什么意思？

faq_en.a: The Canadian Space Agency’s (CSA) Science Satellite-1 (SciSat-1) is designed to make observations of the Earth’s atmosphere to provide data for key climate action goals.
faq_en.q: What does SciSat-1 do?

faq_en.a: The Science Satellite-1 (SciSat-1) was launched by a Pegasus-XL rocket onboard a National Aerospace and Space Administration (NASA) Lockheed L-1011 carrier aircraft on August 12, 2003 from Vandenberg Air Force Base.
faq_en.q: What are the details of SciSat-1’s launch?

faq_en.a: The Atmospheric Chemistry Experiment (ACE) is an experiment onboard the Science Satellite-1 mission. ACE uses the Fourier Transform Spectrometer (FTS) onboard SciSat-1. The satellite is referred to as SciSat-1/ACE in most literature as ACE plays a major role in measurements onboard SciSat-1.
faq_en.q: What does ACE mean in SciSat-1/ACE?

eoportal.name: SDGSAT-1 (Sustainable Development Science Satellite 1)
eoportal.summary: The mission’s primary aim is to depict traces of anthropic activity, with a secondary aim to trial new methods for low-light surface environment detection.

faq.a: SDGSAT-1 is the first satellite of the Sustainable Development Satellite Constellation which will be a series of polar orbiting satellites dedicated to measuring traces of global anthropogenic activities.
faq.q: Is SDGSAT-1 part of a satellite constellation?

faq.a: The Chinese Academy of Sciences (CAS) Big Earth Data Science Engineering Programme aims to utilise Earth observation (EO) data from SDGSAT-1 to support global progress toward the 17 Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) of the United Nations (UN) 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development (2030 Agenda).
faq.q: How does SDGSAT-1 relate to the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) of the 2030 Agenda?

faq.a: SDGSAT-1 synergistically uses three sensor payloads: a Thermal Infrared Spectrometer (TIS), Glimmer Imager (GLI), and Multispectral Imager (MSI).
faq.q: What instruments are onboard SDGSAT-1?

eoportal.name_zh: 可持续发展科学卫星1号（SDGSAT-1）
eoportal.summary_zh: 该任务的主要目标是描绘人类活动的痕迹，次要目标是试验用于低光照地表环境探测的新方法。
eoportal.summary_en: The mission’s primary aim is to depict traces of anthropic activity, with a secondary aim to trial new methods for low-light surface environment detection.

faq_zh.a: SDGSAT-1是可持续发展卫星星座的首颗卫星，该星座将是一系列专注于测量全球人类活动痕迹的极轨卫星。
faq_zh.q: SDGSAT-1是卫星星座的一部分吗？

faq_zh.a: 中国科学院（CAS）地球大数据科学工程计划旨在利用SDGSAT-1获取的对地观测数据，支持全球朝着联合国2030年可持续发展议程（2030年议程）的17个可持续发展目标（SDGs）取得进展。
faq_zh.q: SDGSAT-1如何与联合国2030年议程的可持续发展目标（SDGs）相关联？

faq_zh.a: SDGSAT-1协同使用三种有效载荷传感器：热红外光谱仪（TIS）、微光成像仪（GLI）和多光谱成像仪（MSI）。

faq_en.a: SDGSAT-1 is the first satellite of the Sustainable Development Satellite Constellation which will be a series of polar orbiting satellites dedicated to measuring traces of global anthropogenic activities.
faq_en.q: Is SDGSAT-1 part of a satellite constellation?

faq_en.a: The Chinese Academy of Sciences (CAS) Big Earth Data Science Engineering Programme aims to utilise Earth observation (EO) data from SDGSAT-1 to support global progress toward the 17 Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) of the United Nations (UN) 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development (2030 Agenda).
faq_en.q: How does SDGSAT-1 relate to the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) of the 2030 Agenda?

faq_en.a: SDGSAT-1 synergistically uses three sensor payloads: a Thermal Infrared Spectrometer (TIS), Glimmer Imager (GLI), and Multispectral Imager (MSI).
faq_en.q: What instruments are onboard SDGSAT-1?

eoportal.name: SeaSat (SeaSat-A or SeaSat-1)
taxonomy: Sea ice cover, edge and thickness
eoportal.summary: Despite its short 110-day life in 1978, SeaSat demonstrated the viability of imaging radar for studying our planet and laid the foundations of satellite oceanography, becoming a precursor to many subsequent missions that investigated the Earth's oceans, land and ice.

faq.a: SeaSat collected information on sea surface winds, sea surface temperatures, wave heights, internal waves, atmospheric water, sea ice features, ice sheet topography and ocean topography.
faq.q: What did SeaSat measure?

faq.a: The data from ALT, SASS and SMMR led to a revolution in understanding oceanic processes, providing the first global maps of variability of surface geostrophic currents, significant wave height, oceanic surface wind speed and direction, atmospheric water vapour and liquid water.
faq.q: What is the most important result of SeaSat?

faq.a: SeaSat stopped operating due to an abrupt power system failure in the Agena bus that was used as a part of the spacecraft.
faq.q: What caused SeaSat to end?

eoportal.summary_zh: 尽管SeaSat卫星在1978年的运行寿命仅有短短110天，但它证明了成像雷达在研究地球方面的可行性，并为卫星海洋学奠定了基础，成为后续一系列探索地球海洋、陆地和冰盖任务的先驱。
eoportal.summary_en: Despite its short 110-day life in 1978, SeaSat demonstrated the viability of imaging radar for studying our planet and laid the foundations of satellite oceanography, becoming a precursor to many subsequent missions that investigated the Earth's oceans, land and ice.

faq_zh.a: SeaSat收集了海面风速、海面温度、波高、内波、大气水汽、海冰特征、冰盖地形和海洋地形等信息。
来自ALT、SASS和SMMR的数据引发了对海洋过程理解的革命，首次提供了表面地转流变率、有效波高、海面风速与风向、大气水汽和液态水的全球分布图。
SeaSat停止运行是由于作为航天器一部分的阿金纳推进系统突发电源故障所致。
faq_zh.q: 是什么导致了SeaSat任务的终结？

faq_en.a: SeaSat collected information on sea surface winds, sea surface temperatures, wave heights, internal waves, atmospheric water, sea ice features, ice sheet topography and ocean topography.
faq_en.q: What did SeaSat measure?

faq_en.a: The data from ALT, SASS and SMMR led to a revolution in understanding oceanic processes, providing the first global maps of variability of surface geostrophic currents, significant wave height, oceanic surface wind speed and direction, atmospheric water vapour and liquid water.
faq_en.q: What is the most important result of SeaSat?

faq_en.a: SeaSat stopped operating due to an abrupt power system failure in the Agena bus that was used as a part of the spacecraft.
faq_en.q: What caused SeaSat to end?

eoportal.name: SEMOViS
eoportal.summary: The payload is intended to deliver high-resolution multispectral data for daily Earth observation analytics, with a planned constellation of up to 200 satellites.

faq.a: SEMOViS combines very high spatial resolution (0.8 m in VIS/NIR) with daily revisit capabilities through its planned constellation approach. Its dual-instrument design provides both VIS/NIR and SWIR capabilities in a compact form factor while maintaining spectral compatibility with the Copernicus Sentinel-2 mission.
faq.q: What are the imaging capabilities of the SEMOViS imager?

faq.a: Data will be distributed through Marble Imaging's cloud-based "Kaleidoscope" platform. The platform will offer SEMOViS imagery alongside data from other missions like Sentinel-2 and the Landsat missions.
faq.q: How will SEMOViS data be accessed and distributed?

faq.a: The mission allows for applications requiring high frequency and high resolution monitoring, including deforestation tracking (particularly for EU Deforestation Regulation compliance), agricultural management, maritime security, infrastructure monitoring, and coastal zone management. The short-wave infrared (SWIR) capabilities enable soil moisture assessment, drought detection, and certain mineralogical applications.
faq.q: What applications is SEMOViS particularly suited for?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该载荷旨在为日常地球观测分析提供高分辨率多光谱数据，计划组建一个由多达200颗卫星构成的星座。
eoportal.summary_en: The payload is intended to deliver high-resolution multispectral data for daily Earth observation analytics, with a planned constellation of up to 200 satellites.

faq_zh.a: SEMOViS通过其规划的星座方案，将极高空间分辨率（可见光/近红外波段0.8米）与每日重访能力相结合。其双仪器设计在紧凑的外形中同时提供可见光/近红外和短波红外能力，并与哥白尼哨兵-2（Sentinel-2）任务保持光谱兼容性。
faq_zh.q: SEMOViS成像仪的成像能力如何？

faq_zh.a: 数据将通过Marble Imaging基于云的“万花筒”平台进行分发。该平台将提供SEMOViS图像以及来自哨兵-2（Sentinel-2）和陆地卫星（Landsat）等其他任务的数据。
该任务适用于需要高频和高分辨率监测的应用，包括森林砍伐追踪（特别是为了符合欧盟森林砍伐法规）、农业管理、海上安全、基础设施监测和海岸带管理。短波红外能力可用于土壤湿度评估、干旱探测以及某些矿物学应用。

faq_en.a: SEMOViS combines very high spatial resolution (0.8 m in VIS/NIR) with daily revisit capabilities through its planned constellation approach. Its dual-instrument design provides both VIS/NIR and SWIR capabilities in a compact form factor while maintaining spectral compatibility with the Copernicus Sentinel-2 mission.
faq_en.q: What are the imaging capabilities of the SEMOViS imager?

faq_en.a: Data will be distributed through Marble Imaging's cloud-based "Kaleidoscope" platform. The platform will offer SEMOViS imagery alongside data from other missions like Sentinel-2 and the Landsat missions.
faq_en.q: How will SEMOViS data be accessed and distributed?

faq_en.a: The mission allows for applications requiring high frequency and high resolution monitoring, including deforestation tracking (particularly for EU Deforestation Regulation compliance), agricultural management, maritime security, infrastructure monitoring, and coastal zone management. The short-wave infrared (SWIR) capabilities enable soil moisture assessment, drought detection, and certain mineralogical applications.
faq_en.q: What applications is SEMOViS particularly suited for?

eoportal.summary: The satellite was launched in November 2020, although a failure in the Vega launcher shortly after liftoff created a trajectory deviation, resulting in the loss of the mission.

faq.a: The Spanish System for Earth Observation Satellite (SEOSat) was destroyed 8 minutes after launch aboard the Vega VV17 mission when a thruster control issue led to loss of trajectory.
faq.q: What happened to SEOSat?

faq.a: The Spanish System for Earth Observation Satellite (SEOSat) is operated by Spain and overseen by the European Space Agency (ESA).
faq.q: Who operates SEOSat?

faq.a: The Spanish System for Earth Observation Satellite (SEOSat) is an optical satellite with applications in cartography, land use monitoring, urban management, risk management and security.

eoportal.name: SIR-C (Shuttle Imaging Radar with payload C)
eoportal.summary: SIR-C/X-SAR was a payload on the SRL-1 (Space Radar Laboratory-1) shuttle which operated over April 1994, and the SRL-2 (Space Radar Laboratory-2) shuttle which operated from September to October in 1994.

faq.a: Data obtained by the SIR-C/X-SAR instrument radar system had many applications across scientific disciplines, including ecology, hydrology, geology, and oceanography. SIR-C/X-SAR also provided interferometric data used for topographic mapping and surface change monitoring.
faq.q: What was SIR-C/X-SAR data used for?

faq.a: The SIR-C/X-SAR imaging radar payload operated during two separate ten-day spaceflights, hosted on the SRL-1 and SRL-2 shuttles. SRL-1 was launched on 09 April, 1994 and ended operations on 20 April, 1994; while SRL-2 was launched on 30 September, 1994 and ended operations on 11 October, 1994.
faq.q: When did SIR-C/X-SAR operate?

faq.a: SIR-C/X-SAR was the first instrument to obtain simultaneous multifrequency, fully polarized, repeat-pass interferometric SAR (Synthetic Aperture Radar) imagery.
faq.q: What data did SIR-C/X-SAR collect?

eoportal.name_zh: SIR-C（航天飞机成像雷达，搭载C型有效载荷）
eoportal.summary_zh: SIR-C/X-SAR 是 SRL-1（空间雷达实验室-1）航天飞机上的有效载荷，于 1994 年 4 月运行；也是 SRL-2（空间雷达实验室-2）航天飞机上的有效载荷，于 1994 年 9 月至 10 月运行。
eoportal.summary_en: SIR-C/X-SAR was a payload on the SRL-1 (Space Radar Laboratory-1) shuttle which operated over April 1994, and the SRL-2 (Space Radar Laboratory-2) shuttle which operated from September to October in 1994.

faq_zh.a: SIR-C/X-SAR 仪器雷达系统获取的数据在多个科学学科中都有广泛应用，包括生态学、水文学、地质学和海洋学。SIR-C/X-SAR 还提供了用于地形测绘和地表变化监测的干涉数据。
SIR-C/X-SAR 成像雷达有效载荷在两次独立的十天太空飞行中运行，分别搭载于 SRL-1 和 SRL-2 航天飞机上。SRL-1 于 1994 年 4 月 9 日发射，4 月 20 日结束运行；SRL-2 于 1994 年 9 月 30 日发射，10 月 11 日结束运行。
SIR-C/X-SAR 是首个获取同时多频率、全极化、重复轨道干涉 SAR（合成孔径雷达）图像的仪器。

faq_en.a: Data obtained by the SIR-C/X-SAR instrument radar system had many applications across scientific disciplines, including ecology, hydrology, geology, and oceanography. SIR-C/X-SAR also provided interferometric data used for topographic mapping and surface change monitoring.
faq_en.q: What was SIR-C/X-SAR data used for?

faq_en.a: The SIR-C/X-SAR imaging radar payload operated during two separate ten-day spaceflights, hosted on the SRL-1 and SRL-2 shuttles. SRL-1 was launched on 09 April, 1994 and ended operations on 20 April, 1994; while SRL-2 was launched on 30 September, 1994 and ended operations on 11 October, 1994.
faq_en.q: When did SIR-C/X-SAR operate?

faq_en.a: SIR-C/X-SAR was the first instrument to obtain simultaneous multifrequency, fully polarized, repeat-pass interferometric SAR (Synthetic Aperture Radar) imagery.

eoportal.name: Sky Muster (NBN-Co 1A, 1B)
eoportal.summary: It provides internet connectivity across Australia and consists of two satellites: Sky Muster-I, launched in October 2015, and Sky Muster-II, launched in October 2016.

faq.a: Sky Muster is a constellation of two communications satellites, aiming to provide internet connectivity to Australian households that cannot access fixed line internet services.
faq.q: What is the objective of the Sky Muster mission?

faq.a: Sky Muster essentially acts as a ‘bridge’ between home-mounted National Broadband Network (NBN) dishes and NBN ground stations, providing complete national coverage by dividing Australia into 101 regions based on internet demand, with each area serviced by one of 10 ground stations.
faq.q: How does Sky Muster work?

faq.a: Sky Muster is owned and operated by Australia’s National Broadband Network (NBN), providing internet connectivity to up to 400,000 homes across Australia.
faq.q: Who owns Sky Muster?

eoportal.agency_zh: 澳大利亚国家宽带网络公司（NBN）
eoportal.summary_zh: 该卫星系统为澳大利亚全境提供互联网连接服务，由两颗卫星组成：Sky Muster-I于2015年10月发射，Sky Muster-II于2016年10月发射。
eoportal.summary_en: It provides internet connectivity across Australia and consists of two satellites: Sky Muster-I, launched in October 2015, and Sky Muster-II, launched in October 2016.

faq_zh.a: Sky Muster是一个由两颗通信卫星组成的星座，旨在为无法接入固定线路互联网服务的澳大利亚家庭提供互联网连接。
faq_zh.q: Sky Muster任务的目标是什么？

faq_zh.a: Sky Muster本质上充当家庭安装的国家宽带网络（NBN）卫星天线与NBN地面站之间的'桥梁'。它根据互联网需求将澳大利亚划分为101个区域，每个区域由10个地面站之一提供服务，从而实现全国覆盖。
Sky Muster由澳大利亚国家宽带网络公司（NBN）拥有和运营，为澳大利亚多达40万个家庭提供互联网连接服务。

faq_en.a: Sky Muster is a constellation of two communications satellites, aiming to provide internet connectivity to Australian households that cannot access fixed line internet services.
faq_en.q: What is the objective of the Sky Muster mission?

faq_en.a: Sky Muster essentially acts as a ‘bridge’ between home-mounted National Broadband Network (NBN) dishes and NBN ground stations, providing complete national coverage by dividing Australia into 101 regions based on internet demand, with each area serviced by one of 10 ground stations.
faq_en.q: How does Sky Muster work?

faq_en.a: Sky Muster is owned and operated by Australia’s National Broadband Network (NBN), providing internet connectivity to up to 400,000 homes across Australia.
faq_en.q: Who owns Sky Muster?

eoportal.summary: SkySat-1 and SkySat-2 are called A and B generations, the other 19 satellites are called modernised C generation satellites.

faq.a: SkySat satellites provide high-resolution panchromatic and multispectral imagery of any place on Earth multiple times a day.
faq.q: What do the SkySat satellites do?

faq.a: There are 21 SkySat satellites. Skysat-1 and SkySat-2 are A and B generation satellites and the other 19 are modernised C generation satellites.
faq.q: How many SkySat satellites are there?

faq.a: Planet Labs have more than 200 satellites that together provide an unprecedented dataset of Earth observation imagery. Their three distinct constellations include SkySat, Dove and RapidEye.
faq.q: How many satellites does Planet Labs have?

eoportal.summary_en: SkySat-1 and SkySat-2 are called A and B generations, the other 19 satellites are called modernised C generation satellites.

共有 21 颗 SkySat 卫星。其中 SkySat-1 和 SkySat-2 是 A 代和 B 代卫星，其余 19 颗是经过现代化改造的 C 代卫星。
faq_zh.q: 有多少颗 SkySat 卫星？

faq_zh.a: Planet Labs 拥有超过 200 颗卫星，这些卫星共同提供了前所未有的地球观测影像数据集。它们包含三个独立的星座：SkySat、Dove 和 RapidEye。

eoportal.name: SMAP (Soil Moisture Active/Passive)
eoportal.summary: SMAP uses radio waves to map soil moisture and determine the freeze or thaw state of the same area.

faq.a: Radar acquires soil moisture data with a higher spatial resolution but a lower accuracy than a radiometer. Contrastingly, a radiometer acquires data with a lower resolution but a higher accuracy than radar. By combining methods, SMAP can acquire accurate data with a relatively high resolution.
faq.q: Why does SMAP use radar and a radiometer to measure soil moisture?

faq.a: SMAP launched on 31 January, 2015, onboard a Delta II launch vehicle, manufactured by United Launch Alliance (ULA), from the Vandenberg Space Force Base in the United States.
faq.q: What were the launch details of SMAP?

faq.a: SMAP rotates at 14 revolutions per minute, or approximately once every four seconds. This speed is essential to ensure SMAP maintains a swath width of 1000 km.
faq.q: How fast does SMAP spin?

eoportal.name_zh: SMAP (土壤水分主被动探测卫星)
eoportal.summary_zh: SMAP 卫星利用无线电波来绘制土壤湿度图，并确定同一区域的冻结或融化状态。
eoportal.summary_en: SMAP uses radio waves to map soil moisture and determine the freeze or thaw state of the same area.

faq_zh.a: 雷达获取的土壤湿度数据具有更高的空间分辨率，但精度低于辐射计。相反，辐射计获取的数据分辨率较低，但精度高于雷达。通过结合这两种方法，SMAP 能够以相对较高的分辨率获取精确数据。
faq_zh.q: 为什么 SMAP 使用雷达和辐射计来测量土壤湿度？

faq_zh.a: SMAP 于2015年1月31日发射，搭载由联合发射联盟（ULA）制造的德尔塔 II 运载火箭，从美国范登堡太空军基地升空。
faq_zh.q: SMAP 的发射细节是什么？

faq_zh.a: SMAP 以每分钟14转的速度旋转，大约每4秒旋转一周。这一速度对于确保 SMAP 维持1000公里的幅宽至关重要。
faq_zh.q: SMAP 的自转速度有多快？

faq_en.a: Radar acquires soil moisture data with a higher spatial resolution but a lower accuracy than a radiometer. Contrastingly, a radiometer acquires data with a lower resolution but a higher accuracy than radar. By combining methods, SMAP can acquire accurate data with a relatively high resolution.
faq_en.q: Why does SMAP use radar and a radiometer to measure soil moisture?

faq_en.a: SMAP launched on 31 January, 2015, onboard a Delta II launch vehicle, manufactured by United Launch Alliance (ULA), from the Vandenberg Space Force Base in the United States.
faq_en.q: What were the launch details of SMAP?

faq_en.a: SMAP rotates at 14 revolutions per minute, or approximately once every four seconds. This speed is essential to ensure SMAP maintains a swath width of 1000 km.
faq_en.q: How fast does SMAP spin?

eoportal.name: SMOS (Soil Moisture and Ocean Salinity) Mission
eoportal.summary: SMOS provides global observations on soil moisture and ocean salinity to improve our understanding of the water cycle and our weather forecasting ability.

faq.a: Originally designed as a five-year mission, SMOS mission operations have been approved for beyond 2025.
faq.q: When is the end of life date for SMOS?

faq.a: SMOS measures the intensity of emitted microwave radiation from soil and seawater to determine their moisture and salinity, as these factors decrease the emissivity of soil and seawater respectively.
faq.q: How does SMOS measure soil moisture and ocean salinity?

faq.a: ESA’s Earth Explorer Missions are a set of missions dedicated to addressing major challenges identified by society. Five missions have been or are in operation (GOCE, SMOS, CryoSat, Swarm, Aeolus) and five further missions are approved for development  (EarthCARE, Biomass, FLEX, FORUM, Harmony).
faq.q: What are ESA’s Earth Explorer Missions?

eoportal.name_zh: SMOS（土壤湿度和海洋盐度）任务
eoportal.agency_zh: ESA（欧洲空间局）、CNES（法国国家空间研究中心）、CDTI（西班牙工业技术发展中心）
eoportal.summary_zh: SMOS任务提供全球土壤湿度和海洋盐度的观测数据，旨在增进我们对水循环的理解并提高天气预报能力。
eoportal.summary_en: SMOS provides global observations on soil moisture and ocean salinity to improve our understanding of the water cycle and our weather forecasting ability.

faq_zh.a: SMOS最初设计为五年任务，其任务运营已被批准延续至2025年以后。
SMOS通过测量土壤和海水发射的微波辐射强度来确定其湿度和盐度，因为这两个因素会分别降低土壤和海水的发射率。
faq_zh.q: SMOS如何测量土壤湿度和海洋盐度？

faq_zh.a: ESA的地球探测者系列任务是一组致力于解决社会面临重大挑战的任务。已有或正在运行五项任务（GOCE、SMOS、CryoSat、Swarm、Aeolus），另有五项任务已获准开发（EarthCARE、Biomass、FLEX、FORUM、Harmony）。
faq_zh.q: ESA的地球探测者系列任务是什么？

faq_en.a: Originally designed as a five-year mission, SMOS mission operations have been approved for beyond 2025.
faq_en.q: When is the end of life date for SMOS?

faq_en.a: SMOS measures the intensity of emitted microwave radiation from soil and seawater to determine their moisture and salinity, as these factors decrease the emissivity of soil and seawater respectively.
faq_en.q: How does SMOS measure soil moisture and ocean salinity?

faq_en.a: ESA’s Earth Explorer Missions are a set of missions dedicated to addressing major challenges identified by society. Five missions have been or are in operation (GOCE, SMOS, CryoSat, Swarm, Aeolus) and five further missions are approved for development  (EarthCARE, Biomass, FLEX, FORUM, Harmony).

eoportal.summary_en: Students were the flight controllers monitoring the satellite as well as the flight engineers providing scheduling functions and performance analysis.

eoportal.name: SOAR (Satellite for Orbital Aerodynamics Research)
eoportal.summary: The facility also contains moving sensors to measure the mass and energy of the particles within the oncoming beam of AO and those re-emitted and reflected from the surface of the material samples, allowing observation, characterisation, and mapping of the GSIs and associated scattering behaviour.

eoportal.summary_zh: 该设施还包含移动传感器，用于测量迎面而来的原子氧束中粒子的质量与能量，以及从材料样品表面再发射和反射的粒子，从而实现对气粒相互作用及相关散射行为的观测、表征和制图。
eoportal.summary_en: The facility also contains moving sensors to measure the mass and energy of the particles within the oncoming beam of AO and those re-emitted and reflected from the surface of the material samples, allowing observation, characterisation, and mapping of the GSIs and associated scattering behaviour.

eoportal.name: Space Rider
eoportal.summary: The mission will carry various payloads and return to Earth for refurbishment, with ESA, Thales Alenia Space, AVIO, Telespazio and ALTEC involved in its development.

faq.a: ESA’s Space Rider is a reusable, uncrewed space laboratory designed for scientific research, technology testing, and in-orbit servicing in LEO. It supports microgravity experiments, Earth and deep-space observations, and the validation of new space technologies.
faq.q: What is the purpose of ESA’s Space Rider mission?

faq.a: Space Rider can stay in orbit for over two months before returning to Earth with its payloads.
faq.q: How long can Space Rider stay in orbit?

faq.a: Each re-entry module will be designed for multiple missions, with up to two launches per year.
faq.q: How often can Space Rider be reused?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该任务将搭载多种载荷，并返回地球进行翻新维护。其开发涉及ESA、Thales Alenia Space、AVIO、Telespazio和ALTEC等机构。
eoportal.summary_en: The mission will carry various payloads and return to Earth for refurbishment, with ESA, Thales Alenia Space, AVIO, Telespazio and ALTEC involved in its development.

faq_zh.a: ESA的Space Rider是一个可重复使用的无人太空实验室，旨在为近地轨道上的科学研究、技术测试和在轨服务提供支持。它支持微重力实验、地球与深空观测，以及新太空技术的验证。
faq_zh.q: ESA的Space Rider任务的目的是什么？

faq_zh.a: Space Rider可以在轨停留两个多月，然后携带其载荷返回地球。
faq_zh.q: Space Rider能在轨道上停留多久？

faq_zh.a: 每个再入模块设计用于多次任务，每年可发射两次。
faq_zh.q: Space Rider可以重复使用多少次？

faq_en.a: ESA’s Space Rider is a reusable, uncrewed space laboratory designed for scientific research, technology testing, and in-orbit servicing in LEO. It supports microgravity experiments, Earth and deep-space observations, and the validation of new space technologies.
faq_en.q: What is the purpose of ESA’s Space Rider mission?

faq_en.a: Space Rider can stay in orbit for over two months before returning to Earth with its payloads.
faq_en.q: How long can Space Rider stay in orbit?

faq_en.a: Each re-entry module will be designed for multiple missions, with up to two launches per year.
faq_en.q: How often can Space Rider be reused?

eoportal.name: Space Weather Follow On at Lagrange Point L1 (SWFO-L1)
eoportal.summary: Space weather events can have a range of damaging effects on both in-orbit and ground-based infrastructure, so early warning and forecasting of space weather phenomena are highly valuable capabilities.

faq.a: SWFO-L1 provides data continuity for existing space weather monitoring and forecasting missions
faq.q: What is the aim of SWFO-L1?

faq.a: SWFO-L1 carries the compact coronagraph (CCOR), magnetometer (MAG), Suprathermal Ion Sensor (STIS) and Solar Wind Plasma Sensor (SWiPS)
faq.q: What instruments does SWFO-L1 carry?

faq.a: SWFO-L1 operates in a Lissajous orbit around Sun-Earth Lagrangian Point L1, about 1.5 million km from Earth.
faq.q: Where does SWFO-L1 operate?

eoportal.name_zh: 日地拉格朗日L1点空间天气后续任务 (SWFO-L1)
eoportal.summary_zh: 空间天气事件可能对在轨和地面基础设施造成多种破坏性影响，因此对空间天气现象进行早期预警和预报具有极高的价值。
eoportal.summary_en: Space weather events can have a range of damaging effects on both in-orbit and ground-based infrastructure, so early warning and forecasting of space weather phenomena are highly valuable capabilities.

faq_zh.a: SWFO-L1 旨在为现有的空间天气监测与预报任务提供数据延续性。
faq_zh.q: SWFO-L1 任务的目的是什么？

faq_zh.a: SWFO-L1 携带了紧凑型日冕仪 (CCOR)、磁力计 (MAG)、超热离子传感器 (STIS) 和太阳风等离子体传感器 (SWiPS)。
SWFO-L1 在围绕日地拉格朗日L1点的利萨如轨道上运行，距离地球约150万公里。

faq_en.a: SWFO-L1 provides data continuity for existing space weather monitoring and forecasting missions
faq_en.q: What is the aim of SWFO-L1?

faq_en.a: SWFO-L1 carries the compact coronagraph (CCOR), magnetometer (MAG), Suprathermal Ion Sensor (STIS) and Solar Wind Plasma Sensor (SWiPS)
faq_en.q: What instruments does SWFO-L1 carry?

faq_en.a: SWFO-L1 operates in a Lissajous orbit around Sun-Earth Lagrangian Point L1, about 1.5 million km from Earth.
faq_en.q: Where does SWFO-L1 operate?

space weather follow on at lagrange poin.display: Space Weather Follow On at Lagrange Point L1 (SWFO-L1)

eoportal.name: Space Weather Next (SW Next)
eoportal.summary: The two planned satellites, L1-A and L1-B, will provide early warnings for geomagnetic storms and advance modelling of coronal mass ejections.

faq.a: Space Weather Next aims to provide continuity and improvement to NOAA’s space weather observation capacity, succeeding the planned Space Weather Follow-On mission.
faq.q: What is the aim of Space Weather Next?

faq.a: The program consists of six different instrument types: three coronagraphs, two solar wind plasma sensors, two suprathermal ion sensors, two magnetometers, one X-ray flux monitor and one X-ray irradiance sensor.
faq.q: What instruments are included in Space Weather Next?

faq.a: Both Space Weather Next satellites, L1-A and L1-B, will operate from Lagrange Point 1, where the gravitational attractions of the Earth and Sun allow it to remain stable in space. They will be complemented by ESA’s Vigil spacecraft, operating from Lagrange Point 5.
faq.q: Where will the Space Weather Next satellites operate?

eoportal.name_zh: 空间天气下一代 (Space Weather Next, SW Next)
eoportal.agency_zh: ESA（欧空局）、NASA、NOAA（美国国家海洋和大气管理局）
eoportal.summary_zh: 该计划中的两颗卫星 L1-A 和 L1-B 将提供地磁暴预警，并推进对日冕物质抛射的建模研究。
eoportal.summary_en: The two planned satellites, L1-A and L1-B, will provide early warnings for geomagnetic storms and advance modelling of coronal mass ejections.

faq_zh.a: 空间天气下一代卫星旨在接替计划中的空间天气后续任务，为 NOAA 的空间天气观测能力提供连续性和改进。
faq_zh.q: 空间天气下一代卫星的目标是什么？

faq_zh.a: 该计划包含六种不同类型的仪器：三台日冕仪、两台太阳风等离子体传感器、两台超热离子传感器、两台磁力计、一台X射线通量监测器和一台X射线辐照度传感器。
faq_zh.q: 空间天气下一代卫星包含哪些仪器？

faq_zh.a: 两颗空间天气下一代卫星 L1-A 和 L1-B 将运行于拉格朗日L1点，该点受地球和太阳引力作用可在太空中保持稳定。它们将与运行于拉格朗日L5点的ESA Vigil 卫星形成互补。
faq_zh.q: 空间天气下一代卫星将在何处运行？

faq_en.a: Space Weather Next aims to provide continuity and improvement to NOAA’s space weather observation capacity, succeeding the planned Space Weather Follow-On mission.
faq_en.q: What is the aim of Space Weather Next?

faq_en.a: The program consists of six different instrument types: three coronagraphs, two solar wind plasma sensors, two suprathermal ion sensors, two magnetometers, one X-ray flux monitor and one X-ray irradiance sensor.
faq_en.q: What instruments are included in Space Weather Next?

faq_en.a: Both Space Weather Next satellites, L1-A and L1-B, will operate from Lagrange Point 1, where the gravitational attractions of the Earth and Sun allow it to remain stable in space. They will be complemented by ESA’s Vigil spacecraft, operating from Lagrange Point 5.
faq_en.q: Where will the Space Weather Next satellites operate?

eoportal.summary: SpaceEye-T forms the first component of SI’s plan to create a privately owned constellation of high resolution imaging satellites.

faq.a: SpaceEye-T is owned and operated by Satrec Initiative (SI), a Korean satellite manufacturer under the Hanwha Group conglomerate.
faq.q: Who owns SpaceEye-T?

faq.a: SpaceEye-T was launched on March 15, 2025, aboard SpaceX’s Transporter-13 Rideshare Mission.
faq.q: When was SpaceEye-T launched?

faq.a: SpaceEye-T provide a spatial resolution of up to 0.25 m in its panchromatic band.

faq_en.a: SpaceEye-T was launched on March 15, 2025, aboard SpaceX’s Transporter-13 Rideshare Mission.

faq_en.a: SpaceEye-T provide a spatial resolution of up to 0.25 m in its panchromatic band.

eoportal.summary: Launched in January 2026, it observes ultraviolet emissions from nearby M-dwarf stars to study stellar flares and radiation environments affecting exoplanet habitability.

eoportal.name: SPHEREx
taxonomy: Korea Astronomy and Space Science Institute
eoportal.agency: NASA-JPL, CalTech, Ball Aerospace, Korea Astronomy and Space Science Institute
eoportal.summary: SPHEREx was successfully launched on March 11, 2025.

faq.a: SPHEREx is managed by NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory (JPL) and operated in collaboration with CalTech, Ball Aerospace, and other partners.
faq.q: Who operates SPHEREx?

faq.a: SPHEREx will survey the entire sky in infrared light to study the origins of the universe, galaxy evolution, and the presence of water and organic molecules in planetary systems.
faq.q: What will SPHEREx be used for?

faq.a: Unlike telescopes like JWST that focus on deep, targeted observations, SPHEREx will conduct an all-sky spectral survey in the near-infrared. This wide coverage allows it to map large-scale cosmic structures and provide complementary data for other missions.
faq.q: What makes SPHEREx different from other space telescopes?

eoportal.agency_zh: NASA-JPL、加州理工学院、Ball Aerospace、韩国天文与空间科学研究所
eoportal.summary_zh: SPHEREx 于2025年3月11日成功发射。
eoportal.summary_en: SPHEREx was successfully launched on March 11, 2025.

faq_zh.a: SPHEREx 由 NASA 的喷气推进实验室（JPL）管理，并与加州理工学院、Ball Aerospace 及其他合作伙伴协作运营。
SPHEREx 将对整个天空进行红外波段巡天，以研究宇宙的起源、星系演化以及行星系统中水和有机分子的存在情况。
与像 JWST 这样专注于深度、定向观测的望远镜不同，SPHEREx 将在近红外波段进行全天光谱巡天。这种广泛的覆盖范围使其能够绘制大规模宇宙结构图，并为其他任务提供互补数据。
faq_zh.q: SPHEREx 与其他太空望远镜有何不同？

faq_en.a: SPHEREx is managed by NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory (JPL) and operated in collaboration with CalTech, Ball Aerospace, and other partners.
faq_en.q: Who operates SPHEREx?

faq_en.a: SPHEREx will survey the entire sky in infrared light to study the origins of the universe, galaxy evolution, and the presence of water and organic molecules in planetary systems.
faq_en.q: What will SPHEREx be used for?

faq_en.a: Unlike telescopes like JWST that focus on deep, targeted observations, SPHEREx will conduct an all-sky spectral survey in the near-infrared. This wide coverage allows it to map large-scale cosmic structures and provide complementary data for other missions.
faq_en.q: What makes SPHEREx different from other space telescopes?

eoportal.name: SpIRIT (Space Industry Responsive Intelligent Thermal)
eoportal.summary: The nanosatellite will operate with the High Energy Rapid Modular Ensemble of Satellites (HERMES) to observe X-ray and gamma-ray emissions from space.

faq.a: SpIRIT was developed by a consortium led by the Melbourne Space Laboratory at the University of Melbourne with the cooperation of the Italian Space Agency, and a number of Australian partners: Inovor Technologies, Neumann Space, Sitael Australia, and Nova Systems.
faq.q: Who developed SpIRIT?

faq.a: SpIRIT was launched on 1 December 2023 onboard a SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket from the Vandenberg Space Force Base in the United States.
faq.q: When is SpIRIT being launched?

faq.a: Whilst not strictly part of the HERMES-SP constellation, SpIRIT is part of the same HERMES mission. SpIRIT was launched on 1 December 2023 with the same X-ray and gamma-ray detector onboard all six satellites of the HERMES-SP constellation that will launch in 2024. SpIRIT will assist HERMES in determining the location of radiation events.
faq.q: Is SpIRIT part of the HERMES-SP constellation?

eoportal.agency_zh: ISA（以色列航天局）、墨尔本大学
eoportal.summary_zh: 这颗纳米卫星将与高能快速模块化卫星组合体（HERMES）协同工作，观测来自太空的X射线和伽马射线辐射。
eoportal.summary_en: The nanosatellite will operate with the High Energy Rapid Modular Ensemble of Satellites (HERMES) to observe X-ray and gamma-ray emissions from space.

faq_zh.a: SpIRIT卫星由墨尔本大学墨尔本空间实验室牵头的联盟开发，合作方包括意大利航天局以及多家澳大利亚合作伙伴：Inovor Technologies、Neumann Space、Sitael Australia和Nova Systems。
SpIRIT卫星于2023年12月1日由美国范登堡太空军基地搭载SpaceX猎鹰9号火箭发射升空。
虽然严格来说不属于HERMES-SP星座，但SpIRIT是同一HERMES任务的一部分。SpIRIT于2023年12月1日发射，搭载了与计划于2024年发射的HERMES-SP星座全部六颗卫星相同的X射线和伽马射线探测器。SpIRIT将协助HERMES确定辐射事件的位置。

faq_en.a: SpIRIT was developed by a consortium led by the Melbourne Space Laboratory at the University of Melbourne with the cooperation of the Italian Space Agency, and a number of Australian partners: Inovor Technologies, Neumann Space, Sitael Australia, and Nova Systems.
faq_en.q: Who developed SpIRIT?

faq_en.a: SpIRIT was launched on 1 December 2023 onboard a SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket from the Vandenberg Space Force Base in the United States.
faq_en.q: When is SpIRIT being launched?

faq_en.a: Whilst not strictly part of the HERMES-SP constellation, SpIRIT is part of the same HERMES mission. SpIRIT was launched on 1 December 2023 with the same X-ray and gamma-ray detector onboard all six satellites of the HERMES-SP constellation that will launch in 2024. SpIRIT will assist HERMES in determining the location of radiation events.
faq_en.q: Is SpIRIT part of the HERMES-SP constellation?

eoportal.name: SporeSat Nanosatellite Mission
eoportal.summary: U CubeSat of NASA/ARC to continue technology demonstrations of the PhoneSat CubeSat series.
eoportal.summary_zh: 由NASA/ARC主导的U型立方体卫星，旨在延续PhoneSat立方体卫星系列的技术验证工作。
eoportal.summary_en: U CubeSat of NASA/ARC to continue technology demonstrations of the PhoneSat CubeSat series.

eoportal.name: SPORT (Scintillation Prediction Observations Research Task)
taxonomy: Ion Density, Drift Velocity, and Temperature
eoportal.summary: International Space Station (ISS).

faq.a: SPORT is an effort to study the formation and evolution of equatorial plasma bubbles, which may in turn cause scintillation, a phenomenon that may disrupt radio communications and GPS tracking.
faq.q: What is the aim of SPORT?

faq.a: Nanosatellites are low-cost and can contain several recording instruments. Multiple versions of the same satellite can be launched into low-Earth orbit and satellites of these constellations may pass over a specific area more frequently than single-satellite missions.
faq.q: What are the advantages and applications of Nanosatellites?

faq.a: Scintillations are rapid fluctuations in the phase and amplitude of a wave, and in this context are caused by irregularities in ionospheric electron density in the Earth’s atmosphere. Equatorial plasma bubbles are an ionospheric phenomenon occurring near the Earth’s geomagnetic equator at nighttime.
faq.q: What are scintillations and equatorial plasma bubbles?

eoportal.name_zh: SPORT（闪烁预测观测研究任务）
eoportal.summary_zh: 国际空间站 (ISS)。
eoportal.summary_en: International Space Station (ISS).

faq_zh.a: SPORT 旨在研究赤道等离子体气泡的形成与演化，这些气泡进而可能导致闪烁现象，该现象可能干扰无线电通信和GPS跟踪。
faq_zh.q: SPORT 项目的目标是什么？

faq_zh.a: 纳卫星成本低，并可搭载多种记录仪器。多个相同型号的纳卫星可被发射至近地轨道，这类星座中的卫星比单卫星任务能更频繁地飞越特定区域。
faq_zh.q: 纳卫星有哪些优势和应用？

faq_zh.a: 闪烁是波相位和振幅的快速波动，在此语境下是由地球大气层中电离层电子密度的不规则性引起的。赤道等离子体气泡是一种发生在地球磁赤道附近夜间时段的电离层现象。
faq_zh.q: 什么是闪烁现象和赤道等离子体气泡？

faq_en.a: SPORT is an effort to study the formation and evolution of equatorial plasma bubbles, which may in turn cause scintillation, a phenomenon that may disrupt radio communications and GPS tracking.
faq_en.q: What is the aim of SPORT?

faq_en.a: Nanosatellites are low-cost and can contain several recording instruments. Multiple versions of the same satellite can be launched into low-Earth orbit and satellites of these constellations may pass over a specific area more frequently than single-satellite missions.
faq_en.q: What are the advantages and applications of Nanosatellites?

faq_en.a: Scintillations are rapid fluctuations in the phase and amplitude of a wave, and in this context are caused by irregularities in ionospheric electron density in the Earth’s atmosphere. Equatorial plasma bubbles are an ionospheric phenomenon occurring near the Earth’s geomagnetic equator at nighttime.
faq_en.q: What are scintillations and equatorial plasma bubbles?

eoportal.name: SPOT-1,-2,-3
taxonomy: Sea ice cover, edge and thickness
eoportal.summary: The satellites operated between 1986 and 2009.

faq.a: SPOT-1,-2 and -3 were launched from Kourou, French Guiana onboard the Ariane family of launch vehicles.
faq.q: Where were SPOT-1,-2 and -3 launched from?

faq.a: SPOT-1 and -2 had a design life of three years, and far exceeded the design life by extending its operation until satellite failure. SPOT-3 had a design life of four years, however failed after three years in orbit due to a stabilisation system malfunction.
faq.q: Did SPOT-1,-2 and -3 complete their mission?

faq.a: SPOT-1, -2 and -3 has contributed greatly to Earth Observation databases by producing low-cost high-resolution data for widespread use, and has contributed to various Earth monitoring activities.
faq.q: What has SPOT-1,-2 and -3 achieved?

eoportal.agency_zh: CNES (法国国家空间研究中心)
eoportal.summary_zh: 这些卫星在1986年至2009年间运行。
eoportal.summary_en: The satellites operated between 1986 and 2009.

faq_zh.a: SPOT-1,-2和-3卫星是在法属圭亚那库鲁发射场，由阿丽亚娜系列运载火箭发射升空。
faq_zh.q: SPOT-1,-2和-3卫星是从哪里发射的？

faq_zh.a: SPOT-1和-2的设计寿命为三年，通过延长运行直至卫星失效，其实际寿命远超设计寿命。SPOT-3的设计寿命为四年，但由于稳定系统故障，在轨运行三年后失效。
faq_zh.q: SPOT-1,-2和-3卫星是否完成了它们的任务？

faq_zh.a: SPOT-1,-2和-3卫星通过生产低成本、高分辨率的数据以供广泛使用，为地球观测数据库做出了巨大贡献，并促进了各项地球监测活动。
faq_zh.q: SPOT-1,-2和-3卫星取得了哪些成就？

faq_en.a: SPOT-1,-2 and -3 were launched from Kourou, French Guiana onboard the Ariane family of launch vehicles.
faq_en.q: Where were SPOT-1,-2 and -3 launched from?

faq_en.a: SPOT-1 and -2 had a design life of three years, and far exceeded the design life by extending its operation until satellite failure. SPOT-3 had a design life of four years, however failed after three years in orbit due to a stabilisation system malfunction.
faq_en.q: Did SPOT-1,-2 and -3 complete their mission?

faq_en.a: SPOT-1, -2 and -3 has contributed greatly to Earth Observation databases by producing low-cost high-resolution data for widespread use, and has contributed to various Earth monitoring activities.
faq_en.q: What has SPOT-1,-2 and -3 achieved?

eoportal.name: SPOT-5 (Satellite pour l’Observation de la Terre)
taxonomy: Ocean imagery and water leaving spectral radiance
Sea ice cover, edge and thickness
eoportal.summary: Upon the mission concluding in March 2015, the satellite was repurposed for an experimental commercial mission at a lower orbit, with this mission concluding in August 2015.

faq.a: SPOT-5 images in seven distinct bands. This includes five bands by HRG (one PAN, three MS and one SWIR), one by HRS (PAN), and four by Vegetation-2 (three MS and one SWIR), where the Vegetation-2 bands are identical to the bands imaged in HRG, with one MS band being the distinctly mapped band (B0 Blue).
faq.q: How many bands are used in SPOT-5?

faq.a: The spatial resolution of SPOT-5 varies depending on the band, with its highest resolution of 3.5 m (recorded using dual-image in the panchromatic band) and lowest resolution of 20 m (recorded using the shortwave infrared band).
faq.q: What is the spatial resolution of SPOT-5?

faq.a: SPOT-5 was launched in May 2002 by an Ariane-4 launch vehicle, with the first image data received 3 days later.
faq.q: When was SPOT-5 launched?

eoportal.agency_zh: CNES（法国国家空间研究中心）
eoportal.summary_zh: 该卫星任务于2015年3月结束后，被重新用于一项实验性的商业任务，在较低轨道运行，并于2015年8月结束该任务。
eoportal.summary_en: Upon the mission concluding in March 2015, the satellite was repurposed for an experimental commercial mission at a lower orbit, with this mission concluding in August 2015.

faq_zh.a: SPOT-5 成像使用七个不同的波段。这包括 HRG 传感器拍摄的五个波段（一个全色、三个多光谱和一个短波红外）、HRS 传感器拍摄的一个波段（全色）以及 Vegetation-2 传感器拍摄的四个波段（三个多光谱和一个短波红外）。其中，Vegetation-2 的波段与 HRG 拍摄的波段相同，但有一个多光谱波段（B0 蓝光波段）是其独有的。
SPOT-5 的空间分辨率因波段而异，其最高分辨率为3.5米（使用全色波段双影像合成技术记录），最低分辨率为20米（使用短波红外波段记录）。
faq_zh.q: SPOT-5 的空间分辨率是多少？

faq_zh.a: SPOT-5 于2002年5月由阿里安-4运载火箭发射升空，三天后接收到首幅影像数据。

faq_en.a: SPOT-5 images in seven distinct bands. This includes five bands by HRG (one PAN, three MS and one SWIR), one by HRS (PAN), and four by Vegetation-2 (three MS and one SWIR), where the Vegetation-2 bands are identical to the bands imaged in HRG, with one MS band being the distinctly mapped band (B0 Blue).
faq_en.q: How many bands are used in SPOT-5?

faq_en.a: The spatial resolution of SPOT-5 varies depending on the band, with its highest resolution of 3.5 m (recorded using dual-image in the panchromatic band) and lowest resolution of 20 m (recorded using the shortwave infrared band).
faq_en.q: What is the spatial resolution of SPOT-5?

faq_en.a: SPOT-5 was launched in May 2002 by an Ariane-4 launch vehicle, with the first image data received 3 days later.
faq_en.q: When was SPOT-5 launched?

spot-5 (satellite pour l’observation de.display: SPOT-5 (Satellite pour l’Observation de la Terre)

eoportal.name: SPOT-6 and SPOT-7 (Azersky)
eoportal.summary: The constellation preserves and extends upon the previous SPOT 1-5 missions.

faq.a: SPOT-6 &-7 collect images from over 3 million km2 of land per satellite per day. SPOT-7 has ceased operations since 17 March 2023.
faq.q: How much data do SPOT-6 & -7 collect each day?

faq.a: Data gathered from SPOT-6 & -7 (SPOT-7 has ceased operations since 17 March 2023) is used for understanding land surface processes and topography. Applications vary from digital terrain modelling systems to urban infrastructure development plans, with a wide-range of professionals analysing the data to primarily observe environments.
faq.q: What are the applications of the data gathered by SPOT-6 & -7?

faq.a: SPOT-6 & -7 have five bands. These include one panchromatic band, one Near infrared band, and three multispectral bands (Blue, Red, Green). SPOT-7 has ceased operations since 17 March 2023.
faq.q: How many bands do SPOT-6 & SPOT-7 have?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该星座延续并拓展了此前SPOT 1至5号系列任务的能力。
eoportal.summary_en: The constellation preserves and extends upon the previous SPOT 1-5 missions.

faq_zh.a: SPOT-6 和 SPOT-7 每天每颗卫星可采集超过300万平方公里的陆地影像。SPOT-7 自2023年3月17日起已停止运行。
faq_zh.q: SPOT-6 和 SPOT-7 每天采集多少数据？

faq_zh.a: SPOT-6 和 SPOT-7（SPOT-7 自2023年3月17日起已停止运行）采集的数据主要用于理解地表过程和地形。应用范围广泛，从数字地形建模系统到城市基础设施发展规划，各类专业人员通过分析数据来主要观测环境状况。
faq_zh.q: SPOT-6 和 SPOT-7 采集的数据有哪些应用？

faq_zh.a: SPOT-6 和 SPOT-7 拥有五个波段。包括一个全色波段、一个近红外波段和三个多光谱波段（蓝、红、绿）。SPOT-7 自2023年3月17日起已停止运行。
faq_zh.q: SPOT-6 和 SPOT-7 有多少个波段？

faq_en.a: SPOT-6 &-7 collect images from over 3 million km2 of land per satellite per day. SPOT-7 has ceased operations since 17 March 2023.
faq_en.q: How much data do SPOT-6 & -7 collect each day?

faq_en.a: Data gathered from SPOT-6 & -7 (SPOT-7 has ceased operations since 17 March 2023) is used for understanding land surface processes and topography. Applications vary from digital terrain modelling systems to urban infrastructure development plans, with a wide-range of professionals analysing the data to primarily observe environments.
faq_en.q: What are the applications of the data gathered by SPOT-6 & -7?

faq_en.a: SPOT-6 & -7 have five bands. These include one panchromatic band, one Near infrared band, and three multispectral bands (Blue, Red, Green). SPOT-7 has ceased operations since 17 March 2023.
faq_en.q: How many bands do SPOT-6 & SPOT-7 have?

eoportal.summary: The mission ended in August 2024.
eoportal.summary_zh: 该任务于2024年8月结束。
eoportal.summary_en: The mission ended in August 2024.

eoportal.summary: It was launched aboard PSLV-C18 in October 2011.
eoportal.summary_zh: 该卫星于2011年10月由PSLV-C18运载火箭发射升空。
eoportal.summary_en: It was launched aboard PSLV-C18 in October 2011.

eoportal.name: SSTL S1-4 Satellite Vision-1
eoportal.summary: Data capacity from SSTL S1-4 is leased to several entities, and is also delivered as a sub-1 m imagery service known as Vision-1 used for applications including defence, security, maritime and agriculture.

faq.a: SSTL S1-4/Vision-1 aims to provide land imagery to assist with disaster monitoring, along with agile approaches allowing for imagery to assist with urban planning and natural resource management.
faq.q: What are the main objectives of SSTL S1-4/Vision-1?

faq.a: SSTL S1-4/Vision-1 has a spectral resolution of five spectral bands, one being panchromatic, one near-infrared and the other three as part of the visible spectrum.
faq.q: What is the spectral resolution of SSTL S1-4/Vision-1?

faq.a: Yes, the collected data is provided free for various stakeholders, enabling cutting-edge research and access to data within reasonable timeframes.
faq.q: Is the data collected from SSTL S1-4/Vision-1 widely available?

eoportal.summary_zh: SSTL S1-4卫星的数据容量被租赁给多家机构，同时也以名为Vision-1的亚米级影像服务提供，用于国防、安全、海事和农业等应用。
eoportal.summary_en: Data capacity from SSTL S1-4 is leased to several entities, and is also delivered as a sub-1 m imagery service known as Vision-1 used for applications including defence, security, maritime and agriculture.

faq_zh.a: SSTL S1-4/Vision-1旨在提供陆地影像以辅助灾害监测，并通过敏捷的方式提供影像，以协助城市规划和自然资源管理。
faq_zh.q: SSTL S1-4/Vision-1的主要目标是什么？

faq_zh.a: SSTL S1-4/Vision-1具有五个波段的光谱分辨率，其中一个为全色波段，一个为近红外波段，另外三个属于可见光谱的一部分。
faq_zh.q: SSTL S1-4/Vision-1的光谱分辨率是多少？

faq_zh.a: 是的，收集的数据免费提供给各利益相关方，以支持前沿研究并在合理的时间框架内提供数据访问。
faq_zh.q: 从SSTL S1-4/Vision-1收集的数据是否广泛可用？

faq_en.a: SSTL S1-4/Vision-1 aims to provide land imagery to assist with disaster monitoring, along with agile approaches allowing for imagery to assist with urban planning and natural resource management.
faq_en.q: What are the main objectives of SSTL S1-4/Vision-1?

faq_en.a: SSTL S1-4/Vision-1 has a spectral resolution of five spectral bands, one being panchromatic, one near-infrared and the other three as part of the visible spectrum.
faq_en.q: What is the spectral resolution of SSTL S1-4/Vision-1?

faq_en.a: Yes, the collected data is provided free for various stakeholders, enabling cutting-edge research and access to data within reasonable timeframes.
faq_en.q: Is the data collected from SSTL S1-4/Vision-1 widely available?

eoportal.name: SSTL-Precision
eoportal.summary: The mission employs novel sensor architecture and high agility, with feasibility for constellation deployment.

faq.a: Precision is in development by Surrey Satellite Technology Ltd. (SSTL)
faq.q: Who developed the Precision satellite?

faq.a: SSTL-Precision is a multispectral, high-resolution satellite that supports high priority imaging applications including disaster monitoring, surveillance, urban planning, and risk assessments.
faq.q: What will SSTL-Precision be used for?

faq.a: Precision is intended to be operated in a constellation, providing high spatio-temporal resolutions to support high quality imagery. SSTL are yet to confirm an SSTL-Precision launch.
faq.q: How many SSTL-Precision satellites are there?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该任务采用新型传感器架构和高机动性，具备星座部署的可行性。
eoportal.summary_en: The mission employs novel sensor architecture and high agility, with feasibility for constellation deployment.

faq_zh.a: Precision卫星由萨里卫星技术有限公司（SSTL）开发。
SSTL-Precision是一颗多光谱、高分辨率卫星，支持包括灾害监测、监视、城市规划和风险评估在内的高优先级成像应用。
Precision卫星旨在以星座形式运行，提供高时空分辨率以支持高质量图像。SSTL尚未确认SSTL-Precision的发射计划。

faq_en.a: Precision is in development by Surrey Satellite Technology Ltd. (SSTL)
faq_en.q: Who developed the Precision satellite?

faq_en.a: SSTL-Precision is a multispectral, high-resolution satellite that supports high priority imaging applications including disaster monitoring, surveillance, urban planning, and risk assessments.
faq_en.q: What will SSTL-Precision be used for?

faq_en.a: Precision is intended to be operated in a constellation, providing high spatio-temporal resolutions to support high quality imagery. SSTL are yet to confirm an SSTL-Precision launch.
faq_en.q: How many SSTL-Precision satellites are there?

eoportal.name: Starlette / Stella
eoportal.summary: It is these two contributing factors to Earth’s gravitational field that the satellites are studying.

faq.a: STARLETTE and Stella both have an outer shell that consists of 20 triangular aluminium plates each containing three laser retroreflectors, for a total of 60 retroreflectors.
faq.q: How many retroreflectors did STARLETTE and Stella have?

faq.a: STARLETTE and Stella are 24 cm in diameter, around the same size as a basketball.
faq.q: How big are STARLETTE and Stella?

eoportal.agency_zh: CNES (法国国家空间研究中心)
eoportal.summary_zh: 这两颗卫星旨在研究地球引力场中的这两个主要贡献因素。
eoportal.summary_en: It is these two contributing factors to Earth’s gravitational field that the satellites are studying.

faq_zh.a: STARLETTE 和 Stella 的外层均由20块三角形铝板构成，每块铝板内嵌三个激光反射器，总共60个反射器。
faq_zh.q: STARLETTE 和 Stella 有多少个激光反射器？

faq_zh.a: STARLETTE 和 Stella 的直径为24厘米，大约与一个篮球相当。
faq_zh.q: STARLETTE 和 Stella 有多大？

faq_en.a: STARLETTE and Stella both have an outer shell that consists of 20 triangular aluminium plates each containing three laser retroreflectors, for a total of 60 retroreflectors.
faq_en.q: How many retroreflectors did STARLETTE and Stella have?

faq_en.a: STARLETTE and Stella are 24 cm in diameter, around the same size as a basketball.
faq_en.q: How big are STARLETTE and Stella?

eoportal.name: Starlink Satellite Constellation
eoportal.summary: The first satellite was launched in May 2019 and, since then, more than 7,000 other satellites have been deployed to provide internet coverage to over one hundred countries, with the aim to achieve global mobile broadband coverage.
eoportal.name_zh: 星链卫星星座 (Starlink Satellite Constellation)
eoportal.summary_zh: 首颗卫星于2019年5月发射升空。此后，已有超过7,000颗卫星被部署，为一百多个国家提供互联网覆盖，其目标是实现全球移动宽带覆盖。
eoportal.summary_en: The first satellite was launched in May 2019 and, since then, more than 7,000 other satellites have been deployed to provide internet coverage to over one hundred countries, with the aim to achieve global mobile broadband coverage.

eoportal.summary: Orbit: near-circular orbit, altitude of about 750 km of final orbit, inclination = 85º.
eoportal.summary_zh: 轨道：近圆形轨道，最终轨道高度约750公里，轨道倾角85º。
eoportal.summary_en: Orbit: near-circular orbit, altitude of about 750 km of final orbit, inclination = 85º.

eoportal.name: Stingray VLEO Constellation
eoportal.summary: By operating in VLEO, the Stingray satellites will be closer to Earth than most other satellites, and will be capable of delivering the highest resolution imagery.

faq.a: Very Low Earth Orbit (VLEO) is the region between approximately 100 - 450 km in altitude above the Earth. EOI Space’s satellite constellation is planned to orbit at 250 km in altitude.
faq.q: What is VLEO?

faq.a: The Stingray VLEO constellation is under development by Earth Observant Inc. (EOI) Space.
faq.q: Who built the VLEO constellation?

eoportal.summary_zh: 通过在甚低地球轨道（VLEO）运行，Stingray卫星将比大多数其他卫星更接近地球，并能够提供最高分辨率的图像。
eoportal.summary_en: By operating in VLEO, the Stingray satellites will be closer to Earth than most other satellites, and will be capable of delivering the highest resolution imagery.

faq_zh.a: 甚低地球轨道（VLEO）是指地球表面上方约100至450公里高度的区域。EOI Space的卫星星座计划在250公里的轨道高度运行。
Stingray VLEO星座由地球观测公司（EOI）Space开发。

faq_en.a: Very Low Earth Orbit (VLEO) is the region between approximately 100 - 450 km in altitude above the Earth. EOI Space’s satellite constellation is planned to orbit at 250 km in altitude.
faq_en.q: What is VLEO?

faq_en.a: The Stingray VLEO constellation is under development by Earth Observant Inc. (EOI) Space.
faq_en.q: Who built the VLEO constellation?

eoportal.name: STORK Constellation
eoportal.summary: Once completed, the STORK constellation will collect multispectral medium-resolution imagery and Earth observation data, as well as host Earth observation technology payloads for agricultural and energy customers.

faq.a: The STORK 3U CubeSats features SatREvolutions' Vision-300 imager with a ground resolution of up to 5 m.
faq.q: What is the resolution of the stork satellite?

faq.a: STORK-1 and -2 are operational. However, SatRev has not publicly disclosed any Earth imagery that has been transmitted from the STORK satellites.
faq.q: Which STORK satellites are operational?

faq.a: The satellite was intended to be placed into its target orbit by the space tug operated by Momentus. The separation of the STORK-7 satellite failed, and was irretrievably lost on re-entry. Momentus does not believe STORK-7 was released from the third-party deployer system.
faq.q: Why did the STORK-7 satellite launch fail?

eoportal.summary_zh: 建成后，STORK星座将采集多光谱中分辨率影像和地球观测数据，并为农业和能源客户搭载地球观测技术有效载荷。
eoportal.summary_en: Once completed, the STORK constellation will collect multispectral medium-resolution imagery and Earth observation data, as well as host Earth observation technology payloads for agricultural and energy customers.

STORK-1和STORK-2正在运行。然而，SatRev尚未公开发布从这些卫星传输的任何地球影像。
该卫星原计划由Momentus运营的太空拖船送入目标轨道。STORK-7卫星分离失败，并在再入时无法挽回地丢失。Momentus认为STORK-7未能从第三方部署系统中释放。
faq_zh.q: STORK-7卫星发射失败的原因是什么？

faq_en.q: Why did the STORK-7 satellite launch fail?

eoportal.name: Suomi NPP (National Polar-orbiting Partnership)
taxonomy: Cloud type, amount and cloud top temperature
Ocean imagery and water leaving spectral radiance
Downward long-wave irradiance at Earth surface
Precipitation intensity at the surface (liquid or solid)
Sea ice cover, edge and thickness

Upwelling (Outgoing) long-wave radiation at Earth surface
eoportal.summary: Suomi NPP is operated by NASA and the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA), with the aim of monitoring the environment on Earth and the planet’s climate.

faq.a: Suomi-NPP is a meteorological satellite deployed by the National Oceanographic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) and National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA) designed to provide data continuity to previous NASA Earth Observing System (EOS) missions and to demonstrate new sensor capabilities in Earth observation.
faq.q: What does the Suomi NPP do?

faq.a: No, the orbit of Suomi NPP is sun-synchronous in a near-circular polar orbit with an altitude of 824 km, inclination of 98.74°, and orbital period of 101 minutes.
faq.q: Is Suomi NPP satellite orbit geostationary?

faq.a: As of September 2022, Suomi NPP is operational since it’s launch in 2011, and its operational life has been extended to 2026.
faq.q: Is Suomi NPP operational?

eoportal.name_zh: Suomi NPP（国家极轨伙伴关系）
eoportal.summary_zh: Suomi NPP卫星由NASA和美国国家海洋和大气管理局（NOAA）共同运营，旨在监测地球环境和行星气候。
eoportal.summary_en: Suomi NPP is operated by NASA and the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA), with the aim of monitoring the environment on Earth and the planet’s climate.

faq_zh.a: Suomi NPP是由美国国家海洋和大气管理局（NOAA）和美国国家航空航天局（NASA）部署的一颗气象卫星，旨在为NASA先前的地球观测系统（EOS）任务提供数据连续性，并演示地球观测领域的新传感器能力。
不是，Suomi NPP的轨道是近圆形的太阳同步极地轨道，高度824公里，倾角98.74°，轨道周期101分钟。
faq_zh.q: Suomi NPP卫星是地球静止轨道吗？

faq_zh.a: 截至2022年9月，Suomi NPP自2011年发射以来一直在轨运行，其运行寿命已延长至2026年。

faq_en.a: Suomi-NPP is a meteorological satellite deployed by the National Oceanographic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) and National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA) designed to provide data continuity to previous NASA Earth Observing System (EOS) missions and to demonstrate new sensor capabilities in Earth observation.
faq_en.q: What does the Suomi NPP do?

faq_en.a: No, the orbit of Suomi NPP is sun-synchronous in a near-circular polar orbit with an altitude of 824 km, inclination of 98.74°, and orbital period of 101 minutes.
faq_en.q: Is Suomi NPP satellite orbit geostationary?

faq_en.a: As of September 2022, Suomi NPP is operational since it’s launch in 2011, and its operational life has been extended to 2026.
faq_en.q: Is Suomi NPP operational?

Launched in November 2013, Swarm is a constellation of three satellites operated by the European Space Agency (ESA) with the purpose of mapping Earth’s magnetic field.

faq.a: Swarm is measuring the Earth’s magnetic field strength and direction, as well as the forces of solar radiation and wind experienced by satellites in Low Earth Orbit (LEO).
faq.q: What is Swarm measuring?

faq.a: Swarm-A and Swarm-C travel in the same polar orbit parallel to one another, whereas Swarm-B travels in a slightly higher and less inclined orbit.
faq.q: What are the differences between the orbits of Swarm?

faq.a: One notable discovery made in 2022 from Swarm data was that at the outer edge of the core, there is a magnetic field that oscillates every seven years and is travelling westward at 1500 km/year.
faq.q: What has Swarm discovered?

eoportal.agency_zh: ESA（欧洲空间局）、CNES（法国国家空间研究中心）、CSA（加拿大航天局）
eoportal.summary_zh: Swarm是一个由欧洲空间局（ESA）运营的三星星座，于2013年11月发射，旨在测绘地球磁场。

Launched in November 2013, Swarm is a constellation of three satellites operated by the European Space Agency (ESA) with the purpose of mapping Earth’s magnetic field.

faq_zh.a: Swarm正在测量地球磁场的强度和方向，以及低地球轨道（LEO）卫星所受到的太阳辐射和太阳风的力。
Swarm-A和Swarm-C在同一极地轨道上平行飞行，而Swarm-B则在一个稍高且倾角略小的轨道上运行。
2022年，Swarm数据带来的一项显著发现是，在（地球）地核的外缘存在一个每七年振荡一次的磁场，并以每年1500公里的速度向西移动。

faq_en.a: Swarm is measuring the Earth’s magnetic field strength and direction, as well as the forces of solar radiation and wind experienced by satellites in Low Earth Orbit (LEO).
faq_en.q: What is Swarm measuring?

faq_en.a: Swarm-A and Swarm-C travel in the same polar orbit parallel to one another, whereas Swarm-B travels in a slightly higher and less inclined orbit.
faq_en.q: What are the differences between the orbits of Swarm?

faq_en.a: One notable discovery made in 2022 from Swarm data was that at the outer edge of the core, there is a magnetic field that oscillates every seven years and is travelling westward at 1500 km/year.
faq_en.q: What has Swarm discovered?

eoportal.name: SWIRSAT (LatConnect60)
eoportal.summary: It aims to lower the costs of carbon emission data acquisition and provide spatial resolution five times higher than the current market standard.

faq.a: The constellation will consist of three satellites.
faq.q: How many satellites are in the SWIRSAT mission?

faq.a: The primary aim of the SWIRSAT mission is to reduce the costs of carbon emission data acquisition. Its data also has wider applications in precision agriculture and disaster monitoring.
faq.q: What is the aim of SWIRSAT?

faq.a: The mission consists of two instruments: Chameleon SWIR and MultiScape200. Chameleon SWIR is a nadir scanning multispectral radiometer that will measure CH4 and CO2 atmospheric column mole fractions. MultiScape200 is a high resolution optical payload that will provide high resolution imagery base maps.
faq.q: What instruments will SWIRSAT carry?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该卫星旨在降低碳排放数据获取成本，并提供比当前市场标准高五倍的空间分辨率。
eoportal.summary_en: It aims to lower the costs of carbon emission data acquisition and provide spatial resolution five times higher than the current market standard.

faq_zh.a: 该星座将由三颗卫星组成。
SWIRSAT任务的主要目标是降低碳排放数据获取成本。其数据在精准农业和灾害监测方面也有更广泛的应用。
该任务包含两种仪器：Chameleon SWIR和MultiScape200。Chameleon SWIR是一个天底扫描多光谱辐射计，用于测量CH4和CO2大气柱摩尔分数。MultiScape200是一个高分辨率光学载荷，将提供高分辨率影像底图。

faq_en.a: The constellation will consist of three satellites.
faq_en.q: How many satellites are in the SWIRSAT mission?

faq_en.a: The primary aim of the SWIRSAT mission is to reduce the costs of carbon emission data acquisition. Its data also has wider applications in precision agriculture and disaster monitoring.
faq_en.q: What is the aim of SWIRSAT?

faq_en.a: The mission consists of two instruments: Chameleon SWIR and MultiScape200. Chameleon SWIR is a nadir scanning multispectral radiometer that will measure CH4 and CO2 atmospheric column mole fractions. MultiScape200 is a high resolution optical payload that will provide high resolution imagery base maps.
faq_en.q: What instruments will SWIRSAT carry?

eoportal.name: SWOT (Surface Water Ocean Topography)
eoportal.summary: The data collected by these surveys could improve ocean circulation models and weather predictions, while aiding in freshwater management around the world.

faq.a: Designed to make the first-ever global survey of Earth's surface water, the Surface Water and Ocean Topography (SWOT) satellite will collect detailed measurements of how water bodies on Earth change over time.
faq.q: What is the purpose of the SWOT mission?

faq.a: SWOT is a collaboration between NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory (JPL) and the French Space Agency (Centre National d'Etudes Spatiales, CNES), with contributions from the Canadian Space Agency (CSA) and the United Kingdom Space Agency (UKSA).
faq.q: Which countries collaborated with NASA for the SWOT mission?

faq.a: SWOT makes use of KaRIn, DORIS, GPSP, LRA and AMR-S
faq.q: What instruments does SWOT employ?

eoportal.name_zh: SWOT (地表水与海洋地形卫星)
eoportal.summary_zh: 该卫星收集的数据将改进海洋环流模型和天气预报能力，并有助于全球淡水资源管理。
eoportal.summary_en: The data collected by these surveys could improve ocean circulation models and weather predictions, while aiding in freshwater management around the world.

faq_zh.a: 地表水与海洋地形卫星（SWOT）旨在对地球地表水进行首次全球调查，收集地球水体随时间变化的详细测量数据。
SWOT任务是NASA喷气推进实验室（JPL）与法国国家空间研究中心（CNES）的合作项目，加拿大航天局（CSA）和英国航天局（UKSA）也参与了贡献。
faq_zh.q: 哪些国家与NASA合作了SWOT任务？

faq_zh.a: SWOT使用了KaRIN、DORIS、GPSP、LRA和AMR-S等仪器。

faq_en.a: Designed to make the first-ever global survey of Earth's surface water, the Surface Water and Ocean Topography (SWOT) satellite will collect detailed measurements of how water bodies on Earth change over time.
faq_en.q: What is the purpose of the SWOT mission?

faq_en.a: SWOT is a collaboration between NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory (JPL) and the French Space Agency (Centre National d'Etudes Spatiales, CNES), with contributions from the Canadian Space Agency (CSA) and the United Kingdom Space Agency (UKSA).
faq_en.q: Which countries collaborated with NASA for the SWOT mission?

faq_en.a: SWOT makes use of KaRIn, DORIS, GPSP, LRA and AMR-S
faq_en.q: What instruments does SWOT employ?
