eoportal.name: Tandem-L Interferometric Radar Mission
taxonomy: Ocean imagery and water leaving spectral radiance
Sea ice cover, edge and thickness
eoportal.summary: Inspired by TanDEM-X, the mission is proposed to consist of two identical L-band radar satellites used to investigate dynamic processes in the biosphere, cryosphere, geosphere and hydrosphere.

faq.a: The Tandem-L mission proposes to feature two identical L-band radar satellites.
faq.q: How many satellites are in the Tandem-L mission?

faq.a: Tandem-L will acquire 26 preliminary geophysical products regarding globally dynamic process of the Earth’s surface. Some of the key mission goals in the global measurement of forest biomass, recording of earth’s surface deformations for earthquake research and risk analysis, large scale observation of ocean currents and sea ice drift, and quantification of glacier movements and melting processes.
faq.q: What are the mission objectives for Tandem-L?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该任务受TanDEM-X启发，提议由两颗相同的L波段雷达卫星组成，用于研究生物圈、冰冻圈、岩石圈和水圈中的动态过程。
eoportal.summary_en: Inspired by TanDEM-X, the mission is proposed to consist of two identical L-band radar satellites used to investigate dynamic processes in the biosphere, cryosphere, geosphere and hydrosphere.

faq_zh.a: Tandem-L任务提议由两颗相同的L波段雷达卫星组成。
Tandem-L将获取26项关于地球表面全球动态过程的初步地球物理产品。其关键任务目标包括全球森林生物量的测量、记录地表形变用于地震研究与风险分析、大规模观测洋流与海冰漂移，以及量化冰川移动与融化过程。

faq_en.a: The Tandem-L mission proposes to feature two identical L-band radar satellites.
faq_en.q: How many satellites are in the Tandem-L mission?

faq_en.a: Tandem-L will acquire 26 preliminary geophysical products regarding globally dynamic process of the Earth’s surface. Some of the key mission goals in the global measurement of forest biomass, recording of earth’s surface deformations for earthquake research and risk analysis, large scale observation of ocean currents and sea ice drift, and quantification of glacier movements and melting processes.
faq_en.q: What are the mission objectives for Tandem-L?

eoportal.name: TANGO (Twin Anthropogenic Greenhouse Gas Observers)
eoportal.summary: The mission aims to identify and measure Carbon Dioxide (CO2) and Methane (CH4) emissions at the scale of individual power plants and industrial areas.

faq.a: The TANGO mission has been designed by a consortium of Innovative Solutions in Space (ISISpace), the Netherlands Institute for Space Research (SRON), the Royal Netherlands Meteorological Institute (KNMI) and the Netherlands Organisation for Applied Scientific Research (TNO).
faq.q: Who has developed the TANGO program?

faq.a: TANGO aims to observe and measure carbon dioxide and methane emissions at the scale of individual power plants and industrial facilities.
faq.q: What is the aim of the TANGO mission?

faq.a: The TANGO CubeSats will each carry one of two all-aluminium pushbroom hyperspectral imaging spectrometers, developed by TNO.
faq.q: What instruments are used by the TANGO mission?

eoportal.name_zh: TANGO（双星人为温室气体观测器）
eoportal.summary_zh: 该任务旨在识别和测量单个发电厂及工业区规模的二氧化碳（CO2）和甲烷（CH4）排放。
eoportal.summary_en: The mission aims to identify and measure Carbon Dioxide (CO2) and Methane (CH4) emissions at the scale of individual power plants and industrial areas.

faq_zh.a: TANGO任务由空间创新解决方案公司（ISISpace）、荷兰空间研究所（SRON）、荷兰皇家气象研究所（KNMI）和荷兰应用科学研究组织（TNO）组成的联盟设计。
TANGO旨在观测和测量单个发电厂及工业设施规模的二氧化碳和甲烷排放。
每颗TANGO立方星将各搭载一台由TNO开发的全铝推扫式高光谱成像光谱仪。

faq_en.a: The TANGO mission has been designed by a consortium of Innovative Solutions in Space (ISISpace), the Netherlands Institute for Space Research (SRON), the Royal Netherlands Meteorological Institute (KNMI) and the Netherlands Organisation for Applied Scientific Research (TNO).
faq_en.q: Who has developed the TANGO program?

faq_en.a: TANGO aims to observe and measure carbon dioxide and methane emissions at the scale of individual power plants and industrial facilities.
faq_en.q: What is the aim of the TANGO mission?

faq_en.a: The TANGO CubeSats will each carry one of two all-aluminium pushbroom hyperspectral imaging spectrometers, developed by TNO.
faq_en.q: What instruments are used by the TANGO mission?

eoportal.name: TanSat (Chinese Carbon Dioxide Observation Satellite Mission)
taxonomy: Cloud type, amount and cloud top temperature
eoportal.summary: The Chinese Academy of Sciences (CAS) undertakes the leading role of funding in the satellite development with two scientific instruments designed and manufactured by Changchun Institute of Optics, Fine Mechanics and Physics/Chinese Academy of Sciences (CIOMP/CAS).

faq.a: The TanSat mission is complete, ending operations in January 2020 after a three-year operational life.
faq.q: Is TanSat still operational?

faq.a: TanSat measured the atmospheric column-averaged dry air mole fraction of carbon dioxide with a precision of 1% to improve the understanding of the global carbon dioxide distribution and its contribution to climate change.
faq.q: What did TanSat measure?

faq.a: TanSat was launched on December 21, 2016 on a Chinese Long March 2D vehicle from the Jiuquan Satellite Launch Centre in China.
faq.q: When was TanSat launched?

eoportal.name_zh: 碳卫星（TanSat，中国二氧化碳观测卫星任务）
eoportal.summary_zh: 中国科学院（CAS）在卫星研发中承担主要资金支持角色，其搭载的两台科学仪器由中科院长春光学精密机械与物理研究所（CIOMP/CAS）设计并制造。
eoportal.summary_en: The Chinese Academy of Sciences (CAS) undertakes the leading role of funding in the satellite development with two scientific instruments designed and manufactured by Changchun Institute of Optics, Fine Mechanics and Physics/Chinese Academy of Sciences (CIOMP/CAS).

faq_zh.a: 碳卫星任务已完成，在经历了三年的在轨运行后，于2020年1月结束了运行。
faq_zh.q: 碳卫星（TanSat）目前还在运行吗？

faq_zh.a: 碳卫星测量了大气柱平均干空气二氧化碳摩尔分数，精度为1%，旨在增进对全球二氧化碳分布及其对气候变化贡献的理解。
碳卫星于2016年12月21日在中国酒泉卫星发射中心由长征二号丁运载火箭发射升空。
faq_zh.q: 碳卫星（TanSat）是什么时候发射的？

faq_en.a: The TanSat mission is complete, ending operations in January 2020 after a three-year operational life.
faq_en.q: Is TanSat still operational?

faq_en.a: TanSat measured the atmospheric column-averaged dry air mole fraction of carbon dioxide with a precision of 1% to improve the understanding of the global carbon dioxide distribution and its contribution to climate change.
faq_en.q: What did TanSat measure?

faq_en.a: TanSat was launched on December 21, 2016 on a Chinese Long March 2D vehicle from the Jiuquan Satellite Launch Centre in China.
faq_en.q: When was TanSat launched?

tansat (chinese carbon dioxide observati.display: TanSat (Chinese Carbon Dioxide Observation Satellite Mission)

eoportal.name: TDX (TanDEM-X)
taxonomy: Ocean imagery and water leaving spectral radiance
Sea ice cover, edge and thickness
eoportal.summary: The satellite  launched in June, 2010 and constructs three-dimensional images of Earth’s surface.

faq.a: TanDEM-X (TDX) has been operational since 2010.
faq.q: How long has TanDEM-X been operational?

faq.a: TanDEM-X (TDX) is a joint mission by the German Space Centre (DLR) and Airbus Space and Defence.
faq.q: Which company/government agency owns TanDEM-X?

faq.a: TanDEM-X (TDX) performs Earth observation using the X-band Synthetic Aperture Radar. It records data concerning land, ocean and snow & ice processes such as albedo and reflectance, ocean topography and snow cover.
faq.q: What does TanDEM-X do in space?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该卫星于2010年6月发射，能够构建地球表面的三维图像。
eoportal.summary_en: The satellite  launched in June, 2010 and constructs three-dimensional images of Earth’s surface.

faq_zh.a: TanDEM-X (TDX) 自2010年起一直在轨运行。
TanDEM-X (TDX) 是德国宇航中心 (DLR) 与空中客车防务与航天公司 (Airbus Space and Defence) 的联合任务。
faq_zh.q: 哪家公司/政府机构拥有 TanDEM-X？

faq_zh.a: TanDEM-X (TDX) 使用X波段合成孔径雷达进行对地观测。它记录了关于陆地、海洋以及冰雪过程的数据，例如反照率和反射率、海洋地形和积雪覆盖范围。
faq_zh.q: TanDEM-X 在太空中执行什么任务？

faq_en.a: TanDEM-X (TDX) has been operational since 2010.
faq_en.q: How long has TanDEM-X been operational?

faq_en.a: TanDEM-X (TDX) is a joint mission by the German Space Centre (DLR) and Airbus Space and Defence.
faq_en.q: Which company/government agency owns TanDEM-X?

faq_en.a: TanDEM-X (TDX) performs Earth observation using the X-band Synthetic Aperture Radar. It records data concerning land, ocean and snow & ice processes such as albedo and reflectance, ocean topography and snow cover.
faq_en.q: What does TanDEM-X do in space?

eoportal.name: TecSAR (SAR Technology Demonstration Satellite)
eoportal.summary: IAI), the mission provides high resolution Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) imagery for military use.

faq.a: TecSAR was launched on January 21 2008 onboard a Polar Satellite Launch vehicle (PSLV) from the Satish Dhawan Space Center (SDSC) in Sriharikota, India.
faq.q: When was TecSAR launched?

faq.a: TecSAR is used by Israel’s Ministry of Defense (MoD) and government for military operations.
faq.q: What is TecSAR used for?

faq.a: TecSAR was designed and developed by government owned company Israel Aerospace Industries Ltd (IAI) formerly known as Israel Aircraft Industries Ltd.
faq.q: Who built TecSAR?

eoportal.summary_zh: TecSAR卫星由以色列航空工业公司（IAI）研制，其任务是为军事用途提供高分辨率合成孔径雷达（SAR）图像。
eoportal.summary_en: IAI), the mission provides high resolution Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) imagery for military use.

faq_zh.a: TecSAR于2008年1月21日由极地卫星运载火箭（PSLV）从印度斯里赫里戈达岛的萨迪什·达万航天中心（SDSC）发射升空。
faq_zh.q: TecSAR卫星是什么时候发射的？

faq_zh.a: TecSAR由以色列国防部（MoD）及政府用于军事行动。
TecSAR由国有公司以色列航空工业有限公司（IAI）设计和开发，该公司原名以色列飞机工业有限公司。

faq_en.a: TecSAR was launched on January 21 2008 onboard a Polar Satellite Launch vehicle (PSLV) from the Satish Dhawan Space Center (SDSC) in Sriharikota, India.
faq_en.q: When was TecSAR launched?

faq_en.a: TecSAR is used by Israel’s Ministry of Defense (MoD) and government for military operations.
faq_en.q: What is TecSAR used for?

faq_en.a: TecSAR was designed and developed by government owned company Israel Aerospace Industries Ltd (IAI) formerly known as Israel Aircraft Industries Ltd.

eoportal.name: TeLEOS-1 and -2
eoportal.summary: Together, the TeLEOS satellites aim to offer comprehensive, timely data solutions for disaster management, maritime surveillance, and environmental monitoring.

faq.a: The TeLEOS-1 mission intends to provide increased imaging opportunities over the equatorial region.
faq.q: What is the aim of the TeLEOS-1 mission?

faq.a: It is the first remote sensing commercial satellite operating in a Near Equatorial Orbit at 15° inclination, giving it an advantage over other polar orbit remote sensing satellites in observing the equatorial region.
faq.q: What separates TeLEOS-1 from other Earth observation satellites?

faq.a: Images captured by TeLEOS-1 are available from Apollo Mapping’s database.
faq.q: Where can TeLEOS-1 data be accessed from?

eoportal.summary_zh: TeLEOS 卫星系列旨在共同提供全面、及时的数据解决方案，服务于灾害管理、海事监视和环境监测等领域。
eoportal.summary_en: Together, the TeLEOS satellites aim to offer comprehensive, timely data solutions for disaster management, maritime surveillance, and environmental monitoring.

faq_zh.a: TeLEOS-1 任务旨在为赤道地区提供更多成像机会。
faq_zh.q: TeLEOS-1 任务的目标是什么？

faq_zh.a: 它是第一颗在15度倾角的近赤道轨道上运行的遥感商业卫星，与极轨遥感卫星相比，在观测赤道区域方面具有优势。
faq_zh.q: TeLEOS-1 与其他地球观测卫星有何不同？

faq_zh.a: Apollo Mapping 的数据库提供了 TeLEOS-1 拍摄的图像。
faq_zh.q: 可以从哪里获取 TeLEOS-1 的数据？

faq_en.a: The TeLEOS-1 mission intends to provide increased imaging opportunities over the equatorial region.
faq_en.q: What is the aim of the TeLEOS-1 mission?

faq_en.a: It is the first remote sensing commercial satellite operating in a Near Equatorial Orbit at 15° inclination, giving it an advantage over other polar orbit remote sensing satellites in observing the equatorial region.
faq_en.q: What separates TeLEOS-1 from other Earth observation satellites?

faq_en.a: Images captured by TeLEOS-1 are available from Apollo Mapping’s database.
faq_en.q: Where can TeLEOS-1 data be accessed from?

eoportal.name: TEMPEST-D (Temporal Experiment for Storms and Tropical Systems Technology - Demonstration)
eoportal.summary: Developed by  Colorado State University (CSU), the nanosatellite measured the temporal evolution of clouds until it was deorbited in June 2021.

faq.a: TEMPEST-D was developed by  Colorado State University (CSU).
faq.q: Who developed TEMPEST-D?

faq.a: TEMPEST-D was launched to the International Space Station (ISS) in May 2018 and was released into Low Earth Orbit in June 2018.
faq.q: When was TEMPEST-D launched?

faq.a: TEMPEST-D directly observed the time evolution of clouds and the conditions that control the transition from non-precipitating to precipitating clouds to improve understanding of cloud processes and precipitation, which is essential to an understanding of climate change.
faq.q: What was TEMPEST-D used for?

eoportal.name_zh: TEMPEST-D (风暴和热带系统时间实验技术 - 演示)
eoportal.summary_zh: 该纳卫星由科罗拉多州立大学 (CSU) 开发，用于测量云的时间演变，直至其于2021年6月离轨。
eoportal.summary_en: Developed by  Colorado State University (CSU), the nanosatellite measured the temporal evolution of clouds until it was deorbited in June 2021.

faq_zh.a: TEMPEST-D由科罗拉多州立大学 (CSU) 开发。
TEMPEST-D于2018年5月发射至国际空间站 (ISS)，并于2018年6月释放到近地轨道。
TEMPEST-D直接观测了云的时间演变以及控制云从非降水状态过渡到降水状态的条件，以增进对云过程和降水的理解，这对了解气候变化至关重要。

faq_en.a: TEMPEST-D was developed by  Colorado State University (CSU).
faq_en.q: Who developed TEMPEST-D?

faq_en.a: TEMPEST-D was launched to the International Space Station (ISS) in May 2018 and was released into Low Earth Orbit in June 2018.
faq_en.q: When was TEMPEST-D launched?

faq_en.a: TEMPEST-D directly observed the time evolution of clouds and the conditions that control the transition from non-precipitating to precipitating clouds to improve understanding of cloud processes and precipitation, which is essential to an understanding of climate change.
faq_en.q: What was TEMPEST-D used for?

tempest-d (temporal experiment for storm.display: TEMPEST-D (Temporal Experiment for Storms and Tropical Systems Technology - Demonstration)

eoportal.name: TEMPO (Tropospheric Emissions: Monitoring of Pollution)
eoportal.summary: TEMPO aims to monitor major air pollutants over the North American continent from a geostationary satellite.

faq.a: TEMPO (Tropospheric Emissions: Monitoring of Pollutants) is a single instrument mission, consisting of the TEMPO spectrometer which is to be carried onboard a commercial communications satellite.
faq.q: What is TEMPO?

faq.a: TEMPO is owned and operated by NASA, and carried as a payload of an Intelsat communications satellite.
faq.q: Who operates TEMPO?

faq.a: TEMPO aims to monitor major air pollutants over the North American continent from a geostationary viewpoint.
faq.q: What is the aim of TEMPO?

eoportal.summary_zh: TEMPO旨在从地球静止轨道卫星上监测北美大陆的主要空气污染物。
eoportal.summary_en: TEMPO aims to monitor major air pollutants over the North American continent from a geostationary satellite.

faq_zh.a: TEMPO（对流层排放：污染物监测）是一项单仪器任务，由搭载在商业通信卫星上的TEMPO光谱仪组成。
TEMPO由NASA所有并运营，作为Intelsat通信卫星的有效载荷搭载。
TEMPO旨在从地球静止轨道视角监测北美大陆的主要空气污染物。

eoportal.name: Terra (EOS/AM-1)
taxonomy: Cloud type, amount and cloud top temperature
Ocean imagery and water leaving spectral radiance
Downward long-wave irradiance at Earth surface
Sea ice cover, edge and thickness
Downward short-wave irradiance at Earth surface
Upwelling (Outgoing) long-wave radiation at Earth surface
eoportal.summary: With an original design life of six years, the operation has been extended to continue to provide an extensive variety of valuable data on Earth’s atmosphere, land surface, ocean and cryosphere.

faq.a: Terra uses five onboard instruments to provide extensive data on Earth’s atmosphere, land surface, ocean and cryosphere. Within the atmosphere it takes measurements on cloud properties, radiative energy flux, tropospheric chemistry, aerosol properties, atmospheric temperature and humidity. On the land surface it provides data on land cover and land use change, vegetation dynamics, surface temperature, fire occurrence and volcanic effects. In the ocean and cryosphere it can measure temperature, phytoplankton and dissolved organic matter, sea ice, snow cover and land ice change.
faq.q: What is the purpose of Terra?

faq.a: Launched in December 1999, Terra had an original design life of six years. However, its operation has been extended, with a predicted end-of-life in September 2023 (as of July 2022).
faq.q: Is Terra still working?

faq.a: Terra was developed by NASA as a part of their multi-national ESE (Earth Science Enterprise) program between the US, Japan and Canada. These countries each contributed the mission by developing one or more of the sensors onboard.
faq.q: Who developed Terra?

eoportal.summary_zh: Terra卫星最初设计寿命为六年，其运行时间已延长，旨在持续提供关于地球大气、陆地表面、海洋和冰冻圈的大量宝贵数据。
eoportal.summary_en: With an original design life of six years, the operation has been extended to continue to provide an extensive variety of valuable data on Earth’s atmosphere, land surface, ocean and cryosphere.

faq_zh.a: Terra卫星利用其搭载的五台仪器，提供关于地球大气、陆地表面、海洋和冰冻圈的广泛数据。在大气层内，它测量云特性、辐射能量通量、对流层化学成分、气溶胶特性、大气温湿度。在陆地表面，它提供地表覆盖和土地利用变化、植被动态、地表温度、火灾发生及火山影响的数据。在海洋和冰冻圈，它可以测量温度、浮游植物和溶解有机物、海冰、积雪覆盖和陆地冰变化。
Terra卫星于1999年12月发射，最初设计寿命为六年。然而，其任务运行已延长，预计寿命结束时间为2023年9月（截至2022年7月信息）。
Terra卫星由NASA作为其多国地球科学企业（ESE）计划的一部分研制，该计划由美国、日本和加拿大合作开展。这些国家各自贡献了卫星上搭载的一台或多台传感器。

faq_en.a: Terra uses five onboard instruments to provide extensive data on Earth’s atmosphere, land surface, ocean and cryosphere. Within the atmosphere it takes measurements on cloud properties, radiative energy flux, tropospheric chemistry, aerosol properties, atmospheric temperature and humidity. On the land surface it provides data on land cover and land use change, vegetation dynamics, surface temperature, fire occurrence and volcanic effects. In the ocean and cryosphere it can measure temperature, phytoplankton and dissolved organic matter, sea ice, snow cover and land ice change.
faq_en.q: What is the purpose of Terra?

faq_en.a: Launched in December 1999, Terra had an original design life of six years. However, its operation has been extended, with a predicted end-of-life in September 2023 (as of July 2022).
faq_en.q: Is Terra still working?

faq_en.a: Terra was developed by NASA as a part of their multi-national ESE (Earth Science Enterprise) program between the US, Japan and Canada. These countries each contributed the mission by developing one or more of the sensors onboard.
faq_en.q: Who developed Terra?

eoportal.name: TES (Technology Experiment Satellite)
eoportal.summary: The spacecraft is being operated by ISTRAC (ISRO Telemetry Tracking and Command Network) in Bangalore, India.
eoportal.summary_zh: 该航天器由位于印度班加罗尔的ISRO遥测、跟踪与指挥网络（ISTRAC）负责运营。
eoportal.summary_en: The spacecraft is being operated by ISTRAC (ISRO Telemetry Tracking and Command Network) in Bangalore, India.

eoportal.name: TET-1 (Technology Experiment Carrier-1)
eoportal.summary: The mission aimed to provide a method to validate space technology and remained active until September 2019.

faq.a: TET-1 was developed by Deutsches Zentrum für Luft- und Raumfahrt/German Aerospace Centre (DLR) and operated by GSOC (German Space Operations Centre) within DLR's Space Operations.
faq.q: Who operates TET-1?

faq.a: TET-1 can detect heat irregularities such as volcanoes, burning ships, industry hotspots, gas flares, chemical heat generation and smouldering fires via the Hot Spot Recognition System (HSRS).
faq.q: What phenomena can TET-1 detect?

faq.a: The data from TET-1 can be found in Global Climate Observing System Essential Climate Variables (GCOS ECVs) inventory records.
faq.q: Where can the data products from TET-1 be found?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该任务旨在提供一种验证空间技术的方法，并一直运行至2019年9月。
eoportal.summary_en: The mission aimed to provide a method to validate space technology and remained active until September 2019.

faq_zh.a: TET-1 由德国宇航中心（DLR）研发，并由 DLR 空间运营下属的德国空间操作中心（GSOC）负责运营。
TET-1 可通过热点识别系统（HSRS）探测热异常现象，例如火山、燃烧的船只、工业热点、天然气火炬、化学热产生以及阴燃火灾。
TET-1 的数据可在全球气候观测系统基本气候变量（GCOS ECVs）的清单记录中找到。
faq_zh.q: TET-1 的数据产品在哪里可以找到？

faq_en.a: TET-1 was developed by Deutsches Zentrum für Luft- und Raumfahrt/German Aerospace Centre (DLR) and operated by GSOC (German Space Operations Centre) within DLR's Space Operations.
faq_en.q: Who operates TET-1?

faq_en.a: TET-1 can detect heat irregularities such as volcanoes, burning ships, industry hotspots, gas flares, chemical heat generation and smouldering fires via the Hot Spot Recognition System (HSRS).
faq_en.q: What phenomena can TET-1 detect?

faq_en.a: The data from TET-1 can be found in Global Climate Observing System Essential Climate Variables (GCOS ECVs) inventory records.
faq_en.q: Where can the data products from TET-1 be found?

eoportal.name: The Carruthers Geocorona Observatory
eoportal.summary: The SmallSat has been selected as the science mission to accompany IMAP, and aims to study variability in the Earth’s exosphere from the inner Lagrangian point.

faq.a: The Carruthers Geocorona Observatory mission, originally known as GLIDE (Global Lyman-Alpha Imager of Dynamic Exosphere), was renamed in honour of Dr. George R. Carruthers in December 2022.
faq.q: Was the Carruthers Geocorona Observatory spacecraft renamed?

faq.a: The Carruthers Geocorona Observatory was launched along with IMAP on September 24, 2025, as a part of NASA’s RideShare policy in which excess launch capacity permits the launch of SmallSats with the primary payload.
faq.q: When was the Carruthers Geocorona Observatory launched?

faq.a: NASA has selected Ball Aerospace and University of California (UC) Berkeley to develop The Carruthers Geocorona Observatory mission.
faq.q: Who developed the Carruthers Geocorona Observatory?

eoportal.name_zh: 卡鲁瑟斯地球冕观测卫星
eoportal.summary_zh: 这颗小型卫星已被选为伴随星际测绘与加速探测器（IMAP）的科学任务，旨在从内拉格朗日点研究地球外逸层的变异性。
eoportal.summary_en: The SmallSat has been selected as the science mission to accompany IMAP, and aims to study variability in the Earth’s exosphere from the inner Lagrangian point.

faq_zh.a: 卡鲁瑟斯地球冕观测卫星任务最初名为GLIDE（全球莱曼-阿尔法动态外逸层成像仪），于2022年12月为纪念乔治·R·卡鲁瑟斯博士而更名。
faq_zh.q: 卡鲁瑟斯地球冕观测卫星是否被更名过？

faq_zh.a: 卡鲁瑟斯地球冕观测卫星于2025年9月24日与星际测绘与加速探测器（IMAP）一同发射，这是NASA搭载发射政策的一部分，利用主载荷的剩余发射能力搭载小型卫星。
faq_zh.q: 卡鲁瑟斯地球冕观测卫星何时发射？

faq_zh.a: NASA已选定鲍尔航空航天公司和加州大学伯克利分校来开发卡鲁瑟斯地球冕观测卫星任务。
faq_zh.q: 谁开发了卡鲁瑟斯地球冕观测卫星？

faq_en.a: The Carruthers Geocorona Observatory mission, originally known as GLIDE (Global Lyman-Alpha Imager of Dynamic Exosphere), was renamed in honour of Dr. George R. Carruthers in December 2022.
faq_en.q: Was the Carruthers Geocorona Observatory spacecraft renamed?

faq_en.a: The Carruthers Geocorona Observatory was launched along with IMAP on September 24, 2025, as a part of NASA’s RideShare policy in which excess launch capacity permits the launch of SmallSats with the primary payload.
faq_en.q: When was the Carruthers Geocorona Observatory launched?

faq_en.a: NASA has selected Ball Aerospace and University of California (UC) Berkeley to develop The Carruthers Geocorona Observatory mission.
faq_en.q: Who developed the Carruthers Geocorona Observatory?

eoportal.name: THEMIS (Time History of Events and Macroscale Interactions during Substorms)
eoportal.summary: In 2010, the two outer probes were redirected into a Lunar orbit to form a new mission named ARTEMIS (Acceleration, Reconnection, Turbulence, and Electrodynamics of the Moon's Interaction with the Sun) and collect information about the space environment of the moon.

faq.a: THEMIS was launched to help understand magnetospheric substorms (disturbances in Earth's magnetic field). Data collected by THEMIS probes have been used to determine when and where substorms begin, how the individual components of the substorm interact, and how local disruptions link with global substorm phenomena.
faq.q: What is the purpose of THEMIS?

faq.a: fter THEMIS successfully completed its primary mission in 2010, the two outer probes were sent into orbit around the Moon, and formed a new mission named ARTEMIS (Acceleration, Reconnection, Turbulence and Electrodynamics of the Moon’s Interaction with the Sun). These satellites systematically measure conditions in the  magnetotail — the distant tail of Earth’s magnetic environment. ARTEMIS also collects data on the lunar space environment and the Moon’s surface composition, magnetic field and core structure.
faq.q: What is the ARTEMIS mission?

faq.a: All five THEMIS spacecraft were launched as a secondary payload on the Delta-2-7925-10 vehicle on 17 February, 2007.  The launch site was CCAS (Cape Canaveral Air Station), Florida.
faq.q: When was THEMIS launched?

eoportal.name_zh: THEMIS（亚暴事件与宏观相互作用的时间历史卫星）
eoportal.summary_zh: 2010年，两颗外层探测器被重新定向进入月球轨道，组成了名为ARTEMIS（月球与太阳相互作用的加速、重联、湍流与电动力学探测）的新任务，以收集关于月球空间环境的信息。
eoportal.summary_en: In 2010, the two outer probes were redirected into a Lunar orbit to form a new mission named ARTEMIS (Acceleration, Reconnection, Turbulence, and Electrodynamics of the Moon's Interaction with the Sun) and collect information about the space environment of the moon.

faq_zh.a: THEMIS的发射旨在帮助理解磁层亚暴（地球磁场中的扰动）。THEMIS探测器收集的数据已被用于确定亚暴发生的时间和地点、亚暴各组成部分之间如何相互作用，以及局部扰动如何与全球亚暴现象相关联。
在THEMIS于2010年成功完成其主要任务后，两颗外层探测器被送入环绕月球的轨道，组成了名为ARTEMIS（月球与太阳相互作用的加速、重联、湍流与电动力学探测）的新任务。这些卫星系统地测量磁尾——地球磁环境的遥远尾部——中的状况。ARTEMIS还收集关于月球空间环境以及月球表面成分、磁场和核心结构的数据。
所有五颗THEMIS航天器都作为次要有效载荷于2007年2月17日搭载Delta-2-7925-10运载火箭发射升空。发射地点是佛罗里达州的卡纳维拉尔角空军基地。

faq_en.a: THEMIS was launched to help understand magnetospheric substorms (disturbances in Earth's magnetic field). Data collected by THEMIS probes have been used to determine when and where substorms begin, how the individual components of the substorm interact, and how local disruptions link with global substorm phenomena.
faq_en.q: What is the purpose of THEMIS?

faq_en.a: fter THEMIS successfully completed its primary mission in 2010, the two outer probes were sent into orbit around the Moon, and formed a new mission named ARTEMIS (Acceleration, Reconnection, Turbulence and Electrodynamics of the Moon’s Interaction with the Sun). These satellites systematically measure conditions in the  magnetotail — the distant tail of Earth’s magnetic environment. ARTEMIS also collects data on the lunar space environment and the Moon’s surface composition, magnetic field and core structure.
faq_en.q: What is the ARTEMIS mission?

faq_en.a: All five THEMIS spacecraft were launched as a secondary payload on the Delta-2-7925-10 vehicle on 17 February, 2007.  The launch site was CCAS (Cape Canaveral Air Station), Florida.
faq_en.q: When was THEMIS launched?

themis (time history of events and macro.display: THEMIS (Time History of Events and Macroscale Interactions during Substorms)

eoportal.name: TIMED (Thermosphere, Ionosphere, Mesosphere Energetics and Dynamics)
eoportal.summary: The mission aims to provide a better understanding of the properties of energy and its redistribution in the Mesosphere and Lower-Thermosphere/Ionosphere (MLTI) region.

faq.a: TIMED is a NASA mission aimed at deepening the understanding of the interaction between the sun and the upper region of the Earth’s atmosphere.
faq.q: What is the primary function of TIMED?

faq.a: By dividing the responsibility of instrument operation to each principal investigator, it allows the freedom of control for each instrument to take measurements as needed and operate without necessitating approvals for each variation.
faq.q: Why does TIMED have multiple centres for its operation?

faq.a: Due to the extensions of TIMED, it was able to observe an entire 11-year solar cycle, providing insight into longer solar trends like cooling and contraction of the upper atmosphere due to climate change.
faq.q: What has the extension of TIMED mission achieved?

eoportal.name_zh: TIMED（热层-电离层-中间层能量学与动力学探测卫星）
eoportal.summary_zh: 该任务旨在加深对中间层与低热层/电离层（MLTI）区域内能量特性及其再分配过程的理解。
eoportal.summary_en: The mission aims to provide a better understanding of the properties of energy and its redistribution in the Mesosphere and Lower-Thermosphere/Ionosphere (MLTI) region.

faq_zh.a: TIMED 是一项 NASA 任务，旨在深化对太阳与地球大气上层区域相互作用的理解。
faq_zh.q: TIMED 的主要功能是什么？

faq_zh.a: 通过将仪器操作职责分配给各个首席调查员，每个仪器都能自由地根据需要进行测量，并且无需为每次调整申请批准即可运行。
faq_zh.q: 为什么 TIMED 的运作涉及多个中心？

faq_zh.a: 由于 TIMED 任务的延期，它得以观测了整个 11 年的太阳活动周期，从而深入了解了更长期的太阳变化趋势，例如因气候变化导致的高层大气冷却和收缩。
faq_zh.q: TIMED 任务的延期取得了什么成果？

faq_en.a: TIMED is a NASA mission aimed at deepening the understanding of the interaction between the sun and the upper region of the Earth’s atmosphere.
faq_en.q: What is the primary function of TIMED?

faq_en.a: By dividing the responsibility of instrument operation to each principal investigator, it allows the freedom of control for each instrument to take measurements as needed and operate without necessitating approvals for each variation.
faq_en.q: Why does TIMED have multiple centres for its operation?

faq_en.a: Due to the extensions of TIMED, it was able to observe an entire 11-year solar cycle, providing insight into longer solar trends like cooling and contraction of the upper atmosphere due to climate change.
faq_en.q: What has the extension of TIMED mission achieved?

eoportal.name: Tracking and Data Relay Satellites (TDRS)
eoportal.summary: Launching since April 1983, there are seven operational TDRS platforms as of 2026, four of which are in on-orbit storage to provide redundancy.

faq.a: TDRS was developed to accommodate the needs of a growing fleet of NASA-operated satellites, as the original system of ground stations provided coverage for only approximately 15% of a satellite’s orbit. TDRS allowed for this coverage to increase to 95%, with only the polar regions not covered.
faq.q: What is the purpose of NASA’s Tracking and Data Relay Satellites (TDRS)?

faq.a: TDRSS  has launched 13 satellites over its lifetime, with only three required for operational service.
faq.q: How many TDRSS satellites are there?

faq.a: The main TDRS ground facility is in White Sands, New Mexico. However, remote stations also operate in Guam and Blossom Point, Maryland.
faq.q: Where is the TDRS ground station?

eoportal.summary_zh: 自1983年4月首次发射以来，截至2026年，共有七颗TDRS平台在轨运行，其中四颗处于在轨存储状态以提供冗余备份。
eoportal.summary_en: Launching since April 1983, there are seven operational TDRS platforms as of 2026, four of which are in on-orbit storage to provide redundancy.

faq_zh.a: TDRS旨在满足NASA日益增长的卫星舰队的需求，因为原有的地面站系统仅能覆盖卫星轨道的约15%。TDRS将覆盖范围提升至95%，仅极地地区无法覆盖。
faq_zh.q: NASA的跟踪与数据中继卫星（TDRS）有何用途？

faq_zh.a: TDRS系统在其生命周期内共发射了13颗卫星，其中仅需三颗即可满足运行服务需求。
TDRS的主要地面设施位于美国新墨西哥州白沙。此外，在关岛和美国马里兰州布洛萨姆角也设有远程站点。

faq_en.a: TDRS was developed to accommodate the needs of a growing fleet of NASA-operated satellites, as the original system of ground stations provided coverage for only approximately 15% of a satellite’s orbit. TDRS allowed for this coverage to increase to 95%, with only the polar regions not covered.
faq_en.q: What is the purpose of NASA’s Tracking and Data Relay Satellites (TDRS)?

faq_en.a: TDRSS  has launched 13 satellites over its lifetime, with only three required for operational service.
faq_en.q: How many TDRSS satellites are there?

faq_en.a: The main TDRS ground facility is in White Sands, New Mexico. However, remote stations also operate in Guam and Blossom Point, Maryland.
faq_en.q: Where is the TDRS ground station?

faq.a: TRISHNA scientific objectives include increasing understanding of ecosystem stress and water use, coastal and inland waters, urban microclimate monitoring, solid earth, the cryosphere, and the atmosphere.

eoportal.name: Triton (FormoSat-7R)
eoportal.summary: Equipped with a Global Navigation Satellite System Reflectometry (GNSS-R) receiver, it uses reflected GNSS signals to derive Earth surface characteristics for applications in weather forecasting, disaster management, and climate research.

faq.a: The Triton mission was originally named FormoSat-7R, and was intended as an independent thirteenth satellite in the planned Constellation Observing System for Meteorology, Ionosphere, and Climate 2 (COSMIC-2), known as FormoSat-7, before the constellation was subsequently reduced to Triton and six FormoSat-7 satellites.
faq.q: Is Triton also named FormoSat-7R?

faq.a: Triton has a 82% domestic production rate, with Taiwanese company Aerospace Industrial Development Corporation (AIDC) leading the satellite integration of components supplied by Taiwanese companies such as Fongshen Technology and the Syscom Computer. The satellite was constructed by UK Aerospace company Surrey Satellite Technology Limited (SSTL).
faq.q: Was Triton fully developed in Taiwan?

faq.a: Triton carries a Global Navigation Satellite System Reflectometry (GNSS-R) payload, which is a space grade GPS receiver. It receives signals reflected from the Earth’s surface in low Earth orbit (LEO) emitted by GNSS constellations from which it derives surface parameters for use in typhoon and weather predictions, and research.
faq.q: What instruments are onboard Triton?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该卫星搭载全球导航卫星系统反射计接收机，利用接收到的GNSS反射信号来推导地表特征，可用于天气预报、灾害管理和气候研究等应用。
eoportal.summary_en: Equipped with a Global Navigation Satellite System Reflectometry (GNSS-R) receiver, it uses reflected GNSS signals to derive Earth surface characteristics for applications in weather forecasting, disaster management, and climate research.

faq_zh.a: Triton任务最初被命名为福卫七号R星（FormoSat-7R），原计划作为“气象、电离层和气候星座观测系统2”（COSMIC-2，即福卫七号）星座的独立第十三颗卫星，后来该星座缩减为Triton星和六颗福卫七号卫星。
Triton有82%的国产化率，由台湾的汉翔航空工业股份有限公司（AIDC）主导卫星集成，其部件由台湾企业如丰辰科技和神通电脑等提供。该卫星由英国航空航天公司萨里卫星技术有限公司（SSTL）建造。
faq_zh.q: Triton完全是在台湾开发的吗？

faq_zh.a: Triton携带了一个全球导航卫星系统反射计载荷，它是一个空间级GPS接收机。它在低地球轨道上接收由GNSS星座发射、经地球表面反射的信号，从中推导地表参数，用于台风和天气预测以及研究。

faq_en.a: The Triton mission was originally named FormoSat-7R, and was intended as an independent thirteenth satellite in the planned Constellation Observing System for Meteorology, Ionosphere, and Climate 2 (COSMIC-2), known as FormoSat-7, before the constellation was subsequently reduced to Triton and six FormoSat-7 satellites.
faq_en.q: Is Triton also named FormoSat-7R?

faq_en.a: Triton has a 82% domestic production rate, with Taiwanese company Aerospace Industrial Development Corporation (AIDC) leading the satellite integration of components supplied by Taiwanese companies such as Fongshen Technology and the Syscom Computer. The satellite was constructed by UK Aerospace company Surrey Satellite Technology Limited (SSTL).
faq_en.q: Was Triton fully developed in Taiwan?

faq_en.a: Triton carries a Global Navigation Satellite System Reflectometry (GNSS-R) payload, which is a space grade GPS receiver. It receives signals reflected from the Earth’s surface in low Earth orbit (LEO) emitted by GNSS constellations from which it derives surface parameters for use in typhoon and weather predictions, and research.
faq_en.q: What instruments are onboard Triton?

eoportal.name: TRMM (Tropical Rainfall Measuring Mission)
taxonomy: Cloud type, amount and cloud top temperature
Ocean imagery and water leaving spectral radiance
Downward long-wave irradiance at Earth surface
Precipitation intensity at the surface (liquid or solid)
Downward short-wave irradiance at Earth surface
Upwelling (Outgoing) long-wave radiation at Earth surface
eoportal.summary: As part of the NASA Earth Science Enterprise (ESE) program, TRMM hosted a variety of instruments that gathered information about precipitation and latent heating between the Tropics of Capricorn and Cancer, to further our understanding of global energy, water cycles and climate.

faq.a: The Tropical Rainfall Measuring Mission (TRMM) was a research satellite that operated between 1997 and 2015. It hosted five sensors onboard that monitored and gathered data about precipitation and latent heating within the tropics and subtropics, orbiting the Earth every 93 minutes.
faq.q: What does TRMM do?

faq.a: TRMM had several instruments onboard which collected many different datasets about precipitation in the Tropics. TRMM instruments include cloud profile and rain radars, imaging multispectral radiometers and lightning sensors, which produce datasets including 3D rainfall distribution over land and oceans, rain rate, sea surface temperatures, wind speeds, cloud types, and lightning flash data.
faq.q: What is TRMM data?

faq.a: The goal of the TRMM satellite was to monitor and study precipitation and latent heating over the global Tropics. The data collected is used to increase our understanding of Earth’s global energy and water cycles and contribute to better modelling and predictions of Earth’s climate system.
faq.q: What is the goal of the TRMM satellite and its instruments?

eoportal.summary_zh: 作为NASA地球科学企业计划的一部分，TRMM卫星搭载了多种仪器，用于收集南北回归线之间热带地区的降水和潜热信息，以加深我们对全球能量、水循环和气候的理解。
eoportal.summary_en: As part of the NASA Earth Science Enterprise (ESE) program, TRMM hosted a variety of instruments that gathered information about precipitation and latent heating between the Tropics of Capricorn and Cancer, to further our understanding of global energy, water cycles and climate.

faq_zh.a: 热带降雨测量卫星（TRMM）是一颗于1997年至2015年间运行的研究型卫星。它搭载了五种传感器，用于监测和收集热带及亚热带地区的降水和潜热数据，每93分钟绕地球运行一周。
TRMM卫星搭载了多种仪器，收集了大量关于热带降水的不同数据集。这些仪器包括云廓线雷达、降雨雷达、成像多光谱辐射计和闪电传感器，可产生包括陆地和海洋上的三维降雨分布、降雨率、海表温度、风速、云类型以及闪电数据在内的数据集。
TRMM卫星的目标是监测和研究全球热带地区的降水和潜热。所收集的数据用于加深我们对地球全球能量和水循环的理解，并有助于改进地球气候系统的模拟和预测。
faq_zh.q: TRMM卫星及其仪器的目标是什么？

faq_en.a: The Tropical Rainfall Measuring Mission (TRMM) was a research satellite that operated between 1997 and 2015. It hosted five sensors onboard that monitored and gathered data about precipitation and latent heating within the tropics and subtropics, orbiting the Earth every 93 minutes.
faq_en.q: What does TRMM do?

faq_en.a: TRMM had several instruments onboard which collected many different datasets about precipitation in the Tropics. TRMM instruments include cloud profile and rain radars, imaging multispectral radiometers and lightning sensors, which produce datasets including 3D rainfall distribution over land and oceans, rain rate, sea surface temperatures, wind speeds, cloud types, and lightning flash data.
faq_en.q: What is TRMM data?

faq_en.a: The goal of the TRMM satellite was to monitor and study precipitation and latent heating over the global Tropics. The data collected is used to increase our understanding of Earth’s global energy and water cycles and contribute to better modelling and predictions of Earth’s climate system.
faq_en.q: What is the goal of the TRMM satellite and its instruments?

faq.a: TROPICS aims to measure temperature, humidity, and precipitation so that we may better understand the evolution and structure of storms.

eoportal.name: TRUTHS (Traceable Radiometry Underpinning Terrestrial-and Helio-Studies)
taxonomy: Downward short-wave irradiance at Earth surface
Upwelling (Outgoing) long-wave radiation at Earth surface
Upwelling (Outgoing) Short-wave Radiation at the Earth Surface

faq.a: Twilight conditions will be limited by orbit selection. Calibration generally takes place during the Earth shadow part of the orbit, allowing the systems to be well-protected from solar and other radiation damage. For example, spectral channels can be calibrated at a rate of at least one wavelength per orbit on the Earth shadow side of the orbit.
faq.q: What will TRUTHS scanning do while in twilight conditions and in Earth’s shadow?

faq.a: Using the Hyperspectral Imaging Spectrometer, TRUTHS will radiometrically calibrate the moon allowing it to be used as a true SI transfer standard. This would allow historic missions to be re-calibrated and Fundamental Climate Data Records (FCDRs) to be extended backwards in time with greater accuracy, since many sensors have been observing the moon.
faq.q: Will TRUTHS perform lunar calibrations?

faq.a:
The relatively unique non-sun-synchronous orbit of TRUTHS, whilst providing many observational benefits, presents significant additional challenges to both the spacecraft and payload. Varying thermal and solar viewing (power) conditions adds complexity to the mission.
To account for this, calibrations of the Hyperspectral Imaging Spectrometer will be performed under a variety of simulated and real orbit conditions to assess any variances due to inadequate thermal stabilities.
faq.q: What are some challenges TRUTHS is predicted to face?

eoportal.name_zh: TRUTHS（可溯源辐射测量支撑陆地与太阳研究卫星）

faq_zh.a: 暮光条件下的观测会受到轨道选择的限制。卫星的校准工作通常在地球阴影区内进行，这样可以使仪器系统免受太阳及其他辐射的损害。例如，光谱通道可以在轨道的阴影侧以每个轨道至少一个波长的速率进行校准。
faq_zh.q: TRUTHS在暮光条件及地球阴影区内会进行怎样的扫描操作？

faq_zh.a: 会。TRUTHS将利用高光谱成像光谱仪对月球进行辐射定标，使其成为一个真正的国际单位制（SI）传递标准。这将允许对历史任务进行重新定标，并更准确地将基础气候数据记录（FCDRs）向更早的时间回溯，因为许多传感器都曾观测过月球。
TRUTHS采用的非太阳同步轨道相对独特，虽然在观测方面具有诸多优势，但也给航天器和有效载荷带来了显著的额外挑战。变化的热环境和太阳观测（供电）条件增加了任务的复杂性。为此，将在各种模拟和真实轨道条件下对高光谱成像光谱仪进行校准，以评估因热稳定性不足而产生的任何偏差。

faq_en.a: Twilight conditions will be limited by orbit selection. Calibration generally takes place during the Earth shadow part of the orbit, allowing the systems to be well-protected from solar and other radiation damage. For example, spectral channels can be calibrated at a rate of at least one wavelength per orbit on the Earth shadow side of the orbit.
faq_en.q: What will TRUTHS scanning do while in twilight conditions and in Earth’s shadow?

faq_en.a: Using the Hyperspectral Imaging Spectrometer, TRUTHS will radiometrically calibrate the moon allowing it to be used as a true SI transfer standard. This would allow historic missions to be re-calibrated and Fundamental Climate Data Records (FCDRs) to be extended backwards in time with greater accuracy, since many sensors have been observing the moon.
faq_en.q: Will TRUTHS perform lunar calibrations?

faq_en.a:
The relatively unique non-sun-synchronous orbit of TRUTHS, whilst providing many observational benefits, presents significant additional challenges to both the spacecraft and payload. Varying thermal and solar viewing (power) conditions adds complexity to the mission.
To account for this, calibrations of the Hyperspectral Imaging Spectrometer will be performed under a variety of simulated and real orbit conditions to assess any variances due to inadequate thermal stabilities.
faq_en.q: What are some challenges TRUTHS is predicted to face?

eoportal.name: TSX (TerraSAR-X)
taxonomy: Ocean imagery and water leaving spectral radiance
Sea ice cover, edge and thickness
eoportal.summary: It provides high-resolution X-band data for use in climatology, environmental and disaster monitoring, hydrology, geology, oceanography and cartography.

faq.a: TSX uses an X-band SAR sensor for scientific applications in the fields of hydrology, geology, climatology, oceanography and disaster monitoring systems.
faq.q: What is TerraSAR-X (TSX) used for?

faq.a: As of June 2022, TSX is still operational. TSX has completed its expected service of five years but remains in a condition that is still functional. The satellite is expected to serve several more years before deorbiting.
faq.q: Is TerraSAR-X still operational?

faq.a: Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) works via transmitting microwave signals and then receiving the microwave signals that have returned. The SAR instrument functions by measuring the time taken to receive a signal back to the Earth's surface, and uses this to calculate distance.
faq.q: How does synthetic aperture radar work on TSX?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该卫星提供高分辨率X波段数据，广泛应用于气候学、环境与灾害监测、水文、地质、海洋学和制图等领域。
eoportal.summary_en: It provides high-resolution X-band data for use in climatology, environmental and disaster monitoring, hydrology, geology, oceanography and cartography.

faq_zh.a: TSX 使用 X 波段 SAR 传感器，为水文、地质、气候学、海洋学和灾害监测系统等领域的科学应用提供服务。
截至 2022 年 6 月，TSX 仍在运行。该卫星已超出其预期的五年服务寿命，但目前功能依然正常，预计将在退役前继续服役数年。
合成孔径雷达（SAR）通过发射微波信号并接收从地球表面返回的信号来工作。它通过测量信号返回所需的时间来计算距离。
faq_zh.q: TSX 上的合成孔径雷达是如何工作的？

faq_en.a: TSX uses an X-band SAR sensor for scientific applications in the fields of hydrology, geology, climatology, oceanography and disaster monitoring systems.
faq_en.q: What is TerraSAR-X (TSX) used for?

faq_en.a: As of June 2022, TSX is still operational. TSX has completed its expected service of five years but remains in a condition that is still functional. The satellite is expected to serve several more years before deorbiting.
faq_en.q: Is TerraSAR-X still operational?

faq_en.a: Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) works via transmitting microwave signals and then receiving the microwave signals that have returned. The SAR instrument functions by measuring the time taken to receive a signal back to the Earth's surface, and uses this to calculate distance.
faq_en.q: How does synthetic aperture radar work on TSX?

eoportal.name: TSX-NG (TerraSAR-X Next Generation)
eoportal.summary: TSX-NG was planned for launch in 2018 but was cancelled before this.

faq.a: The TSX-NG mission was planned for launch in 2018, but has since been cancelled.
faq.q: Has TSX-NG been launched?

faq.a: TSX-NG was planned to collect high-resolution X-band synthetic aperture radar (SAR) data, however the mission was cancelled. Comparable data is currently being collected by  TerraSAR-X and TanDEM-X missions.
faq.q: What data will TSX-NG collect?

faq.a: The planned applications of TSX-NG SAR data included the creation of DEMs (Digital Elevation Models), monitoring critical infrastructure, and situational awareness and maritime applications. However, no data will be obtained by the TSX-NG mission as it has been cancelled.
faq.q: What will TSX-NG SAR (Synthetic Aperture Radar) data be used for?

eoportal.summary_zh: TSX-NG卫星原计划于2018年发射，但在此之前任务被取消。

计划中TSX-NG SAR数据的应用包括生成数字高程模型（DEM）、监测关键基础设施以及态势感知和海事应用。然而，由于任务已被取消，TSX-NG将不会获取任何数据。

eoportal.name: Van Allen Probes
eoportal.summary: The two probes were launched in August 2012 and decommissioned in 2019.

faq.a: The objective of Van Allen Probes was to study the Van Allen Radiation belts and their effect on the spacecrafts electronics and materials in orbit.
faq.q: What did Van Allen Probes do?

faq.a: The provision of two probes enabled simultaneous two-point measurements by identical spacecraft. This helped determine if an event occurred throughout the belts or travelled across the belts.
faq.q: Why are there two Van Allen Probes?

faq.a: Van Allen probes were launched in August 2012 from Cape Canaveral, USA.
faq.q: When did Van Allen Probes launch?

eoportal.name: Veery Constellation
taxonomy: Sea ice cover, edge and thickness
eoportal.summary: The constellation currently consists of 15 pathfinder and technology demonstration satellites, with the earliest launched in March 2021.

faq.a: The Veery constellation aims to provide low-cost, high frequency and global radio scatterometer observations of ocean wind speed and direction, soil moisture content and sea ice coverage.
faq.q: What is the aim of the Veery constellation?

faq.a: There are 15 pathfinder or technology demonstration Veery satellites, however, none carry the compact scatterometer to conduct the intended measurements, with the final constellation planned to consist of 8-12 satellites.
faq.q: How many Veery satellites are there?

faq.a: The Veery scatterometer will have a spatial resolution of 25 km for individual satellites, but can be processed to 6 km resolution using supplementary imagery from other satellites in the constellation.
faq.q: What is the spatial resolution of the Veery compact scatterometer?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该星座目前由15颗探路者和技术验证卫星组成，其中最早的一颗于2021年3月发射。
eoportal.summary_en: The constellation currently consists of 15 pathfinder and technology demonstration satellites, with the earliest launched in March 2021.

faq_zh.a: Veery 星座旨在提供低成本、高频率、全球覆盖的无线电散射计观测，用于测量海洋风速与风向、土壤含水量以及海冰覆盖范围。

faq_zh.a: 目前有15颗 Veery 探路者或技术验证卫星，但它们均未搭载用于执行预定测量任务的紧凑型散射计。最终星座计划由8至12颗卫星组成。
faq_zh.q: 有多少颗 Veery 卫星？

faq_zh.a: 单颗 Veery 卫星的散射计空间分辨率为25公里，但通过处理星座中其他卫星的补充影像，可将分辨率提升至6公里。
faq_zh.q: Veery 紧凑型散射计的空间分辨率是多少？

faq_en.a: The Veery constellation aims to provide low-cost, high frequency and global radio scatterometer observations of ocean wind speed and direction, soil moisture content and sea ice coverage.
faq_en.q: What is the aim of the Veery constellation?

faq_en.a: There are 15 pathfinder or technology demonstration Veery satellites, however, none carry the compact scatterometer to conduct the intended measurements, with the final constellation planned to consist of 8-12 satellites.

eoportal.name: VENuS (Vegetation and Environment monitoring on a New MicroSatellite)
eoportal.summary: It is a scientific mission, with the goal of vegetation and environment monitoring, and a technological mission, with the goal of testing the IHET in orbit maintenance and transfer.

faq.a: The VENµS mission is a joint program by the French CNES (National Centre for Space Studies) and ISA (Israel Space Agency).
faq.q: Who owns VENµS?

faq.a: The VENµS mission has two objectives, to monitor vegetation in 50 select areas, and to test a pair of HET (Hall Effect Thrusters), in terms of orbit maintenance and transfer.
faq.q: What does VENµS do?

faq.a: The VSSC (VENµS SuperSpectral Camera) operates in 12 bands in the Visible and Near-Infrared regions.

eoportal.name_zh: VENuS（Vegetation and Environment monitoring on a New MicroSatellite）
eoportal.agency_zh: CNES（法国国家空间研究中心）、ISA（以色列航天局）
eoportal.summary_zh: 这是一项兼具科学目标与技术验证的飞行任务。其科学目标是监测植被与环境，技术目标是在轨道维持和转移过程中对离子混合电推进系统（IHET）进行在轨测试。
eoportal.summary_en: It is a scientific mission, with the goal of vegetation and environment monitoring, and a technological mission, with the goal of testing the IHET in orbit maintenance and transfer.

faq_zh.a: VENµS 任务是由法国国家空间研究中心（CNES）和以色列航天局（ISA）联合开展的项目。
VENµS 任务有两个主要目标：一是监测选定的50个区域的植被，二是测试一对用于轨道维持和转移的霍尔效应推力器（HET）。
VENµS 的超光谱相机（VSSC）在可见光和近红外区域共有12个波段。

faq_en.a: The VENµS mission is a joint program by the French CNES (National Centre for Space Studies) and ISA (Israel Space Agency).
faq_en.q: Who owns VENµS?

faq_en.a: The VENµS mission has two objectives, to monitor vegetation in 50 select areas, and to test a pair of HET (Hall Effect Thrusters), in terms of orbit maintenance and transfer.

faq_en.a: The VSSC (VENµS SuperSpectral Camera) operates in 12 bands in the Visible and Near-Infrared regions.

eoportal.name: WildFireSat
taxonomy: Cloud type, amount and cloud top temperature
Downward long-wave irradiance at Earth surface
Sea ice cover, edge and thickness
Upwelling (Outgoing) Short-wave Radiation at the Earth Surface
eoportal.summary: The WildFireSat system will consist of one or more microsatellites in Low Earth Orbit, each carrying a purpose built multispectral radiometer.

faq.a: WildFireSat aims to monitor active wildfires through an infrared sensing satellite system, to help manage the extreme fire seasons experienced in Canada.
faq.q: What is the aim of WildFireSat?

faq.a: CWFMS primarily measures Fire Radiative Power (FRP), the rate of outgoing thermal energy from an active fire, integrated across all wavelengths.
faq.q: What does the WildFireSat instrument CWFMS measure?

faq.a: WildFireSat will operate in a low Earth, sun synchronous orbit at an altitude of 650 km, with an orbital inclination of 98° and an orbit period of 98 minutes.
faq.q: What are the WildFireSat orbit details?

eoportal.agency_zh: CSA（加拿大航天局）、NRCAN（加拿大自然资源部）、ECCC（加拿大环境与气候变化部）
eoportal.summary_zh: WildFireSat系统将由一颗或多颗运行在低地球轨道的微卫星组成，每颗卫星将搭载一台专为野火监测设计的多光谱辐射计。
eoportal.summary_en: The WildFireSat system will consist of one or more microsatellites in Low Earth Orbit, each carrying a purpose built multispectral radiometer.

faq_zh.a: WildFireSat旨在通过红外传感卫星系统监测活跃的野火，以帮助加拿大应对极端的火灾季节。
CWFMS主要测量火辐射功率（FRP），即活跃火场向外释放的热能速率，该测量整合了所有波长的数据。
WildFireSat将运行在高度为650公里、轨道倾角为98°的太阳同步低地球轨道上，轨道周期为98分钟。

faq_en.a: WildFireSat aims to monitor active wildfires through an infrared sensing satellite system, to help manage the extreme fire seasons experienced in Canada.
faq_en.q: What is the aim of WildFireSat?

faq_en.a: CWFMS primarily measures Fire Radiative Power (FRP), the rate of outgoing thermal energy from an active fire, integrated across all wavelengths.
faq_en.q: What does the WildFireSat instrument CWFMS measure?

faq_en.a: WildFireSat will operate in a low Earth, sun synchronous orbit at an altitude of 650 km, with an orbital inclination of 98° and an orbit period of 98 minutes.

eoportal.name: XCUBE
eoportal.summary: The satellite was launched on 21 December 2024 and is designed to capture hyperspectral images for applications in Earth Observation (EO), space domain awareness, maritime surveillance, and astronomy.

faq.a: XCUBE is operated by Xplore, a US-based commercial space company focused on providing advanced remote sensing data. The mission supports Xplore's vision to build the XCRAFT satellite constellation.
faq.q: Who operates XCUBE?

faq.a: XCUBE was successfully launched into orbit on December 21, 2024, at 3:34 a.m. PST onboard a SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket, as a part of SpaceX’s Bandwagon-2 rideshare mission.
faq.q: When was XCUBE launched?

faq.a: XCUBE carries the Simera HyperScape100 hyperspectral camera, delivering a spatial resolution of 4.2 - 4.8 m per pixel, with a 19.4 km swath width, using 1 nm spectral increments across the 442 - 884 nm wavelength range.
faq.q: What is XCUBE's imaging resolution and coverage?

eoportal.summary_zh: 该卫星于2024年12月21日发射，旨在获取高光谱图像，应用于地球观测、空间态势感知、海事监控和天文学等领域。
eoportal.summary_en: The satellite was launched on 21 December 2024 and is designed to capture hyperspectral images for applications in Earth Observation (EO), space domain awareness, maritime surveillance, and astronomy.

faq_zh.a: XCUBE由Xplore运营，这是一家总部位于美国的商业航天公司，专注于提供先进的遥感数据。该任务支持Xplore建设XCRAFT卫星星座的愿景。
XCUBE于2024年12月21日太平洋标准时间凌晨3:34成功发射入轨，搭乘SpaceX的猎鹰9号火箭，作为SpaceX“Bandwagon-2”拼车任务的一部分。
XCUBE搭载了Simera HyperScape100高光谱相机，空间分辨率为每像素4.2至4.8米，幅宽为19.4公里，在442至884纳米波长范围内以1纳米的光谱增量进行数据采集。
faq_zh.q: XCUBE的成像分辨率和覆盖范围是多少？

faq_en.a: XCUBE is operated by Xplore, a US-based commercial space company focused on providing advanced remote sensing data. The mission supports Xplore's vision to build the XCRAFT satellite constellation.
faq_en.q: Who operates XCUBE?

faq_en.a: XCUBE was successfully launched into orbit on December 21, 2024, at 3:34 a.m. PST onboard a SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket, as a part of SpaceX’s Bandwagon-2 rideshare mission.
faq_en.q: When was XCUBE launched?

faq_en.a: XCUBE carries the Simera HyperScape100 hyperspectral camera, delivering a spatial resolution of 4.2 - 4.8 m per pixel, with a 19.4 km swath width, using 1 nm spectral increments across the 442 - 884 nm wavelength range.
faq_en.q: What is XCUBE's imaging resolution and coverage?

eoportal.name: XPoSAT
eoportal.summary: XPoSat was successfully launched on January 1, 2024.

faq.a: XPoSat aims to study various dynamics of bright X-ray sources in extreme conditions, including the emission mechanisms of black holes and pulsars.
faq.q: What is the aim of XPoSat?

faq.a: The satellite carries the X-ray Spectroscopy and Timing (XSPECT), and the Polarimeter Instrument in X-rays (POLIX).
faq.q: What instruments does XPoSat carry?

faq.a: The satellite was successfully launched aboard ISRO’s PSLV-C58 mission on January 1, 2024.
faq.q: When was XPoSat launched?

eoportal.summary_zh: XPoSat 于 2024 年 1 月 1 日成功发射。
eoportal.summary_en: XPoSat was successfully launched on January 1, 2024.

faq_zh.a: XPoSat 旨在研究极端条件下明亮 X 射线源的各种动力学过程，包括黑洞和脉冲星的发射机制。
该卫星搭载了 X 射线光谱与定时仪（XSPECT）以及 X 射线偏振仪（POLIX）。
该卫星于 2024 年 1 月 1 日搭载 ISRO 的 PSLV-C58 火箭成功发射。

eoportal.name: Yunyao Aerospace Constellation
taxonomy: Soil moisture in the roots region
eoportal.summary: This is achieved using Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS) Radio Occultation (GNSS-RO) and GNSS Reflectometry (GNSS-R) instruments.

faq.a: The first phase of the YunYao Aerospace Constellation includes 90 LEO nano-satellites, all part of the YunYao-1 series.
faq.q: How many satellites are included in the first phase of the YunYao Aerospace Constellation?

faq.a: YunYao-1 satellites carry the YunYao Receiver, which combines GNSS-R and GNSS-RO instruments into a single unit. It collects atmospheric and surface data using signals from GNSS constellations. The GNSS-RO component is used to gather temperature, humidity, and electron density profiles.
faq.q: What instruments are carried aboard YunYao-1 satellites?

faq.a: The GNSS-RO measurements are highly accurate, with an absolute mean bias of less than 1.5%, making the data suitable for weather forecasting and scientific research. Accuracy is highest between altitudes of 4 km and 40 km. The precision can vary at lower altitudes due to factors such as atmospheric moisture and signal interference.
faq.q: How accurate are GNSS-RO measurements from YunYao satellites?

eoportal.name_zh: 云遥宇航星座（Yunyao Aerospace Constellation）
eoportal.summary_zh: 该星座通过搭载全球导航卫星系统（GNSS）无线电掩星（GNSS-RO）和 GNSS 反射测量（GNSS-R）仪器来实现其功能。
eoportal.summary_en: This is achieved using Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS) Radio Occultation (GNSS-RO) and GNSS Reflectometry (GNSS-R) instruments.

faq_zh.a: 云遥宇航星座第一阶段包括90颗近地轨道纳米卫星，均属于云遥-1系列。
faq_zh.q: 云遥宇航星座第一阶段包含多少颗卫星？

faq_zh.a: 云遥-1卫星搭载了云遥接收器，该接收器将GNSS-R和GNSS-RO仪器集成在一个单元中。它利用GNSS星座的信号来收集大气和地表数据。其中，GNSS-RO组件用于获取温度、湿度和电子密度廓线。
faq_zh.q: 云遥-1卫星搭载了哪些仪器？

faq_zh.a: GNSS-RO测量精度很高，绝对平均偏差小于1.5%，因此数据适用于天气预报和科学研究。在4公里至40公里的高度范围内精度最高。在较低高度，由于大气湿度和信号干扰等因素，精度可能有所变化。
faq_zh.q: 云遥卫星的GNSS-RO测量精度如何？

faq_en.a: The first phase of the YunYao Aerospace Constellation includes 90 LEO nano-satellites, all part of the YunYao-1 series.
faq_en.q: How many satellites are included in the first phase of the YunYao Aerospace Constellation?

faq_en.a: YunYao-1 satellites carry the YunYao Receiver, which combines GNSS-R and GNSS-RO instruments into a single unit. It collects atmospheric and surface data using signals from GNSS constellations. The GNSS-RO component is used to gather temperature, humidity, and electron density profiles.
faq_en.q: What instruments are carried aboard YunYao-1 satellites?

faq_en.a: The GNSS-RO measurements are highly accurate, with an absolute mean bias of less than 1.5%, making the data suitable for weather forecasting and scientific research. Accuracy is highest between altitudes of 4 km and 40 km. The precision can vary at lower altitudes due to factors such as atmospheric moisture and signal interference.
faq_en.q: How accurate are GNSS-RO measurements from YunYao satellites?
